news_summary: US non-farm payrolls beat expectations, lifting the US dollar and pressuring copper. Fed/BoE signals support earlier monetary tightening. On the supply side, China’s cathode output is higher, concentrate TCs rose, and smelters stay profitable, while imported inflows and off-season inventory weigh on prices. Net: rangebound SHFE/LME copper. | SMM says copper social inventories in China’s major trading markets fell 1,000 mt over the weekend to 170,000 mt. Shanghai rose slightly, while Guangdong dropped sharply. The decline was linked to reduced arrivals from Guangxi and overseas. Traders will watch this week’s lower import arrivals, which may push inventories down if consumption holds. | Amex Exploration announced discovery of a copper-rich VMS zone at its Perron property, reporting 2.40% Cu over 7.8 m (including 3.92% Cu over 4.4 m) and accompanying Zn/Au/Ag grades. It also mentions a follow-up hole at depth, awaiting assays. The article is copper-focused and exploration-stage. | Hannan reports soil geochemical sampling at the Tabalosos East target in Peru, extending sub-cropping copper-silver mineralized horizons over 24 km and confirming continuity of previously reported high-grade copper-silver zones. Additional sampling and trenching are planned, with permitting for potential drilling underway. | Hannan soil sampling continues at Tabalosos East in Peru, extending the scale and confirming continuity of copper-silver mineralization. The update supports the potential quality and extent of the mineralization at the project, which can modestly boost sentiment around future copper supply prospects, though it is not immediate production impact. | Antaike said China’s 22 surveyed smelters produced 747,200 tonnes of refined copper cathodes in July, roughly flat versus June, with output expected to rise in August. Copper concentrate treatment charges rose to $57.50/t. The report covers tin separately and includes no aluminium or nickel impact. | Latin Metals acquired an additional 700 hectares at its Auquis copper project in Peru, expanding total property size to 3,600 hectares. The company plans Phase I follow-up stream sediment and surface sampling and is negotiating local community agreements. The news is exploratory and does not affect immediate copper supply or trade flows. | The piece highlights rising copper prices alongside risks around BHP Escondida in Chile, where workers voted to reject a final labor contract and may strike unless mediation ends Aug. 13. It also cites production declines, tight supply, and generally supportive demand signals, pointing to strong bullish near-term pressure. | An interview argues the electronics industry is facing inflation alongside copper raw-material shortages. It cites growing PCB layer counts, 5G infrastructure demand, and electric-vehicle/battery needs, stressing copper-foil capacity constraints and long lead times for key equipment. Shipping costs are also elevated. | Workers at JX Nippon’s Chile operation are set to strike after wage negotiations fail. The labor action raises the risk of short-term production disruption and tighter copper supply availability. That can lift market expectations for near-term copper fundamentals and add upward pressure to copper prices.

Copper prices rose as supply worries grew in Chile, with mine workers at Caserones walking off and mediation talks extending at BHP’s Escondida to avoid strikes. Separately, power consumption restrictions in China’s Guangxi region could temporarily shut a large share of aluminum smelting capacity, supporting aluminium. | MAX Resource Corp. reports assay results from its CESAR North URU zone in northeastern Colombia, highlighting widespread visible copper mineralization over 12 sq km and channel/grab results with copper grades up to 3.9% (plus silver). The release focuses on ongoing exploration and pending additional assays. | Copper prices rebounded as concerns about Chile supply disruptions intensified. BHP’s Escondida labor talks were extended to avoid a strike, while Caserones workers planned a walkout after contract talks failed. Traders also weighed China demand worries and a firmer U.S. dollar, but Chile risk supported copper. | Awalé Resources reports >5 km of copper–gold (up to 0.18% Cu in soil) anomalism at its Odienné project in Côte d'Ivoire after reinterpreting geochemistry following an IOCG discovery at Charger. It also describes associated gold and coincident nickel/arsenic/lead anomalies, plus planned geophysics and drilling. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. reports progress on a 10,000m diamond drilling program at its Kenbridge nickel deposit in Ontario, with two rigs operating and 3,200m completed. It notes sampling for analysis and potential additional nickel sulphide and a 400m conductor. No assay results are provided; options are granted. | Forum Energy Metals began drilling at its 100% owned Love Lake nickel-copper (and PGM) project in Saskatchewan after EM survey targets. The release outlines planned holes and cites historical copper and nickel grades from prior drilling/trenching. As exploration, it’s more sentiment than immediate supply, likely limiting near-term price effects. | Forum Energy Metals began drilling at its 100%-owned Love Lake nickel-copper-PGM project in Saskatchewan after EM survey targeting. The release discusses planned follow-up drilling and prior intersections/grades for copper and nickel. However, it does not announce new assays or production, limiting near-term price impact. | Eagle Plains reports Rockridge will mobilize a summer/fall exploration program at the Knife Lake copper VMS project in Saskatchewan, using VTEM plus geophysics and follow-up drilling. The update references prior copper results and plans funded by Rockridge, but provides no near-term production or supply change. Impact on metals prices is likely limited. | The article headline says BHP is getting closer to a wage agreement at the world’s largest copper mine. A finalized wage deal typically lowers the likelihood of strikes or work stoppages, improving near-term copper production certainty and supporting copper price sentiment. | Golden Birch Resources begins a ~3,000 m drilling program at PNG’s Keveri project targeting copper-gold porphyry prospects (Waki, Urua, Omu) and a separate nickel target (Doriri). It is an exploration drill test with no reported production changes or firm resource results. The firm also changes its corporate name to PNG Copper Inc. | Camino Corp. says it has received drilling permits and will start core drilling at the Lidia copper-gold zone of its Los Chapitos project in Peru. The program targets new copper-bearing structures with reported assay highlights, but provides no production or supply changes, making near-term copper-price impact likely small. | Forum Energy Metals commenced drilling at its Love Lake nickel-copper-PGM project in Saskatchewan. The program targets electromagnetic anomalies for magmatic nickel-copper-PGM mineralization, with follow-up planned on copper and nickel occurrences reported in prior work. The release is exploration-focused and does not indicate production or trade changes. | Nornickel awarded Metso Outotec a contract to supply dewatering, flotation and automation equipment for the Talnakh concentrator expansion (Line 3). The modernization and new flotation equipment aim to increase ore processed, supporting production ramp expectations for Nornickel’s nickel and, indirectly, copper outputs.

Peruvian miners are expected to pay a record 12 billion soles ($3 billion) in taxes in 2021, more than double 2019. The industry group attributes higher taxes to rising copper prices and currency weakness boosting miners’ dollar-denominated export revenue. Peru and Chile are also discussing higher mining taxes. | Sandfire Resources America acquired key surface property for the Black Butte copper project in Montana and outlined ongoing permitting, a pending environmental legal challenge, and continued construction. The company also seeks about US$12 million in 2021 funding through mid-2022, which could affect development timing but not current copper supply immediately. | SMM reports secondary copper-based rod producer operating rates fell to 59.53% in July, down 7.59 points month-on-month, amid power curtailment, late-July floods, and furnace repairs in Henan. Tight secondary copper supply forced plants to reduce or suspend output, with August rates expected to decline further. | SMM says operating rates of copper billet producers fell to 57.64% in July, down month on month, with backlog from June only partially supporting output. Demand for copper-zinc alloy billets was weak due to off-season conditions and reduced refrigeration orders, while August rates are expected to decline further. | Sandfire Resources America acquired key surface property needed for infrastructure at its fully permitted Black Butte copper project in Montana. The release notes construction continues despite an ongoing environmental legal challenge that could delay timelines, and the company seeks additional US$12m funding to progress toward mid-2022. | Surge Copper provided an update on summer work at its Ootsa and Berg projects in British Columbia. The company completed access trail upgrades for Berg, finished airborne geophysical data acquisition, and is continuing drilling at Ootsa. Assay results and a later resource update are expected, with no production or supply change announced. | Seabridge Gold said it will begin core drilling at its Iskut project in British Columbia to test targets for a possible gold-copper porphyry system. The program follows successful geophysical work and prior 2020 drill intercepts with gold and copper. Results could expand long-term copper prospects, but no production is affected now. | BHP reached an agreement with the union at Chile’s Escondida copper mine, following calls for a strike over a one-off COVID bonus. The deal finalizes the collective contract ahead of a worker vote. Avoiding a disruption at the largest producer should help support short-term copper prices. | Udokan Copper says it has completed installation of a gyratory crusher at its coarse crushing plant for rougher ore from the Zapadniy open pit. The company is building a mining and metallurgical plant targeting copper cathodes and concentrate, with construction of conveyors and related infrastructure ongoing. | Interview with Nippon Denkai on copper market conditions. Demand for copper far exceeds current manufacturing, with copper foil supply tightening in Asia. Electronics recovery and rising lithium-ion battery/EV orders are straining capacity and driving allocations away from PCB use. Copper prices rose sharply earlier in 2021, later eased after China reserve releases. | Codelco miners have voted to strike as disruption threats mount, raising the risk of near-term copper supply interruptions from one of the world’s largest producers. Markets typically react quickly to strike risk, with reduced output expectations weighing on copper prices over the first days of any labor action.

Interra Copper Corp. appointed Jason Nickel as CEO and reported its Thane Project drilling program is on schedule and has been extended after promising early results at the Cathedral Area. Core samples have been sent for analysis with initial results expected soon, supporting potential copper development upside. | Interra Copper Corp. appointed Jason Nickel as CEO and reported that its drilling program at the Thane copper project is on schedule. Initial core samples from the first drill holes were sent for lab analysis, with results expected soon. The company extended drilling after promising early shows in the Cathedral Area. | World Copper released exploration results from its Chilean Escalones project, reporting high-grade copper in oxide targets (including assays around 16.95–18.2% Cu and native copper confirmation) and delineating the extent of the Mancha Amarilla lithocap. Management says results support stronger resource potential and a possible heap-leach pathway.

Analysts expect copper to rise short term as macro conditions eventually tighten less than feared while liquidity easing and stronger fundamentals improve. Supply is constrained by smelter maintenance and power restrictions, electrolytic output may fall in August, and scrap availability tightens. Demand looks supported by grid and post-flood projects and accelerating destocking. | SMM data show Shanghai bonded copper stocks declined 11,400 mt to 380,700 mt over Aug 6–13, falling for five straight weeks, though destocking slowed. Smelter output disruptions and refined copper replacing scrap reduced availability, while favorable price ratios and inflows of godown warrants after customs clearance further lowered bonded inventories. | London copper prices rose 0.4% after strikes at two Chilean mines (Codelco’s Andina and JX Nippon’s Caserones) heightened supply disruption risk. Escondida approved a contract, limiting upside. Fitch cited tightened copper and aluminum markets from supply constraints, while weaker China demand weighed sentiment. | Peruvian copper miner Cerro Verde paid $245.5 million in a disputed tax debt “under protest,” as arbitration over Peru’s tax calculations continues. With President Pedro Castillo seeking higher taxes on miners, similar disputes affecting large producers (including Buenaventura) can increase near-term cost and supply-risk expectations for copper.

CATL’s growth and related lithium-battery supply-chain expansion are boosting copper foil demand. Jiayuan Technology plans acquisitions and to build high-performance electrolytic copper foil capacity in Guangdong, while other foil makers expand in Qinghai and Huizhou. With copper-foil supply tight, processing fees and demand outlook remain strong, supporting copper sentiment. | Copper prices fell as China economic data (slower factory output and retail sales, weaker exports, COVID-19 disruptions) raised worries about reduced near-term demand. LME copper dropped 1.6% and ShFE copper eased. The report also notes China aluminium output slipped amid power shortages, keeping aluminium under pressure. | The columnist cites escalating copper supply disruptions from Chile strikes and a Canada wildfire suspension, yet LME three-month copper is steady around $9,500/t. Funds’ speculative interest is low, with positioning at lows, while weaker China growth indicators and Delta-related demand worries outweigh micro supply hits in the near term. | Copper prices fell as weak Chinese economic data intensified demand worries and drove broader risk-off sentiment, pulling LME three-month copper down 1.3% to $9,442/t. Aluminium rose slightly after China’s aluminium output slipped for a third month, but copper’s macro-demand pressure outweighed in the near term. | Freeport announced it has completed the acquisition of the Yandera copper project. The deal is expected to strengthen its copper asset base and supports market expectations for future copper supply. In the near term, the news is more likely to be sentiment-positive than to change physical balances quickly. | Azarga Metals reported an updated PEA for its Unkur copper-silver project, proposing ~11.7ktpa copper production over 14 years with SART heap leaching for oxide material and flotation for sulphides. Economics are shown at $3.86/lb copper (and $4.54/lb spot), but no immediate aluminium linkage is provided. | Latin Metals reports outcropping copper-molybdenum mineralization at its Lacsha South project in Peru, with composite rock-chip results including ~0.20–0.22% copper over continuous channel sections and anomalous molybdenum. The release outlines further surveys to define drill targets but provides no resource or production figures.

EMX Royalty Corp. will acquire a 0.418% net smelter return (NSR) royalty on the operating Caserones copper-molybdenum mine in northern Chile for $34.1 million, alongside Altus. The royalty value can vary with LME copper price thresholds, but the deal mainly transfers royalty ownership, not production. | Copper Fox announces a desktop data gap analysis to support EPA and ADEQ permitting for its Van Dyke in-situ copper recovery project in Arizona. The study will review hydrogeological information, refine permitting guidance, and scope field work. No production changes are reported, suggesting limited immediate impact on copper prices. | Ivanhoe Mines’ Kamoa Copper extended financing with DRC’s SNEL to upgrade turbine 5 at the Inga II hydropower complex, expected to add 162 MW of renewable electricity. This, combined with Mwadingusha output, will provide priority clean power for Kamoa-Kakula copper Phase 3 and future expansions. | Kodiak Copper Corp. temporarily pauses drilling at its MPD copper-gold porphyry project in British Columbia due to wildfire risk and safety concerns. Workers are demobilized and work will resume once deemed safe. The company says it does not expect a material impact on its fully funded drill program. | Nova Royalty Corp. agreed to buy a 1.0% net smelter return royalty on Aura Minerals’ Aranzazu copper-gold-silver mine in Mexico. Royalty payments depend on copper prices versus an LME threshold and on ongoing copper concentrate production, but the transaction itself is unlikely to move copper fundamentals in the next weeks. | Fabled Silver Gold reported drill assay results from the Santa Maria property in Mexico, describing a “lower feeder” zone with significant silver-equivalent and listed grades including copper (28.38 lb/tonne) alongside lead and zinc. The news is drilling-focused and provides no explicit copper supply impact or trade disruption. | Sombrero Resources signed an option agreement to acquire Peru’s Takana copper-nickel district, highlighting high-grade copper and nickel occurrences and plans to advance the project to drilling. The announcement is exploration-focused with no established resource or production, implying limited immediate impact on near-term copper or nickel prices. | U.S. Gold says it is expanding its Wyoming copper and gold projects and appointing Francis to lead exploration. The update is supportive for longer-term copper supply expectations, but without details on near-term output, it is unlikely to meaningfully move copper prices within days to four weeks.

Residents near MMG’s Las Bambas copper mine in Peru restarted a road blockade, halting use of the mining corridor after a two-week truce. Communities accuse MMG of failing to attend a scheduled dialogue. Las Bambas produces about 400,000 tonnes of copper annually, and the disruption may impede shipments. | Copper prices rebounded after two days of losses as investors turned to “buy the dip.” Support came from concerns that ongoing labor strikes in Chile and road blocks near Peru’s Las Bambas mine could disrupt copper supply. LME three-month copper rose 0.6% to $9,305.50/ton. | Copper prices fell for a third day to the lowest in almost two months, driven by weak economic data reinforcing demand concerns and nervousness ahead of central bank messaging at Jackson Hole. The selloff is echoed by declines in LME aluminum and nickel, reflecting broader risk-off pressure on industrial metals. | Copper surged after fears of renewed supply disruption at Peru’s Las Bambas mine. Residents resumed road blockades involving the MMG-owned operation, following a prior truce break under President Castillo. With Las Bambas producing about 400,000 tonnes annually, the market priced higher near-term copper tightness; LME three-month rose 0.8%. | White Metal Resources announced an updated Inferred mineral resource estimate for the Okohongo Cu-Ag project in Namibia. Using a 0.30% copper cut-off, it reports ~7.7 Mt grading 1.55% Cu (CuEq 1.82%). The news relates directly to prospective copper supply growth expectations. | The EU proposes a CBAM on certain imports to prevent carbon leakage, phasing in from 2023 and fully implemented from 2026. It would require importers to buy and surrender certificates based on embedded emissions, using EU ETS auction prices. Aluminium is explicitly in scope; carbon-costs may raise import costs and weigh on demand. | Three CODELCO unions began a strike at the Andina division after rejecting a labor agreement. The strike involves 1,300 of 1,437 workers and affects underground Rio Blanco and open-pit Sur Sur mines that produced over 184,000 tonnes of copper last year. The labor action increases near-term Chile copper supply risk.

Copper prices fell to a more than four-month low as a stronger U.S. dollar weighed on sentiment and investors saw easing supply disruptions. Blockaded transport near MMG’s Las Bambas mine in Peru reopened and operations resumed at Teck’s Highland Valley Copper after evacuations ended. The piece also shows aluminium down and nickel slightly higher. | Antofagasta reported sharply higher H1 profit as revenue rose on higher realized copper prices and strong copper demand. Despite lower sales volumes, results supported copper strength. The company adjusted FY copper production guidance for 2021 to 710–740 kt, while maintaining cost guidance at $1.25/lb. | SMM says operating rates for copper rod (made of concentrate) companies averaged about 71% in July, above expectations, helped by stronger cable/wire demand and a narrower concentrate–scrap spread. Early August rates fell on COVID-19 transport and production disruptions, but SMM expects recovery in the second week with continued pandemic influence. | Antofagasta reported strong H1 earnings and raised interim dividends, but said Chile’s 12-year-driest conditions could erode copper output. A desalination plant redesign at Los Pelambres may trigger about 50,000 tonnes of production at risk next year, cutting 2022 estimates and weighing on near-term supply expectations. | Copperstone’s interim report highlights the Swedish government’s final decision to confirm the Viscaria exploitation concession (No. 7) and the reopening progress, with plans for further drilling, permitting, and potential increased mining and processing. It also notes support from Norrlandsfonden and an expectation of higher annual copper production. | The report says drought conditions in Chile are forcing copper production cuts in the world’s top producing country. The immediate effect is lower mine supply, which can tighten the market and raise near-term expectations for copper prices over coming days to weeks. | Teck Resources resumed operations at its Highland Valley copper mine in British Columbia after an evacuation linked to wildfire threats. The company had restarted an oxygen plant to address smelting idling from poor air quality, but warned further smoke could cause outages. It may update guidance once wildfire risk eases. | Gungnir Resources said ongoing drilling at its Lappvattnet nickel sulphide project in Sweden intersected massive and semi-massive sulphides, including a 10-metre interval. Samples have been submitted for priority nickel (and PGEs) assays in the next couple of weeks; mineralization includes chalcopyrite indicating potential copper. | Gungnir Resources reported drilling at its Lappvattnet nickel deposit in Sweden intersecting massive and semi-massive sulphides, including a 10-metre core interval. Samples have been submitted for priority assaying for nickel (and PGEs); copper and cobalt are also planned, with assays expected within weeks. | Three Valley Copper reported Q2 2021 results, including 1,035 tonnes of copper cathode production and revised 2021 guidance due to Chilean COVID restrictions, equipment shortages, and labor competition impacting construction and ore delivery. An offtake amendment increases 2021–2022 sales at prevailing spot prices, improving near-term cash flow. | SMM reports SHFE nonferrous metals closing mostly lower as a higher US dollar and hawkish Fed meeting minutes hurt risk appetite. Copper fell 3.27% and LME copper declined. Aluminium dropped 1.68% on soft macro and slowing de-stocking, while nickel slid 2.09% amid weaker stainless steel prices. | The piece reports that copper prices are tumbling alongside iron ore as worries about economic growth roil metals markets. The negative macro sentiment implies weaker near-term demand expectations for industrial commodities, creating downward pressure on copper prices over the following days. | Copper fell 2.9% to the lowest in more than four months as China dampened growth measures, the Fed signaled reduced bond buying (supporting a stronger dollar), and COVID-19 cases spurred demand worries. The sell-off also weighed on aluminium and nickel, with both down on the day. | Emerita Resources reports high-grade drill hole results from the Infanta Deposit in Spain, including copper mineralization (reported as 3.8% copper in the title) over 86.2–112.8 meters. The company describes step-out drilling to extend strike length and depth, with further results expected. | The piece reports that copper prices have dropped to a “dangerous” level and characterizes the move as a negative signal for economic recovery. While it provides no specific drivers, the clear focus on an abrupt copper price decline implies near-term bearish pressure on copper. | Sego Resources reports gold assay results at its Miners Mountain project near Princeton and says the geology may represent a gold-copper porphyry system. Further drilling is planned, contingent on wildfire conditions, to assess deposit size and determine whether mineable copper is present. Copper assay data is not yet provided. | Fabled Silver Gold closed a C$6.9 million private placement to support the planned spin-out of its Muskwa copper project into a separate listed entity. Proceeds are earmarked for working capital and flow-through exploration expenses. The news focuses on corporate structuring, financing terms, and exploration plans rather than copper fundamentals.

SMM says Shanghai bonded-zone copper inventories fell 12,200 mt to 368,500 mt by Aug 20, the sixth straight weekly decline. The improved SHFE/LME copper price ratio, electricity curtailment, scrap shortages, and limited imported cargo inflows boosted customs clearances, sending Yangshan premiums above $50/mt and likely pushing inventories lower. | SMM says copper inventories across major Chinese markets fell 8,900 mt by Aug 20 to 150,800 mt, driven mainly by declines in Shanghai and Guangdong. Limited imported-copper arrivals and improved demand from lower prices reduced stocks. With strong cathode consumption and no surge in inflows, SMM expects continued inventory drops next week. | Antofagasta said Chile’s worsening water shortage forced it to lower its 2022 copper production target to 710,000–740,000 tonnes versus a prior 730,000–760,000 range. It also warned desalination redesign delays at Los Pelambres could endanger about 50,000 tonnes of copper output. | London copper is set for its steepest weekly loss in two months despite a Friday uptick, as China’s slowing growth, COVID-19 outbreaks, and U.S. bond-buying taper worries dent metal sentiment. Copper hit four-month lows on weak data. The piece also reports day moves in nickel and aluminium amid China reserve release uncertainty. | Copper prices bounced on the day but remain headed for the biggest weekly fall since June. Traders cite worsening demand concerns: slower growth in China, tighter U.S. monetary-policy expectations boosting the dollar, and rising global COVID/Delta infections. Technicals look oversold, but negatives still dominate sentiment. | Nomad Royalty agreed to buy an additional 0.351% net smelter return royalty on Chile’s Caserones copper mine for $27.25m cash, raising total exposure to 0.63% NSR. It will also acquire CMC shares and expects closing in Aug/early Sep 2021, mainly to boost cash flow, not change production materially. | Copper prices rebounded 0.4% but are still headed for the biggest weekly drop since June, driven by concerns over slowing Chinese demand, expectations of U.S. monetary tightening boosting the dollar, and rising COVID-19 Delta cases. Aluminium and nickel also edged up slightly, reflecting mixed market sentiment. | The article is a “live futures” update noting copper at a one-month low on the LME and nickel down 1.6%. This implies immediate bearish price pressure for both metals. Other content focuses on Fastmarkets/Metal Bulletin assessment methodology, timing, and corrections, with no clear new supply-demand catalyst. | The piece is a live futures update noting copper at a one-month low on the LME and nickel down 1.6%, signaling near-term bearish sentiment. The rest of the article mostly covers Fastmarkets/Metal Bulletin publication timing and procedural corrections to specific assessments, with limited new fundamental impact detail. | Noront Resources reiterated its board’s recommendation that shareholders accept BHP’s $325 million cash offer, following a dispute with largest shareholder Wyloo Metals. Noront’s main asset is its Eagle’s Nest nickel-and-copper deposit. While the outcome may influence nickel supply expectations, deal scale likely limits broad near-term price impact.

EMX Royalty Corp. completed the initial closing of its purchase of an NSR royalty interest on the Caserones copper-molybdenum mine in northern Chile, acquiring 0.418% effective NSR royalty for US$34.1 million. Further closing is expected by September 1. The deal is presented as improving cash flows, not changing copper supply. | Copper prices rose on Monday as risk-on sentiment returned, supported by signs China’s COVID outbreak is tapering and bargain hunting after recent weakness. Additional support came from a labor contract rejection at Chile’s El Teniente mine, raising supply concerns. Nickel and aluminum also increased alongside broader base-metal strength. | Copper jumped 2% on the LME as investors returned after a recent slump, attracted by lower prices and hopes for improving global growth and electrification-driven demand. The piece also references improving euro-zone activity and fewer China COVID cases, alongside a May refined-copper surplus shift. Aluminium and nickel rose too. | SMM expects SHFE and LME copper prices to dip further despite supportive domestic supply-demand fundamentals. July data disappointed and Fed minutes signaled tapering, weighing sentiment. Electric restrictions eased slightly but cathode supply limits still linger, while falling scrap and weaker price momentum constrain rod makers and reinforce bearish price expectations. | Playfair says core drilling is expected to begin in early September on its 100%-owned RKV copper project in Norway. Permits are approved, drill targets have been defined, and prior sampling showed strong mobile metal ion copper anomalies (with values >6,000 ppb). | Playfair Mining announced mobilization of a core drilling program for its RKV Copper Project in Norway, with permits approved and a lightweight, hand-carried drill machine arriving for September drilling. The project targets multiple high-grade copper soil anomalies (MMI Cu) consistent with potential weathering copper sulphides. | Edgemont Gold Corp. said drilling equipment is on site and Phase I drilling is about to start at its Dungate copper-gold porphyry project in B.C., targeting IP anomalies and a quartz-pyrite breccia zone reported in older drilling. It is an exploration update without assays or production impact, limiting immediate price effects. | Norden Crown Metals, with Boliden, has started ~2,500 metres of diamond drilling at the Burfjord copper-gold project in Norway, targeting IOCG-style mineralization and following up prior drill intercepts. The release details target zones and planned exploration activities, but signals no near-term mine output changes. | DR Congo has formed a commission to re-evaluate reserves and resources at China Molybdenum’s Tenke Fungurume copper-cobalt mine to verify compliance and potentially renegotiate contract terms and extensions. The review may affect perceived future copper supply, but no immediate production changes are confirmed.

Metals Creek Resources announced it will receive 1.25 million shares of Class 1 Nickel and Technologies Limited in exchange for a 100% interest in 500 hectares of PGE/nickel-related claims in Ontario, plus a 2% net smelter returns royalty. It also mentions exposure to copper/nickel/PGE exploration, without production. | McEwen Mining announced its subsidiary McEwen Copper closed the first tranche of a $40 million private placement to fund the Los Azules copper project in Argentina, including permitting, access road construction, and drilling. A second tranche is expected by Sept. 30. No immediate production or trade changes were stated. | London copper slipped 0.5% as traders turned cautious ahead of a U.S. Fed symposium that may clarify stimulus tapering timing. A potential tightening outlook could support the dollar and hurt demand expectations. LME aluminium eased slightly, while ShFE nickel rose, showing mixed regional reactions. | China’s copper cathode imports fell further in July as the closed Q2 import window continued to suppress customs declarations and trading. Premiums weakened and traders slowed import pace, shifting or warehousing cargoes. The SHFE/LME ratio improved from mid-July, but a demand boost is expected mainly in August. | Fastmarkets’ live futures report says LME prices rose slightly while cancellations of copper (and other metals) continued. It also covers Fastmarkets’ procedural corrections to certain European low-carbon aluminium differential and extrusion billet premium assessments. The net near-term signal is mild upward pressure, mainly via the copper/LME move. | Copper prices rose for a third session on renewed physical demand, with buyers seeking physical metal and China’s Yangshan premium improving. Gains were restrained by uncertainty ahead of U.S. Fed comments at Jackson Hole affecting the dollar. Separately, alumina hit six-month highs after a Jamalco refinery blaze, supporting aluminum. | Granite Creek reported diamond drill results at its Carmacks project in Yukon, including 105.52 meters of 1.18% copper equivalent and a higher-grade 21.22 meters of 2.55% CuEq. The company says results extend Zone 2000S below the current model and may support an updated resource estimate. | Quaterra Resources announced a key appointment to lead the MacArthur oxide copper project and completion of a metallurgical sampling program ahead of its pre-feasibility study. The company drilled over 1,355 meters for representative samples, with assays from additional oxide and sulfide drilling expected soon. It also filed a judicial review petition on water permits. | Capella Minerals signed a binding LOI to earn-in to Cullen’s Katajavaara and Aakenus gold(-copper) projects in northern Finland, requiring upfront and multi-year exploration payments. The release frames the geology and permits but does not indicate immediate production changes, supply disruptions, or policy actions impacting base-metal prices. | Quaterra Resources announced a key appointment to lead its MacArthur oxide copper mine PFS and reported completion of a metallurgical sampling program, with assays from further oxide and sulfide drilling expected soon. It also filed a judicial review petition regarding forfeiture of Nevada water permits. No immediate production or supply impact is stated. | Global Palladium Fund cut the TER on its gold ETC and cited research expecting European pension funds to increase gold allocations. It also notes it launched physically backed copper and nickel ETCs and claims it is the only provider for both. The implied fundraising/inflow outlook is modestly supportive for copper and nickel sentiment. | Blackstone Minerals reports final infill drilling at its Ban Phuc DSS deposit in Vietnam, intersecting 375m of nickel sulfide and improving confidence in its JORC nickel resource. The results are expected to underpin its Ta Khoa UBU PFS and potentially increase mine inventory, supporting near-term nickel supply expectations.

London three-month copper fell 0.3% to $9,350/t as investors stayed cautious before the Fed’s Jackson Hole meeting, weighing possible tapering and its impact via the US dollar and growth expectations. LME aluminium edged up 0.2%, with alumina prices rising after a Jamalco refinery blaze, suggesting limited near-term pressure. | Altus Strategies began a 135 line-km induced polarisation (IP) and gradient array geophysical survey at its Agdz copper-silver project in Morocco. The survey will refine targets for trenching and drilling across several prospects with prior rock-chip copper grades up to 8% and other encouraging results. | Codelco’s chairman said copper’s recent dip is temporary, citing strong demand for wiring and clean-energy infrastructure. China’s Covid containment boosted sentiment after earlier slowdown worries. Codelco begins a $1.4 billion Salvador investment and expects slightly higher output, though a strike at Andina is ongoing. | The article says the Taliban controls Afghanistan and holds deposits worth over $1 trillion, including copper (and iron ore and gold). It argues improved stability could enable much greater mineral development over time, but it does not describe imminent production or export changes, limiting near-term price implications. | Copper is slipping as the dollar and bond yields rise, while virus-related concerns continue to simmer. The piece frames these macro moves as weighing on near-term risk appetite and copper demand expectations, creating downward price pressure in the immediate market window. | Comex copper’s front-month contract settled 0.24% higher at $4.2650 per pound, gaining 1.00 cent. The market rose for four consecutive sessions, with the largest four-day dollar and percentage gain since late July and the highest settlement value since Aug. 16, 2021. | Altus Strategies started a 135 line-km induced polarisation geophysical survey at its Agdz copper-silver project in Morocco to prioritize targets for trenching and drilling. The release reiterates historical rock-chip copper grades up to 8% and additional positive results, but provides no direct aluminium-related market impact.

Chile’s Congress is reviewing a royalty bill that would levy a 3% royalty on copper sales. Private miners warn the rates could pressure operations and even cause some mines to shut down, while supporters cite social spending needs. The bill’s progress and potential supply impact are bearish for copper. | SMM reports subdued trading in Shanghai’s imported copper market as import losses widen to 200 yuan/mt. Backwardation in LME cash versus the three-month contract remains $6.5/mt, while near-term arrivals see lower quotes and purchases remain scarce, indicating weaker physical demand conditions. | Kodiak Copper Corp. said it is remobilizing staff to restart its drill program at the MPD copper-gold porphyry project in Southern British Columbia. The company attributes the restart to improved conditions and reduced wildfire risk, expects continued drilling and to provide updates later in the season. | Copper prices have reportedly fallen below scrap for the first time since April 2020. This suggests spot copper is trading at weaker levels relative to scrap metal values, pointing to deteriorating near-term pricing sentiment and potential pressure on copper demand or tightness expectations.

ThreeD Capital says it participated in a private placement to maintain its ~9% ownership in Premium Nickel Resources, which is pursuing acquisition of the former BCL Limited and TNMC assets in Botswana. The projects target development of separate Ni-Co and Cu concentrates, though completion is not assured. | London copper slid 0.1% as investors stayed cautious ahead of the Jackson Hole Fed symposium, where expected plans to cut stimulus could boost the dollar and make greenback-priced metals costlier. Copper also faced softer Chinese industrial profit growth. Aluminium rose on Xinjiang smelter output-limit risks. | Aluminum prices jumped to their highest since April 2018 after Xinjiang imposed output limits on five smelters and curbed electricity usage, reducing domestic supply. Benchmark aluminum rose about 1.5% and ShFE prices hit multi-year highs. The piece also mentions upcoming Chinese metal reserve auctions, including aluminum. | SMM reports the State Reserve Bureau will release the third batch of state metals soon: 30,000 mt of copper and 70,000 mt of aluminium, alongside 50,000 mt of zinc. The added official supply is likely to weigh on copper and aluminium prices over the coming days as inventories rise. | SMM reports that China’s State Reserve Bureau will begin releasing its third batch of strategic metals into the market soon, including copper (30,000 mt) and aluminium (70,000 mt), alongside zinc. The added government supply likely increases short-term availability expectations and pressure prices downward for copper and aluminium. | Shanghai bonded-zone copper inventories fell by 23,400 mt to 345,100 mt as of Aug. 27, the seventh consecutive week of decline. The import window opened recently, and cargoes booked under warrants amid import profits were brought in after customs clearance, further reducing bonded inventory.

Sandfire Resources reported record profit and revenue and announced its biggest final dividend, attributing it to red-hot copper prices and higher earnings from its DeGrussa mine as it transitions into decline. The company also says it is positioned to build a new Botswana copper project as decarbonisation boosts demand. | Fathom Nickel reports Q2 results and timelines for an exploration program at its Albert Lake Project, including survey work and planned drilling. The company targets high-grade nickel sulfide and notes the deposit is recognized for nickel and copper. The update is mainly sentiment-oriented with no direct supply or inventory impact mentioned. | Aluminum jumped to a 10-year high on the LME as demand surged and Chinese supply was squeezed by pollution rules, power consumption caps, and provincial production cuts. Tightening conditions, limited ability to ramp output, and rising orders supported the rally, lifting related base metals including copper and nickel. | Pancon reports new drilling results from its Brewer Gold & Copper Project, including copper-bearing intervals (up to ~0.21% Cu) and chalcopyrite-bornite mineralization. The company frames results as extending a gold-copper zone and potential porphyry copper prospects, using copper spot pricing for AuEq. No nickel or aluminium linkage or supply impact is stated. | Sanatana Resources announced it has begun exploration at its optioned Oweegee Dome Cu-Au porphyry project in British Columbia. The work includes 3-D IP/RES geophysics, mapping, and sampling that confirm copper-bearing mineralization at the Delta and Malloy zones and define drill targets for the 2022 field season. | Angola’s Benguela Railway opening to private firms is expected to ease copper haulage from Zambia to the DRC. By improving regional transport and enabling exports through the Port of Lobito, the route could facilitate quicker copper shipments to global markets. | Democratic Republic of Congo mines ministry said copper sales rose 10% in H1 2021 to 871,956 tonnes, with exports and local sales increasing, while cobalt sales climbed 15%. Congo is Africa’s top copper producer, so higher output/sales suggests improved near-term supply that may weigh on copper prices. | Interra Copper reported completion of its drilling program at the Thane Property, with additional holes at the Cathedral/Pinnacle zones. Early core logging described arsenic-associated mineralization and chalcopyrite-bearing breccia, consistent with a porphyry-style model. It also began IP and other surveys; assay results are pending. | Sanatana Resources announced initial exploration at its Oweegee Dome Cu-Au porphyry project in British Columbia, including mapping, sampling, and starting a 3-D IP/RES survey to define 2022 drill targets at the Delta and Malloy zones. The release is exploration-stage with no near-term supply changes, limiting price impact. | The piece’s headline says a copper royalty bill is clearing further hurdles in Chile. Because Chile is a major driver of global copper supply, progress on royalty changes can shift market expectations toward higher costs and weaker investment incentives, generally pressuring copper prices in the near term.

Chile’s environmental court approved JX Nippon’s Caserones copper mine compliance plan after SMA charged it in 2019 over alleged overpumping and tailings leaks. The $42 million plan includes returning desalinated water to the Copiapo River to address drought. Farmers challenged scope, but the court upheld SMA’s approach. | Nevada Copper reported completion of a key underground development crossing at its Pumpkin Hollow project, enabling faster development and scheduled backfilling, plus ongoing installation of ventilation fans. Financing updates include a KfW waiver extension and added liquidity via an amended promissory note to support the mine ramp-up. | Copper prices fell as China’s factory activity data pointed to slowing demand. August factory activity entered contraction due to COVID-19 containment, supply bottlenecks and high input costs. With China consuming roughly half of global copper, the weaker outlook weighed on both LME and Shanghai copper futures. | Murchison Minerals reports final assay results confirming near-surface nickel and copper mineralization at multiple targets (Syrah and 4048) within its HPM project in Quebec, with high NiEq/CuEq grab sample grades. The company plans additional prospecting and an upcoming ~3,500 m drilling program to test further mineralization. | Copper prices dropped more than 2% as global manufacturing slowed, with China reporting contraction for the first time in nearly 1.5 years. China’s state reserves also released 150,000 tonnes via auctions to curb prices. LME copper, aluminum and nickel were all lower in trading despite nickel’s stronger cash premium. | Namibia’s High Court interdicts the Ministry of Mines from granting mineral licences over the Haib Copper project area until a court review of the denied EPL renewal is finalized. The company says work was halted after non-renewal. A further hearing is scheduled for Sept. 16. | Vedanta said it wants to open dialogue with Zambia’s ZCCM and reiterated its commitment to invest $1.5bn in Konkola Copper Mines, including payments to local suppliers and funding for ESG and community projects. The comments follow a partial final arbitral award involving disputes with ZCCM and Zambia. | Nobel Resources announced a 10,000–12,500 meter diamond drilling program starting September 10, 2021 at its Algarrobo copper-gold (IOCG) project in Chile. The company targets multiple geophysical anomalies associated with near-surface copper mineralization, alongside a marketed offering and private placement to fund exploration. | Norden Crown completed Phase I (~400 line km) of UAV airborne magnetic geophysics over its Burfjord copper-gold project in Norway and plans Phase II in coming weeks. The results aim to refine and prioritize drill targets, building on prior high-grade copper intercepts from earlier drilling. Exploration continues via a Boliden-funded option. | Mower County authorities report multiple incidents of copper theft and damage to EDF Renewable Services wind turbines. Five turbines were damaged and copper wire stolen, with eight additional turbines damaged. Estimated damage for eight turbines is about $38,000, and high-voltage conditions make wire removal dangerous. Police request public help identifying vehicles. | Chile’s mining minister said the country needs about $150 billion in investment to nearly double copper output by 2050, supported by a new mining blueprint aimed at boosting traceability, reducing water use and shaping future royalties. Chile’s target depends on investment approvals for 49 projects. | Panama has begun talks with First Quantum’s local unit to renegotiate the contract for the Cobre Panama copper mine, aiming for higher royalties and stronger environmental conditions. The government indicates a “completely new” deal and possible operator change if no agreement is reached, creating near-term uncertainty for copper supply.

Chile’s mining minister said about $150 billion is needed for a blueprint to expand “green copper” production, aiming for record output targets and requiring large project approvals and investment. The plan targets environmental measures and traceability, with current projects and royalties/regulatory constraints influencing investor expectations for copper supply. | Battery Mineral Resources announced encouraging initial assays from drilling at its Punitaqui copper-gold mine in Chile, including copper grades up to 2.06% over 9m (and other intercepts). Three drills are active and the company expects resumption of copper production mid-2022, supporting bullish copper sentiment. | Blackstone Minerals reported assay results from its maiden drilling at the Ban Khoa disseminated sulfide prospect within the Ta Khoa nickel–copper–PGE project in northern Vietnam. Highlights included 147m of disseminated nickel sulfide and potential to extend operations and complement nearby mining, supporting optimism for nickel and copper resources. | Battery Mineral Resources reports positive initial drill core assays from the Punitaqui complex in Chile, including intercepts such as 9m at 2.06% copper and other zones grading above 1% copper. Punitaqui is slated for production resumption, and these results may support near-term copper supply expectations.

Sber, Gazprombank and the Russian Copper Company signed a $2.7 bln syndicated loan to fund the Malmyzhskoe copper deposit development in Russia. The project is expected to increase Russian copper concentrate output by nearly 30%, supporting regional economic growth and boosting future copper supply expectations. | EMX Royalty Corp. completed the final closing to acquire an additional interest in an NSR royalty on the Caserones copper-molybdenum mine in northern Chile. The transaction is for US$34.1 million cash and increases EMX’s royalty cash flow tied to copper production, but it does not cite operational or market changes. | Copper prices edged higher as the dollar weakened and markets looked ahead to U.S. non-farm payrolls, seen as guiding Fed taper timing. Aluminium was virtually flat near a decade high on tight-supply fears, while nickel was slightly lower. The jobs data and currency move supported near-term copper demand sentiment. | The article headline says Chile’s copper strike has ended as a key bill advances in the Senate. A strike ending typically improves near-term mine operations and reduces supply risk, which can weigh on copper prices in the coming days as markets recalibrate disruption expectations. | Cochilco data cited by the article show Chilean copper output fell 0.02% year-on-year in July. As Chile is a key driver of global supply expectations, the small decline can modestly pressure pricing lower, though the change is minimal and likely limits immediate market movement. | Copper climbed 0.6% on the LME after weak U.S. jobs data pushed the dollar down, boosting optimism that stimulus tapering may be more gradual. Copper support also came from falling visible inventories. The article notes aluminium up 1% and nickel up 1.5% in the same risk-on, softer-dollar move. | Peru’s copper output fell in July but remains higher over the first seven months of the year. The decline in the latest month can reduce near-term mine supply expectations for the market, potentially pressuring copper prices in the short run, even though year-to-date production is still growing. | Aranjin Resources said it closed the acquisition of the Sharga copper project in Mongolia, paying US$1.5 million and issuing shares, financed via a convertible debenture. The Sharga technical report cites copper assay intersections and outlines early-phase exploration surveys and drilling plans.

Lumina Copper agreed with workers at Chile’s Caserones mine on a three-year contract, ending an almost month-long strike that began in early August. The deal followed mediation and wage negotiations. Caserones produced about 127,000 tons of copper in 2020, making the strike an immediate local supply risk. | Battery Mineral Resources reports early drill core assay results at its newly acquired Punitaqui copper complex in Chile and outlines plans to revise resources and restart the mill around mid-2022. The program confirms copper grades near 2% in intersections, but current details suggest no immediate output change. | SMM’s roundup highlights labor and contract developments for major overseas copper producers. Chile’s Andina strike was ended via new union agreements, and Codelco reached an early bargaining deal for El Teniente. Panama is negotiating royalties and environmental terms for First Quantum’s Cobre Panama, supporting expected supply stability. | SMM data show China copper cathode output fell 1.3% MoM in August to 819,400 mt, driven by power curtailment and some maintenance, while tighter domestic scrap supply and sharply lower RCs squeezed smelter profits. September output is forecast to rise 1.6% MoM, as maintenance winds down and some plants ramp up. | A Guinea coup attempt has raised fears of disrupted bauxite exports from the West African nation, where major miners operate. Since bauxite is a key input for alumina and aluminium, the uncertainty supported a rally in aluminium prices to a 10-year high. Iron-ore project timelines were also questioned. | OZ Minerals halted operations at its Prominent Hill copper-gold mine in South Australia after a contractor was killed in an underground machinery incident. The stoppage follows police and Safework SA investigations. Prominent Hill is a major part of the company’s copper production, raising short-term supply concerns for copper.

The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service rejected a request by Hudbay Minerals to remove critical-habitat protections for jaguars tied to the proposed Rosemont open-pit copper mine in Arizona. The decision continues opposition to mining in the Santa Rita Mountains, potentially delaying mine development and copper supply. | Chakana reported significant drilling intercepts at its Soledad property in Peru, including 46 meters grading 5.64% copper, along with elevated silver and gold values. The company provided an update on resource drilling. The news may improve the project’s long-term potential but is unlikely to materially move near-term copper prices. | Brigadier Gold reported additional trench and channel sampling at its Picachos project, expanding a copper-silver discovery and adding high-grade gold-silver-copper results at the historic La Gloria mine. Reported copper assays (e.g., ~0.54% Cu over 0.72 m) may modestly bolster copper sentiment. | Camino Minerals begins discovery drilling at its Los Chapitos copper project in Peru after receiving permits. The company targets extensions of the Lidia Zone and reports early trace chalcopyrite and alteration assemblages, with historical chip samples up to 5.1% copper. It is an exploration update without near-term production impact. | Camino Corp. announced it has started core discovery drilling at its Los Chapitos copper project in Peru, targeting mineralization extensions at the Lidia Zone. The company notes trace chalcopyrite and historic rock samples with up to 5.1% copper, suggesting potential for new copper discoveries. | Arcadia Minerals reported grab-sampling results from its Karibib Copper-Gold Project in Namibia, confirming mineralisation across a 20 km by 2 km structural feature. Skarn and vein samples returned average copper grades of about 4.32% and 1.94%, supporting upside to copper prospectivity. It remains exploration, not production.

Malaysia extended by two months the grace period for tighter metal scrap import standards. The new rules require high copper content and cap impurities, effectively blocking low-grade copper scrap categories. If implemented after October 31, Malaysia’s secondary copper scrap exports to China may contract, tightening China’s scrap supply and supporting copper prices. | C3 Metals reported assays from further drilling at its Jasperoide copper-gold project in southern Peru, including multiple long intercepts with copper grades and visible copper sulphides, plus continued drilling at the Cresta Verde Zone. Results suggest extensive skarn continuity, but they are exploratory. | SPC Nickel announced final drill assay results from the Janes Ni-Cu-PGM Project, highlighting continuity of high-grade Trench 1 mineralization. The company reports Ni and Cu-bearing sulphides and notes potential for high-grade mineralization close to surface. As exploration news, it may modestly lift sentiment for nickel and copper. | Eagle Plains said its option partner Rockridge has started a helicopter-borne EM and magnetic geophysical survey at the Knife Lake Cu-Zn-Ag-Co VMS project in Saskatchewan. Prior drilling results show meaningful copper and other metals grades, but the release does not change resources, production, or near-term supply. | Washington H. Soul Pattinson is considering an IPO of its wholly owned Round Oak Metals unit, with Mick Wilkes as chairman. The subsidiary has operating mines in Western Australia and Queensland plus non-operating growth projects. The news signals copper asset monetization, but no immediate supply impact is specified. | C3 Metals announced additional assay results from the Jasperoide copper-gold project in southern Peru, reporting broad, laterally extensive copper mineralization (including intervals with ~0.67% and higher Cu) and visible copper sulphide in newly drilled zones. The update is exploration-focused with no production change, so near-term copper price effects are likely limited. | Teck and BCIT announced installation of more than 1,000 Health Canada-certified antimicrobial copper patches on high-touch surfaces across five BCIT buildings. Funded through Teck’s Copper & Health program, the initiative highlights copper’s antibacterial properties based on prior trials on transit vehicles. Teck notes copper quantities are small and mainly for safety. | The U.S. Agriculture Secretary says a decision on approving Minnesota’s Twin Metals copper mine is pending a legal opinion from the Interior Department, which controls the underground deposit. Approval would add a potential major U.S. copper supply, while environmental opposition could delay or block the project.

Blackstone Minerals reports infill drilling at its Ban Chang massive sulfide target within the Ta Khoa nickel–copper–PGE project in Northern Vietnam, including 5.35m of massive sulfide nickel. The company says drilling supports a forthcoming maiden resource and mine-plan inputs, but no immediate production or trade disruption is stated. | Record-high copper prices are pushing air-conditioning makers (e.g., Daikin) to consider replacing some copper with aluminium to reduce costs, though timelines are longer-term. It also notes aluminium prices are rising even faster due to supply concerns in Guinea and China’s smelting constraints, offsetting any near-term aluminium demand boost. | Surging record copper prices are pushing air-conditioning makers (e.g., Daikin) to plan increased aluminum use in heat exchangers to cut costs. Yet LME aluminum is also rising sharply on Guinea-related supply concerns and China’s emission-reduction actions that strain energy-intensive smelting, creating offsetting price pressures. | Golden Lake Exploration reported initial assays from grab samples at its West Copperview property near Kodiak’s MPD claims, including up to 0.95% copper and high silver values. The release is early-stage exploration with limited grab samples and pending soil results, implying no immediate copper market supply impact. | NorthWest Copper reported assays from initial and expansion-confirmation drilling at its Kwanika project in British Columbia, highlighting consistently high-grade copper equivalent intervals (e.g., ~1%+ CuEq starting near bedrock surface). Management says results expand the near-surface high-grade footprint supporting an updated Q4 2021 resource estimate. | SMM says Guangdong copper cathode inventories have fallen for 10 sessions, dropping to around 21,400 mt, close to a multi-year low. Tightening supply (limited smelter restarts, lingering effects of power restrictions, constrained imports) plus strong downstream replenishment ahead of Mid-Autumn/National Day is expected to keep inventories declining until after National Day. | Copper Mountain Mining reported positive drill results from 18 holes at its New Ingerbelle copper-gold open pit, including long intercepts and doubled vertical extent of mineralization. The company plans to add two drill rigs and continue drilling to expand and potentially increase copper resources and reserves, supporting bullish sentiment for copper. | The piece highlights aluminum strength driven by political unrest in Guinea, a major bauxite supplier, raising expectations of tighter supply and a larger deficit. For copper, it notes the market remains range-bound amid slowing growth momentum, weaker macro data, and reduced speculative positioning, keeping near-term upside limited. | Candente Copper announces Ausenco has been awarded a contract to start an updated 12-week Preliminary Economic Assessment for its Cañariaco Norte copper project in Peru. The study will develop updated mining, process plant, and infrastructure concepts to improve project economics and cost competitiveness, potentially supporting copper market sentiment. | The EPA is moving to block the Pebble Mine project in Alaska, targeting a development that includes copper alongside gold. The decision raises risk to planned copper output, tightening market expectations for future supply and providing upward support to copper prices in the near term.

SMM data show China’s major copper inventories fell 700 mt from Monday to 126,900 mt, driven mainly by declines in Shanghai and Guangdong. Shipments may not quickly lift warehouse stocks due to downstream routing, while smelter maintenance keeps arrivals weak. Inventories are expected to decline further ahead of Mid-Autumn Festival. | SMM reports August operating rates for domestic copper cathode rod producers rose 3.06 percentage points MoM, despite weaker orders from Yangzhou lockdowns. The key driver was copper falling below scrap prices, improving the cathode-vs-scrap spread and encouraging downstream purchases and restocking of cathode rods, even as inventory willingness stayed low. | Nickel prices surged to record highs in Shanghai and a seven-year peak in London, driven by strong demand and dwindling inventories. It also reports aluminium hitting its highest since 2008 on China and Guinea supply worries, while copper rose on improving smelter output and byproduct returns, keeping sentiment firmly bullish. | Nickel surged to record highs in Shanghai and above London’s seven-year peak as inventories dwindled and demand stayed strong, with refined nickel expected to remain in short supply in September. The same piece also highlights elevated prices in aluminium (supply worries in China and Guinea) and copper, reinforcing a broad bullish tone.

US legislators passed an amendment that adds the Save Oak Flat Act to a Biden spending package, creating fresh doubt over Rio Tinto and BHP’s planned Arizona copper mine. The proposed legislative hurdle raises the risk of delays or cancellation, weakening near-term copper supply expectations and weighing on prices. | North Korea says it will continue backing the Pebble copper-and-gold project as U.S. policy and court actions evolve over environmental veto authority affecting Bristol Bay. The dispute centers on mining licensing and discharge protections. The project could expand domestic copper supply, but timing uncertainty limits short-term price impact. | SMM says copper social inventories in China’s major trading markets fell 3,700 mt to 123,200 mt over the weekend, led by declines in Shanghai and Guangdong. Downstream quickly consumed imports, and SMM expects domestic inventories to likely fall further this week on restocking demand before the Mid-Autumn Festival, absent higher imports. | Jiangxi Copper and MCC said they are monitoring Afghanistan after Taliban control and will proceed with the Mes Aynak copper mine once conditions allow; substantial construction has not started. Mes Aynak is estimated to hold 11.08 million tonnes of copper, with Jiangxi holding 25%. Near-term output impact is unclear. | Jiangxi Copper and MCC say they are monitoring Afghanistan after the Taliban takeover and will proceed with the Mes Aynak copper mine only when conditions allow. The mine has large estimated copper reserves, but construction is not yet substantial. Jiangxi links benefits to current high copper prices, not new immediate supply. | LME benchmark aluminium rose to $3,000/tonne for the first time since 2008, up around 1% on Monday, as China’s smelters face output limits from power shortages and environmental rules. Guinea-related bauxite project delays add supply risk, with analysts estimating over 1 million tonnes disrupted this year. | SMM expects copper to remain rangebound this week. LME copper bounced after a USD-driven dip, supported by macro liquidity and less restrictive ECB stance, while upcoming US CPI/retail sales are watched. Fundamentals remain mixed: tighter scrap supply due to smelter inspections, but large late-September imports from LME warehouses weigh spot quotes. | SMM reports mixed Shanghai nonferrous closes, but aluminium led gains (+4.38%) amid rumours/notice of tighter Yunnan output and energy-driven curbs in other provinces. Copper also rose (+2.52%) with falling inventories and tight scrap. Nickel fell (-2.24%) as supply-demand signals conflicted. | Jiangxi Copper says it and MCC are monitoring Afghanistan’s situation and will push forward with the Mes Aynak copper mine when possible, after instability prevented substantial construction. The mine holds large copper reserves and the company notes recent high copper prices, supporting expectations for sustained strength. | Cruz Battery Metals reported three diamond drill holes at its Hector cobalt-silver property, including a broad silver-copper zone with assay ranges over tens of metres and additional cobalt-copper intervals. Nickel is mentioned only in the analytical scope, with no nickel results provided, limiting near-term price relevance.

Malaysia extended a grace period for stricter copper scrap import standards until Oct 31, requiring at least 94.75% metal content. This blocks lower-grade Category Seven and some Category Six scrap from entering Malaysia. With limited substitution sources, scrap supply available for China tightens further, likely supporting firmer copper prices. | Rob McEwen discusses inflation concerns and expects stronger prices in gold and silver, but he also turns bullish on copper. He references McEwen Copper’s vehicle and a US$80 million financing tied to the Los Azules copper property, suggesting improving outlook and potential upward sentiment for copper. | EMX Royalty Corp. said it expects an initial quarterly after-tax royalty payment of about US$974,000 from its 0.418% net smelter return interest in Chile’s Caserones copper-molybdenum mine. The payment is based on Q2 distributions for copper and molybdenum production, reflecting royalty cash flow. | US Copper reports that evacuation orders from the Dixie Fire are being lifted, but access to its Moonlight-Superior copper property remains restricted. Although the drill rig wasn’t damaged, the drill contractor is moving off-site, power was interrupted, and drilling is unlikely to resume fully this season, delaying assay progress. | Jiangxi Copper and MCC are reportedly working to revive stalled development of Afghanistan’s Mes Aynak, a large high-grade copper deposit, by negotiating amid Taliban rule. The project remains constrained by security instability and lack of substantial construction, but efforts to restart mining improve forward copper supply expectations. | Latin Metals reports additional rock chip sampling results from its Lacsha project in Peru, highlighting broad copper and molybdenum mineralization (e.g., 72m at 0.2% Cu) and expansion of the mineralized footprint across Lacsha North and Lacsha South. The update is exploration-stage and not tied to production. | Cyprium Metals reports ongoing drilling at its Nifty Copper Project in Western Australia, including eastern limb RC drilling, metallurgical diamond holes, and assessment of waste dump material for heap leach restart. It also starts infill and extension drilling at the nearby Maroochydore project. First assay results are expected soon. | The article’s headline says the world’s top copper supplier reduced its copper price forecast as China slows. That points to softer near-term demand expectations from the main end-market, which typically pressures copper prices downward over the following days as market sentiment adjusts. | Chile’s Cochilco revised its copper price estimate downwards. The downward revision can signal weaker expected copper pricing for the upcoming period, potentially reducing near-term support for copper futures as investors adjust expectations around supply-demand balance and realized copper revenues.

Mealtop, via a local partner in the Democratic Republic of Congo, signed contracts to supply finished copper (and cobalt) to buyers over three years. The release highlights EV-driven demand and claims early fulfillment starting in late 2021, suggesting improved near-term availability expectations for copper. | China’s copper cathode output rose in Jan–Aug 2021, mainly from blister copper tied to higher concentrate demand. In the second half, copper scrap supply tightened as high prices increased scrap consumption, while imports were limited by pandemic port controls. Next four months depend on restricted anode and scrap imports. | Three Valley Copper announced the start of 2021 near-mine exploration drilling on its Minera Tres Valles property in Chile, targeting high-potential copper occurrences between existing Don Gabriel and Papomono deposits. The first phase plans about 6,000–8,000 meters of drilling, aiming to define new near-surface copper mineralization. | Aluminum prices are reported to have surged 47% this year, reaching $2,907/tonne on the LME three-month offer, raising construction material costs and project pricing. The article links higher aluminum (and other inputs like copper) to increased builder expenses and reduced confidence, but does not suggest near-term relief. | Universal Copper announced that it has mobilized drill rigs and geological staff for a planned 3,000-metre 2021 drilling program at its Poplar copper project in British Columbia. The company aims to expand mineralization and higher-grade limits, citing prior resource growth and expecting value gains if copper prices strengthen. | Chile’s Cochilco lowered its copper price forecasts for this year and next, warning of a possible oversupply as global growth slows and stimulus is phased out. It expects supply to grow faster than demand. The end of a BHP Billiton labor shutdown in Chile may add supply. Investors are split on the outlook. | European indexes were mostly down, but the FTSE 100 avoided a bigger fall as oil and commodity prices rose. The article notes gains for mining companies such as Antofagasta and Glencore that benefited from higher copper prices, implying near-term bullish sentiment for copper.

Azure Minerals says drilling at its Andover project in Western Australia intersected nickel sulphide with both higher grade and longer mineralised intervals, describing the results as the highest grades yet. The update also references nickel-copper drill numbers, supporting modest upside sentiment for both nickel and copper markets. | China’s NDRC said the State Food and Material Reserves Bureau released a third batch of national reserves (starting Sept 1) including copper and aluminium, totaling 150,000 mt for copper, aluminium and zinc, with 420,000 mt accumulated across batches. Authorities will continue releasing reserves to stabilize prices and move them back to a reasonable range. | SMM reports August imported copper concentrate TCs rose to $58.55/mt, but weekly gains slowed as spot TCs moved above $60. While labor negotiations, strikes, and Peru logistics disruptions add uncertainty, expected mine output and smelting restart/support improve supply-demand for buyers. Copper prices remained rangebound. | China’s NDRC said the State Food and Material Reserves Bureau launched a third batch of national reserves, including copper and aluminium (plus zinc), totaling 150,000 mt, and plans further reserve sales. Officials aim to ensure supply and curb high prices, implying near-term downward pressure on copper and aluminium. | India’s government will sell up to 10% of its stake in Hindustan Copper via an offer-for-sale over two days, raising about Rs 1,120 crore. The filing sets a specific sale price and includes a greenshoe option. The article is about equity divestment, not copper supply or demand changes. | Copper fell 2% as markets focused on the upcoming U.S. Fed meeting for signals on stimulus tapering, a macro driver that can pressure metals. The article also cites China’s plan to release copper and other metals from state reserves. Aluminium softened amid China supply issues and higher LME inventories. | BeMetals extended an option agreement to allow further surface drilling at the South Mountain polymetallic (zinc-silver-gold-copper) project in Idaho. The release focuses on completing a drilling campaign to update resources and support a future PEA. It does not indicate any imminent production, supply change, or market-moving impacts for copper, nickel, or aluminium. | Pan Global Resources reported continued expansion of the La Romana copper-tin discovery at Spain’s Escacena Project, with multiple drill intercepts showing copper grades of up to 5% CuEq over multi-meter intervals and extension of mineralization beyond 1km strike length. The results remain exploration-focused with no production guidance. | Pan Global Resources reports continued drilling expanding the La Romana copper-tin discovery in southern Spain, with multiple high-grade copper intercepts and a >1 km strike extension open along strike and down dip. The news is exploration-focused and does not signal immediate supply disruptions or new production. | Battery Mineral Resources announced further drill results from its Punitaqui copper mine complex in Chile, including several high-grade copper intercepts (up to ~2.10% Cu over short intervals) and continued drilling activity.
