The contract snapped a two-session winning streak, falling in four of the past six sessions and remaining off recent highs after a strong run earlier in 2021.

Copper and aluminum prices are shown as dipping amid signs the global growth outlook is deteriorating. Weaker growth expectations tend to lower forecasts for industrial activity and metal demand in the near term, creating bearish sentiment and downward pressure on copper and aluminum prices. | Copper and aluminum are reported to be declining as the global growth outlook worsens. The market is likely repricing expectations for slower economic activity and reduced industrial demand, which typically weighs on base-metal prices in the near term over coming days. | Copper and aluminium are falling as market sentiment shifts toward a worsening global growth outlook. The weaker macro demand outlook tends to lower expectations for industrial consumption and related end-use demand, putting downward pressure on prices in the near term. | BeMetals announced results from 4,353 m of shallow aircore drilling at the Pangeni copper project in Zambia, reporting elevated bedrock copper grades and defining extension and new standalone copper targets (D-Prospect, F-Target, Q-Target). The company has started ~1,400 m core drilling to test these targets. | Quaterra Resources reported positive core drill assay results at its MacArthur copper project in Nevada, including multiple intervals with measurable copper grades and indications of potential oxide and sulfide resource expansion and upgrades from inferred to indicated. An updated resource estimate is expected after drilling and consultant review. | Quaterra Resources reported positive core-drilling assay results from its MacArthur copper project in Nevada, including multiple oxide and chalcocite intercepts and indications for potential expansion and upgrading of resources from inferred to measured/indicated. The company is updating its resource estimate, supporting incremental bullish sentiment for copper. | Peru’s government brokered a truce between MMG and the Chumbivilcas community to avoid a road blockade at the Las Bambas mine. The agreement includes local employment and logistics support, addressing community demands. The move follows protests that previously prevented transporting copper, threatening output and boosting near-term supply clarity.

Cannindah Resources says it has achieved a large “monster-sized” copper drill hit in Queensland, with only half the hole assayed so far. The discovery news can lift expectations for future copper supply potential, though incomplete assay limits certainty and reduces immediate impact on broader copper pricing. | Tinka Resources announced high-grade copper results from initial surface sampling at its Silvia NW copper-gold skarn target in Peru, including trench and rock chip values up to 12.3% copper. The find is near Antamina and remains under exploration, supporting bullish sentiment for copper prospects despite no production impact. | Copper Fox provides an update on its 100%-owned Van Dyke oxide copper project in Arizona. Work focuses on a conceptual hydrogeological model for permitting, including groundwater level/flow measurements and comparing historical water data. Preliminary core inspections confirm copper mineralization, with planned logging and sampling to estimate grade and extent. | Torq Resources commenced its first drill program at the Margarita IOCG copper-gold project in northern Chile. The initial phase targets copper-gold sulphide mineralization beneath surface copper oxide zones, with drilling planned around defined southern and northern anomalies. The news is an exploration step, not production. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. announced drilling assay results from its Kenbridge nickel-copper-cobalt project in Ontario, reporting zones with nickel and copper grades and noting conductive sulphide likely extends below intersections based on TDEM interpretation. Additional drilling assays are pending, and no near-term production impact is provided. | Pan Global Resources announced new drilling results at its Escacena, Spain La Romana copper/tin target, reporting expanded continuity of near-surface high-grade copper mineralization and commencing new IP targets. While no production change is stated, the positive exploration update may modestly boost copper sentiment over coming weeks. | Thiess was named preferred mining-services contractor for Austral Resources’ Anthill Copper Project in Queensland, with negotiations toward a three-year deal to run two mining fleets and support copper production. Austral plans to start mining this year and produce about 40,000 tonnes of LME Grade A copper cathode over four years. | Bam Bam Resources reports sampling of previously unsampled historic drill core at the DeSoto area of its Majuba Hill copper project in Nevada. Surface rock chips and sampled intervals show elevated copper grades, with assays submitted for analysis. The update is exploration-focused and unlikely to materially move near-term copper benchmarks. | Orestone Mining announced an MT geophysical survey at its Captain gold-copper project in British Columbia, outlining a large conductive sulphide-rich target and validating the company’s porphyry-style model. The company expects next drilling to begin, but no resource or production update was provided. | Tinka Resources announced high-grade copper-gold surface sampling at its Silvia NW copper-gold skarn target in Peru, including copper assays up to about 12.3%. Results cover a 400m by 100m trend and show copper-gold correlation, suggesting strong prospectivity. This is early exploration, not production or reserve updates. | Copper Fox provided an update on its 100% owned Van Dyke oxide copper project in Arizona. Work focuses on a conceptual hydrogeological model for permitting, including groundwater level/flow measurements and review of historical water data. Preliminary inspection of Azurite drill cores confirms copper mineralization and planned sampling/logging. | Copper is described as gaining, with investors weighing improving growth momentum and China’s return. The framing suggests expectations for stronger demand and risk appetite are supporting copper prices in the near term, rather than highlighting supply disruptions or policy changes specific to copper.

The piece links China’s property slowdown and power rationing to weaker copper demand, with downstream impacts outweighing smelter supply effects. It also says aluminium demand has turned down in China as plate/sheet producers cut or halt, implying prices may ease. Global aluminium may still be tight, but near-term demand risks dominate. | Credit Suisse warns that China’s copper demand turned negative in June amid the property downturn, and power rationing will likely hit downstream fabricators more than smelters, worsening demand contraction. It also expects China aluminium prices to ease as restrictions reduce downstream output despite continued smelter limits. | SMM reviews post-holiday conditions: LME three-month copper is largely stable, with swings driven by US PMI/dollar and Powell’s comments. Attention centers on upcoming US payrolls, power-rationing impacts on inventories, and potentially a soft short squeeze before SHFE delivery if inventory rises slightly. Scrap imports may increase. | SMM says China’s major trading-market copper inventories rose by 20,900 mt from Sept 30 to 111,000 mt after the National Day holiday. Increases were led by Shanghai and Guangdong. Power rationing continued to affect smelters and processors, with limited imported inflows; demand should improve slightly, so inventories may ease next week. | Playfair Mining reports successful drilling at its RKV copper project in Norway, moving from the Rødalen target to a second target at Storboren. Four short holes intersected sulphide-bearing amphibolite and strong MMI copper anomalies, suggesting potential copper mineralization. Samples are pending analysis; no production or concrete supply impact is announced. | Mongolia’s Justice Minister warned Rio Tinto that patience may run out unless it makes progress on the Oyu Tolgoi copper mine. The government cited delays and cost blowouts, and said it was surprised by Rio’s response to an independent July report disputing reasons for a two-year schedule slip and $1.45 billion overrun.

SMM said China’s mainstream copper inventory rose 1,400 mt to 112,400 mt from Oct 8 to Oct 11, mainly due to higher cathode stocks and imported arrivals, especially in Shanghai. Downstream shipments stayed sluggish on power rationing in parts of the region. A weekly drawdown is expected later. | Copper fell 0.3% on the LME as a firmer dollar strengthened on expectations the Fed will begin tapering stimulus in November. Separately, the article cites aluminium production halts from high electricity prices and a nickel shutdown due to higher energy costs, but LME nickel and aluminium were up on the day. | Nickel prices rose to a two-week high as power shortages and higher electricity costs in China and Europe raised supply fears. Nickel inventories fell on the LME and ShFE, while a ferro-nickel producer shut output and smelters faced possible curtailments. Higher energy costs were also cited for aluminium production cuts. | Valor Resources reported widespread high-grade copper sampling at its Picha (Peru) copper-silver project, including channel results up to 35.6m averaging 1.3% Cu and a 13.39% Cu grab sample at Maricate. The news may be sentiment-supportive for copper, but it is exploration-stage and not near-term supply-relevant. | SMM forecasts copper prices to trend lower in October as intensifying power rationing disrupts production and weakens downstream demand. The power cuts are expected to pressure the consumption side more, keep imports of secondary copper from rising sharply, and leave scrap demand stronger than supply. | Habakkuk Trust launched a documentary on copper cable thefts in Bulawayo, Zimbabwe, linking the crime to recurring power cuts. It says thieves increasingly target both underground and overhead copper cables almost daily. Residents often replace stolen copper with aluminum cables due to shortages and the power utility’s limited capacity. | Codelco is offering Europe physical copper premiums of $128/tonne for 2022 versus $98 in 2021, the highest since 2015, seen as a market benchmark likely to be followed by others. Aurubis also set a high premium. Tightness is supported by deficit expectations from the ICSG. | Bam Bam Resources added a second drill rig at the Majuba Hill Porphyry Copper Project in Nevada to expand the drill footprint and test in-fill/expansion of oxide copper mineralization. Drilling targets a copper-gold zone, with ongoing results driving the expanded program, potentially supporting copper supply expectations. | Vale International S.A. was added as a member of the International Copper Association (ICA). The release frames the move around ESG and decarbonization efforts, with ICA working on a roadmap to decarbonize copper processes. No new production, supply, or demand shock is announced. | Codelco reportedly increased the Europe copper premium by 31%, a bullish signal for the regional physical market. A larger premium generally reflects tighter availability or stronger demand in Europe, which can raise expectations for near-term copper pricing. The move would likely support copper sentiment over the coming days. | Activists and the Fond du Lac Band won multiple federal and state rulings affecting PolyMet’s proposed NorthMet copper-nickel open-pit mine in Minnesota. EPA “may affect” findings and court decisions suspend permits and require further review, heightening delays and potential supply constraints for copper and nickel. | A global energy crisis is weighing on copper sentiment. Investors worry that power shortages and factory slowdowns will curb near-term demand, even as long-term bullish views persist. Rising natural gas and electricity costs could contribute to stagflation-like conditions, with demand weakening while input costs stay elevated.

Codelco said copper production this year will be about 2% higher than previously estimated even after a strike cuts output at the Andina project. August production at Codelco fell year-on-year due to the strike. The firm cites strong cash flow and confidence in planned mine developments. | Antofagasta’s CEO said global transport and energy bottlenecks have temporarily increased production costs for copper miners, including higher logistics and shipping/airfreight expenses. He expects energy and shipping costs to normalize as Chile’s seasonal demand changes, while maintaining that copper market fundamentals remain strong despite supply tightness. | Newcrest said it expects production costs to halve over coming years and higher copper output from its Australian and Canadian mines, projecting a 37% rise to about 175,000 tonnes by the end of the decade. The firm’s gold-focused model includes copper as a by-product and signals growing exposure to green-energy demand. | Taseko said its Gibraltar Mine produced 34.5 million pounds of copper in Q3, up 29% from Q2, supported by improved grades and recoveries, and efficient processing of new ore during a transition into the Gibraltar pit. The company also links results to improved unit costs and financial performance. | Copper Mountain Mining reported positive drill intercepts from 48 holes at its Cameron Copper Project in Australia, identifying multiple large mineralized zones and potential for a larger copper-gold system. The release supports longer-term resource potential but provides no immediate evidence of supply disruptions or production changes. | Surge Copper Corp. announced assay results from resource definition drilling at its Ootsa Property in British Columbia. The company reports a 495-metre intercept grading 0.54% CuEq, including 126 metres at 0.85% CuEq, and outlines progress and upcoming pending results, which may support copper sentiment. | Ivanhoe Mines reports Kamoa-Kakula copper operations progressing ahead of schedule: Phase 2 concentrator construction is over 50% complete and expected to begin operations in Q2 2022, while Phase 1 throughput exceeds design and a second filter press produced a daily record 721 tonnes of copper concentrate. | A GCC non-ferrous metals market outlook report links Vision 2030 to higher aluminum demand from construction, packaging and EV-related lightweighting, plus copper value-chain initiatives such as secondary smelting, recycling, and reduced cathode import dependence. The piece is mainly strategic and forecast-based, implying limited immediate price impact. | Nevada Copper announced extensions to its KfW senior project facility and consolidation of shareholder loans, adding about US$41 million liquidity and deferring loan repayments to support ramp-up of its underground copper operations and progress toward open-pit and exploration milestones. Closing is subject to approvals and conditions precedent. | Ivanhoe Mines says Kamoa-Kakula’s Phase 2 concentrator expansion is progressing ahead of schedule, targeting full operations in Q2 2022. Phase 1 throughput and a second filter press are exceeding design parameters, with record filtered concentrate output. The project aims to double copper output to over 400,000 tonnes annually. | Indonesia has begun construction of what President Joko Widodo calls the world’s largest copper smelter, with capacity to process 1.7 million tons of concentrate per year. Built by PT Freeport Indonesia, the project is part of Indonesia’s downstream push, with an investment of $3 billion and an initial copper metal output of about 480,000 tons annually. | President Joko Widodo has kicked off construction of what is described as the world’s largest copper smelter in JIIPE. A new smelter project can signal increased future refined copper capacity, which may support copper prices through improved supply expectations, even if production gains are not immediate. | Newcrest completed a pre-feasibility study for the Red Chris block cave gold and copper mine in Northern BC and approved moving to feasibility. The study estimates reserves of 2 million oz copper and projects about 80k tonnes of copper per year from 2028 onward, which can signal future supply despite the long ramp-up.

China’s September copper imports rose 3% month-on-month after five months of declines, as delayed cargoes cleared port congestion following eased COVID curbs. However, Q3 imports fell to a two-year low. Shanghai bonded refined inventories dropped sharply, and copper concentrate imports increased. | Freeport has started building a $3bn copper smelter in Gresik, Indonesia, targeting 600,000t/year of copper cathode and up to 1.7Mt/year of concentrate processing. The project, near existing operations, is scheduled to start in late 2023 or early 2024 and would shift value-added output to Indonesia. | Freeport Indonesia began building a $3bn copper smelter and precious metals refinery in East Java with 1.7 million tonnes/year concentrate capacity, producing copper cathodes, pipes and wires. The project, in a Gresik industrial zone, is linked to Freeport’s Grasberg supply and targets commissioning in 2024, supporting copper downstream growth expectations. | Nobel Resources announced it signed an option agreement to acquire 100% of the La Salvadora IOCG copper-gold project in Chile, including scheduled option payments and a future NSR royalty. The release highlights copper mineralized zones and adjacent operations, but provides no near-term production impact, limiting broader price effects. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. reported additional drilling results at its 100% owned Kenbridge nickel-copper project in Ontario, including strong Ni grades over 25.5 metres (with associated copper) and additional zone intercepts. TDEM surveys support conductive zone continuity, guiding further drilling toward down-dip and along-strike extensions. | Tartisan Nickel reported additional drilling results from its Kenbridge Ni-Cu project in Ontario, including 25.5 metres of 1.13% Ni with 0.61% Cu, plus higher-grade nickel-copper intervals. Interpretation is based on TDEM-conductive zones and supports ongoing down-dip/along-strike drilling, aiding sentiment for nickel and copper. | Norilsk says Botswana has not implemented an agreed settlement and the dispute over liquidation and sale of BCL’s assets remains in London arbitration. Observers warn PNRB’s plan to acquire and restart the Selebi nickel-copper-cobalt assets may fall through without a resolution, affecting near-term supply outlooks. | District Copper announced a fall induced polarization geophysical survey over its Copper Keg project in British Columbia and completed a one-week mapping and geochemistry program. Historic interpretations suggest a buried porphyry copper system; grab samples showed copper values up to 124.5 ppm. It remains early exploration with limited immediate market impact. | The article argues copper should rebound, pointing to declining LME inventories and a potential supply squeeze after copper fell on COVID-19 Delta-driven economic worries and weaker China output. It expects EV and renewable grid growth to raise demand, supporting a near-term rally in copper prices. | Fathom Nickel reported preliminary interpretation of 2021 soil geochemistry from its Albert Lake property, showing multiple anomalous Ni zones with associated Cu and other elements, and noted drilling is ongoing with assays pending. It also received DTC eligibility approval for OTC trading, mainly impacting investor access rather than metal fundamentals. | Bitterroot Resources reported assay results from two holes at its LM Project, stating massive and semi-massive sulphides are enriched in nickel and copper, with disseminated mineralization in one hole. The company continues drilling toward additional targets, but no near-term production or supply disruption is indicated. | Copper output at Taseko Mines’ Gibraltar facility near Williams Lake rose 29% this quarter to over 34 million pounds. The company has mobilized drilling to continue spring expansion and will investigate geophysical anomalies, while operations transition smoothly to the Gibraltar Pit. Management cites strong copper costs and $50M credit acquisition for the year ahead.

South32 is buying a 45% stake in Chile’s Sierra Gorda copper mine for up to $2.05 billion (about $2.8 billion). Management says the mine’s reputation helped secure a “cheap” price. The deal expands South32’s copper exposure, but the article doesn’t mention immediate production changes. | Charger Metals announced SkyTEM aerial survey results over its Coates Ni-Cu Au-PGE project near Perth, delineating 22 potential sulphide conductor targets and anomalous Ni-Cu-Au-PGE values near Target T1. The findings support forthcoming drilling, but no production impacts are described. | Charger Metals reported SkyTEM helicopter EM survey results at the Coates Ni-Cu Au-PGE Project near Perth, delineating multiple sulphide-conductor targets, including strong Ni-Cu-Au-PGE anomalies at the northern end of target T1. The update supports drilling plans but does not indicate immediate production or supply changes. | Metal market coverage highlights that the LME zinc has risen while focusing on copper and aluminium. Copper cash/3-month trading is described as being in backwardation, implying tighter prompt conditions. For aluminium, the text references supply curtailments and disrupted shipping (Strait of Hormuz) but suggests pricing moves have been limited. | Indonesia’s President attended the groundbreaking of PT Freeport Indonesia’s 3 billion USD copper smelter in Gresik, East Java. The project, backed by the government’s 51% stake, is planned for 1.7 million tonnes of annual copper capacity, potentially increasing domestic processing. Timing is construction-stage, limiting immediate price effects. | Chakana Copper reported 14 resource definition drill holes at its Soledad Project in Peru’s Breccia Pipe 5, including copper-bearing intersections (e.g., 0.55% Cu and 1.17 g/t gold) and continued IP geophysical surveys. The update is early-stage and does not signal immediate production or supply changes. | Rockridge completed a helicopter-borne VTEM Plus geophysical survey at the Knife Lake VMS project in Saskatchewan, targeting copper-bearing mineralization. The release summarizes prior drilling copper grades and notes planned follow-up work for future diamond drilling. It is a development-stage exploration update without any immediate production or trade effects. | Bam Bam Resources reports expanded soil geochemical anomalies at its Majuba Hill project in Nevada, identifying larger copper/silver/gold target zones, including an extended DeSoto oxide copper/silver anomaly and a new copper-gold target. Core drilling is planned in late October, but no production or supply impacts are indicated. | Battery Mineral Resources reported additional assay results from ongoing drilling at the Punitaqui project in Chile, highlighting multiple copper intercepts in the San Andres target. The company says results demonstrate continuity and grade and could add to copper ore sources as it aims for potential resumption of operations in 2022. | Sumitomo plans to sell its entire 45% stake in the Sierra Gorda copper mining project in Chile to a preferred purchaser. The deal is an ownership exit rather than an explicit production change, so it is unlikely to drive immediate copper supply expectations materially. | Bank of America technical analysis says copper is poised for record highs as traders rotate from oil into copper, with CME copper futures jumping and breaking key resistance. The article adds that contractors report copper material shortages and producer inflation, reinforcing bullish fundamentals despite some near-term oil correction signals. | Battery Mineral Resources announced additional drill-core assay results from the Punitaqui mine complex in Chile, including multiple copper intercepts with grades up to ~2%+ Cu over substantial intervals. The company says results support continuity and grade and may help resume operations and cash flow, which could be supportive for copper sentiment.

SMM says mainstream Chinese copper inventories fell 7,200 mt from Oct 11 to 105,200 mt, led by declines in Guangdong, Shanghai, and Jiangsu. The drop reflects fewer imported copper arrivals and tighter domestic smelter output. While Tianjin rose due to processing curbs, overall supply remains tight and could stay so next week. | South32 agreed to buy a 45% indirect stake in Chile’s Sierra Gorda copper mine from Sumitomo for up to $2.05bn, with payments tied to copper production thresholds and prices. The mine is large and long-life, but the deal is primarily ownership transfer, not immediate production change. | The piece focuses on how illicit copper trading is harming South Africa’s copper industry. By siphoning copper from legitimate channels or disrupting mining/refining and export flows, it can reduce effective market supply in the near term. That supply tightness typically supports copper prices. | EnGold Mines reports completion of a 916.5m first drill hole at its Lac La Hache Ann North porphyry copper-gold prospect, with visible chalcopyrite and bornite and magnetite increasing at depth; assays are pending. It also details follow-up drilling at the G1 copper deposit, targeting the porphyry source.

Nevada Copper’s Pumpkin Hollow copper project secured additional financing and debt facility deferrals via agreements with its lender and largest shareholder. The company says this improves balance-sheet flexibility for ramping underground mining and advancing other phases. The article notes strong TSX gains in Nevada Copper, which is linked to copper supply prospects. | SMM reports that Chinese copper scrap users cut output due to tight scrap supply, production losses, and demand suppression from the energy crisis (power rationing) and higher natural gas prices. Copper alloy firms in Jiangsu/Zhejiang reduced runs. Scrap inventories are low and imports are constrained by strict customs inspections, tightening supply further. | SMM says Chinese mainstream copper inventories rose 3,900 mt from Oct 15 to 109,100 mt, adding for the second Monday after the weekend. Buyers avoided restocking amid surging prices, and slightly higher arrivals also lifted stocks. With power rationing and weaker cathode demand, inventory is expected to add this week. | Pancon paused drilling at its Brewer gold-copper project until lab results are received, and is updating its geologic discovery model and environmental remediation plan for historic acid mine drainage. The company reports progress on drilling meters and pending assays, aiming to refine targeting for a funded Phase 3. | Candente Copper said it reached conditional commercial terms with Gold Fields to option the Arikepay copper-gold porphyry property in southern Peru. The deal involves staged cash payments and exploration commitments toward increasing ownership interests, potentially supporting future copper development. No immediate production or trade impacts are cited. | South Africa says illicit theft of copper cables from Eskom and Transnet has surged, with scrap often smuggled and processed abroad. The losses include replacement costs and service disruption. With tight global scrap supplies and low inventories, reduced accessible copper scrap supply supports firmer copper expectations near term. | The piece highlights that the copper spread has widened to its widest level in more than 25 years, attributing the move to a supply squeeze. Such a widening spread usually reflects tighter spot/near-term availability, which tends to put upward pressure on copper prices over the coming days. | Norilsk Nickel announced recommissioning of its Ust-Khantayskaya hydropower plant after replacing the seventh unit, raising capacity from 441 MW to 511 MW and improving reliability. The upgrade enabled delivery of the first batch of carbon-neutral nickel cathodes to an LME-approved warehouse and supports ramping up carbon-neutral production. | US Copper Corp announced positive assay results from the first five of seven drill holes at the Superior copper deposit in northeast California. The company says grades show excellent continuity with historic drilling, with reported intervals averaging above the PEA cutoff and indicating silver/gold credits may contribute to economics.

BHP and Andrew Forrest’s Wyloo are bidding for Canada’s Noront, which holds a major undeveloped “Ring of Fire” resource. The board prefers Wyloo’s higher offer, while BHP has a deadline to respond. The stakes are in nickel and copper potential, supporting bullish sentiment. | Max Resource Corp. reported new reconnaissance rock assay results expanding the CONEJO zone within its CESAR North copper-silver project in NE Colombia, including intervals with up to 3.3% copper over 15 m. The news is exploration-based and does not indicate near-term mine output or market supply changes. | Max Resource Corp. reports reconnaissance rock assays expanding the CONEJO copper-silver zone in Colombia, with outcrops up to 3.3% copper over 15m and other high-grade copper values. The release highlights ongoing exploration but provides no direct near-term supply or demand changes beyond commentary on rising copper. | Fastmarkets’ live futures update says LME base metals prices were all up. It highlights copper’s C/3M spread at $740/t in backwardation, pointing to stronger near-term copper prices. Aluminium is mentioned as part of the broader upward futures move, but without a specific causal catalyst. | Brixton Metals says it drilled an 821m copper porphyry interval at the Camp Creek target, including higher-grade sections based on CuEq. While positive exploration results may support future copper supply expectations and sentiment, the announcement alone is unlikely to cause a significant immediate move in copper prices. | The piece is headlined around a “historic flash squeeze” in the London copper market, implying rapid upward pressure driven by very tight positioning/liquidity. While the provided excerpt lacks details, the direct reference to copper and flash-squeeze dynamics point to near-term bullish price pressure for copper. | The article’s headline says Trafigura helped drain LME copper inventories. Reduced warehouse stocks on the London Metal Exchange generally signals tighter near-term availability, which can raise expectations for prompt copper supply. That inventory draw would likely provide upward price pressure in the days after publication. | Orion, a South African miner focused on copper, is exploring a partnership to build a copper refinery in the Northern Cape. The company is sounding out potential players and could supply base feedstock and/or a refinery site. The initiative remains exploratory, but it points to improved copper refining prospects.

SMM analysis links electrolytic copper prospects to supply-demand dynamics. High copper prices earlier draw down recycled-copper channel inventory, while Malaysia scrap disruptions and stricter customs restrain recycled imports. At the same time, improved concentrate tightness and higher processing fees/acid prices support smelting. Macro/demand concerns exist but supply tightness dominates. | BHP raised its takeover offer for Canadian nickel miner Noront Resources, outbidding Wyloo. The bid targets Noront’s Eagle’s Nest nickel deposit in Canada’s Ring of Fire, and the article notes copper is also present. The scramble underscores efforts to secure battery-metal supply ahead of EV growth. | Cannindah Resources announced initial full-hole results from its Mt Cannindah copper-gold project, reporting 282m at 1.28% copper equivalent and multiple high-grade copper intercepts. The company links the news to a strong copper price environment and says the next hole assays are pending soon. Results support improved copper supply outlook. | Antofagasta said Q3 group copper output increased 1.5% quarter-on-quarter to 181,100 tonnes, driven by higher production at Centinela partly offset by lower throughput at Los Pelambres. Full-year copper production guidance of 710,000-740,000 tonnes was unchanged, supporting near-term copper supply expectations. | SMM reports copper prices jumped after the National Day holiday, driven by record-low global LME inventories, high canceled warrants, and rapidly expanding LME backwardation. LME cash-to-three-month backwardation rose to about $257/mt, while SHFE/China inventories remained tight. Macro factors like a weaker dollar and inflation expectations are viewed as supportive. | LME 0-3 copper backwardation jumped to above $1,000/mt and spot import losses widened to about 500 yuan/mt, driven by a high share of cancelled warrants and falling warrant liquidity. With import economics deteriorating, some smelters decided to export copper cathode, tightening nearby copper supply conditions. | A government-ordered study in Minnesota could lead to a mining ban, threatening a proposed copper-nickel mine. If regulatory action prevents development, market expectations for future copper and nickel supply would weaken, likely pressuring prices in the coming days as investors reprice supply risk. | A government-ordered study may result in a mining ban that would significantly undermine a proposed copper-nickel mine in Minnesota. The prospect of reduced or halted development increases uncertainty around future supply, affecting market expectations in both copper and nickel in the near term. | The Biden administration ordered a mineral withdrawal study that could impose up to a 20-year ban on mining upstream from Minnesota’s Boundary Waters, dealing a blow to the proposed Twin Metals copper-nickel mine. The action blocks new prospecting permits or leases, raising risk of delayed or reduced future copper and nickel supply. | The article says a Biden administration move could block a copper mine near the Boundary Waters Canoe Area (BWCA). If approval or development is halted, it could reduce near-term copper supply prospects, supporting expectations of tighter inventories and downward pressure on copper prices in the coming weeks. | The update says a U.S. plan would block Antofagasta’s Minnesota copper mine. If the project is halted or delayed, market expectations for future copper supply could worsen, affecting near-term pricing through anticipated production disruption and permitting risk. | LME moved to calm copper market turmoil after warehouse inventories plunged to the lowest since 1974, driving extreme backwardation and cash prices. It tightened rules for large holders’ metal lending, imposed temporary price/backwardation limits, and let copper shorts delay delivery to settle. Goal: restore liquidity and orderliness. | The Biden administration ordered a mineral withdrawal study that could impose up to a 20-year ban on mining upstream from Minnesota’s Boundary Waters, potentially blocking the Twin Metals copper-nickel project. The move pauses new prospecting permits or leases in the watershed, while valid private rights may be affected by ongoing court challenges.

The US Forest Service proposed a 20-year ban on mining in Minnesota border waters, freezing new permits and blocking Antofagasta’s bimetallic copper-nickel project. The move cuts expected domestic supply for EV-related copper and nickel, potentially tightening near-term market expectations while the approval process is reviewed. | The US Forest Service proposed a 20-year ban on mining in Minnesota border waters, freezing new mining leases for two years and effectively blocking Antofagasta’s bimetallic copper-nickel project. Environmental reviews could extend uncertainty for copper and nickel supply, weighing on price expectations for both metals. | Guangdong power rationing eased after the National Day, allowing most copper processing firms to resume production. A few rod/tube areas still face output cuts, but the article says power is no longer the key driver. Instead, a sharp copper price rise widened cathode–scrap spreads and reduced downstream orders, lowering operating rates. | BHP is working to make its Western Australia Nickel West nickel operations lower-carbon, including a solar power purchase agreement for part of Kwinana refinery electricity and studies on mineral carbonation of tailings. The company also seeks higher nickel supply agreements tied to EV batteries, while copper is cited as another growth priority. | Freeport reported a quarterly profit jump, attributed to higher copper prices. The update links the company’s earnings performance to improved copper pricing conditions, which can influence short-term sentiment and expectations for industrial metal prices in the near term. | Constantine Metal Resources announced it will acquire 100% of the Hornet Creek copper-gold property in Idaho and plans additional staking, geophysical surveys, and diamond drilling. Historical drilling indicates copper-gold mineralization and possible porphyry/VMS potential, but no immediate production or supply changes are described.

Ugandan Vice President Jessica Alupo said Uganda will seek investors to restart copper and cobalt production at the long-abandoned Kilembe mine and will begin the bidding process soon. The mine is reported to have about 4 million tons of ore with 1.98% copper, supporting copper-supply expectations. | LME copper futures dropped over 3% as perceived tightness in nearby supply eased and traders shifted focus to weakening Chinese demand amid growth concerns and power shortages. The piece also notes nickel and aluminium fell more than 5% and 4%, respectively, in a broader risk-off move. | SMM reported Chinese mainstream copper inventories fell 14,400 mt to 94,700 mt, with declines in Shanghai, Guangdong, Jiangsu and Tianjin, while Zhejiang rose slightly. The drop was attributed to lower customs clearance/poor import ratios and regional delivery shifts. Next week imports may rise, but demand could weaken due to high premiums and month-end. | SMM data show China’s copper bonded-zone inventories fell 8,300 mt to 237,200 mt between Oct 15 and Oct 22. Shanghai bonded inventories dropped 8,300 mt to 213,200 mt, while Guangdong stayed at 24,000 mt. The company is stepping out to expand the deposit and plans more drilling, with geophysics suggesting continued mineralization to depth and along strike. | Copper and aluminium reportedly erased earlier gains as an energy crisis roiled broader markets. The wording suggests rising energy-related uncertainty and potential cost/availability concerns are pressuring sentiment for industrial metals, which typically move with supply risk and near-term demand outlook. | Copper and aluminium erased earlier gains as an energy crisis roiled broader markets. The headline suggests worsening energy-related conditions and investor risk appetite are weighing on industrial metals prices in the near term. | An appeals court hearing questioned whether it can override an act of Congress enabling Rio Tinto to receive Arizona land for the Resolution copper mine. Judges appeared likely to back the U.S. government’s plan, reducing legal risk. The mine holds large copper resources and remains unproducing, so outcomes can affect copper expectations soon.

A Peru community failed to agree with the government and will block road access to Minmetals’ Las Bambas, potentially disrupting copper-concentrate logistics. COVID-19 has also hit northwest China land transport, causing concentrate accumulation at ports and slightly lower spot TCs. Meanwhile, Dongying Fangyuan’s smelting project start may add feedstock demand during long-term TC talks. | The overseas copper industry roundup highlights Freeport’s Q3 copper output of 987 million lb versus analyst estimates near 1 billion lb, intensifying concerns over tighter global supply. It also notes BHP’s FY2022 first-quarter nickel output fell 20% y/y amid planned maintenance, potentially supporting near-term prices for copper and nickel. | SMM data shows copper inventories in major Chinese markets rose 3,900 mt over the weekend to 98,600 mt, led by increases in Shanghai and Guangdong. Higher imported copper arrivals and downstream suspensions contributed. Inventories are expected to rise slightly again, amid power rationing and softer cathode demand. | Zijin Mining commissioned its Timok copper-gold mine in Serbia on October 22. The $474 million project uses modern technology and is described as Serbia’s first “green mine.” Start of production increases expectations for additional copper supply, supporting a bullish near-term tone for copper prices despite limited ramp details. | Zijin commissioned the Čukaru Peki copper-gold mine near Bor, Serbia, expanding the only copper mining complex in the country (RTB Bor). The project includes an upper zone processing 3.3 million tonnes of ore per year and targets 50,000 tonnes of copper production in 2021. | Indigenous advocates seek to stop a proposed copper mine at Oak Flat in Arizona through federal court action. Led by Apache Stronghold, the challenge targets a project run by Resolution Copper, a BHP and Rio Tinto joint venture. Halting or delaying the mine could tighten expected copper supply.

A trust tied to Metallica’s lead singer sold 20 acres in Arizona’s Santa Rita Mountains to Rosemont Copper for $1.2 million. Hudbay plans exploratory drilling to assess feasibility of up to three open pits at its “Copper World” copper project, including $34 million in studies this year. | LME copper rebounded 1.68% to close near $9,867/t as low inventory levels and investor demand for inflation hedges supported prices. LME and SHFE copper stocks were cited as falling to multi-year lows. The article also reports higher LME nickel, reflecting tighter short-term supply. | An appeals court hearing questioned whether it can overturn a Congressional bill transferring Arizona land to Rio Tinto for a copper project challenged by Native Americans. Judges signaled potential support for the government’s plan, with Congress considering repeal. A clearer path to proceed is modestly supportive for copper supply expectations. | Sibanye-Stillwater says it is in advanced talks to buy Brazilian assets including Atlantic Nickel’s Santa Rita (nickel sulphide and copper) and Serrote (copper and gold). The roughly $1 billion deal would add to battery-metal supply as miners race to secure inputs for EVs. The buy-side interest supports sentiment for nickel and copper. | Sibanye-Stillwater says it is in advanced talks to buy Brazilian assets including Santa Rita (nickel and copper) and Serrote (copper and gold). The deal could be about $1 billion. The targets expand battery-metal supply plans, but no near-term production disruption or volume change is specified. | Fitch Solutions, via MiningWeekly, projects global copper mine output will rise 7.8% in 2021 versus last year, helped by new project commissioning and a low 2020 base from Covid-19. It highlights BHP’s Spence expansion starting production in December 2020 and a slower growth outlook for China thereafter. | SMM reports progress from several copper projects, including drilling results at the Marimaca mine in Chile and the Valinza/Warintza expansion in Ecuador, with oxidized ore grades and thick mineralization. It also details drilling updates at the Soledad project in Peru. Overall, improved copper resource potential may support copper sentiment. | UBS raised its forecast for international copper prices after observing higher physical premiums and a sharp decline in global exchange stocks, particularly in China. In October, copper rose 18% and supply risk grew as power rationing could constrain cathode supply. UBS expects copper to stabilize around $12,000/mt in coming quarters. | Appian agreed to sell its Brazilian battery-metals portfolio—Atlantic Nickel’s Santa Rita nickel mine and MVV’s Serrote copper-gold project—to Sibanye-Stillwater for US$1.0 billion plus additional royalties. The assets are already restarting/ramping, indicating no immediate supply shock, so metal price impact is likely limited. | Sibanye Stillwater agreed to buy Brazil’s Santa Rita nickel mine and Serrote copper-gold mine for about $1 billion plus a 5% NSR royalty. The producing assets are expected to add immediate cash flows. The transaction supports sentiment toward nickel and copper used in energy-transition supply chains. | Sibanye Stillwater agreed to buy Brazil’s Santa Rita nickel mine and Serrote copper-gold mine for about $1 billion, adding producing low-cost assets to its “green metals” portfolio. The deal targets battery-metal expansion and is expected to boost cash flows, supporting sentiment for nickel and copper. | Comex copper for October delivery settled 1.43% lower at $4.4865 per pound, down 6.50 cents. The contract fell on five of the past seven sessions, posting the lowest settlement since Oct. 12, 2021, and is off recent highs, showing near-term bearish momentum for copper. | Interra Copper Corp. reports completion of additional geophysical and geochemical work at its Thane Property, including grid soil sampling over a historical copper anomaly, and notes mineralized signatures with chalcopyrite/bornite plus discovery of supergene native copper. Assay results are being received and Gail is prioritized for drilling.

WTIA Group in South Korea says it has secured a contract to purchase 50,000 tons of cobalt- and copper-rich ore from mines in the Democratic Republic of Congo and will begin full-scale production at an appointed ore-processing plant next month. Sales are planned after inspections. The group also targets EV battery processing locally. | LME copper futures fell 0.8% to $9,788.5/t as investors worried that an energy crisis and slowing Chinese growth would weaken metal demand. Analysts also flagged uncertainty about how production curbs from high energy prices and China’s emissions measures may spill over to processors, while LME inventories rebounded slightly. | Colombia’s environmental regulator ANLA shelved AngloGold’s license application for the Quebradona copper-and-gold project, citing technical issues in the environmental impact assessment (area definition, hydrology, geotechnical/hydrogeological and slag disposal). The decision halts development pending appeal or resubmission, implying delayed copper supply. | A Reuters poll expects copper to keep falling as China’s growth slows and mine supply ramps up, shifting the market to a surplus. Aluminium price gains are expected to ease as the deficit shrinks. Nickel is also seen sliding as Indonesia’s supply increases and the market turns to a surplus in 2022. | Lara Exploration said it received new drill permits for additional step-out diamond drilling at the Planalto Copper Project in Brazil, funded by partner Capstone Mining. The drilling targets potential extensions to the Homestead discovery identified by earlier IP surveys. The release is exploration-focused with no production timeframe stated.

ASX-listed Soul Patts is seeking $180m–$200m to fund its copper spin-off, Round Oak Minerals, valuing the miner at up to $330m. The company is presenting deal terms to cornerstone investors and aiming to price the transaction this week, but no near-term production impact is described. | Newark plans to replace toxic lead pipes with copper. Such municipal replacement projects increase copper demand for water infrastructure, which can provide mild near-term support for copper prices. The provided text does not specify project size or timing, limiting confidence in the magnitude of price impact. | Shanghai aluminum hit a three-month low as thermal coal prices tumbled, easing concerns about rising production costs for the energy-intensive metal. The piece also notes sharp selloffs in Shanghai copper and nickel alongside broader LME/LME contract moves, attributing the shift largely to coal policy and energy-cost relief. | SMM reports intensified power rationing under China’s dual control policies in copper semi-processing hubs in Zhejiang, Shandong and parts of Jiangsu, cutting production via staggered shifts and causing order backlogs and delivery delays. Higher electricity and gas bills (plus tariff-related adjustments) are leading firms to raise processing fees. | The Biden administration, via the U.S. Forest Service, initiated a mineral withdrawal process that could lead to a 20-year ban on copper mining near Minnesota’s Boundary Waters. The policy move raises short-term supply concerns for copper and nickel. | The Biden administration moves toward banning copper mining near Minnesota’s Boundary Waters Canoe Area Wilderness. The Forest Service applied for a mineral withdrawal covering 350 square miles, starting an environmental study that could yield a 20-year ban. Twin Metals also seeks to mine copper and nickel, raising supply concerns. | SMM reports China’s coal-market controls and falling thermal coal/coking coal prices coincided with declines in SHFE copper and spot #1 copper cathode. Power rationing and higher electricity/processing costs cut operating rates for copper rods, wires/cables, and enamelled wire makers, weakening orders and cash flows despite a late-month demand pickup. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. reports progress on a 10,000m drilling program at its Kenbridge Ni-Cu-Co project in Ontario, including TDEM targeting and preliminary results with notable nickel and copper intersections in one hole. The update focuses on expanding exploration targets and outlines historical high-copper intercepts, without any production or market supply impact.

Libero Copper & Gold CEO Ian Harris says the company has restarted exploration at its Mocoa porphyry copper-molybdenum project in Colombia, including metallurgical and geotech test work, and has begun a diamond drill program at its Big Red copper–gold project in British Columbia. Results indicate mineralization and new copper zones. | Teck (Tektronix Resources) said construction of Quebrada Blanca Phase 2 in Chile is two-thirds complete but expected costs to rise about 5% above an initial $5.3 billion due to offshore port and tailings work challenges. Nickel production fell 13% to 71,100 tonnes due to maintenance at Murrin Murrin and operating issues at Koniambo. | SMM reported China’s copper inventories across major markets rose 3,900 mt to 102,500 mt from Oct 25-29. Stock built in Shanghai and Guangdong, attributed to slightly higher customs clearance for imports and weak month-end consumption. Tianjin fell due to low spot premium, with expected supply decline but consumption improving next week. | SMM reports that maintenance on a natural gas pipeline in Shangrao caused gas supply to be suspended for one day. Normal supply should resume shortly. | Codelco’s copper sales reportedly increased as higher prices offset lower output. The headline suggests producers are receiving stronger pricing even with reduced production volumes, which can support near-term market sentiment for copper by signaling pricing power and sales resilience.

The US and EU agreed to replace Section 232 tariffs on EU steel and aluminum with a tariff-rate quota system, allowing historically based volumes to enter duty-free. The EU will suspend retaliatory tariffs and both sides will pause related WTO disputes, aiming to ease costs and shortages for downstream users. | Codelco’s quarterly copper production fell 7.6% due to labor disruptions, including a strike at the Andina mine, amid delayed post-pandemic maintenance. The drop comes as global copper remains tight with low inventories. The company expects a rebound this quarter, but near-term supply looks constrained. | Demonstrators in Peru set up roadblocks that led Antamina’s copper-zinc operations to be suspended. The company said it was unsafe for employees to continue working during the protest and called for the government to restore order and resume dialogue. Output loss from the mine is expected to tighten copper supply. | SMM’s overseas copper roundup cites Teck’s Quebrada Blanca Phase 2 expansion in Chile completed two-thirds but expected cost 5% higher, with start of production pushed to 2H 2022. It also notes Colombia’s regulator shelved AngloGold’s Quebradona copper-gold license application. Separately, South Africa/Sibanye discusses acquiring Brazilian nickel-copper assets. | Antamina’s operations were suspended after demonstrators set up roadblocks over alleged unmet community support commitments. The mine, Peru’s largest copper producer and partly owned by Glencore and BHP, says it cannot operate safely while protests continue, calling for order and dialogue. Peru copper supply risk should pressure near-term prices. | SMM says imported copper concentrate treatment charges edged lower to $62.84/mt. Weaker second-half trading, COVID-related effects at north China ports (notably Alashankou), and ongoing concentrate transportation disruptions hit northwest smelters. With some increased seaborne concentrate purchases and December stockpiling, spot TCs remain pressured. | London copper rebounded after two weeks of losses, driven by tight LME refined inventories and worsening expectations for mined supply. The update primarily reflects company performance, not a clear near-term shift in copper supply/demand. | The U.S. and EU agreed to dial back Section 232 tariffs using tariff-rate quotas tied to emissions cuts, aiming to reduce steel and aluminum oversupply distortions. SMM reports Codelco plans a 2022 benchmark premium for long-term China copper cathode contracts at $105/mt (up from $88/mt). However, import demand is sluggish, Yangshan premiums have fallen, and traders show low acceptance. Current warrant/bill premiums near ~$63/mt lag the benchmark, suggesting bearish copper sentiment. | Peru’s Antamina copper mine suspended operations after a rural community strike blocked the road used to transport output via a pipeline, with protests escalating into violence at other sites. Antamina said it cannot operate without restored order, risking a short-term copper supply cut. | Murchison Minerals begins an inaugural helicopter-supported drill program at Quebec’s HPM project, targeting the PYC nickel-copper-cobalt prospect. The company aims to test subsurface extensions of earlier surface mineralization (reported Ni and Cu grades via NiEq/CuEq). Results are pending, with no immediate production or supply impact described. | Peru’s Antamina copper-zinc mine has suspended operations amid protests. The shutdown reduces near-term copper supply availability from the mine, which can tighten balances and raise expectations for higher copper prices in the coming days as markets react to potential delays in output. | Bullet Exploration reports completion of phase-one fieldwork on its Copper Canyon copper property, including prospecting, mapping, and geochemical sampling, plus magnetics surveying. It also completed assessment work to keep its Anita property in good standing. Results are described as encouraging, but no production or major supply change is indicated. | The U.S. and EU agreed to ease trade tensions: the U.S. will lift tariffs on some EU steel and aluminum imports starting Dec. 1 via a duty-free quota, while the EU removes retaliatory tariffs on U.S. goods. The partial tariff relief should support aluminum supply and prices near term.

Copper prices edged up as low LME exchange inventories provided support after steep recent declines. LME three-month copper rose 0.8% and Shanghai’s December contract climbed 0.6% after two weeks of declines. The planned lifting of Peru’s Antamina blockade further supported sentiment, alongside generally firmer aluminium prices. | Glencore’s Q3 production report cited lower mining grades across business units. Self-owned copper output fell 4% year-on-year to 895,500 tonnes, with weaker copper by-products. Nickel from owned sources declined 13% to 71,100 tonnes due to maintenance and operational problems, while other asset notes were largely unrelated. | SMM reports copper inventories have fallen sharply, with LME cancelled warrants rising and cash-to-three-month contango surging, tightening apparent supply and previously lifting copper prices. However, coal price limits, macro fears, power rationing on downstream demand, and sluggish orders are expected to keep copper volatile and perform poorly in November. | Max Resource reports finding a 3.7-km high-grade copper-silver zone at its CONEJO project in northeastern Colombia. If confirmed, the discovery can improve expectations for future copper supply. Such positive exploration/resource news usually supports copper sentiment in the near term, though timing and output remain uncertain.

LME copper closed down $37/t to $9,458.5 after uncertainty ahead of the Fed meeting and worries about China’s slowdown. Traders expected potential scaling back of QE and focused on the dollar outlook. Copper also saw weak warehouse positions, while nickel stocks fell to multi-year lows. | BHP and Wyloo are negotiating BHP’s acquisition of Canada’s Noront, driven by the need to secure battery-metal supply. BHP has raised its offer and extended the bid period. The Noront Eagle Nest project contains high-grade nickel (and copper), with delays from government negotiations, keeping supply expectations tight. | Japan’s trade minister urged the U.S. to abolish extra tariffs on steel and aluminium imports from Japan in a meeting with the U.S. trade representative. Removing tariffs would likely ease aluminium supply constraints facing U.S. buyers, supporting prices in the near term, while steel is not directly tracked here. | The US and EU agreed to ease Section 232 tariffs on EU-origin steel and aluminum using tariff-rate quotas, with duty-free treatment for imports within quota levels and tariffs above. The deal should reduce trade friction and improve near-term aluminum supply expectations, while steel talks on overcapacity proceed separately. | London copper rose 1% after the U.S. Fed signaled no immediate interest-rate hike, supporting expectations for sustained economic recovery. The piece cites resilient demand and supply uncertainty. It also notes LME aluminium and nickel gaining modestly, reinforcing a broad supportive macro backdrop for industrial metals. | Aztec Minerals and Kootenay Silver approved a 22-hole, 5,000-meter RC drilling program at the Cervantes gold-copper project in Sonora, Mexico. The program targets extensions and depth of mineralization, including copper-bearing porphyry styles, but it provides no near-term supply change beyond exploration activity. | SMM highlights strong Q3 earnings at several Chinese copper producers (Zijin Mining, Tongling Nonferrous, Yunnan Copper, Jintian Copper, Jiangxi Copper), attributing results to higher copper prices. The firms cite increased production/sales and rising main product prices such as cathode copper, with improved profitability, reflecting prior copper strength rather than new catalysts. | Mirasol Resources introduces its 100%-owned Sobek copper project in Chile and completed a high-resolution helicopter aeromagnetic survey to guide exploration. The company cites the copper potential of the surrounding district, including nearby Filo del Sol CuEq intercepts, and plans ground exploration this season. | Brixton Metals updated progress on its Hog Heaven copper-gold-silver project in Montana, where Ivanhoe Electric is re-logging drill core, resampling samples, and running IP and gravity surveys. The release cites historic copper production but provides no new assay or development timelines that would materially change near-term supply expectations. | Fluor-led joint venture has been awarded a contract to install a grinding mill at Indonesia’s Grasberg copper and gold mining district. The equipment investment supports copper production capacity at the mine, though timing suggests only mild near-term upside for copper market expectations rather than an immediate supply shock. | The US and EU agreed to replace Section 232 tariffs with tariff-rate quotas for steel and aluminum from Jan 1, 2022. EU steel and aluminum products within quota enter duty-free; over-quota items face residual duties. This shifts near-term import costs and predictability for aluminum into the US market.

Peru copper production is reported to rise to almost 1.7Mt despite ongoing social conflicts. The output increase suggests weaker disruption risk and steadier near-term supply from Peru, which can pressure copper prices in the coming days as market expectations for availability improve. | The article highlights Granite Creek Copper’s Carmacks project in Yukon, citing a copper resource and ongoing drilling aimed at expanding and upgrading reserves. It claims the world is short of copper due to green-energy needs and suggests prices will rise, making the project more valuable. | Bam Bam Resources reported chip sampling results from the Majuba Hill Project in Nevada, with several samples returning above 0.2% copper and additional gold assays. The company is expanding and infilling known copper oxide mineralization via reverse circulation drilling. The release is exploratory and not a production update. | Antamina’s CEO in Peru held a town-hall after last week’s blockade forced the mine to suspend operations. Malaysia postponed implementation of planned metal scrap import standards to next year, leaving details unclear and potential scrap-flow impacts uncertain. Separately, Chinese copper scrap supply was tight as sellers held cargoes after price falls and COVID-related transport risks reduced scrap availability, forcing some Hebei copper-rod plants to pause production. | Nobel Resources plans to commence drilling at its newly acquired Salvadora copper project in Chile after receiving a drill permit around Nov. 15. It will continue testing its Algarrobo copper target despite earlier mechanical and logistics issues, including establishing water infrastructure. The updates signal exploration progress, not near-term production. | Kintavar Exploration began drilling on its Wabash copper project in Quebec, targeting multiple mineralized zones along an 8 km corridor. The program of up to 3,000 meters aims to test mineralization at depth and lateral extent, with first results expected early 2022 based on prior high-grade copper assays. | Goldplay Mining began an approximately 1,000m underground drilling campaign at the historical Aparis copper mine in Portugal. The project targets expanding and defining a copper-rich shear zone (>1% Cu) with existing underground development and flotation mill on site. It signals potential future copper development but no near-term production changes. | Goldplay Mining began drilling at the historical Aparis underground copper mine in Portugal. The release cites extensive existing development, prior high-grade copper sampling, and plans to expand delineate a copper (>1% Cu) shear-zone target. It discusses exploration potential, but does not indicate near-term production impacts. | Copper rose about 1.2% as China’s export growth beat forecasts, LME-registered copper inventories fell sharply, and canceled warrants suggested further stock declines. Sentiment was reinforced by passage of a $1 trillion U.S. infrastructure bill, though traders remain cautious about potential Chinese demand weakness.

The piece describes community concerns around Freeport-McMoRan’s proposed expansion of New Mexico’s Tyrone-area copper mining, while stressing that clean energy and EV/renewables growth are driving sharply higher copper demand. It argues copper is essential for electricity grids and electrification, supporting a bullish copper price narrative despite environmental risks. | A ResearchAndMarkets report on copper pipes and tubes cites that demand fell in 2020 as non-critical construction, residential building, manufacturing output, and automotive demand were disrupted by COVID-19. It projects recovery and growth later via HVAC, plumbing, and heat exchange uses, but the pandemic shock is demand-bearish. | World Copper Ltd. announced an upcoming management conference call and an interim court order supporting a planned arrangement with Cardero Resource Corp. Shareholders will vote on the deal at a scheduled meeting, with completion targeted for about December 20, 2021. The company is focused on copper projects in Chile and Arizona. | Fluor’s JV with Petrosea will install a new third semi-autogenous grinding (SAG) mill at PT Freeport Indonesia’s Grasberg underground copper and gold district in Papua. The expansion aims to increase milling capacity as underground output ramps up post-2019 open-pit completion. Construction is scheduled to start later in 2021 and finish in 2023. | Minnesota’s DNR is accepting public comments as it reviews rules governing nonferrous (copper-nickel) mining restrictions near the BWCAW after a lawsuit sought to expand protections to the Rainy River Watershed. Comments are due Dec. 8. The process relates to the proposed Twin Metals mine near Ely. | Copper prices fell on fears that China’s deepening property debt crisis will reduce demand. The article links the turmoil to liquidity stress for developers and highlights China’s large share of global copper consumption. Energy shortages and higher electricity prices add to downside risk, though falling inventories limit losses.

LME three-month copper fell 0.88% as traders flagged uncertainty over Chinese demand. Concerns center on ongoing real-estate stress, China’s energy shortages and higher electricity prices in Europe, plus a risk-off warning from the Fed. Copper stocks fell sharply, but the demand outlook dominated. Aluminum warehouse receipts also dropped. | Tinka Resources announced new trench results from its Silvia NW copper-gold skarn target in Peru, including intervals with up to 2.7% copper (with gold co-mineralization) and an expanded outcrop strike footprint. The news is exploration-focused and does not indicate near-term production or supply disruption, so broader copper price impact is likely limited. | Commentary highlights tight copper supply, rising demand (China emissions goals and US decarbonization goals), and fears of shortages as drivers for higher copper prices. It also discusses feasibility studies, resource expansion, and ongoing drilling/exploration at several copper projects, reinforcing bullish sentiment. | Playfair reports completion of drilling on targets at its RKV Copper Project in Norway. The Storboren hole intersected multiple sulphide zones and samples were submitted for analysis. Results are supported by strong MMI Cu anomalies (up to 53,300 ppb) suggesting weathering copper sulphides, but no development impacts are stated. | Granite Creek Copper retained SGS to prepare an updated NI 43-101 resource estimate for its Carmacks copper-gold-silver deposit in Yukon. The company also notes Yukon’s Carmacks Bypass road/bridge contract to improve access for mining activities, with work expected to complete in 2024. The news is development-oriented, not production-related. | Ero Copper reported Q3 2021 copper output of 10,057 tonnes from its MCSA Mining Complex, with mill maintenance completed to support higher Q4 throughput. It also announced an optimized feasibility study for the Boa Esperança project, doubling expected life-of-mine copper production. Overall guidance is toward the high end of 2021 ranges. | The piece’s headline says that after “copper chaos,” the LME is mulling rules aimed at preventing metal shortages. While such measures could reduce short-term supply risk and support sentiment, the article text provided lacks details on the proposals, making the net price impact unclear.

Ero Copper reported Q3 2021 copper production of 10,057 tonnes from the MCSA complex and completion of mill maintenance to support higher throughput in Q4. It also announced an optimized feasibility study for the Boa Esperança project that doubles life-of-mine copper output, reinforcing growth-oriented copper supply expectations. | Ero Copper reported Q3 2021 results including 10,057 tonnes of copper production at its MCSA complex, completion of mill maintenance, and guidance to achieve the high end of 2021 copper-in-concentrate output. It also discussed capex inflation impacts and growth projects aimed at higher future throughput. | Copper Fox Metals’ CEO discusses the preliminary economic assessment for its Schaft Creek copper-molybdenum-gold-silver project and plans to collect metallurgical samples to cut processing costs. The interview highlights copper-price sensitivity of project economics, but provides no new supply or trade factors that would materially shift near-term copper prices. | Codelco said a landslide occurred on the eastern slope of its Ministro Hales open-pit copper mine in Chile, but it did not impact worker safety, equipment, or ongoing operations. The company attributed this to early warning and slope monitoring, with the area quarantined.
