It’s financing-focused, with only indirect near-term copper supply impact. | The Cabinet of Ministers introduced a temporary moratorium on licensing activities for geological exploration, prospecting, and development of gold (placer), silver, and copper subsoil use, with exceptions for technogenic formations. The policy may delay copper project development, increasing near-term supply uncertainty and weakening copper sentiment. | Trigon Metals reported geotechnical/assay drill results from Namibia’s Kombat copper project, finding multiple copper intersections, including 8m of 6.56% Cu (with a higher-grade subinterval). The company suggests the mineralization could expand pit tonnage, grade, and mine life, supporting bullish copper sentiment. | Peru’s finance minister said the government has not decided how much to raise taxes on miners, though it plans rate adjustments while seeking IMF input. The mining sector is a major tax source in the No. 2 copper-producing country. Industry groups warn higher taxes could hurt competitiveness and affect copper supply outlook. | Nevada Copper upsized a C$114 million overnight unit offering, citing strong demand, to fund ramp-up of its Pumpkin Hollow underground copper mine and open-pit exploration, plus debt repayment. The company plans to close around Nov. 29, with over-allotment possible. No immediate production change is specified.

London copper eased slightly as the dollar firmed on expectations of an earlier U.S. rate hike after strong inflation data, which can slow global recovery and raise dollar-priced metals. LME aluminium fell while nickel rose; losses were partly offset by tight exchange inventories. | SMM’s copper analyst discusses how China’s zero solid-waste import policy, stricter customs checks, and tightening scrap-metal standards in Malaysia/EU affect flows of recycled copper/brass scrap into China. Despite some flexibility for standards-met scrap, overseas supply is expected to stay tight, supporting copper scrap and copper market prices. | At SMM’s 2021 Nonferrous Metals Industry Chain conference, speakers said China faces a prominent copper resource shortage, with copper processing capacity exceeding smelting and smelting exceeding concentrate supply. It also noted RUSAL’s ambition to produce most aluminum with carbon-free energy by 2025 and growing secondary-aluminum demand, affecting aluminum supply expectations. | SMM reported China’s domestic bonded zone copper inventories fell 10,700 mt from Nov 5 to 216,200 mt by Nov 12, the fifth straight weekly decline. Shanghai bonded inventories dropped 8,700 mt and Guangdong fell 2,000 mt, attributed to low port arrivals and ongoing smelter exports. | Copper gained after U.S. inflation-related weakness in consumer confidence eased the dollar, supporting dollar-priced metals; tight LME/shanghai inventories also helped. China power curbs cut 7% of aluminium capacity, supporting aluminium. Nickel faces Indonesia export-pricing scrutiny, with nickel quoted lower on the day.

SMM reports copper plate/sheet and strip producers’ operating rates fell to 74.19% in October as power rationing tightened after September energy controls, expanding to more provinces. Small and mid-sized firms faced production hindrance amid high copper prices and maintenance. November operating rates are expected to rise to 75.51% with easing rationing intensity and slightly better downstream demand. | SMM reports copper concentrate supply shocks (roadblocks in Peru, Chilean port high tides), China power rationing causing smelter suspensions, and tighter inventories (falling SHFE stocks, rising LME cancelled warrants) alongside a weaker import window early in 2021. These shortages and squeeze dynamics lifted copper treatment charges and Chinese premiums, supporting bullish copper pricing. | Japan and the U.S. agreed to begin talks on additional U.S. tariffs on steel and aluminium imports from Japan. No specific measures were set in the meeting, but the U.S. said talks could lead to easing tariffs, a longstanding irritant. This may support aluminium prices in the near term. | Solus Advanced Materials will restart a previously owned copper foil plant site in Quebec, Canada, with 60,000 tons annual capacity. The company plans to convert it into a battery foil production line for North American customers from battery and auto industries, aiming for mass production by 2024. | A proposed 211-mile industrial access road to Alaska’s Ambler mining district is described as enabling extraction of over 43 million tons of copper (and zinc). The report emphasizes environmental and Native community opposition and notes the Biden administration has not interrupted approvals, but it doesn’t indicate imminent production start. | Copper prices fell as China’s property sector showed weakness, reducing expectations for metals demand. Coal prices dropped, easing smelter energy costs, but a stronger U.S. dollar lifted metal prices for non-dollar buyers. The LME warehouse squeeze eased. LME aluminium and nickel also fell on the same risk backdrop. | Cordoba Minerals provided updates on its San Matias project in Colombia, including expected completion of a pre-feasibility study and submission of a mining technical work plan permit application, alongside ongoing environmental impact assessment work. The company is advancing the Alacran copper-gold-silver deposit toward construction.

Copper prices rose 0.5% on the LME and edged up on the SHFE after improved risk sentiment following a Biden-Xi summit. The meeting supported wider market confidence, with the yuan strengthening. In the same snapshot, nickel was up while aluminium and zinc fell, showing mixed effects across metals. | Solus Advanced Materials reacquired its Quebec copper foil plant with capacity of 60,000 tons of copper foil for lithium-ion battery negative electrodes. The firm plans investments to expand the facility and target mass production in 2024, aiming to grow exposure to North America’s EV market. | Power Nickel started a ~4,000-metre drilling program at its Nisk project to expand a historic nickel-copper mineralization and produce a new NI 43-101 resource estimate. The company expects assay results in January and discusses potential nickel sulphate for EV batteries, but provides no new production or assay data. | Battery Mineral Resources reports encouraging San Andres drill results at Chile’s Punitaqui copper project, including multiple intercepts around ~0.7–1.4% copper and wider zones like 28.1m at 0.98% Cu. The company highlights progress toward restarting mine production mid-to-late 2022, potentially supporting copper supply expectations. | Battery Mineral Resources reported assay results from the San Andres drill program at its Punitaqui copper project in Chile, with several intercepts grading around ~0.5% to 1.4%+ copper over multiple meters. The company also cites progress toward a mid-to-late-2022 restart, supporting bullish copper sentiment. | Palladium One provided updates on its Tyko copper-nickel exploration project, including geophysical EM surveys, additional exploration permit applications, and expectations to resume drilling after permits are received. The release also cites recent high-grade Ni and Cu intercepts used in Ni-equivalent calculations, but offers no near-term production impact. | Palladium One provides updates on its Tyko copper-nickel project, including ongoing EM surveys, new exploration permit applications, and plans to schedule drilling after permits are received. It reiterates earlier drill intercept grades (Ni and Cu) and notes nickel-equivalent calculations, but no immediate production or supply impact is stated.

Vale has ramped down its Copper Cliff Smelter into a “hot” standby state for two weeks to optimize concentrate stock levels after an incident halted production at the Totten Mine. The move pauses ore smelting temporarily, with refinery operations continuing and no customer impact expected in the article. | Cyprium Metals announced an updated Mineral Resource estimate for its Nifty copper deposit, reporting higher contained copper, including a larger heap-leachable component. The company says the increase supports its Nifty Heap Leach Restart Study and future SX-EW cathode potential. The update is project-focused rather than supply-impacting immediately. | SPC Nickel announced staking of a large land package covering Nunavut’s Muskox Ni-Cu-PGM intrusion and acquisition of an extensive historical geoscience database. The release emphasizes future evaluation and potential for feeder-dyke mineralization, but it does not change near-term production, inventories, or trade flows for nickel or copper. | BHP’s executive said a decarbonised world will require much higher metal supply, projecting nearly double copper demand and about four times nickel demand over the next 30 years. The comments were presented as modelling results supporting the long-run investment case, potentially boosting short-term sentiment. | Kodiak Copper temporarily paused its 2021 drilling program at the MPD copper-gold porphyry project in British Columbia after an evacuation order for Merritt due to flooding and related road closures. The program is largely completed, with demobilized personnel and no damage risk, and drilling will resume after orders lift. | Mayor Alfredo Marañon III said Tambuli Mining Company has resumed diamond drilling in Sagay City, targeting gold and copper under an environment permit. Drilling restarted after a 2012-2017 period, with seven holes totaling about 4,400 meters under new management. No production or near-term output changes are mentioned.

Merger talks between IGO Ltd and nickel producer Western Areas showed no progress at annual meetings, with the deal now past three months. Despite the lack of signs of resolution, both companies’ shares rose modestly, implying limited near-term optimism related to nickel and some exposure to copper markets. | SMM says copper concentrate transport/commercial terms (TCs) have fallen to 10-year lows, signaling tightening concentrate supply since 2015. It expects contract terms and copper cathode premiums for 2022 to rebound (e.g., Codelco higher). Logistics recovery, limited refined supply, and stronger cathode demand support firmer copper. | SKC plans to invest about $761 million to build a copper foil plant in Poland as part of expanding its EV battery materials business. The project, tied to SK Nexilis production, is intended to grow corporate value substantially by 2025, implying higher future copper demand from battery supply chains. | Kamoa Copper awarded China Nerin a basic engineering contract for a planned 500,000 tpa direct-to-blister flash smelter at the Kamoa-Kakula complex. The facility will use Metso Outotec technology and renewable power, processing most site concentrate into blister copper and improving in-country beneficiation. | Ivanhoe Mines’ Kamoa Copper awarded China Nerin a basic engineering contract for a planned 500 ktpa direct-to-blister flash smelter at Kamoa-Kakula in DR Congo. The project targets on-site beneficiation and sulphuric-acid byproduct output, but timing is months away, implying limited near-term copper price impact. | SKC plans to invest 900 billion won in a battery copper foil factory in Poland. Producing copper foil for battery applications adds to projected industrial copper demand, which can be supportive for copper prices over the coming weeks. Details on start date and output ramp are not provided. | SKC will invest 900 billion won ($761m) to build a battery copper foil plant in Poland, targeting 50,000 tons of annual capacity by 2024. The copper foil is used as an anode current collector in lithium-ion cells for EVs, supporting expectations of stronger copper demand in Europe. | Usha Resources said it has resumed exploration planning for its Lost Basin gold-copper project in Arizona after receiving a notice to excavate trenches. The company reports anomalous gold and “Copper Blowout” chip samples above 1% Cu and plans subsequent core drilling to define copper mineralization later in 2022. | Kamoa Copper awarded China Nerin Engineering a basic engineering contract for a planned 500 ktpa direct-to-blister flash smelter at the Kamoa-Kakula complex in the DRC. The facility is designed to process most concentrate output, produce blister copper and sulphuric acid, and use clean hydropower and sulphur capture technology. | Ridgestone Mining says it has received permits from Mexico’s SEMARNAT to begin a maiden reverse-circulation drill program targeting the El Cobre copper structure at its Rebeico copper-gold project. The program follows surface sampling with reported copper grades up to 3.5%, supporting potential future copper resource definition. | Experts forecast higher copper prices, citing that an infrastructure bill should boost demand for copper used in power generation, transmission, and energy storage. The piece also references low copper inventory concerns and analysts’ expectations for LME prices returning toward/above $10,000 per tonne by year-end. | Experts forecast a copper price rise as demand is expected to jump from an infrastructure bill. The piece cites copper’s role in energy transmission, generation and storage, and references low copper inventory concerns alongside optimistic banking targets and mining developments, reinforcing near-term bullish expectations for copper. | Fireweed Zinc reported step-out drill results at its Macmillan Pass (Yukon) Boundary Zone, including a copper-rich intersection grading 1.54% Cu over 2.0 m and 2.35% Zn over 203.05 m. The update highlights a new copper-zinc mineralization style but is unlikely to materially shift near-term metals prices beyond copper expectations. | Copper rebounded after hitting a month low, turning positive as buoyant U.S. data improved sentiment and raised hopes for stronger demand. The move followed risk-asset selling earlier, but thin inventories limited downside and supported the reversal, bringing prices back into gains on Thursday.

The report says market calm has returned to the LME after last month’s action to protect short holders. The LME cash premium for copper collapsed from an extreme US$1,103.50/t before the intervention to about US$15.50/t at Tuesday’s close, indicating normalization in the copper market. | SMM reports global copper cathode inventories have fallen 56.8% since Q2 2021 to 519,000 mt by Nov 12, alongside strong LME backwardation reflecting tight supply. It cautions that “resource exhaustion” is unlikely as smelter output should rebound after power rationing, with a potential inventory pivot in December. | Copperstone Resources’ Q3 2021 interim report says Sweden confirmed the Viscaria exploitation concession and the company is scaling up plans to reopen the mine in Kiruna. It highlights drilling positives, plans for higher production and concentrator capacity, and emphasizes copper demand/supply tightness. | SMM says domestic bonded copper inventories fell 9,700 mt to 206,500 mt by Nov 19, including declines in Shanghai and Guangdong zones. With the import window reopened and invoice restrictions boosting domestic spot premiums to a record 2,200 yuan/mt, importers transferred goods from bonded warehouses, tightening supply and supporting prices. | SMM reports Chinese copper inventories fell 4,800 mt to 79,500 mt by Nov 19, with declines in Guangdong and Jiangsu and first time below 80,000 mt this year. Late-week import arrivals slowed, though the import window may reopen next week. Overall inventory is expected to rise only marginally, supporting copper. | DW reports that Chile benefits because higher worldwide copper prices lift revenues from its mining sector. It notes Chile’s position as the world’s largest copper producer and highlights the financial gains to public coffers, including due to state ownership in major mining. | Korea’s SKC plans to invest $761 million to build its first European copper foil plant in Poland. Produced by its battery-focused subsidiary SK Nexilis, the facility will make copper foil for electric vehicle batteries, indicating expanded output and potentially stronger copper demand expectations. | SKC finalized plans with the Polish government to build a copper foil manufacturing facility in Stalowa Wola, targeting Europe’s EV battery supply chain. Copper foil is used as anode current collectors in lithium-ion cells. The KRW 900bn investment aims for 50,000 tons/year capacity, with construction starting next year and completion in 2024.

Cyprium Metals reports assay results from final diamond holes at Nanadie Well, highlighting extensive copper sulphide mineralisation with several high-grade intercepts and confirming the deposit remains open. The company also notes elevated nickel values associated with some copper intervals, but provides no near-term production or supply impact. | SMM data show Chinese copper cathode inventories fell 300 mt week-on-week to 76,500 mt, the lowest level this year. The drawdown occurred in Guangdong and Tianjin as copper was shipped to higher-premium East China (Shanghai, Jiangsu). With premiums normalizing and import invoicing easing, inventories may tick up slightly by Friday. | SMM reports spot copper cathode premiums rising above 2,000 yuan/mt as social inventory falls and customs tariffs are suspended. Downstream orders weaken under high premiums, while copper rod producers facing bearish shipments and higher finished-stock levels suspend or cut output. Persistent high premiums could further lower copper rod operating rates. | Nickel prices recently fell due to pessimism on real-estate demand, then rebounded as sentiment improved. Lower coal prices and weaker demand failed to drive sharp nickel losses. Discounts to NPI narrowed, nickel sulphate demand stayed steady, inventory is low, and customs tax-control issues may raise spot premiums. | Copper Fox Metals’ CEO discusses the Schaft Creek copper-molybdenum-gold-silver project and its preliminary economic assessment. The company highlights efforts to reduce processing costs and improve recoveries, and notes project economics are highly sensitive to copper price movements and exchange rates. No near-term supply shock or policy action is mentioned. | Mosaic Minerals reports drilling intersected a 70-meter anomalous nickel zone (Pike Nickel East) and nearly 80 meters of nickeliferous mineralization, extending a nickel corridor by about 3.4 km and prompting plans for further drilling. Copper is also mentioned on the property, but aluminium appears only in risk wording. | Copper prices rose following positive Xi-Biden virtual talks, with a weaker dollar and improved investor risk sentiment. The commentary frames the move as a potential rebound from the prior month’s decline and highlights copper producers likely to benefit. The piece also references a feasibility study using high base copper assumptions. | Norilsk Nickel’s board recommended an interim dividend for nine months of 2021 totaling RUB 232.84 bn, with EGM set for Dec. 27 and dividend record date Jan. 14. The company is a major producer of nickel and copper, but the announcement does not change metal supply or demand expectations. | Ridgestone Mining announced a maiden RC drill program (about 14 holes/2,700 m) at its El Cobre high-grade copper property in Sonora, Mexico. The company cites past and recent surface sampling with copper grades up to 3.5%, targeting down-dip extensions under historical workings. | Cyprium Metals reported extensive copper sulphide intersections across all diamond drill holes at Nanadie Well, with mineralisation open along strike and across strike. The results will support a mineral resource estimate for a scoping study and preliminary metallurgical work. Elevated nickel and palladium values were found with key copper intervals.

Universal Copper reports progress at its Poplar copper exploration project, including drilling, a mineral resource estimate update, and additional target work plus property acquisitions. It also reiterates that rising global demand for copper (EVs and electronics) should support higher copper prices, but no immediate supply or production change is announced. | Guangdong copper cathode inventories fell to 6,527 mt (below 10,000 mt), driving Guangdong spot premiums to multi-year highs on Nov 22. Elevated premiums led downstream rod plants to cut output: six halted production and operating rates dropped to 65.8%. Firms expect further rate declines this week if premiums stay high. | White Metal Resources says its partner Noronex is drilling the DorWit Cu-Ag project in Namibia with two rigs and reports initial anomalous copper from the first 10 RC holes at Otjiwaru, including an intercept of 7.0 m at 0.5% Cu. Further drilling is ongoing at other targets. | DRC’s Kamoa-Kakula mine signed contracts to build a 500,000 t/yr blister copper smelter to process concentrate from its concentrators into blister copper. The project also includes a 10-year processing deal to route early-phase concentrate to local smelting. Expansion plans target over 800,000 t/yr concentrate supply. | The article says the approved U.S. infrastructure bill will fund electrification, transit and EV charging stations, expected to increase demand for industrial metals. It links this to copper and mentions battery materials such as nickel. It also highlights Kutcho Copper’s feasibility study as supportive for future copper supply.

Morningstar cuts iron ore assumptions but lifts average copper price expectations, citing potential US infrastructure-driven demand growth. Credit Suisse estimates the infrastructure bill could add 50–250ktpa copper demand and see supply risks easing. Nickel pig iron in China is said to have softened after exchange nickel price declines. | Morningstar highlights US infrastructure stimulus as a potential demand tailwind for copper, estimating additional demand of 50–250ktpa. Separately, Credit Suisse notes nickel pig iron prices in China have softened after nickel declines on exchanges, with expectations of a surplus as production recovers—signaling mixed near-term pricing forces for both metals. | The U.S. and EU agreed to replace Section 232 tariffs on EU steel (25%) and aluminum (10%) with tariff-rate quotas effective Jan. 1, 2022. Eligible EU products enter the U.S. duty-free up to quota volumes; above-quota shipments face remaining tariffs. Quota administration and exclusions rules add some uncertainty. | Zijin Mining approved internally the construction of a 500,000 mt/year copper smelter at the Kamoa-Kakula Copper Project in the DRC. The investment totals about $769 million, with $699 million for construction, funded from the project’s cash flow and planned over three years, boosting expectations for future copper processing capacity. | Indonesia’s president reiterated plans to halt exports of commodity raw materials, including suspending bauxite exports in 2022 and copper in 2023. The article argues short-term bauxite supply impact is limited because imports from other regions and large domestic port stocks provide about three months of feed. The policy targets alumina output, supporting aluminium-linked sentiment; copper effects are more future-oriented. | Indonesia’s president reiterated possible export bans: tin may stop in 2024, bauxite in 2022, and copper in 2023. SMM argues bauxite restrictions should have little short-term effect because inventories at ports are large and supply can be covered by other regions, limiting immediate aluminium disruption; copper effects are still pending assessment. | Surge Copper Corp. announced assay results from a drill hole at its Ootsa Property, including 422 metres grading 0.52% CuEq and 200 metres at 0.60% CuEq, plus ongoing work toward an updated resource estimate. Investors awaited remaining drill results and the updated resource; the release is copper-focused. | Rockcliff Metals reported phase-one drill results at its Copperman property in Manitoba, highlighting near-surface, high-grade copper mineralization (e.g., several intervals above ~2% CuEq). The company cites encouraging widths and VMS-hosted copper-zinc potential, with an ongoing drill program planned. | Zijin and Ivanhoe approved a $769m direct-to-blister copper smelter adjacent to the Kamoa-Kakula concentrators in the DRC. The 500,000 tpa facility is designed to process most Kamoa-Kakula concentrates and reduce reliance on third-party smelters. Construction is expected to take about three years. | Indonesia plans to stop exports of raw materials after its nickel ore shipment ban, aiming to shift trade toward semi-finished and finished goods to boost downstream investment. The explicit nickel restriction is the immediate driver, while copper export shutdown is mentioned as a next-step possibility; aluminium is not addressed.

LME copper futures closed about 1.3% higher at an one-month high, supported by China’s plan to increase real estate credit lines and signs of tighter supply, with falling warehouse inventories and premiums for fast delivery. Nickel also rose more than 2%, with receipts falling sharply, indicating tighter availability. | SMM reports China spot premiums for 1# copper cathode have fallen about 85% to around 350 yuan/mt from a historical high of ~2,200 yuan/mt. SHFE backwardation weakened as well. Causes cited include slower domestic inventory drawdowns, easing of VAT invoice issues for imports, and reduced downstream processing amid weak terminal demand. | Rio Tinto’s CEO met Mongolia’s prime minister over unresolved issues threatening the expansion of Oyu Tolgoi, Mongolia’s major copper project. Delays, cost overruns, and investigations have led to potential contract renegotiations and possible restructuring. Uncertainty over timelines and financing may weigh on near-term copper supply expectations and sentiment. | Zijin approved a US$769 million investment for a smelter tied to its Kamoa-Kakula copper project in the DRC. The plant (blister copper) is intended to process more concentrate internally, reducing third-party smelter reliance. First phase output is underway, with construction expected to take about three years. | Indonesia plans to halt exports of raw materials as it moves toward downstream processing. After implementing a nickel ore shipment ban, President Widodo suggests bauxite export limits next year, copper exports later, and further restrictions thereafter. The policy aims to force exports of semi-finished/finished products. | Three Valley Copper closed a bought-deal equity offering to fund advancement and exploration of its Minera Tres Valles copper project in Chile, including plans for first caving operations in January 2022 and rising production in 2022. The release also cites supportive copper-price conditions and EV/infrastructure demand. | Puma Exploration reports progress on its transaction with Melius Metals to unlock value from Puma’s copper projects. Melius has advanced approvals, completed technical reports, funded additional activities, and started a 2,000-meter drilling program at the Chester copper deposit, with claims transferred to Puma. | Puma Exploration reported progress on its transaction with Melius Metals, granting Melius an option to acquire Puma’s copper projects. Melius completed technical reports, closed financings, and started a 2,000-meter drilling program at the Chester copper deposit, including additional payments and related corporate approvals for listing and financing.

Nickel and other industrial metals rose as optimism about China’s property sector returned. The headline implies improved demand expectations for construction-related materials, providing near-term support to prices. By extension, the move is likely to affect copper and aluminium alongside nickel. | SMM reports that Xinxiang, Henan issued a red heavy-air-pollution warning, prompting local secondary copper rod plants to suspend production from Nov 25 to Nov 29. The temporary shutdown aims to curb pollution peaks and likely tightens short-term copper rod supply expectations, providing upward support for copper prices. | Peruvian opposition lawmakers submitted a bill to impeach President Pedro Castillo. The article notes Castillo had promised higher taxes on mining and oil, which previously alarmed investors about stagnant copper production. The impeachment effort is framed as a potential boon for miners, easing perceived policy risk for Peru’s copper market. | Forum Energy Metals reports final drill assay results from Rio Tinto’s 2021 program at the Janice Lake copper/silver project in Saskatchewan, highlighting expanded high-grade copper at Rafuse and related targets, plus copper boulder discoveries. It also notes Rio Tinto will not fund 2022 exploration and a $100,000 payment is due to complete the earn-in. | TrendForce reports Indonesia intends to end exports of unprocessed nickel ores (and also mentions copper and tin), which could disrupt global raw-material supply chains for EV batteries. With rising nickel demand for NCM cathodes, potential tightening supports expectations for higher nickel prices within weeks, though copper effects are less direct. | South32 will buy a 45% stake in Chile’s Sierra Gorda copper mine for $1.55bn upfront, plus up to $500m contingent payments, after KGHM Polska failed to exercise pre-emptive rights. The project is expected to produce about 180kt of copper this year, but closure is planned for early 2022. | Chile’s election could determine the fate of a proposed royalty tax reform that would base Chile’s copper royalty on output rather than profits and could rise up to 75% when copper prices exceed $4/lb. Industry warns this may force mine closures and cause investment delays, pressuring copper supply expectations.

Indonesia is considering a ban on exporting copper concentrates from 2023 to boost domestic processing. Traders expect limited impact because China relies only slightly on Indonesian concentrate imports and new smelter capacity in Indonesia should absorb concentrates by 2024, keeping supply sufficient and reducing urgency for price moves. | Aitkin County’s Tamarack project in Minnesota, a planned high-grade nickel mine (with copper by-product potential), is entering the licensing process amid environmental opposition. The U.S. is investing to expand nickel refining capacity, and demand for nickel in stainless steel and EVs is rising, supporting bullish sentiment despite uncertain permitting timelines. | Reports say Indonesia is considering a ban on copper concentrate exports from 2023. Yet analysts expect limited effect on China because Indonesian concentrate is only a small fraction of China’s imports and supply should remain sufficient. Planned smelter capacity (Grasberg and others) would process most domestic concentrate after 2024. | SMM reports a second round of 2022 long-term copper concentrate contract negotiations among major Chinese smelters and miners, with parties largely agreeing on next-year supply/demand but differing on treatment charges (TCs). Lower proposed TCs and smelter reluctance to purchase—linked to Olympic Games environmental controls affecting Q1 operating rates—signal near-term weakness for copper demand. | SSR Mining reported positive exploration results at its Copper Hill project and increased its ownership stake to 70%. The news is copper-related via project prospects, but it is early-stage exploration rather than operating or production guidance, so any impact on near-term copper market supply expectations is likely limited. | Rio Tinto Exploration Canada will acquire a 51% majority stake in Forum Energy’s Janice Lake copper-silver project after funding additional exploration. The move follows prior earn-in conditions and drilling that expanded high-grade copper mineralisation. Forum will pay $100,000 cash to fulfil obligations, but no production change is indicated. | Battery Mineral Resources said drilling at its Punitaqui mine complex’s Dalmacia target returned encouraging assay results, including multiple high-grade copper intercepts. The company expects Punitaqui to resume copper concentrate production in mid-to-late 2022, implying improved prospects for future copper supply and supporting copper sentiment. | Norilsk Nickel’s Capital Markets Day highlights a large increase in capex, including upgrades to concentration and smelting capacity in Russia and capacity expansions for nickel refining and copper refining. The company also raised long-term production growth targets for nickel and copper, reinforcing expectations of future supply. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. announced that drilling at its Kenbridge nickel project reached the deepest hole to date and intersected 3.18% nickel over 1.05 metres, with 0.19% copper. It also updated progress at a nickel-copper-cobalt project in NW Ontario, supporting positive sentiment for nickel. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. announced drill results at its Kenbridge Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project in northwestern Ontario, including a 3.18% Ni intersection and a small associated copper grade (0.19% Cu) over 1.05 metres. The update signals exploration progress but does not mention production changes or market supply disruption. | Norilsk Nickel outlined an expanded 2021-2030 capex plan, raising investment by $6bn to $35bn with significant allocations to ESG and energy modernization. The company also raised strategic production growth targets for 2030+ including 25-35% higher nickel volumes and 25-35% copper growth, supporting demand-linked sentiment.

Golden Deeps reports diamond drilling at its Nosib project intersected thick copper-bearing mineralisation, including semi-massive bornite with chalcopyrite grading up to ~6% Cu on spot XRF, with overall intervals up to ~1% Cu from surface to ~46m. Results are pending lab assays. | Norilsk Nickel forecasts nickel production of 205,000-215,000 tons in 2022 and higher output for 2023-2024, while estimating a global nickel surplus of 60,000 tons in 2022. This points to increased near-term nickel supply. The firm also mentions copper output guidance and sizable capex plans. | Capstone Mining and Mantos Copper announced a definitive merger to form Capstone Copper, positioning the combined company as a premier copper producer. The release highlights large near-term growth ramp-ups at Chilean operations (including Mantoverde and Mantos Blancos) and provides output, reserves, and funding details that can influence short-term copper market expectations. | Capstone Mining and Mantos Copper agreed to combine, forming Capstone Copper, a larger copper producer focused on Chilean assets. The deal highlights near-term production ramp-ups and growth plans at Mantoverde and Mantos Blancos, aiming for higher annual output by 2024. The transaction is presented as transformational for copper supply expectations. | Copper prices fell 0.9% as Omicron uncertainties raised concerns about near-term demand and growth. China manufacturing data was stronger, and LME copper stocks are falling, limiting losses. Nickel also eased with low stocks and high cash premiums, while aluminium slipped slightly amid broader logistics and supply tightness worries. | Talon Metals reported new assay results from 11 drill holes at its Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project, including thick intersections with high nickel and copper grades outside the main resource area. The company expects the results to expand the size and extent of known resources, but it provides no immediate production or supply changes. | Talon Metals reported continued high-grade nickel and copper assay intercepts from new drill holes at its Tamarack Nickel Project in Minnesota, particularly in the CGO West area outside the main resource zone. Management says results may expand nickel-copper resources, but no immediate production or supply-impact data is provided. | Copper fell to a two-week low as Omicron uncertainty hurt demand and growth expectations and Powell’s comments suggested faster Fed tapering. LME copper stocks are declining, but further selling followed the U.S. open. The piece also highlights tight nickel inventories and easing aluminium prices amid the same macro backdrop.

Capstone Mining and Mantos Copper will merge to form Capstone Copper, an Americas-focused copper producer. The company plans to expand output by about 45% to 260,000 tonnes per year by 2024 via ongoing projects and stakes in mines in the US, Mexico, and Chile. Deal closure is targeted for Q1 2022. | Turquoise Hill approved a US$75 million transitional budget to keep Oyu Tolgoi’s underground expansion moving amid ongoing negotiations with Mongolia and Rio Tinto. The deal reduces delay and funding risk for the project’s copper output, which had faced cost overruns and possible contract disputes. | Altamira Gold Corp. started an initial drill program at its Santa Helena porphyry copper-gold project in Brazil. Surface quartz stockwork shows copper- and gold-bearing mineralization over at least 100m with IP chargeability/resistivity anomalies extending to depth. Drilling aims to test the porphyry-style copper-gold potential. | Copper rose about 1% on the LME as Omicron demand concerns eased and investors regained risk appetite. The piece cites low warehouse inventories as extra support. It also flags Chile royalty bill progress, nickel mine depletion, and aluminium’s tight nearby supply via 14-year-low stocks and a cash premium. | Pacific Ridge announced assay results from its first drill hole at the Kliyul copper-gold project, reporting 437m of 0.61% copper equivalent and including higher-grade intervals. Management highlights the best results from the project to date and discusses plans for expanded drilling, with no near-term production changes. | Capstone Mining will merge with Mantos Copper to form Capstone Copper, targeting copper expansion across operations in the US, Mexico and Chile. The combined firm is planned to raise production from 175,000t (2021 base) toward 260,000tpa by 2024, and potentially 380,000tpa after Santo Domingo. | SMM expects copper prices to remain volatile and weak in December. It cites low LME/SHFE inventories providing some support, but notes bearish demand risks from COVID lockdowns and sluggish real estate, while smelters plan year-end output catch-up, premiums normalized, and support weakened. | Adyton Resources announced significant copper intersections from its Feni Island drill hole ADK004, including 5.1% Cu over 6.4m and higher-grade sub-intervals. The results confirm a potential copper system at the Kabang corridor, but remain early exploration with no immediate supply change.

Copper prices slipped as Omicron fears and slowing China growth reduced demand outlooks. LME copper was down 0.3% on the day and around 4% on the week, despite strong year-to-date performance. China growth and property guidance stayed cautious, while aluminium inventories rose 16% and other base metals moved mixed. | The US Commerce Department is delaying removal of Trump-era tariffs on UK steel and aluminum, citing UK threats to trigger Article 16 of the Northern Ireland protocol. The 25% steel and 10% aluminum tariffs remain, while negotiations with the UK stall, adding uncertainty over future trade terms despite UK denial of protocol linkage. | Russia’s Nornickel increased its investment guidance by billions of dollars, citing construction cost inflation and expanded modernization for energy and production assets in the Arctic. It plans upgrades including new/expanded concentrators and smelter capacity, plus a new copper production facility in Monchegorsk, alongside Arctic logistics debottlenecking.

London copper eased as Asian markets turned risk-averse on renewed Sino-U.S. tensions. Reuters cited signs of a China copper inventory glut: ShFE stockpiles hit the highest since late October and the Chinese refined copper spot premium fell from a record. LME copper fell about 0.5% to $9,460/t. | Cochilco data cited by SMM show Codelco’s October copper output fell 9.9% YoY to 144,100 mt. BHP’s Escondida output dropped 15.2% to 84,700 mt, while Glencore/Anglo American’s Collahuasi rose 17.3% to 49,500 mt. | SMM data shows Guangdong copper cathode inventories at 5,900 mt on Dec 3, the lowest for the year and near historical lows. A smelter’s output is still recovering and import invoice issues limit arrivals, while downstream restocking resumed in December. Inventories likely stay low into late December, supporting copper prices. | SMM reported China’s domestic bonded copper inventories fell 5,700 mt to 183,500 mt by Dec 3, the eighth straight week of decline. Shanghai and Guangdong inventories also dropped. Warrant-related inflows were smaller and stronger customs quarantine plus slower port efficiency reduced incoming shipments, tightening near-term supply expectations. | HK-listed MMG said it will stop copper production from its Las Bambas mine in Peru by mid-December, citing unresolved demands from the Chumbivilcas community despite government meetings. The mine has faced prior shipment blockades, and concentrate stockpiles have risen, but output loss threatens near-term copper supply. | HSBC’s analyst argues copper demand is being driven by manufacturing, residential/construction, EVs, and renewable power, which require substantially more copper. On the supply side, Peru and Chile (large producers) face political/regulatory risks, keeping the market tight and potentially in deficit, supporting a bullish copper outlook.

Peru’s Ministry of Energy and Mines said copper production increased 10% in Jan–Oct 2021 versus the same period in 2020, reaching 1.89 million fine metric tons. Growth was attributed to higher output at Antamina and Cerro Verde, with Antamina hitting its highest 2021 level in October. | MMG will shut down copper production at the Las Bambas mine in Peru by mid-December because road closures prevent supplies reaching the site. Communities along the transport route demand more logistics contracts and compensation, while the company cannot reach agreement. Las Bambas is a major global copper source, implying near-term supply loss. | SMM reports SHFE copper trended lower as markets priced the South Africa COVID variant and digested Powell’s hawkish taper remarks, strengthening the U.S. dollar. November cathode output beat expectations with power rationing easing, but imports stayed weak. Inventories may pivot higher, and demand shows little support. | Southeast Copper in China produced over 46,000 mt of copper anode in November, an all-time high since commissioning. Cathode output from January–November reached 383,000 mt, ahead of plan, and the 400,000 mt target is expected to be met by mid-December. The faster supply build may pressure copper prices. | SMM says copper inventories across major Chinese markets rose 8,500 mt to 91,200 mt over the weekend, mainly in Shanghai and Guangdong. Growth was linked to concentrated imported-copper arrivals and reduced downstream activity amid high premiums. The report expects limited additional imports this week, but continued slight inventory increases as consumption stabilizes. | The piece forecasts near-term copper weakness: traditional demand is soft, real estate performance is poor, and smelting/refining capacity and mine supply expectations are rising. Although low inventories could make the downside “more tortuous,” the net setup is described as pullback pressure on copper prices, with strength expected later after 2023. | Three Valley Copper increased its ownership in Minera Tres Valles SpA to 95.1% by taking up shares after a bought-deal financing. The funding, about $9 million, supports operations of the Salamanca, Chile copper mining complex. Further dilution is expected if the minority shareholder doesn’t participate in future issuances. | Copper Mountain Mining said heavy rains, flooding, and mudslides in British Columbia have caused no material impact to its mine operations, production, or concentrate shipments. Temporary road closures did not significantly affect trucking concentrate to Vancouver port. The company will continue monitoring the situation. | Peru’s copper output reached nearly 1.9Mt over January–October, highlighting ongoing supply from a major producing country. As a direct production statistic, it can influence short-term expectations for copper availability. The provided excerpt lacks context on whether production rose or fell versus earlier periods. | U.S. Gold Corp says its $4 million pre-feasibility study for the former Copper King open-pit project in Wyoming is encouraging enough to proceed to full feasibility. The company cites higher copper demand from wind, EVs and grid modernization and expects concentrate shipping after permitting.

China’s imports of unwrought copper and copper semis rose 24.34% to 510,402.3 mt in November versus the prior month, according to customs data cited by SMM. While November imports were down 9.07% year on year, the month-on-month increase suggests improved demand/support for copper prices. | Zambia’s Project Roan is virtually complete, with commissioning starting in December and first commercial production expected in March 2022. Jubilee Metals says the project will deliver copper concentrate to the upgraded Sable Refinery from Q1 2022, adding 10,000 t/y of copper units and planning more concentrating capacity in Northern Zambia. | Cornish Metals announced new drilling at the United Downs copper–tin project, intersecting multiple zones of high-grade copper mineralisation beneath the historic United Mines. The company says high-grade copper has now been confirmed over a 500m strike length and remains open along strike and at depth, with additional drilling planned. | SMM reports quiet trading in China’s copper import market as premiums ease and buyers stay cautious. The market is described as oversupplied, with spot activity low despite high LME backwardation. Bonded zone inventories kept falling, but arriving shipments declined amid delays from customs quarantine and low port efficiency. | SMM reports China’s copper cathode imports fell to 2.71 million mt in the first ten months of 2021 (down 30.03% YoY) with sluggish import premiums. SHFE/LME ratio issues limited inflows, while global shipping shortages stranded cargoes overseas, reducing expected total imports to 4.52 million mt (down 28.10%). | Azimut and Mont Royal report discovering a highly prospective nickel-copper ultramafic intrusion at Wapatik. The headline suggests potential future mineralization, but the report is exploratory and does not indicate immediate changes to mine output, inventories, or refining capacity. Near-term price impact on nickel and copper is therefore limited. | Battery Mineral Resources reported assay results from step-out and infill drilling at the Dalmacia target within the Punitaqui copper complex in Chile, including multiple intercepts above 2% copper. The company states Punitaqui is expected to resume copper concentrate production in mid-to-late 2022, supported by ongoing drilling and permitting progress. | Chile’s copper exports rose more than 35% in November to $4.92 billion, supported by all-time-high global copper prices. The strong results contributed to a trade surplus, amid expectations of post-pandemic demand recovery and comments from Codelco that prices may dip next year due to increased supply. | Dalmacia Drilling reports a step-out hole delivering 14 meters of mineralization grading 2.44% copper at Battery Mineral Resources’ Punitaqui mine. Such exploration results can support expectations for higher future copper resources and production potential, which may provide modest short-term bullish sentiment for copper. | Sanatana Resources reports strong grab-sample results from the Molloy Zone at its Oweegee copper-gold project in British Columbia, including up to ~1.09% copper and elevated gold and silver. Management says the zone may be a copper-gold porphyry window that will be drill-tested in 2022, supported by alteration mapping and soil anomalies.

Emmerson Resources reported strong RC drill results at its Hermitage Project in Australia, including 117m at 3.38% copper and additional copper-rich intervals. The company plans further drilling and notes assay results are still pending for some sections. The update supports positive near-term sentiment for copper exploration prospects. | Inner Mongolia’s new materials development plan (2021-2025) calls for expanding downstream capacity for high-performance aluminium (over 2 million tons by 2025) and high-performance copper products (e.g., copper poles, conductors, foil). The policy supports industry chain buildout that may boost supply expectations for both metals. | Inner Mongolia’s new materials development plan (2021-2025) calls for expanding downstream non-ferrous metal industries, targeting high-performance aluminum (including alloys, foil, rods, cables) and high-performance copper (copper poles, conductors, foil). Planned capacity additions and industrial-chain extension can lift near-term demand expectations for aluminium and copper prices. | Copper fell for a third day as Omicron concerns offset China’s stimulus effects. A stronger yuan reduced costs for buyers, but investors cut Comex longs and remained cautious. Tight Chinese stockpiles and strong imports were supportive, while rising LME availability and weak deliveries tempered gains. | SMM expects LME copper to trade rangebound at $9,350–9,680/mt and SHFE at 68,500–70,500 yuan/mt. Support comes from relatively low inventories and stable smelter/downstream operating rates, but downside risk from Omicron-driven caution, a firmer dollar, mixed US data, and year-end demand/fund outflows keeps prices from trending higher.

LME copper futures rose for a third day as China cut reserve requirements to boost liquidity, strengthening the renminbi and making dollar-denominated copper cheaper. The piece also cites stronger Chinese imports, rapidly falling inventories in China and a shift toward lower speculative longs, with supply still described as relatively tight. | Copper prices rose Wednesday on strong Chinese import demand, but the article highlights ICSG expectations of a large refined copper surplus in 2022. It attributes the surplus to higher refining production and increased secondary scrap output, arguing demand growth won’t absorb supply, which could weigh on copper sentiment going forward. | Rockridge completed a helicopter-borne VTEM EM and magnetic survey at its Knife Lake copper project in Saskatchewan and reports encouraging identification of conductors, while field analytical results are still pending. The update supports future drilling targeting for a Cu-Zn-Co VMS deposit, with no immediate production or supply change indicated. | Copper prices fell as Omicron-related concerns eased and the dollar firmed, adding pressure despite still-low LME inventories. LME on-warrant stocks rose to a one-month high, signaling easing nearby tightness. Nickel declined as well, while aluminium rose slightly, showing mixed pressure across industrial metals. | Fathom Nickel announced updated 2021 soil geochemistry at its Albert Lake property near the historic Rottenstone mine, identifying extensive Ni-Cu-Co anomalies consistent with magmatic nickel signatures and planning winter drilling. Nickel and copper exploration potential is increased, but no immediate production changes are stated, limiting near-term price impact. | Nevada Copper reported significant operational improvements at its Pumpkin Hollow underground copper mine, including higher equipment availability, improved contractor productivity, development rates up 50% vs August, and consistent milling with >90% recoveries and ~4,700 tpd processing. Commissioning and ventilation upgrades support ramp-up into H1 2022.

SMM discusses tight short-term recycled copper supply and worsening availability of copper scrap due to stricter customs/import controls, COVID disruptions, and energy ‘double control’ costs. It argues that recycled copper helps offset mineral supply gaps, but constrained supply and long clearance times keep scrap-driven copper pricing under upward pressure. | SMM reports that China’s secondary copper raw-material policy (zero solid-waste imports from 2021 and stricter standards) is tightening supply of copper scrap/feedstock. With saturated copper-rod capacity but insufficient scrap availability, competition for raw materials is expected to intensify, keeping copper-related spreads tight and supporting bullish near-term pricing. | SMM summarizes analysis that copper demand in emerging applications—EVs, charging piles, wind and solar—will rise quickly, lifting copper’s share of total demand. It cites supply tightness, including refined copper and concentrate shortages in 2021-2022, implying tighter near-term balances and supportive price conditions for copper. | SMM reports that booming EVs, charging piles, and renewable power (PV and wind) are set to lift copper demand in 2021-2023, with emerging uses breaking the prior supply-demand balance. Industrial Securities estimates show a sizable copper supply gap and cathode/concentrate shortages in 2021-2022. | SMM forecasts copper prices to decline, citing stronger US dollar/lower liquidity amid tightening expectations and weakening demand from construction, housing, and power grid cable volumes. It also notes export order declines and squeezed downstream margins. Despite a LME stock drawdown and contango/backwardation, the overall outlook remains bearish for copper. | The piece forecasts copper prices slipping moderately in 2022 after record highs, driven by macro headwinds like a stronger dollar and US rate tightening. It also cites supportive factors such as mine and refined supply growth, low visible inventories, and ongoing “green” demand, but expects overall moderation from 2021 levels.

Major copper miners and Chinese smelters are nearing agreement on 2022 treatment and refining charges (TC/RC). The stated gap is about $8/t, with smelters near $69/t and miners near $61/t. Analysts expect supply tightness to ease, potentially affecting copper market balance and profitability. | SMM says copper inventories across major Chinese markets rose 4,700 mt over the weekend to 94,800 mt, marking consecutive weeks of increases. Gains were concentrated in Shanghai and Guangdong due to more imported copper arrivals and shrinking demand, worsened by COVID-related logistics disruptions and higher premiums. Inventory is likely to keep building this week. | SMM says PBOC easing and US debt-ceiling progress may support copper, but market signals point to weaker end-year demand: SHFE backwardation shows pessimism, imports remain strong, and logistics risks persist. With spot premiums expected to weaken and sellers likely liquidate after deliveries, copper faces resistance near 70,000 yuan/mt. | China Color Corp. signed a $550 million purchasing contract for a copper smelter project in Oman/Indonesia with PT AMMANMINERAL INDUSTRI. The plan includes ~900,000 t/y copper-concentrate smelting and related port/water facilities, with China Color supplying equipment for Bennett South/Bennett Bay smelters. The project supports future copper supply processing. | South African rail operators report recurrent theft of copper cables, sometimes causing power failures and train delays or cancellations. Police and rail agencies say thieves steal and sell copper for drugs, while replacement and lost-revenue costs run into the hundreds of millions. This implies additional near-term copper demand for repairs. | Castillo Copper reported progress on exploration and drilling at its Arya copper prospect in Queensland and continued work on Zambia assets, including IP surveys targeting areas with copper anomalies. Laboratory assay delays extend some lithium option timelines, but the article contains no immediate changes to copper production or supply. | Aston Bay reports that ground EM surveys at its Storm Copper Project in Nunavut identified multiple new conductive anomalies and drill-ready shallow and deep copper targets ahead of a planned 2022 campaign. Results show conductors correlate with previously drilled high-grade copper, supporting exploration growth potential rather than near-term supply changes. | Copper Mountain Mining announced updated economics and formally approved advancing the Eva Copper Project in Queensland, citing improved NPV mainly from higher metal prices despite higher capex from inflation and COVID-related material/labour constraints. The company plans to advance detailed engineering, secure financing, and target commissioning in late 2024, supporting copper sentiment.

LME three-month copper fell 0.62% to $9,447.5/t after higher warehouse inventories and a stronger dollar prompted selling. LME registered and total stocks rose markedly in recent weeks, easing supply concerns. China’s call for earlier policy efforts helped limit the drop, but USD strength pressured demand. | Kodiak Copper reported assay results from three new drill holes at its MPD copper-gold porphyry project, including multiple long intervals with elevated copper grades and the Gate Zone target remaining open. The company also plans ~25,000 m of additional drilling in 2022 to expand appraisal and test new targets. | Royal Road Minerals reported initial drill results from its Guintár copper-gold project in Colombia, including porphyry-style altered intrusive rocks with mineralized stockwork and sheeted veinlets. The release cites intersections featuring copper (e.g., 0.62% Cu) alongside gold and silver, supporting bullish sentiment for copper. | Aztec Minerals and Kootenay Silver start a 22-hole, 5,000 m RC drilling program at the Cervantes gold-copper porphyry project in Sonora, targeting oxide and deeper sulfide mineralization. The work aims to expand and define gold-copper zones at multiple prospects, but no production or supply data are provided. | Camino Minerals announced discovery drilling at its Los Chapitos copper project in Peru, reporting anomalous copper up to 1.05% Cu and associated cobalt, plus oxidized and sulphide mineralization. The company expects drilling permits soon, but the news is exploratory and does not signal near-term production impacts for copper markets. | Aranjin Resources reported promising maiden drilling results from the Sharga Copper Project in Mongolia, confirming a substantial VMS mineralized system. Intercepts included up to 15.4m at 1.33% copper (and 0.94 g/t gold), with wider mineralization zones. Phase 2 drilling is planned. | The Market Herald publishes an interview highlighting QuestEx Gold & Copper’s induced polarization survey results at its KSP property in British Columbia. The IP survey reportedly identified a new top-tier porphyry copper-gold exploration target. The news is exploratory and does not indicate immediate production, but it may slightly support copper sentiment.

Funds have reportedly closed their copper trading books earlier this year. Speculative positioning on LME and CME copper futures has dropped sharply since October’s high-volatility spike, when LME three-month copper briefly surged to about $10,452.50/tonne amid unusual time-spread turbulence. Reduced speculative exposure can pressure prices downward. | LME three-month aluminium fell 0.96% after LME warehouse inventories jumped by 67,750 tonnes, though declines were capped as demand stayed strong and China supply was expected to remain tight. The article also says copper dropped 0.38% with LME registered stocks rising for a fourth day. | SMM expects copper prices to remain rangebound in December due to improving global liquidity from China’s RRR cut, while Fed expectations remain a key focus. Offsetting factors include weaker fundamentals, lower concentrate TC sentiment over Peru’s Las Bambas risk, disruptions to smelter imports, and inventories likely turning up. | London copper was flat as investors awaited a key U.S. Fed decision on timing of tapering and potential earlier rate hikes. The dollar’s strength and possible liquidity tightening were a focus. LME aluminium eased, nickel was steady, and Shanghai contracts were slightly lower, indicating limited net near-term momentum. | Amarc Resources said Freeport-McMoRan will continue its earn-in into a second drilling season at the JOY copper-gold district in British Columbia, following $5.94 million invested in 2021. The update focuses on exploration, data integration, and next-season planning, with results to be released later.

Peru’s PM warned that a 26-day road blockade blocking MMG’s Las Bambas copper mine may lead to a full production suspension later this week. Negotiations with local residents in Chumbivilcas have stalled, with residents refusing to lift the blockade, raising near-term copper supply disruption concerns. | MMG said it will be unable to continue production at the Las Bambas copper mine in Peru after failing to agree with a community blocking the transport road used to move ore. The shutdown represents an immediate copper supply disruption, likely tightening near-term market availability and supporting prices. | Barksdale Resources announced completion of a 5,000m drilling program at the Cerro Verde zone in Sonora, Mexico, with all holes intersecting assay-confirmed copper mineralization above a 0.20% cut-off and reporting several high-grade near-surface intercepts. Metallurgical column tests suggest heap-leach recovery is promising. | Global Energy Metals signed an agreement to sell a 1% NSR on Norway’s Rana nickel-copper-cobalt project to Electric Royalties, while Metals One plans to acquire 90% and fund exploration. The deal is aimed at unlocking remaining Ni-Cu potential, but implies no immediate supply change. | Global Energy Metals signed a definitive agreement to sell a 1% net smelter royalty on Norway’s Rana Ni-Cu-Co project to Electric Royalties, with Metals One planning a funded exploration program and resource update timeline. The transaction is supportive for nickel supply expectations and, secondarily, copper via the mixed deposit grades. | Peru’s Las Bambas suspended copper operations after talks to end a month-long road blockade failed. The government considered a state of emergency “as a last resort.” Las Bambas—about 2% of global supply—will end processing, likely reducing near-term copper output and supporting copper prices. | TNR Gold announced a new investment loan and updated its royalty holdings. For Los Azules in Argentina, McEwen Copper is reopening access roads and preparing a large drilling program and pre-feasibility study work. The release is broadly positive for the copper project but does not imply immediate production. | Talon Metals updates its Tamarack nickel-copper project, reporting new high-grade drill intersections and outlining an aggressive 2022 exploration program to progress toward pre-feasibility and expand resource potential. It also establishes an ATM program to fund operations, citing strong US interest in domestic battery nickel supply.

SMM reports the inner metal market trading higher. Shanghai nickel rises over 2.7% and Shanghai aluminium over 2.7% (around 2%+). Copper also stays high and volatile, with spot prices up for two days, though downstream buying weakens after replenishment. Overall sentiment remains supported by a weaker USD. | SMM reports a broad metal rally in China as the dollar moved higher then the market stayed bid. Shanghai nickel rose about 2.75% and Shanghai aluminium about 2.7% on supportive spot dynamics. Copper remained high and volatile for two straight days, despite weaker downstream replenishment. | MMG’s Las Bambas copper mine in Peru will shut down after talks with the Chumbivilcas community failed. The decision follows negotiations breaking down, prompting a Credit Suisse downgrade. MMG shares fell sharply, reflecting heightened risk of copper supply loss, which can tighten near-term availability and lift prices. | China will adjust tariffs effective Jan 1, 2022. Per SMM, the provisional export tariff for unrefined copper and copper anodes (electrolytic refining, HS 74020000) is being cancelled for 2022, but the cited tariff level changes from 15% in 2021 to 30% in 2022. Exports totalled 66 mt in 2020 and 122 mt Jan–Oct 2021. | SMM reports copper tube/pipe producers’ operating rates rose to 85.18% in November, signaling peak-season demand. Air-conditioner exports lifted planned output by 10% and orders by 29.3% year-on-year, helping clear previously delayed orders. December operating rates are forecast higher, supporting copper tube/pipe demand. | Peru reportedly faces social conflicts that disrupt operations and result in the loss of around 150,000 tonnes of copper production. The disruption would tighten near-term copper supply expectations from an important producing country, likely putting upward pressure on copper prices over coming weeks.

SMM notes early mixed trading: Shanghai aluminium rose ~1.6% on low inventories and tight supply, while nickel rose slightly as tight absolute inventories eased via limited imports, though fundamentals remain weak. Copper was pressured by a stronger dollar amid BoE/ECB tightening and Covid cases, keeping moves mixed overall. | SMM says copper inventories in major Chinese markets rose 900 mt to 86,700 mt over the weekend, with most gains in Shanghai. COVID-19 keeps East China consumption weak, while lifting a Ningbo lockdown released stranded scrap into the cathode market. Guangdong arrivals were limited, and SMM expects inventories to rise further this week. | SMM says copper prices edged up after the Fed signaled faster tapering but a less-hawkish tone, weakening the US dollar. China liquidity improved following an RRR cut. Copper also got support from falling China inventories and a Las Bambas disruption reducing concentrate supply. | Trigon Metals announced the first successful flotation of copper concentrate at the newly refurbished Kombat Mill in Namibia, with most refurbishment complete and commissioning underway. Long-lead processing equipment has arrived and installation is progressing, targeting first concentrate production ahead of year-end and restart of the Kombat Mine. | Lundin agreed to buy a copper-gold project in Argentina for $483 million. The deal is more about future development and supply prospects than immediate output changes, but it can still support copper sentiment by reinforcing investment interest in new sources of supply.

China’s copper ore and concentrate imports rose 22.3% month-on-month in November to 2.19 million tonnes, with year-on-year growth of 18.8%. Chile remained the largest source at 861,031 tonnes (+26.5% MoM), while Peru totaled 458,145 tonnes (-12.2% MoM). | Philex says it will launch the Silangan copper-gold mine in 2025 after shifting from open-pit to underground mining due to the 2017 ban. The project, a major revenue source after Padcal’s closure in 2024, aims for gradual ramp-up, targeting copper production from the first years. | Norilsk Nickel plans about $8.1B (600B rubles) to 2030 to upgrade energy infrastructure in Russia’s Krasnoyarsk north, including new power units, heat and hydro plant replacements, 1,000+ km of transmission lines, and gas pipeline works. As a top nickel producer, this could support supply continuity. | Kintavar announced it has completed drilling on its Wabash copper project, reporting confirmed mineralization at depth over more than 2.5 kilometers combined. The update improves the project’s potential for resource growth, which can provide mild bullish sentiment for copper expectations. | Deep-South Resources said Namibian counsel amended a case seeking a court order requiring the Minister of Mines and Energy to grant renewal of its Haib Copper EPL 3140 licence within 30 days. The company claims refusal lacked documented administrative evaluation. Management is also seeking other copper projects.

LME copper for three months rose about 0.9% to $9,534/t as oil and equities climbed, the dollar weakened, and investors turned slightly more optimistic amid Omicron concerns. Coverage highlights low copper warehouse receipts and a reported refined copper market shortage, suggesting limited near-term buffering against supply risks. | Nevada Copper reported continued positive operational performance at its Pumpkin Hollow underground mine. Development is running at the highest rate of 2021, nearly 50% higher than November, with additional bolters and ventilation fan infrastructure being delivered and commissioned. The company remains on track to mine the first high-grade stope in Q1 2022. | Wyloo Metals agreed to buy Canada’s Noront Resources after BHP exited its bid to acquire Noront. The deal targets Noront’s Eagle Nest nickel asset in the Ring of Fire. Both companies pursued the nickel supply for EV batteries, with the transaction likely supporting tighter near-term nickel supply expectations. | Ivanhoe Mines says its Kamoa-Kakula Phase 1 concentrator in the DRC has produced over 100,000 tonnes of copper in concentrate year-to-date, surpassing the top end of 2021 guidance. Phase 2 remains on track for a Q2 2022 start, targeting roughly doubling annual output. | Young environmentalists plan to push changes to Chile’s copper mining rules. If the proposed rewrite leads to tighter permitting, higher compliance requirements, or delays/constraints on production, near-term expectations for copper supply could weaken. That would likely be supportive for copper prices over the coming days to weeks. | Peru villagers have agreed to lift a copper mine blockade for a short truce, easing disruption risk at the operation. By improving prospects for near-term production and reducing supply loss expectations, the development is likely to support copper prices over the next several trading sessions.

SMM reports that under China’s carbon peak/neutrality push, photovoltaic (PV) installed capacity is expected to rebound in 2022, with global PV copper demand rising. It estimates copper consumption from PV at 564,000 mt in 2021 (189,000 mt China) and 1.23 million mt by 2026 (538,000 mt China). | Chinese smelters including major players settled the 2022 copper concentrate benchmark TC/RC with Freeport. Benchmark charges are set at $65/mt and 6.5¢/lb, up from 2021’s $59.5/mt and 5.95¢/lb. Antofagasta confirmed it will follow the benchmark from Dec 22. | SMM reports that China’s PV installed capacity outlook may decline for 2021 but is expected to rise sharply in 2022, with significant global PV growth thereafter. It estimates copper consumption for new PV projects at 564,000 mt in 2021 and 1.23 million mt by 2026, boosting copper cathode demand. | Newcrest agreed to increase its stake to 70% (with options to 75%) in the Havieron gold and copper project with Greatland Gold in Western Australia. The pre-feasibility work points to output including 6,900 tonnes of copper over nine years, but the news is mainly ownership restructuring. | SMM survey commentary argues copper prices face downside pressure as liquidity tightens with Fed tapering, while mine output is expected to rise and traditional demand (e.g., real estate, power investment) may decline. Scrap copper supply remains supported by import-policy frictions and low inventories, but net outlook stays weak. | Copper and alumina operations in northern China have been ordered to reduce output to curb emissions. Such curtailments are typically seen as near-term supply tightening for copper and for alumina feedstock into aluminium refining, supporting price expectations over the coming days and weeks. | The news says copper and alumina operations in northern China were ordered to reduce output to curb emissions. Such production curtailments can constrain near-term supply expectations. That typically adds upward risk to prices, but in the short run may also pressure metals if demand expectations weaken alongside compliance-related disruptions. | Mundoro Capital announced a two-year strategic alliance with Vale to generate copper (porphyry) exploration projects in Arizona and New Mexico, with Vale sole-funding generative work and earn-in options for designated projects. The deal centers on potential future resources, not current production changes.

Sandvik received an $18.5m order from Newcrest to supply drilling equipment and aftermarket tools for the Red Chris open-pit copper and gold mine in British Columbia. The rigs and service support aim to improve reliability and productivity, with delivery slated for Q4 2021. | SMM survey notes risk appetite improving and copper prices continuing to rebound. It describes weakening spot discounts as holders show higher willingness to exchange for cash, while Guangdong electrolytic copper inventory falls for four straight days. Scrap and import windows remain unfavorable, and LME/SHFE warehouse levels are discussed. | SMM reports a CITIC Securities note saying lithium battery copper foil production capacity is expected to jump sharply in 2023, shifting competition toward customer channels and supply-chain management. It highlights risks of oversupply in the copper foil sector and points to growth in high-performance electronic circuit foil.

China’s top copper smelters kept Q1 treatment and refining charges (TC/RCs) flat versus the previous quarter at $70/tonne and 7 cents/lb for concentrate processing. The floor is higher year-on-year, and relates to concentrate supply tightness; analysts expect easing supply in 2022. | SMM reports that China Smelters Purchase Team (CSPT) finalized Q1 guidance for spot copper concentrate procurement terms (TC/RC) at $70/mt ($0.07/lb). It also notes the weekly SMM Imported Copper Concentrate Index was $62.02/mt on Dec 24. The guidance directly affects smelter concentrate costs. | Copper rallied to a one-month high after the LME reopened following a two-day holiday break, rising 1.2% to about $9,679/t, with prices supported by COMEX-LME arbitrage. The piece also notes mixed price action in aluminium and nickel on LME and Shanghai, but no metal-specific drivers beyond trading conditions. | SMM reports CSPT set the Q1 2022 copper concentrate spot procurement processing fee at $70/ton (7.0 cents/lb). It reviews Q4 2021 fee movements, notes increased 2021 concentrate imports, and cites Las Bambas disruptions plus northern China port delays that affected concentrate flows and early-December processing fees.

Copper traded in a narrow range ahead of the New Year holiday, slightly lower on the LME and Shanghai. Supportive factors include falling inventories and expectations of expanding demand. Copper risk comes from uncertainty over Las Bambas’ restart. Aluminium rose on supply concerns and Alcoa’s temporary Spain shutdown; nickel also ticked up. | SMM reports Shanghai nonferrous metals closed mixed. Aluminium rose sharply as European aluminium output is cut due to surging electricity prices, with falling social inventories. Nickel also gained on tight supply and lower SHFE/LME inventories, despite future Philippine ore pickup. Copper edged down slightly amid a quiet spot market. | NorthWest Copper agreed to buy POSCO International’s remaining 31% stake in the Kwanika Copper Corporation JV for $8.5m in NorthWest shares, making Kwanika a wholly owned subsidiary. The deal ends existing JV/offtake rights, pending TSX Venture Exchange approval, but no immediate production changes are stated. | The report describes early-session mixed moves across metals: Shanghai aluminium up ~1.56% and Shanghai nickel up ~1.27%, while copper fluctuates around 70,000 yuan/ton. It attributes aluminium strength to European energy price shocks and Alcoa planning a two-year Spanish electrolytic shutdown, but overall trading remains range-bound.

Biden issued proclamations replacing some Section 232 tariffs with TRQs for EU steel and aluminum. Aluminum faces in-quota treatment only up to specified TRQ volumes (and certain exclusions may count against quota), while out-of-quota volumes remain subject to tariffs. The U.S. will monitor implementation through Dec. 2023. | China’s bonded copper inventories rose 15,600 mt to 187,300 mt (Dec 24–Dec 31), the first uptick after 11 consecutive declines. Shanghai and Guangdong bonded zones both increased. Subdued import trading and severe import losses reduced demand and shipments, shifting arrivals into bonded warehouses and stopping the drawdown.

Copper fell about 1% on the LME as the dollar strengthened and Treasury yields rose, with traders pricing an early U.S. Fed hike. Rising Omicron cases also weighed on sentiment. The same macro pressure pulled down LME aluminium and nickel, alongside other base metals. | Copper prices fell as a firmer U.S. dollar and uncertainty about Omicron’s impact weighed on industrial-metals sentiment. Three-month copper on the LME dropped 1%. The article also notes declines in LME aluminum and nickel on the day, reflecting broader macro-driven bearish pressure. | Ivanhoe Mines said it will release year-end 2021 production results and 2022 production and cost guidance for the Kamoa-Kakula copper complex in the DRC on January 10. London copper fell 0.4% from a six-week high as the U.S. dollar held firm on expectations for early Federal Reserve rate hikes, potentially reducing market liquidity. LME aluminium dipped 0.1% and nickel slipped 0.7% in tandem, reflecting broad macro pressure. Separate data points were also cited. | LME aluminum rose after traders cited supply concerns from long-term high European electricity prices and low inventories, with more metal expected to leave warehouses. Copper also increased alongside a weaker US dollar, which can spur demand for dollar-priced commodities. Energy/gas uncertainty in Europe remains a key risk factor. | Adventus Mining formed a joint venture with Salazar Resources to develop Ecuador’s Curipamba copper-gold project. LME aluminium surged to a two-month high amid high European electricity prices and sharply lower LME inventories, with large cancellations of warehouse receipts suggesting more metal could leave. Aluminium spot prices rose sharply, reinforcing tight European supply fears. LME copper meanwhile settled lower on the day, limiting net cross-metal bullishness. | LME aluminium jumped to a two-month high as high European electricity prices and sharply lower LME stocks/warehouse receipt cancellations raised expectations of severe shortages. Aluminium’s supply tightness is reinforced by soaring spot prices and estimates of European capacity shutdown. LME copper settled lower during the same recovery session. | Culpeo Minerals increased its stake to 66% in the Las Petacas copper project in Chile, after earlier reports of encouraging copper intercepts over 500m and a >6km mineralized trend. Drilling is continuing into 2022 and planned for additional copper-gold prospects, including Diego East and Far East. | Trigon Metals says it has produced the first copper concentrate at the Kombat Mine in Namibia after a 14-year shutdown due to flooding.
