The first hole will test for nickel and copper massive sulphides based on earlier high-grade intersections, with drilling serving exploration rather than production. | Bitterroot Resources and its JV partner plan to resume drilling at the LM nickel-copper project in Michigan, targeting the keel of a massive sulphide body with prior high-grade nickel and copper intercepts. The news is exploration-focused, with no near-term production or supply change implied. | ATEX announced contracts to mobilize drilling rigs and complete geophysical surveys at its Valeriano copper-gold project in Chile. A planned 6,000-metre program will follow historical copper intersections and aims to confirm the mineralized system. The release provides an inferred resource but no production or supply impact. | Bitterroot Resources and Below Exploration plan to resume drilling in January 2022 at the LM nickel-copper project in Michigan, targeting massive sulphide zones for new intersections. Prior holes reported high-grade nickel and copper results. The update is exploration-focused with no production or supply disruption details. | Bitterroot Resources and Below Exploration plan to resume drilling during the week of January 10, 2022 at the LM nickel-copper property in Michigan. Drilling targets massive sulphide zones for further testing. The release cites prior intercepts with high nickel and copper grades but no production changes. | BHP is cutting more jobs at a water-stressed copper mine in Chile. The move signals operational strain from insufficient water availability, raising expectations of reduced mine output and potentially tighter near-term copper supply conditions.

Zenith Minerals entered a trading halt after reporting broader, high-grade copper intercepts at its Develin Creek VMS project in Queensland, including multiple drill hits above ~1.7% to 2.5% copper. The company is also preparing details of a lithium alliance, but copper results are the immediate price-relevant takeaway for markets. | LME copper futures closed down after Federal Reserve minutes signaled interest rates could rise faster, weighing on risk appetite and liquidity. The stronger dollar reduced demand for dollar assets. Smelter operating rates eased after New Year, customs-cleared imports dropped, and demand firmed after recent price falls. Inventories are expected to keep declining next week. | China’s domestic bonded copper inventories rose 16,200 mt to 203,500 mt by Jan 7, with Shanghai up 13,000 mt and Guangdong up 3,200 mt. With the import window closed, losses were mainly 700 yuan/mt and outflows slowed, while overseas arrivals increased, lifting stocks and pressuring sentiment for copper. | Chile’s central bank reported that the value of copper exports rose 21.9% in December and 40.6% in 2021, supported by strong copper prices. Carnaby Resources announced strong drilling at its Nil Desperandum prospect, including 60.3m @ 0.9% copper and confirmation of down-plunge continuity, with further results pending. The plant uses scrap copper and supports EU electro-mobility and climate targets, signaling incremental copper demand for battery materials. | Indonesia’s President said the country will halt bauxite ore exports in 2022 and copper ore exports in 2023 to drive downstream investment and shift exports toward higher-value semi-finished and finished products. Indonesia is a major exporter, and the move is expected to affect near-term supply expectations for copper concentrates and aluminium feedstock. | SMM’s afternoon review notes Shanghai nickel rising about 2.56% and Shanghai aluminium gaining roughly 1.1% by midday, while copper also moved higher. For copper, Tianjin epidemic-related disruption left spot trading deserted, with electrolytic copper discounts narrowing. Overall moves appear intraday and mixed across the strip. | Indonesia’s President said the country will stop exporting bauxite ore from 2022 and copper ore in 2023 to accelerate downstream processing and exports of higher-value products. It already banned nickel ore exports from Jan 1, 2020. These actions tighten supply of key raw inputs for aluminum and copper markets.

BHP will invest about $100m in Tanzania’s Kabanga nickel project, including buying an 8.9% stake with potential to raise it further. The move reflects a shift back to nickel amid green-energy demand. Kabanga also targets copper production, but output is planned from 2025 onward, limiting near-term price impact. | Ivanhoe Mining raised/confirmed its 2022 output outlook for the Kamoa-Kakula copper mine, targeting 290,000–340,000 tonnes of copper concentrate, after exceeding prior production guidance. Copper was essentially flat, though LME copper inventory fell. | SMM’s daily review reports mixed metal moves in Asia: Shanghai nickel rose more than 3% and stainless steel also jumped, while Shanghai aluminium climbed nearly 2%. Copper edged down slightly (~-0.01%). The piece attributes some nickel support to low inventories and improved spot tightness. | Golden Lake Exploration acquired additional claims to expand its North Copperview property, citing historic soil copper anomalies and a newly identified geophysical resistivity anomaly near other copper-linked properties. Such results can strengthen expectations for the project’s resource potential and support broader copper sentiment, though it is not a confirmed production or supply-change event in the immediate term. | Chile’s central bank reported that copper export values rose 21.9% in December and 40.6% over the year, as copper prices hit record highs. Some analysts expect weaker prices later due to supply growth. | Battery Mineral Resources reports exploration and drilling results at Chile’s Punitaqui project, including Dalmatia mineralization with 1.77% copper overall and higher-grade zones, plus copper grades at San Andres. Teck said it received strike notice for Highland Valley Copper (HVC) Operations in B.C., affecting 1,048 workers. The union could begin strike action after a 72-hour notice period plus additional steps, with the earliest potential start around Jan. 16. Talks with mediators are scheduled for Jan. 14. | Teck has received a strike notice for its Highland Valley Copper operations. A potential strike can disrupt mine output and tighten near-term copper supply expectations. Markets often respond with downside pressure on copper prices as traders reassess short-term availability and shipment timing. | Teck said it received a strike notice from USW Local 7619 for 1,048 workers at its Highland Valley Copper operations in British Columbia. A 72-hour notice period applies, with mediators reporting before the Labour Relations Board, and strike action could start as early as Jan. 16 if talks fail. | Zijin Mining and Tibet Julong Copper completed and commissioned the first phase of China’s largest world-class copper project in Tibet. After reaching production, the project targets about 160,000 tons of annual copper output, supporting China’s copper self-sufficiency and expanding expected supply capacity. | The piece headline claims copper is facing shortages, quoting a Barrick CEO with a Saudi-related update. If shortages are real and near-term, they would raise expectations for tighter supply and likely support copper prices over coming days. The excerpt provides limited detail on the drivers and timing. | Nickel prices jumped to a 10-year peak as investors focused on dwindling LME/ShFE inventories, tight nearby supplies (cash premiums), and forecasts of a widening market deficit for EV batteries. The broader industrial metals complex also rose, with copper and aluminium moving higher as supply worries and China stimulus expectations improved sentiment. | Pedro Resources announced sulphide mineral indicator results from stream sediment surveys on its Voisey’s Bay West property and staked additional adjacent licenses to cover potential upstream sources. Such developments often lead to short-term selling pressure in copper futures as markets price a potential reduction in output and shipment schedules. | Teck says it has received strike notice from United Steelworkers for workers at the Highland Valley Copper mine in British Columbia. The union could begin job action after expiry of notice and mediation steps, with talks scheduled Friday and potential earliest strike Sunday. Highland Valley is Teck’s largest Canadian copper mine. | Teck said the United Steelworkers issued a strike notice for its Highland Valley Copper operations in British Columbia, covering 1,048 workers. The earliest job action could start Sunday, after a 72-hour notice expiry and mediation steps. Talks are set for Friday, while workers remain without a contract at another site. | Teck Resources says it received a strike notice from USW Local 7619 for 1,048 workers at its Highland Valley Copper operations in British Columbia. After a 72-hour notice and mediator steps, work stoppage could begin as early as Jan. 16, with further meetings scheduled Jan. 14. Negotiations are ongoing. | Teck Resources said a union at its Highland Valley Copper mine near Kamloops issued a strike notice. Job action could begin after a 72-hour notice window and mediation steps, with the earliest possible start Sunday. The mine is Canada’s largest open-pit copper operation, risking near-term copper output disruption.

Culpeo Minerals reported additional near-surface drilling assays at its Las Petacas copper-gold target in Chile, including multiple high-grade copper intercepts close to surface. The company says results expand confidence in a large copper-gold system and plans further drilling over a 1,000m strike length. No production impact is cited. | China’s stocks turned positive after a four-day losing streak, helped by upbeat global sentiment and a jump in crude oil prices. Resource and energy shares supported the index, including gains in Jiangxi Copper and Aluminum Corporation of China (Chalco), implying mild near-term support for copper and aluminium. | Nickel prices surged to their highest in a decade, driven by continued LME and Shanghai inventory declines, strong backwardation, and tightening warehouse stocks that signal a potential squeeze. Copper also rose to fresh peaks on renewed supply fears. Aluminium edged higher amid broader industrial metals strength and improving China sentiment. | SMM says copper prices should receive strong support from China’s expected monetary easing, improving overseas macro conditions, and unusually low domestic social inventories entering 2022. Smelter output is expected to stay near full capacity with easing anode supply, while bonded inventory build risks are mainly in the off-season. | Tinka Resources announced expanded high-grade copper-gold mineralization at its Silvia NW project in Peru, including new “Skarn Veins” with samples up to ~1.1% copper and 7.9 g/t gold and plans to prepare an Environmental Impact Declaration for drilling. It advances zinc-silver work at Ayawilca but provides no production. | SMM says copper prices rose slightly, with improving demand expectations and sharply lower inventories. Aluminium also rebounded, aided by falling inventories, downward domestic operating capacity, and reduced imports. It attributes supply disruption to European smelters cutting output from power shortages and notes profit recovery in electrolytic aluminium. | The SMM review says copper prices rose slightly as demand expectations improved and inventories fell, citing Zijin Mining. Overall, price action is supported by both demand and supply-side factors. | Awalé Resources announced follow-up soil and termitaria sampling at its Sceptre IOCG copper-gold target in Odienné, Côte d’Ivoire, defining new copper–gold anomalies and planning geophysics and drilling in 2022. The release highlights elevated Cu/Au footprints and compares the geology to major IOCG provinces, but provides no production or supply changes. | The piece highlights drilling results at Cortadera, describing “wide & shallow” copper-gold findings that lift the project’s open-pit potential. By improving confidence in copper resource economics and development prospects, the update is likely to influence short-term market sentiment toward higher expected copper supply. | Golden Dawn Minerals says its subsidiary is in the final stages of obtaining a $20,000 security bond for a drill permit at the Tam O’Shanter copper-gold property in Greenwood, BC. The permit would allow up to 10,350 metres of drilling in 2022, targeting Au-Cu anomalies and expanding resources.

Saudi Arabia plans two more copper mine license auctions after the Al-Khunayqiyah tender, aiming to broaden exploration and meet domestic needs. It also promotes bauxite-to-aluminum value-chain development for EV-related aluminum sheets. However, no production volumes or near-term output changes are specified. | SMM said copper inventories across major Chinese markets rose 200 mt from Jan 10 to 86,600 mt, a relatively rare increase. Shanghai climbed sharply, helped by smelter shipments from Tianjin and slightly higher imports. Guangdong and Jiangsu fell, but demand is expected to weaken after the holiday as downstream activity pauses, pushing inventories higher. | SMM’s midday review shows downside pressure across most non-ferrous markets. Shanghai aluminium fell about 2.18% at midday, while Shanghai copper dropped around 0.78%. Shanghai nickel ultimately recovered and closed up about 0.97%. The US dollar eased slightly, but demand/spot activity remained soft, weighing on prices. | China’s domestic bonded copper inventories rose 13,300 mt from Jan 7 to 216,900 mt as of Jan 14, extending gains for a third straight week. Shanghai and Guangdong zones increased, while closed import/export windows kept customs activity quiet. Falling Yangshan premiums and warehousing of newly arrived goods into bonded zones boosted totals. | Teck Resources said United Steelworkers Local 7619 issued a strike notice for workers at its Highland Valley Copper Operations in British Columbia. A Peruvian community near MMG’s Las Bambas signed a memorandum to avoid blockading the southern mining corridor used to haul copper concentrate. The deal commits to no planned protests and further local hiring/training discussions, lowering near-term copper supply disruption risk. | Firefighters responded to explosions at the Hussey Copper headquarters after a malfunctioning valve released about 4,000 pounds of molten copper. For copper, it notes inflation and financial indicators lifting prices, with low inventories and tight short-term supply/demand keeping prices firm. | SMM says China’s major copper inventories rose 5,900 mt over the weekend to 92,500 mt, after seven straight weeks of gains and with the pace accelerating. Shanghai and Tianjin fell slightly, but Guangdong and Jiangsu increased on arrivals from smelters and north China. This week, inventories may keep rising amid weakening demand. | China’s 2021 aluminium production rose 4.8% to a record 38.5 million tonnes, even with energy-consumption curbs at smelters. December output was slightly up month-on-month but down year-on-year. Aluminum closed slightly higher on supply concerns from potential European smelter suspensions, but China UHV line proposals failed to boost prices meaningfully, suggesting limited directional follow-through. | The article reports copper fell sharply, attributing the move to USD fluctuations, though it notes low global inventories and ongoing supply disruption from European outbreaks and energy problems. Aluminium closed slightly higher on concerns about possible European smelter pauses, while China UHV-line plans reportedly failed to meaningfully boost prices. | The piece forecasts 2022 copper demand by sector: China power/electroweb investment supports demand near historical averages, while real estate and air-conditioner/channel inventory pressures reduce copper use. Overseas demand (US/Europe/Japan) may add support. New-energy vehicles and renewables are expected to add additional copper demand, netting mixed signals for near-term copper prices. | China’s raw aluminum output rose 4.8% in 2021 to a record 38.5 million tons, despite energy-consumption limits. December primary aluminum production was near November but still high versus the prior year. The article also notes overall non-ferrous output, including copper and nickel, strengthening near-term supply expectations. | China’s official data shows 2021 primary aluminium output rose 4.8% to a record 38.5 million tonnes, even with factory power consumption curbs. December output edged down versus November, but remains high. The report also tracks other non-ferrous output (including copper and nickel) lower year-on-year, suggesting pressure from ample supply. | Teck said the United Steelworkers Local 7619 at its Highland Valley Copper (HVC) operations agreed to put the mediators’ recommendation to a ratification vote by no later than Jan. 24. The update suggests progress toward an agreement, lowering immediate risk of labor disruption and supporting copper supply expectations. | The piece references talks between Teck and a union at B.C.’s Highland Valley Copper over a mediator’s contract proposal. Talks between Teck Resources and the United Steelworkers at Highland Valley Copper in B.C. ended, with mediators issuing settlement recommendations. The union’s ratification vote for about 1,000 workers must be completed by Jan. 24, and members are told to keep reporting while contract details await voting. | Adventus Mining and Salazar secured US$235.5 million (including Wheaton streaming funding and Trafigura offtake financing) to construct the Curipamba copper project in Ecuador. The financing supports pre-construction and staged construction funding tied to feasibility-based concentrate offtake, strengthening expectations for future copper supply. | SMM attributes copper’s recent oscillation to macro policy expectations and weak fundamentals. With Fed tightening risk and accumulation pressures, it expects the high-price copper decline to continue. | SMM reports that Chinese bonded and social copper inventories rose alongside accumulating imported cargoes, while downstream buying was sidelined by higher SHFE prices. With the traditional low season approaching, copper is expected to face pressure, and spot premiums should have downside room this week. | Platinex completed the purchase of the W2 copper-nickel-PGE project from Springer Mineral Resources in Ontario’s Ring of Fire area, including potential smelter returns royalties. The deal targets known Cu-Ni-PGE mineralisation but does not indicate near-term output, so any impact on copper and nickel prices is likely limited. | Zijin Mining reported a major 2021 profit jump and said it expects 2022 copper output to rise sharply, supported by higher copper prices and contributions from several copper mines. The update reinforces near-term copper production and price optimism, though gold underperformance in 2021 is noted as a partial offset. | Rystad Energy projects a large copper supply deficit by 2030 as demand rises strongly from EVs and renewables. This is supportive for future production, but the article provides no near-term operational or trade changes, so immediate copper price effects appear limited. | Fathom Nickel announced it bought back a 2% net smelter return royalty tied to its Albert Lake nickel-copper property in Saskatchewan for $175,000 and started a 5,000m Q1-2022 drill program. The release highlights improved project economics and future assays, without any stated production or supply impact. | Southern Silver Exploration reports it has obtained all required approvals to begin an approximately 4,000-metre diamond drilling program at its Oro copper porphyry/skarn property in New Mexico, expected to start in early February. The update relates to potential future copper resource development rather than current production. | The Market Herald released interviews including Fabled Copper, which reported results from surface field work at its Muskwa Copper Project in Canada’s Muskwa area. More than 1,000 Teck Resources Highland Valley Copper miners in British Columbia are voting on mediator recommendations for a contract with low wage increases, with threats of strike or lockout if management rejects terms. Near-term copper output disruption is the key effect. | Northern Dynasty comments on the one-year anniversary of its USACE appeal submission for the Pebble Project and notes no decision yet. It also points to higher copper price forecasts from major banks, citing looming supply/demand imbalances and rising demand tied to the energy transition as drivers of near-term upside. | Gold and Mineral Co. says a $200 million mine in Saudi Arabia will be built after gold and copper were discovered. The firm targets production in 2025, pending mine design and environmental/community approvals. The CEO expects growing copper demand, but the timeline suggests limited short-term price impact. | Northern Dynasty’s 6-K discusses a year without progress in USACE appeal processing for the Pebble Project and highlights recent bank forecasts for significantly higher copper prices. It attributes the upside to anticipated copper supply/demand imbalance and increasing industry calls for higher copper production. | Barrick reported Q4 production results, including 126 million pounds of copper. Quarterly output figures can shift near-term expectations for copper supply and inventories, potentially affecting prices. However, without context on whether this level beat or missed forecasts, the immediate directional impact appears limited. | The piece spotlights Zambia’s mining reform prospects under President Hakainde Hichilema, with copper production having fallen in Q2 2021 due to Covid-19 and rainy-season impacts. Economists expect a moderate but sustained decline in copper prices, citing US rate normalization and risks of slower Chinese demand, though copper output urgency remains. | Imperial Metals agreed with the Province of British Columbia to surrender its Giant Copper mineral claims near Hope, BC, citing challenges in obtaining permits. The agreement effectively ends future exploration/development of this copper project in the area. | Comex Copper front-month for January delivery settled up 8.65 cents per pound, or 1.98%, at $4.4625, reversing a three-session losing streak. Antofagasta said Chile’s decades-long drought is cutting copper production, with 2022 output expected at 660,000–690,000 tonnes versus 2021’s 721,500 tonnes. Los Pelambres is heavily affected by rainfall declines, while higher costs and sulfuric acid increases add pressure, despite plans for a desalination plant. | Nickel prices surged to the highest since 2011 as a supply shortage cut LME and Shanghai warehouse stocks, pushing tom/next and cash premiums to multi-year highs. The piece also links higher power prices to reduced smelter output, tightening aluminium, while copper rises as well. | SMM says base metals rose overnight, with copper up ~1.5%, aluminium up ~1.1%, and nickel surging over 5% on tighter overseas inventories and supportive demand/infrastructure expectations. Copper gains were linked to stronger oil prices/IEA demand outlook, a weaker US dollar, and potential changes in premiums as market conditions evolve. | The U.S. and UK agreed to begin talks to resolve their tariff dispute on steel and aluminum, covering global excess capacity and the U.S. tariffs on imports from Britain. U.S. producers warn negotiations could increase import volumes and pressure prices. | Antofagasta says Chile’s worst drought in decades is already reducing its copper output and will likely keep 2022 production below 2021 levels. Copper output fell 1.7% in 2021, and 2022 guidance of 660,000–690,000 mt reflects water shortages and low-grade ore mix. | Alara Resources said its Omani unit secured a binding process-water agreement with Oman Water & Wastewater Services for its Wash-hi Majaza copper-gold project. This supports construction continuity. | Capstone Mining entered an 18-month access agreement with BHP to drill and conduct metallurgical tests on the Copper Cities copper project near Pinto Valley. The company plans a $6.7 million two-phase drill program aimed at validating historical shallow copper targets and selecting holes for testing, signaling continued copper development interest. | Imperial Metals agreed with the British Columbia government to surrender its Giant Copper mineral claims in the Skagit River “donut hole” and will receive C$24m. The company cites challenges securing permits for exploration and development. Authorities will consult First Nations on watershed protection. | USM Holding’s Alisher Usmanov says copper demand is likely to outpace supply as brownfield production nears peak, few new discoveries emerge, and low-carbon technologies consume more copper. He highlights longer discovery-to-production lead times and calls for exploration, productivity gains, and scrap recycling to expand supply.

Alderan Resources and Kennecott Exploration (Rio Tinto subsidiary) have identified three new drill targets at the Frisco copper project in Utah using drone magnetics. Field work will determine whether drilling proceeds. Earlier assays reported high-grade and porphyry-style copper mineralisation, including broad copper intersections. | Indonesia’s Ministry of Energy targets higher ferronickel production in 2022 (to 1.67 million tons), including increased nickel pig iron and nickel matte. It also forecasts a slight increase in electrolytic copper production. The news is about production targets, implying limited near-term price impact. | China’s bonded copper inventories rose for a second straight week, increasing by 7,700 mt to 224,500 mt as of Jan 21. The Shanghai bonded zone led the gain (+9,000 mt) as import trading weakened near CNY holidays and some smelters exported cathode into bonded warehouses. | SMM says Shanghai nonferrous metals closed mixed amid US inflation pressure. SHFE copper rose 0.64% as copper inventories fell on the week; aluminium fell 0.28% due to resumed capacity in Yunnan/Qinghai and weaker pre-CNY demand; nickel surged 4.21% on low inventories and backwardation.

BHP’s recent unification vote is expected to open the door to copper acquisitions, according to Macquarie analysts. The prospect of BHP pursuing copper miners adds bullish sentiment around copper supply/demand expectations, though the piece is speculative and does not confirm any deal or timing. | The piece argues that copper demand is rising rapidly due to EVs and renewables, stating supply is already short by several million tonnes, supporting higher prices. It discusses Pakistan’s Reko Diq delays from legal issues and negotiations to restart, highlighting ongoing supply uncertainty. | Solis Minerals said diamond drilling has begun at its Mostazal Copper Project in Chile after a funding raise. The program targets near-surface manto-style copper and deeper porphyry feeder structures, using historic high-grade intersections and new IP geophysical targets. No production or supply changes are announced. | The piece argues copper’s rally is underpinned by decarbonization and clean-energy buildout, which should sustain demand, and by supply constraints including energy-crisis disruptions and earlier LME inventory plunges. Analysts cited expect prices to remain in an uptrend through 2022 despite potential China slowdown.

Rio Tinto and Mongolia agreed to end their dispute over expanding the Oyu Tolgoi $6.9 billion copper-gold project. Mongolia will waive $2.4 billion in owed debt, and underground construction is set to start, with first production expected in 1H 2023, improving near-term copper sentiment. | Three Valley Copper says its Chilean Minera Tres Valles mine will temporarily suspend operations due to underperformance at the Don Gabriel open pit (lower ore and grades), and halt the ramp-up of a new block-caving project due to insufficient cash flows. The company retracts 2022–2023 guidance and seeks financing. | Three Valley Copper said it has suspended its mining operations and issued a strategic review update. The suspension implies reduced near-term copper output, likely tightening supply expectations. This would generally pressure copper prices downward over the following days as markets assess the duration and scale of reduced production. | Rio Tinto said it reached a new agreement with Mongolia to proceed with a $6.9 billion expansion of the Oyu Tolgoi copper mine. The deal enables the “undercut” work to start within days, removing a long-running obstacle and improving the near-term copper supply outlook. | Rio Tinto, Turquoise Hill and Mongolia agreed to restart underground operations at the Oyu Tolgoi copper project after years of dispute. Turquoise Hill will waive a $2.4 billion carry loan, amend funding terms, and production is expected to begin in days with first sustainable output in the first half of 2023. | SMM expects domestic policy to remain loose ahead of CNY, with PBoC rate cuts supporting ample liquidity. It forecasts copper prices to stay supported around 70,000 yuan/mt due to optimistic overseas manufacturing indicators and low inventories, though pre-holiday consumption weakness may widen discounts later. | SMM reports base metals fell overnight as investors took profits amid rising risk aversion, with a stronger U.S. dollar weighing on non-ferrous prices. SHFE copper fell 1.74% and nickel plunged 6.88%, pressured by high matte nickel output sentiment. Aluminium was near-flat, with weak/seasonal demand and rising inventories. | Shanghai nickel fell sharply as traders took profits after a blistering rally to record highs; March nickel hit an 8% daily limit lower on profit-taking ahead of the U.S. Fed meeting and China holidays. The move was reinforced by Shanghai exchange fee/margin changes, with nickel also falling at the LME. | Rio Tinto and Mongolia settled a dispute over the Oyu Tolgoi (OT) copper project. Mongolia’s government, which holds a 34% stake, agreed on a comprehensive package including debt waivers and power supply terms. The OT board approved underground operations and expects first production in the first half of next year. | Libero Copper announced it has signed an exploration drilling contract for the Mocoa porphyry copper-molybdenum project in Colombia. The decision relates to progressing exploration toward potential future copper production. It is unlikely to change current output quickly, but it can provide modest bullish sentiment for copper. | Hannan Metals reports it extended mineralization at its San Martin, Peru sediment-hosted copper-silver project by 3 km, with channel results including 0.4 m @ 10.8% copper. The company plans social and geophysical programs before a maiden drill program, boosting exploration sentiment for potential future copper supply. | BeMetals reported new 2021 core drilling results at its Pangeni copper exploration project in Zambia, including intersections up to 0.62% Cu over 4.14 metres and multiple meaningful copper zones at the D-Prospect. The company plans follow-up work, but the announcement is exploration-focused. | Nickel led a broad base-metals decline as investors turned cautious ahead of a key Fed meeting and amid rising Ukraine tensions. Nickel futures and spot fell after prior rallies, driven partly by exchange fee/margin adjustments and profit-taking. Copper and aluminium also declined in Shanghai, reflecting risk-off sentiment despite low nickel inventories. | Newcrest has withdrawn from an option and farm-in agreement tied to the Caña Brava/Tioloma gold-copper project in Ecuador. The change introduces uncertainty around project progression and future copper development, which can modestly support copper sentiment, though any immediate impact on prices is likely small. | Playfair plans additional 1,300 metres of shallow drilling in 26 holes to trace MMI copper anomalies at its RKV copper project in Norway. 2021 results highlighted strong copper anomalies and a sulphide-rich block with notable copper and nickel grades. Drilling is delayed to spring for logistics and terrain. | Teck is reported to be joining peers with a stake in a startup focused on extracting copper from waste rock. The initiative could increase future copper recovery, implying incremental supply potential. With no near-term production scale provided, the immediate market impact is likely modest but mildly bearish for copper prices. | Big Tree Copper seeks a JSE/Cape Town listing and plans to raise over R100m. It processes copper oxide ore from old tailings and waste rock at Nababeep. Management highlights a “copper supply crunch” and EV demand growth as support for high copper prices in the near term. | Mongolia and Rio Tinto have settled their dispute over underground expansion at the Oyu Tolgoi copper-gold project, allowing underground mining construction to start. The government expects the mine to produce about 430,000 tons of copper annually once fully operational, improving forward supply visibility for copper markets. | Comex copper for January delivery settled 0.91% higher at $4.4430, gaining 4.00 cents per pound and snapping a two-session losing streak. The settlement is noted as the seventh highest this year and remains down versus recent highs, with month-to-date and year-to-date declines cited. | Mongolia and Rio Tinto settled a dispute over the underground expansion of the Oyu Tolgoi copper-gold project, enabling underground construction to start. The government says the mine is expected to produce about 430,000 tons of copper annually once fully operational, supporting copper supply expectations. | Teck Resources reached a new five-year collective agreement with United Steelworkers at Highland Valley Copper after a brief contract impasse and a 72-hour strike notice. A vote of over 1,000 members approved the deal, avoiding a likely near-term work stoppage at Canada’s largest open-pit copper mine.

Environmental groups sued the U.S. Forest Service to overturn a 2017 land transfer enabling PolyMet to develop an open-pit copper (and gold) mine in Minnesota. They argue endangered species habitat would be destroyed and seek court review/resumption of land. Regulators are also being asked to reconsider PolyMet wastewater permits. | State media reports that Rio Tinto has begun underground work at a vast copper mine in Mongolia. The start of mine development can be read as progress toward future copper supply, potentially supporting sentiment around copper in the coming weeks, though detailed production timelines are not provided. | SMM’s daily review says metals rose overall, with Shanghai copper rebounding strongly after two losses (+1.30%) and Shanghai aluminium ending a losing streak (+1.87%). Shanghai nickel remained slightly down (-0.57%), though the decline narrowed. LME copper futures also rose in the afternoon. | SMM’s daily review says metals rose generally, with Shanghai copper rebounding strongly (+1.30%) after two losses and LME copper also firmer. Shanghai aluminium ended a losing streak (+1.87%) on supply and Europe-energy worries, while Shanghai nickel fell slightly (−0.57%) but the drop narrowed. | Three Valley Copper suspended operations at its Minera Tres Valles (MTV) copper mine near Salamanca, Chile, after sustained underperformance and lower-grade ore deliveries undermined mine economics. The company also paused exploration and some capex, demobilizing contractors and placing the Don Gabriel pit into care and maintenance. | BHP has started autonomous drilling at its Spence copper mine in northern Chile, using remote-operated Pit Viper 351 drills and planning to deploy five autonomous units. BHP says the program improves worker safety and is part of broader autonomy plans at Spence and nearby operations like Escondida. | Los Andes Copper reports financial results and a corporate update for its Vizcachitas copper project in Chile. The company emphasizes ongoing and planned drilling to support a pre-feasibility study, including expansion from three to five rigs and receipt of a drill permit. No production or market-moving copper supply changes are announced.

Aurania halted most work at Ecuador’s Lost Cities copper project because of Covid-19 concerns. The stoppage can delay project development and potentially reduce future copper supply expectations, which typically supports higher prices in the near term as markets weigh supply disruption risk. | LME three-month copper rose about 1.2% as investors expected the Fed to take a moderate approach to March rate hikes, supporting economic recovery and near-term metal demand. SHFE copper also gained. LME aluminum was slightly higher, lifted by Russia-Ukraine supply-risk concerns. | The Biden administration cancelled two mineral leases for Antofagasta’s Twin Metals proposed copper-nickel mine in Minnesota, halting the project. The Interior Department said renewals were improper under regulations. Twin Metals plans to challenge the decision. The move complements a longer Minnesota mining ban proposal, affecting expected North American supply. | Wolfden filed an NI-43-101 technical report for its Rice Island nickel project and reiterated a mineral resource estimate using a 0.5% NiEq cut-off. The release includes upcoming expansion drilling to test extensions of nickel mineralization and references copper grades within the NiEq calculation. | The piece argues copper inventories could be critically low, citing a projected multi-million-ton deficit by 2030 tied to EV-driven demand and growth. It also references supply constraints and disruptions: BHP production near lower guidance, potential concentrate sale delays at Teck, and logistics/export uncertainty for Rio Tinto. | The Philippines ended its 2017 open-pit mining ban, aiming to revive a mining sector hurt by the pandemic. GlobalData says the ban delayed projects, including the King-King mine. Now-allowed open-pit projects are forecast to add copper and nickel production, potentially improving near-to-medium-term supply expectations. | Kootenay Silver reports that its Aztec joint venture completed the first 14 RC drill holes of a planned 5,000 m Phase 2 program at the Cervantes gold-copper property in Sonora, Mexico. Drilling targets oxide gold and deeper copper-gold porphyry sulfides; assay results are pending. | Chile’s state mining agency Cochilco forecasts the start of a copper price downward cycle. The guidance implies weakening price momentum ahead, influencing market expectations for copper demand and pricing over the coming weeks. This creates near-term bearish sentiment for copper, even though details are not provided in the excerpt.

World Copper and Cardero updated their plan of arrangement, with Cardero’s shares halted and expected delisting as the companies combine. The exchange ratio would give Cardero holders 40% of World Copper post-closing. The update concerns transaction timing and approvals, not changes to copper supply or demand. | Anglo American said Q4 overall output was flat, with a 4% decline in copper production offset by stronger diamonds. It expects higher copper output in 2022, citing progress at its Quellaveco Peru mine, targeting 680,000–760,000 tonnes for the year and noting risks from COVID disruptions and Chile water shortages. | Anglo American lifted its 2022 copper production outlook to 680,000–760,000 tonnes as Peru’s Quellaveco moves toward concentrate production mid-year, while Chile’s Collahuasi benefits from strong plant performance. The firm still faces drought-related water constraints in Chile, but guidance implies additional supply without clearly shifting the near-term balance. | SMM data shows China’s domestic bonded-zone copper inventories rose 18,600 mt to 243,100 mt over Jan 21–Jan 28, with gains in Shanghai and Guangdong. Imports slowed ahead of CNY holidays due to high losses and slower warehouse transport, while shipment arrivals and domestic smelter exports boosted inflows. | Conservation groups filed suit to overturn U.S. agencies’ approvals for the PolyMet open-pit copper mine in Minnesota, arguing violations of the Endangered Species Act tied to habitat destruction. Challenges to related permits could delay the mine’s progress, potentially tightening near-term copper supply expectations. | A Reuters poll expects copper to soften in 2022 as rising interest rates weaken demand and more mine output—mainly from South America—adds supply. Aluminium remains supported by past power curtailments, but tighter conditions are seen as already priced in. Nickel is forecast lower as inventories rise and Indonesia supply increases. | The Biden administration canceled two Twin Metals Minnesota leases near the Boundary Waters, where the project sought to mine sulfide ore for copper and nickel. The rollback follows earlier political blocks and years of contention over environmental harm. Twin Metals vowed to challenge the decision, but cancellation reduces near-term mine prospects. | The article discusses a rebound in copper concentrate treatment and refining charges (TC/RCs) during a holiday lull, suggesting easing concentrate tightness. It also references Fastmarkets publication and correction updates for several metals assessments, but the only direct price-relationship highlighted is for copper smelting terms via TC/RC movements.

Callinex reported additional drilling results expanding the Rainbow VMS copper-gold-zinc deposit at its Pine Bay Project, including deeper intersections and a newly defined Red Zone with high copper-equivalent grades. Assays support potential continuation at depth, but there is no near-term production or supply change, limiting immediate price impact. | Tucson is challenging the Rosemont copper mine amid a dispute over a water storage project. Such local opposition can delay approvals, permitting, or related site works, raising expectations of reduced or postponed copper supply, which can support near-term copper prices. | Sohar Port invites investors to develop downstream metals processing, highlighting copper concentration from Oman’s mined ore (with copper ore exports curtailed to support in-country value) and aluminium downstream uses from Sohar Aluminium’s smelter feedstock. The piece is promotional and doesn’t specify near-term output increases affecting prices. | Sego Resources reports additional drill results expanding the Southern Gold Zone at the Miner Mountain Cu-Au project near Princeton, BC. Intervals include disseminated chalcopyrite and one reported copper grade of about 0.11%, with proposed 2022 holes to extend the mineralized zone and test deeper porphyry potential. | Copperstone Resources announces a directed share issue of about SEK 130 million and intends to use the net proceeds to accelerate investment for the re-opening process of the Viscaria mine in Kiruna. The release emphasizes Viscaria’s high copper grade, implying improved near-term copper supply outlook.

Chile’s national statistics agency reported copper production fell 1.9% in 2021 to 5.68 million tonnes, with December output down 0.6% to 503,605 tonnes. Chile is the world’s largest copper producer. The data points to weaker supply conditions versus the prior year. | Comex copper futures for February delivery ended the month at $4.3180, down 3.08% (13.70 cents/pound), the largest one-month decline since Sept. 2021. Copper has been down in four of the past six months and remains below key highs despite a small uptick on the day. | Norilsk Nickel’s Global Palladium Fund launched a physically-backed EV metals ETC. The tracked index weights include Copper (40%) and Nickel (18.65%) and stores metal in LME/LPPM warehouses. The news is primarily an investment product launch, without clear immediate supply-demand impact, and Aluminium is not referenced. | Aguila Copper completed a deal to acquire a 90% interest in Manitoba copper-zinc-gold mining properties, including 28 claims covering the Sherridon Mining District. The transaction requires cash payment and staged exploration spend, with a joint venture formed post-closing, potentially supporting expectations for future copper supply development. | This Proactive roundup contains several nickel and copper headlines tied to company developments: FPX Nickel begins a scoping study for low-cost, low-carbon nickel from its Baptiste project, while Talon Metals reports nickel-copper mineralization drill results and Lion Copper and Gold discusses a Nevada property option spin-out. Impacts are supportive but not immediate price-altering.

LME copper rose about 2% as euro zone, Japan, and US manufacturing expanded and the dollar weakened, improving affordability. Nickel climbed to multi-year highs because LME and Shanghai inventories fell close to record lows, tightening supply; the spot premium over three-month rose above $500/t for the first time since 2009. | Acadia Parish authorities are investigating thefts where thieves cut copper wiring for water well pump sites, damaging conduit and delaying field flooding. Farmers face about $3,000 per repaired site. The story seeks tips for the arrest, but it indicates only local copper scrap/replacement demand. | LME three-month copper rose about 2% after improving manufacturing surveys in the euro zone, Japan and the US and a weaker dollar. Nickel also jumped, with exchange warehouse stocks falling markedly and spot premiums rising above $500/t, indicating tight near-term supply conditions. | Glencore reported FY21 production with own-sourced copper down 5% to 1.2 million tonnes, attributing declines to Mopani disposal, weaker grades at Antapaccay and lower copper by-products from mature zinc/nickel mines. Cobalt rose on limited Mutanda restart. FY22 guidance was unchanged, with copper around 1.15 mt. | Authorities report numerous theft incidents targeting telephone cable, believed to be for the copper in the wire, causing widespread phone and internet outages across Irish counties. Gardaí are urging public vigilance and working with metal dealers, but the episode appears localized and unlikely to materially move copper prices. | Copper prices rose 1.5% on a softer U.S. dollar and buying driven by concerns over low LME inventories, though gains were tempered by slowing China activity around the new year holiday. China’s smelter shutdowns for pollution controls supported supply tightness, aiding the broader base-metals tone. Aluminium shortages also remain stressed. | Irish police report 66 phone-cable theft incidents over six months, disrupting phone and internet services for over 1,500 households. Investigators say cables are targeted for copper contained in the wiring. Garda appeals include reporting unusual purchases of copper cable or copper in large amounts to police.

The piece weighs a potential Western Australia supply shock (prompted by an omicron spread) and its possible impact on WA-linked commodities, including nickel and alumina. It also notes Oxford Economics expects improved metal supply and new copper projects coming online, while nickel production rises in Indonesia/Philippines, offsetting disruption risk. | Atalaya Mining announced drill results from Proyecto Masa Valverde in Spain, highlighting the best continuous copper intercept to date: 125 metres at 1.19% Cu (including 19m at 2.56% Cu). The firm expects higher grades to lift the upcoming NI 43-101 resource estimate and potentially support increased copper production. | Atalaya Mining announced new infill drill results from its Masa Valverde (PMV) copper project in Spain, including a best continuous copper intercept of 125m at 1.19% Cu and other high-grade intervals. Management expects these results to lift the planned NI 43-101 resource estimate and support future copper production growth without rate changes. | Enduro Metals has completed the option to acquire full interest (100%) in the Newmont Lake copper-gold project in British Columbia from Romios, meeting exploration, payment, and royalty milestones. The company can focus on its next exploration program, supported by multiple identified copper-gold and related mineralization targets. | Peru’s mining ministry (Minem) reported that Peruvian copper output declined 5.5% in December. The headline also notes zinc production down 19%, but the tracked focus is copper. Weaker copper output can influence near-term supply expectations and affect copper price sentiment over coming sessions. | The article reports that Peru’s copper production rose in 2021 even with ongoing social conflicts. The implication is that output disruption was less severe than feared, supporting improved copper supply expectations, which can pressure prices downward over the near-term trading horizon.

Orogen Royalties agreed to sell its Onjo copper-gold porphyry project in British Columbia to Pacific Ridge Exploration for cash and equity, plus a 2% NSR royalty. The property lies near the Mount Milligan mine and contains alkalic porphyry/copper-gold skarn targets. The transaction is exploratory, with no immediate output changes. | Allied Copper has secured an option to buy a 100% stake in the Klondike copper property in Colorado’s Paradox Copper Belt. The deal requires about $4.75m of exploration spending over four years plus cash and share issuances. The project remains at exploration stage with no near-term output. | Comex Copper futures ended the week up 4.19% to $4.4835/lb, gaining 18.05 cents. The contract rose seven of the past nine weeks and was higher on most recent sessions, with today’s settlement also up. The note is a data summary with no additional drivers.

Peru’s copper production rose nearly 7% to 2.3mn tons in 2021, helped by the Mina Justa start-up. However, growing protests since President Castillo took office have disrupted operations and heightened fears of future copper shortages, as Peru supplies a large share of global copper exports. | Kingston Resources began exploration drilling at its Mineral Hill copper-gold project in New South Wales, targeting near-mine and brownfields deposits to support future ore feed beyond tailings reprocessing. While the Southern Ore Zone resource includes about 1.2% copper, the article lacks near-term copper output changes, limiting immediate price impact. | SMM’s daily review notes a general metal rise: Shanghai nickel jumped 3.25% with a “black series” strength and tight post-festival spot supply, while aluminium (Shanghai) rose about 3.03% on rapid opening gains. Shanghai copper closed nearly flat (-0.04%). Nickel’s supply/destocking outlook remains key. | ResearchAndMarkets previews a 2022–2027 forecast for the global copper pipes and tubes market, citing demand growth from construction, HVAC, industrial heat exchangers, medical and water systems, and oil & gas. While it implies steady copper end-use consumption, it offers no immediate supply, trade, or pricing shock for near-term copper moves. | ORCA mapped pollution hotspots in Florida’s St. Lucie River, finding high copper levels (river peak ~170 ppm; canal near Palm City Bridge) alongside glyphosate and nutrient pollution. The report suggests legacy boat-bottom paint and vegetation-control chemicals could contribute, calling for source identification and mitigation studies.

The B.C. government has rejected a proposal for a gold and copper mine for the third time. By blocking the planned copper development, the decision lowers the probability of future copper supply coming online, which can provide some short-term support to copper prices despite uncertainty around timing and size. | SMM reports a mixed metal tape on March 8: Shanghai copper is slightly lower while Shanghai aluminium turns up marginally after improving demand and expectations of tight spot ingot supply post-holiday. Nickel rises as pure-nickel supply remains tight and LME/d omestic inventories decline. Overall direction is mixed across tracked metals. | British Columbia rejected an environmental assessment certificate for the proposed open-pit Morrison mine on Lake Babine Nation territory, citing risks to water quality and fish, including sockeye salmon habitat. The project would have produced substantial ore containing copper (plus gold and molybdenum), lowering future copper supply prospects. | Lion Copper and Gold Corp. said it released an updated geology and resource presentation for its MacArthur copper project in Nevada, referencing a prior 55% increase in measured and indicated copper resources and planning an updated PEA. The news is specific to one company’s project advancement, not broader market supply shocks. | Solis Minerals said diamond drilling at its Mostazal copper project in Chile has intersected widespread copper sulphides (including chalcocite/bornite with chalcopyrite) from surface through the first two holes. Assays are underway with expected lab turnaround of at least four weeks, and the rig has moved to a third hole. | The piece focuses on Chilean copper mining and claims transnational firms exported large volumes of copper concentrate while paying low taxes under Chile’s post-1990 tax laws. It argues this has harmed Chile and discusses nationalisation as a possible remedy. The thrust is heightened political/regulatory risk for Chilean copper in the near term.

LME aluminium surged to the highest since 2008 on investor worries over tight supply and declining inventories. China’s pollution-related production restrictions and disruption concerns in Guangxi support the move, while LME warehouse stocks are at multi-year lows. The piece also flags a sharp decline in Peru’s Las Bambas copper output after protests blocked transport. | LME aluminium futures surged to a new 2008 high as inventories in LME warehouses hit the lowest since 2007, with spot premiums widening amid tight supply fears. China’s production limits and COVID-related disruptions are cited, alongside smelter cuts and sanctions risk. Separately, Las Bambas copper mine output is declining after road blockades, tightening copper supply expectations. | Antofagasta has agreed to option Pacific Ridge’s RDP copper-gold project in Canada, with a move to acquire 75% via cash plus a success payment and exploration spending. The deal is focused on funding drilling and assessments rather than immediate output, implying limited near-term impact on copper prices. | An Onawa, Iowa man was sentenced to 15 years for stealing copper wiring from rural Monona County electrical substations, causing thousands of dollars in damage. Authorities also arrested others for similar thefts, including missing copper cable powering streetlights along I-80 interchanges. | SE Asia copper premiums have risen to their highest level in five years, while EU and US premiums are mostly steady. This implies stronger regional demand or supply tightness in Asia relative to other markets, supporting expectations of upward near-term copper pricing through premium-driven settlement.

SMM reports a midday rally in China’s base metals as the US dollar weakened. Shanghai copper rose over 3% to a three-month intraday high, while Shanghai aluminium gained over 2% for the eighth day on tight domestic supply, low inventories and improving downstream spot demand. Shanghai nickel also rose. | SMM’s midday review notes a weaker US dollar and rising industrial metals. Shanghai copper climbed over 3% to a new intraday high for more than three months. Shanghai aluminum rose over 2% for eight straight sessions, supported by tight inventories and expected supply shortages versus imports in the near term. | CATL will form a joint venture with copper-foil supplier Jiayuan Technology to build electrolytic copper foil manufacturing with 100,000 tonnes/year capacity in Guangdong, in two phases. The project targets high-performance lithium-ion battery current-collector foils, implying higher expected copper demand. | Comex copper for February delivery settled up 14.10 cents per pound, or 3.16%, at $4.6005, snapping a two-session losing streak. The move was the largest one-day dollar and percentage gain since Jan. 12, with the settlement at the highest level since Oct. 20, 2021. | Nebraska State Patrol says copper thieves are cutting the copper wire from street lights along Interstate 80 and Highway 75, leaving many lamp posts unlit and costing taxpayers over $100,000 in repairs and rewiring. The theft is linked to scrap-metal sales, but the impact appears highly localized. | SMM reports Chinese secondary copper rod production will not resume until around the Lantern Festival and recovery will be gradual. Output is delayed by Winter Olympics environmental restrictions until Feb. 20 in northern provinces. Additionally, weaker orders after Chinese New Year suppress production. The market also awaits March 1 VAT refund policy effects. | EMX assigned its Arizona state exploration permit covering part of the Parks Salyer copper target to Cactus 110 LLC, with cash and a 1.5% NSR royalty. The deal funds exploration and adds milestones tied to copper resource size, but provides no near-term production or output changes. | Aston Bay Holdings announced expanded leased land for its Mountain copper-zinc project in Virginia and extended its diamond drilling program to 3,500 metres. Copper and zinc mineralization have been observed in multiple holes, but assay results are pending, limiting any immediate impact on copper market expectations. | Los Andes Copper reported initial drill results at its Vizcachitas Chile project: 732m at 0.51% copper equivalent (with copper and molybdenum grades) and continued drilling to expand resources. The update can bolster bullish sentiment for copper given improved resource expectations, though nickel is not meaningfully addressed. | KGHM has registered its copper cathodes on China’s INE stock exchange, enabling the metal to be traded through that platform. The update may affect near-term expectations for physical copper supply and liquidity in China, potentially supporting prices, while it is not directly related to nickel or aluminium fundamentals. | Allied Copper Corp. signed a three-year option agreement to acquire 100% of the Stateline copper property near the Lisbon Valley Copper Mine in Colorado/Utah. The company plans exploration and highlights historical copper assays and future drilling targets, but provides no near-term production, smelting, or inventory impact. | ThreeD Capital says its investee Premium Nickel Resources has completed the acquisition of the Selebi and Selebi North Ni-Cu-Co mines and expects exploration to begin immediately. Metallurgical testing supports producing separate copper and nickel-cobalt concentrates, with drilling and studies planned, suggesting improved supply outlook for nickel and copper. | North American Nickel’s affiliate Premium Nickel Resources has completed the purchase of the Selebi and Selebi North Ni-Cu-Co mines in Botswana from the liquidator, and exploration is expected to begin immediately. Metallurgical testing supports redevelopment to produce separate nickel and copper-cobalt concentrates, which is mildly bullish for nickel and copper. | Mongoose Mining agreed to acquire the Steele Run IOCG copper-cobalt-gold deposit in Nova Scotia, citing historic drill core with reported high copper grades and cobalt assays. The company plans further exploration, but the release provides no new production, financing, or near-term supply change. | Nebraska State Patrol says copper thieves are cutting copper wiring from interstate and highway light posts, leaving areas in the dark. Repairs and rewiring are reportedly over $100,000 so far. Authorities cite safety risks and escalating thefts, but the impact appears localized with limited effect on broader copper markets. | Canadian firm CopperCorp was granted six exploration licences in western Tasmania covering 1,208km² across its Alpine and Skyline projects. Management says the area is prospective for copper and other metals, with planned drilling this year. The news expands tenure but does not imply near-term supply changes. | Lundin Mining said it discovered a new copper-gold mineralized system called Saúva about 15 km north of its Chapada mine in Goiás, Brazil. The announcement can boost market expectations for copper resources and future output, typically providing modest bullish sentiment for copper prices over coming days.

Comex Copper (front month, Feb delivery) settled 1.24% higher at $4.6575, gaining 5.70 cents per pound. The contract was up for two consecutive sessions and had the highest settlement since Oct. 20, 2021. It is also up month-to-date and year-to-date. | SMM said copper inventories in major Chinese markets rose 27,800 mt from Feb 7 to 185,300 mt by Feb 11, and were up 86,600 mt versus the pre–CNY holiday level. The increase reflects downstream taking longer holidays; inventories are expected to keep climbing next week, but more slowly. | Allied Copper agreed to acquire the Stateline property in Colorado and Utah from Cloudbreak Discovery Canada and Alianza Minerals via cash payments, exploration spending, and share issuance. The project is tied to the Paradox Copper Belt with potential for copper production, but it is an exploration-stage move. | Copper tumbled as COMEX and MCX futures slid around 2–3% on a surge in the US dollar index and weakness in equities, plus traders waiting for Chinese copper import data. Despite a prior jump supported by demand hopes, the session saw a sharp correction and choppy outlook. | SMM says copper inventories in China’s domestic bonded zones increased 17,150 mt from Feb 7 to 265,250 mt by Feb 11.
