The piece also cites tighter market conditions and rising clean-energy demand, though tax risk dominates. | SMM reports Shanghai copper import premiums for warrants are stable but remain low, while transactions are scarce. Deteriorating COVID-19 conditions have limited logistics operations, kept key warehouses closed, and reduced downstream willingness to collect goods. Traders are mostly inactive, awaiting unsealing, supporting a near-term bearish tone for copper. | Max Resource Corp. reported new high-grade copper-silver discovery results at its URU area within the Cesar copper-silver project in Colombia. Channel sampling returned multiple open-ended intercepts around 8–12% copper and high silver grades, with mineralization extending over strike and vertical ranges. | The article reports Trump plans 25% tariffs on steel and 10% tariffs on aluminum starting next week, with markets falling sharply on the policy. Aluminum is directly targeted, likely tightening import supply. The broader risk-off tone could weigh on industrial demand, creating offsetting forces for copper. | EU is considering sanctions on Oleg Deripaska, a major shareholder in Aughinish Alumina via Rusal, alongside broader measures targeting Russia’s financial sector and trade. Proposed bans on coal, limits on bank transactions, port access restrictions, and potential further action on fossil-fuel revenues increase supply-chain and risk concerns for industrial metals. | SMM says copper inventory across major Chinese markets fell 800 mt from Apr 1 to 136,900 mt, with destocking slowing during the Qingming holiday. Pandemic-related logistics delay picking up goods, while south China consumption stayed weak as rod factories were on holiday. Overall, inventory is expected to rise slightly this week. | Sun Summit Minerals announced results from ongoing drilling and rock-chip sampling at its Buck Property, central British Columbia, including assays up to 0.47% copper in newly discovered mineralized zones. It outlines further planned follow-up work and geophysics, but remains exploration-stage and unlikely to move near-term copper prices. | Core Assets reports an additional mineralized copper occurrence found during a successful winter accessibility assessment at its Blue property. The update increases confidence in the site’s copper potential, which can be marginally supportive for near-term copper market sentiment, though details on size and economics are not provided here. | The article highlights copper’s supportive near-term backdrop: Chile reported a 7% YoY decline in February output and tight supply is expected to underpin prices. It also notes China’s draconian COVID lockdowns may weaken growth, but maintains a bullish copper view while price holds above the 200-day moving average.

SMM reports the INE bonded copper contract dropped sharply overnight to around 66,000 yuan/mt, falling below the daily moving average with bulls reducing positions. The move is attributed to Fed minutes signaling potential faster tapering and at least one 50 bp hike, lifting the dollar and weighing on copper. | A Shanghai COVID lockdown suspended warehousing logistics, leaving the spot copper market stagnant in the city. Downstream firms diverted purchases to smelters and warehouses in bordering Jiangsu and Zhejiang, where inventories were limited. With demand rising, local copper premiums jumped from about 300 yuan/mt before Qingming. | Georgia’s copper ore exports rose 65% year-on-year in January–February 2022 to $156.2 million. Bulgaria and China increased purchases sharply, with China taking $73.8 million (+73.6%). The report points to stronger export flows that may bolster short-term expectations of copper supply. | Copper Fox provided an update on its 100%-owned Van Dyke in-situ copper recovery project, including completion of initial ADEQ/EPA meetings and steps toward UIC and aquifer protection permits for Class III wells. The company retained Ausenco for process and hydrogeological services and plans further work programs pending approvals. | Goldman Sachs says global refined copper inventories are set to decline faster than expected, warning of a potential supply shock. It raised its 2022–2024 shortfall estimates materially, suggesting visible inventories could be depleted by Q4. Despite only modest price gains so far, analysts view fundamentals as tightening. | SMM reports Shandong bare bright copper prices holding at 66,700-66,800 yuan/mt while copper scrap prices remain firm. Pandemic-related transport restrictions have hindered scrap supply, worsening after Tomb-Sweeping Day. Downstream recycling and end-user production cannot run at full capacity due to difficulty obtaining scrap, likely supporting nearby copper sentiment. | Moxico Resources plans to invest $100m to expand its majority-owned Mimbula copper mine in Zambia. The project would create jobs and increase tax revenues for the Zambian government. The copper-rich oxide and sulphide deposit on the Copperbelt underpins expectations of higher future copper supply. | Go Metals Corp. contracted an operator for a 2,400m summer diamond drill campaign at its Quebec nickel-copper sulphide project. Targets include near-surface nickel-copper sulphides (with reported grab-sample grades) and permitting for up to 20 holes. The release is exploratory and does not indicate immediate supply changes. | Go Metals Corp. announced a contracted 2,400m summer diamond drill program at its Quebec nickel-copper sulphide HSP project. Targets are surface-proven for nickel-copper sulphide mineralization with stated Ni (0.3–1.8%) and Cu (0.3–4.0%) grades, with permitting underway for up to 20 holes. | Deep-South Resources signed an earn-in agreement to acquire up to 80% of three copper exploration licences in Zambia’s Copper Belt. The deal includes payments and share issuance subject to TSX Venture Exchange approval, with soil sampling and potential drilling plans. No resources or economic viability are estimated yet.

Azure Minerals’ managing director discusses a newly defined nickel/copper/cobalt resource in Western Australia. The piece frames the discovery as arriving while nickel and copper prices are “booming,” which could boost sentiment around future supply prospects and provide a modest near-term supportive effect for nickel and copper. | SMM reports Shandong secondary/copper-related self pickup prices (bare bright copper) at 66,700–66,800 yuan/mt, unchanged from April 7. Supply tightness worsened after Qingming, but secondary copper prices stayed stable despite slight overall copper declines. Poor recycling and transport efficiency may reduce secondary copper rod operating rates this week. | Shanghai aluminium prices fell to a three-week low as fresh China lockdowns and expectations of tighter monetary policy raised worries about weaker growth and metals demand. A stronger dollar pressured dollar-priced commodities, pulling LME and ShFE copper lower too, with nickel trading/infrastructure concerns adding sentiment drag. | Organized gangs are stripping copper cables from Prasa’s electrified East London railway line, forcing commuters to switch to costly taxis. Police report seizures including 47kg of copper cables and arrests over infrastructure theft. The losses reduce accessible copper cable supply, potentially supporting copper prices.

St-Georges Eco-Mining disclosed additional core assay results from Quebec’s Manicouagan Project, including high-grade nickel (up to 9.49% Ni) and copper values (up to 2.2% Cu), along with significant palladium and other PGEs. The company is re-logging unsampled core to extend the Ni–Cu–PGE discovery corridor. | The report cites temporarily stronger China spot support for copper: domestic warehouse receipts fall and spot premiums versus futures remain firm, while mine supply disturbances in Chile and Peru are increasing. It also notes weak recycled copper supply and logistics disruption from epidemics, but net near-term fundamentals are price-supportive. | SMM reports the Imported Copper Concentrate Index at about $79.28/mt, above the prior week, with buyers’ inquiries above $80/mt. Peru’s curfew and transport strikes, plus some Chilean port disruptions, challenge copper cathode logistics. Smelters and abundant inventories still keep TCs near $80/mt. | Cochilco said Chile’s copper output fell sharply in February. While Codelco output rose slightly year-on-year to 123,600 mt, other major mining companies’ production dropped materially. The report implies weaker Chilean supply into the coming weeks, which is generally bearish for copper prices. | SMM said copper inventories in major Chinese markets fell 5,000 mt from Friday to 135,300 mt, dropping on Mondays for six straight weeks. COVID-19 traffic and testing rules limited warehouse arrivals (e.g., Shanghai loading/unloading delayed until April 15), and arrivals are expected to stay subdued, keeping weekly stocks declining. | DW reports Germany relies significantly on Russian nickel, with Russia a major supplier of high-grade “class 1” nickel. With EV-driven nickel demand rising, fears of Russian export or EU/Moscow sanctions have previously sparked price spikes and suspended LME trading, keeping supply anxiety elevated. Copper is mentioned but less central. | Aston Bay announced that ore-sorting tests at its Storm Copper Project in Nunavut produced a very high-grade direct shipping product, with a reported 53.9% Cu grade (chalcocite). A June 2022 diamond drilling program is planned to define resources and new zones, but no production or market supply changes are stated. | Norden Crown reported results from 12 diamond drill holes at its Burfjord copper-gold project in Norway, including several copper intercepts (up to 1.27% Cu over 12m). The company says the follow-up drilling outlines broad copper zones and notes discrete cobalt and nickel mineralization, but no production changes. | Ivanhoe Mines says its Kamoa-Kakula complex in the DRC set a record Q1 2022 copper-in-concentrate output of 55,602 tonnes, including a record March and daily production. Phase 2 achieved commercial production on April 7 and is exceeding throughput, supporting higher 2022 copper guidance. | A judge in Montana ruled that state officials unlawfully approved a copper mine. The decision could overturn or delay the project, potentially affecting near-term copper supply plans and tightening market expectations. That supply-risk angle is supportive for copper prices, while no evidence of nickel or aluminium effects is indicated. | A Montana judge ruled the state DEQ unlawfully approved construction of the Black Butte copper mine near the Smith River. Environmental groups alleged inadequate environmental analysis and disregard for public comments. DEQ says it will appeal and the operator reviews the decision, leaving uncertainty around timing and output. | A Montana judge ruled the state DEQ unlawfully approved construction of the Black Butte Copper Mine, citing inadequate environmental analysis and failure to consider alternatives. The DEQ plans to appeal, while the mine operator reviews the decision. The outcome could delay or disrupt project progress, affecting near-term copper supply expectations. | Playfair Mining begins reviewing its RKV nickel-copper assets in Norway, focusing on historical Vakkerlien, Kaltberget and Olkar showings. The release reiterates that nickel prices have surged and argues Class 1 nickel tightness may worsen if Russian supply is embargoed. No new drilling or production changes are announced. | A Montana judge ruled the DEQ unlawfully approved construction of the Black Butte copper mine, finding deficiencies in environmental review and failure to consider alternatives. DEQ plans to appeal and the operator is reviewing the decision. Legal uncertainty could delay operations, potentially tightening near-term copper supply expectations. | A Montana judge ruled the DEQ unlawfully approved the Black Butte Copper Mine’s permit, saying required environmental analyses and consideration of alternatives were not properly done. DEQ plans to appeal and Sandfire Resources will review the decision. The legal challenge could delay or disrupt copper project timelines, affecting supply expectations. | Oil prices fell about 4% on concerns about weakening Chinese demand and expectations of additional IEA strategic reserve releases. The stronger dollar and macro data focus contributed to risk sentiment. Industrial metals dropped, led by aluminium (-3.64% offshore; SHFE -3.09%) and nickel (-2.37%), while copper also fell (~-1.61%).

AEX Gold published 2021 exploration results from its Sava IOCG/porphyry-copper target in Greenland. Historical assays reported up to 3.4% copper, and 2021 rock chips found anomalous copper up to 0.9%. The company highlights coherent multi-element anomalies supporting further copper-focused exploration in 2022. | Ivanhoe said its Kamoa-Kakula copper mine in the DRC set a production record in Q1 2022 and that Phase 2 achieved commercial production quickly, supporting expectations of higher output through 2022–2023. Copper market coverage noted Comex-LME premiums and rising U.S. cathode imports from Chile. | Fabled Copper Corp. reports geophysical and surface prospecting results at its Muskwa Project, identifying an EM-1 copper anomaly and collecting float samples assaying up to 10.55% Cu. The company increased land holdings by 2,924.43 hectares and plans further work, but no production impact is mentioned. | The article headline reports that Fabled Copper has identified a new copper discovery using geophysics and expanded its land position by about 2,924 hectares. Exploration upside can improve expectations for future copper supply, typically supporting copper sentiment in the near term despite details being limited here. | Altus Strategies said Morocco’s Ministry of Energy Transition granted a 10-year mining license for its Agdz copper and silver project near the Bou Skour Cu-Ag mine. The license gives long-term tenure security for the company’s copper prospects. No production schedule or output figures were provided. | Altus Strategies said Morocco’s ministry granted a 10-year mining licence for its Agdz copper-silver project. The licence provides long-term tenure for the company’s 100% owned assets, supporting development of multiple copper and silver prospects in the eastern Anti-Atlas. The announcement is copper-relevant but not an immediate production catalyst. | Candente Copper said it received a second financing offer for up to $10 million to advance the Cariaco copper project. The update is directly tied to copper project development, but it provides no timeline for production or supply changes, implying limited near-term influence on copper prices.

China’s March unwrought copper imports fell 8.8% year-on-year to 504,009 tonnes for the third straight monthly decline, attributed to sluggish manufacturing demand and COVID disruptions. Manufacturing PMI slipped below 50. The article also mentions aluminium exports rising in March, but the copper demand weakness is the key signal. | Goldman Sachs says copper fundamentals have tightened as global exchange inventories fell for the first time in a decade. It points to reduced Chilean output from water shortages and rising mine costs, plus sanctions-driven risks to Russian exports and freight. The firm forecasts larger refined supply gaps and higher copper prices. | Barrick Gold says it has revised plans with Pakistan to restart the large Reko Diq copper-gold project, estimating copper output of about 200,000 tons per year and investment around $7 billion. The company positions it as timely amid global copper shortages, which is likely to support near-term copper sentiment and pricing. | SMM says copper cathode rod producers’ average operating rate rose in March but remained below normal historical levels. It attributes the shortfall to late production resumption and high copper prices, plus mid-March COVID outbreaks in major regions that reduced downstream output, disrupted procurement and transportation, and forced some output cuts. | Infinitum Copper reported trench results from its La Adelita project in Mexico, including intervals averaging 1.90% copper and additional copper-bearing skarn samples up to ~1.34% copper. The company is advancing geophysics and a new drilling program, but provides no near-term production or supply disruption details. | Talon Metals purchased three additional drill rigs to expand exploration outside the main resource area at its Tamarack nickel-copper project in Minnesota. The new drilling targets multiple zones to find high-grade nickel-copper mineralization for potential battery supply, but current output is not reported as changing. | CopperBank reports initial drill core re-sampling and assays from its Copper Creek project in Arizona, confirming historic high-grade breccia-hosted copper and identifying mineralization in previously unsampled core. Over 2,800 metres of a 5,000-metre drilling program are completed; additional assays are pending for broader evaluation and resource work.

Southwest Copper (Yunnan) reports first-quarter progress in copper smelting output, cost reduction, and “green development” measures, including ongoing optimization of energy consumption per unit and amoeba-style performance management across branch plants. It highlights safety and environmental spending but offers no specific capacity or trade change affecting short-term copper pricing. | Southern Copper said its Peruvian Cuajone copper mine is still closed after six weeks of protests, with production halted since Feb. 28. A deal to end protests required withdrawal of complaints, but a railway blockade persists and the government has not ensured security. Peru’s copper production disruption is weighing on the economy. | SMM’s daily review shows industrial metals mixed on LME but generally higher in China, led by Shanghai nickel (+2.39%) and Shanghai aluminium (~+1.5%). The piece attributes nickel strength to tighter pure nickel supply amid Shanghai epidemic disruptions and weaker demand, while LME copper is slightly up (~+0.45%). | White Metal Resources reports Noronex drilling updates from the DorWit Cu-Ag project in Namibia’s Kalahari Copper Belt. Results include numerous high-grade copper intercepts (e.g., 27m at 1.6% Cu) and mineralized strike-length extensions, with infill sampling underway. Overall, it supports bullish sentiment toward copper. | Chile’s Cochilco increased its 2022 copper price forecast to $4.40/lb, attributing the revision to lower inventories and concern that global supply may not meet expectations amid the Russia-Ukraine conflict. It also projected Chilean copper production rising 2.6% to 5.78 million tonnes. | EMX Royalty Corporation acquired an additional 0.3155% NSR royalty on Chile’s Caserones copper-molybdenum mine for US$25.74 million, increasing its effective NSR to 0.7335%. Financing is completed via a C$12.58 million private placement with Franco-Nevada. Caserones produces copper and molybdenum concentrates and copper cathodes. | EMX Royalty acquired an additional effective 0.3155% NSR royalty on the Caserones copper-molybdenum mine in northern Chile for US$25.74 million and will be financed via a C$12.58 million private placement with Franco-Nevada. The update increases royalty exposure to copper output but does not change production or supply. | SMM’s overnight wrap says oil rose on possible action to ban Russian exports and the dollar strengthened. Despite macro headwinds, the article cites expectations of China stimulus supporting metals demand. It reports Shanghai copper up about 0.3%, nickel up about 3.49%, and aluminium up about 1.73%.

ANZ says copper remains supported by a tight supply situation that could worsen with potential renewed COVID disruptions affecting China’s port traffic, while anticipating demand resilience from electricity grid investment. It also notes Russia’s role as a major producer could pressure inventories lower. Overall, near-term copper price momentum looks positive. | Culpeo Minerals completed induced polarisation surveys at its Quelon iron oxide-copper-gold project in Chile, identifying large geophysical anomalies and stating three zones are compelling drill targets. The company is pursuing earn-in stakes and plans a maiden drilling program. The update is exploratory and does not change near-term copper supply. | Minmetals disclosed that Las Bambas will halt its ability to sustain copper output from April 20 (Peru time) for safety reasons amid ongoing community protests over social investment commitments. Authorities and stakeholders have not resolved the impasse, with a follow-up meeting expected in coming days. | LME copper rises 1.7% to a two-week peak as low inventories and China stimulus hopes coincide with supply worries. MMG’s Las Bambas mine plans to suspend operations after a local protest begins. Aluminium stabilizes near $3,214 per tonne on technical support, suggesting near-term resilience. | SMM reports Yangshan copper premiums for warrant and B/L trades stayed flat but import market liquidity remained sluggish. Weak customs clearance and logistics efficiency, plus rising LME copper prices that worsened the price ratio, discouraged offers. Traders cited inability to transfer documents, limiting order pickup and sustaining wider losses for imports. | Copper Fox provided an update on the Schaft Creek copper-gold-molybdenum-silver project, operated by Teck, including preparation for a planned 5,000m summer drill program and progress on geotechnical, metallurgical testing, environmental baseline work, and indigenous engagement. It is mainly a near-term development planning update. | DRC border delays, truck shortages and congestion are making it far costlier and slower to move copper cathodes and raw materials to export ports (Durban, Dar es Salaam, Beira). With Kamoa-Kakula starting early and offering 30–50% higher trucking fees, producers face mounting logistics costs and potential delivery disruptions, supporting higher copper pricing.

AuKing Mining raised $7.1m to fund drilling and exploration at the Koongie Park copper-zinc project in Western Australia, including buying Anglo Australian Resources’ remaining 25% stake. The funds will support the planned program, but the timeline is exploration-focused, implying limited immediate impact on copper prices. | China’s NBS reported copper semis output of 1.9 million mt in March (+4.6% YoY) and 4.83 million mt in Q1 (+6.8% YoY). Higher production from processors suggests stronger near-term copper consumption demand, which can be supportive for copper prices. | Zijin Mining agreed to buy stakes in Xanadu Mines, including a path to a 45.9% interest in Mongolia’s Kharmagtai copper-gold mine via its subsidiary Khuiten Metals. The Kharmagtai resource includes sizable copper, but the transaction signals investment rather than immediate production, implying limited near-term copper price pressure. | London copper edged up as hopes for additional China stimulus offset a firm dollar and growth concerns. A suspension at Peru’s Las Bambas mine added a copper supply-tightness risk. Aluminium and nickel in LME and Shanghai were broadly softer, reflecting uneven sentiment tied to China lockdown-related demand worries. | MMG said it will suspend Las Bambas operations in southern Peru from Wednesday due to safety concerns amid local protests demanding higher financial contributions and accusing MMG of unmet compensation agreements. Las Bambas is a major copper producer (~2% of global supply), so the halt points to near-term supply losses. | SolGold says its Cascabel copper project in northern Ecuador, supported by major shareholders BHP and Newcrest, could become one of the world’s lowest-cost and lowest-carbon copper mines. The feasibility study underpins a $2.7 billion development plan and notes potential gains if a second nearby deposit is successfully developed. | Latin Metals reported positive soil sampling at its Auquis copper project in Peru, outlining a 3km x 2km area of anomalous high-grade copper (up to 2,300 ppm) with coincident molybdenum. The company plans additional rock-chip sampling and mapping, but no production or near-term supply impact is indicated. | VR Resources says drilling has started on its Hecla-Kilmer copper-gold breccia target in northern Ontario, with 4–6 holes planned and assay results expected by June/July. The company previously intersected “copper and gold locally,” but this update contains no new copper assay, production, or supply-impact information.

BHP said labour shortages forced it to downgrade output at its Australian nickel business and its Chilean copper operations. The company’s iron ore division is still tracking its full-year target, but the specific nickel and copper production cuts point to near-term supply constraints that could weigh on copper and nickel prices. | BHP said labour shortages have forced it to downgrade output from its Australian nickel division and its Chilean copper division, even as its iron ore operations remain on track. The update signals near-term supply constraints for nickel and copper, which can weigh on short-term price expectations over coming weeks. | Mining protests in Peru are reportedly halting roughly 20% of output in the world’s No. 2 copper producing country. The disruption reduces near-term supply, tightening market availability and supporting higher copper prices over the coming days as traders price the production loss. | Peru will declare a state of emergency near Southern Copper’s Cuajone mine after protests halted roughly 20% of the country’s copper output. Peru, the world’s No. 2 copper producer, faces disruptions across major mines including Las Bambas and Antapaccay, raising short-term copper supply expectations. | BHP reportedly cut its copper output outlook amid Chile protests and environmental concerns. The update suggests potential disruption or slower ramp-up at copper operations, lowering expected supply in the near term. Such developments can influence copper balances and prompt short-term price moves as traders reassess production expectations. | Peru declared a state of emergency around Southern Copper’s Cuajone mine after protests and water cutoffs disrupted output, while MMR suspended production at Las Bambas for safety. The article cites disruptions affecting about 20% of Peru’s copper production, a notable supply risk. | BHP shares fell after the miner said Q3 copper output dropped 6% to 369.7 kt and it cut FY22 copper guidance to 1,570–1,620 kt from 1,590–1,760 kt, citing lower Escondida volumes. BHP also lowered nickel guidance to 80–85 kt due to COVID-19 labor constraints. | Anglo American said Q1 copper equivalent production fell 10% year on year, with copper output down 13% amid Covid-related absenteeism and high rainfall disrupting operations in South Africa and Brazil. Company guidance for copper remains unchanged, but the weaker recent production supports a bearish near-term supply outlook. | Antofagasta said Q1 copper production fell to 138,800 tonnes, down 24.2% year-on-year, mainly because drought reduced throughput at Los Pelambres and lower grades at Centinela Concentrates affected output. Full-year guidance remains unchanged, but the Q1 decline may pressure near-term copper supply expectations. | Gold State Resources finalized acquisition of the Panteria copper-gold porphyry project in Peru. Prior drilling reported long porphyry-related copper intervals (via copper-gold grades and copper-equivalent assumptions). The deal involves exploration-stage milestones and a net smelter return royalty, with no immediate production impact. | White Gold Corp. reports broad, zoned soil anomalies at its Hayes and Pilot properties, including anomalous copper and a potential Cu-Mo porphyry core. The findings are based on geochemistry with no drilling yet, but they may slightly improve expectations for future copper supply in the region. | Norilsk Nickel notified ADR holders that Russian federal law will require termination of depositary receipt programs. From April 27, 2022, shares underlying ADRs will become non-voting and no dividends will be paid on those shares. ADR holders face restrictions on trading their ADRs for entitlements. | Peru’s President Pedro Castillo hardened his stance on mining protests, mobilizing the army as tensions with communities against mining firms escalated. Mining has been halted at Southern Copper’s Cuajone since late February, demanding compensation and profit sharing, raising near-term disruption risk for Peru’s copper supply. | A North Texas woman was indicted for allegedly stealing over $150,000 worth of copper wiring from an abandoned Alcoa site in Rockdale. Authorities say deputies tracked a van leaving the property after wires were cut and stacked. The case is criminal in nature with localized supply impact.

LME copper is on track for a third weekly decline as China’s COVID lockdowns cloud demand, while LME-approved inventories have risen to the highest since last October. Rising inventories and a stronger U.S. dollar add pressure. Limited support comes from hopes of Chinese stimulus and supply disruptions, but overall bias remains down. | Antofagasta raised capex for its Chile Los Pelambres expansion by $900m to $2.2bn, citing revised Covid-19 costs, general inflation (wages, labor incentives, logistics), and contingency. The company said delivery schedules are unchanged and copper output guidance remains, but higher costs can support near-term copper pricing expectations. | ArcWest Exploration provided an exploration update for its Oxide Peak Cu-Au project, including planned 2022 drilling of an untested porphyry target and prior geophysical/geochemical results indicating copper anomalies. The release is company/project specific with no immediate changes to mine output, inventories, or pricing mechanisms for copper, nickel, or aluminium.

Michigan State Police are investigating reports of copper wiring theft from an agricultural irrigation pivot in St. Joseph County, Michigan. Several feet of copper were cut from pivot towers between November 2021 and April 15, causing damage. The incident is local and unlikely to affect broader copper pricing. | Industrial metals fell as China’s COVID-19 lockdown restrictions raised fears of weaker demand and economic growth. Copper hit its lowest level in over a month, while expectations for larger Fed rate hikes supported a stronger dollar, pressuring dollar-priced metals. Additional headlines include a negative outlook for copper projects and Peru mine restart. | SMM reports higher imported copper concentrate prices and TCs, with trading concentrated on May–June shipments. Protests force Las Bambas to stop production from April 20, while Peru considers a state of emergency near Cuajone; Chile is also reshaping mining rules. The market worries about long-run concentrate supply despite short-term easing. | Trinidad and Tobago scrap dealers urge the public and authorities to curb copper wire theft after viral incidents. The association says theft harms livelihoods and has been linked to fires and past deaths. It calls for faster regulation, arrests, and refusing to buy stolen items, aiming to reduce theft-driven scrap supply. | SMM says Chinese copper inventories fell 12,400 mt to 114,900 mt over the weekend, with declines across Shanghai, Guangdong, Jiangsu and others. The drawdown is attributed to downstream restocking after sharp price drops and improving inter-provincial logistics. SMM expects inventories to keep declining into the week before May Day. | Norilsk Nickel reported preliminary 1Q22 production: consolidated nickel output rose 10% y/y to 52kt, driven by recovery of Oktyabrsky and Taimyrsky mines and ramp-up of the Norilsk Concentrator. Copper production stayed roughly unchanged at 91kt, with logistics and geopolitical issues not disrupting operations. | Copper fell on Monday, dropping below $10,000/t on the back of demand fears. Funds increased bearish CME copper bets, while China’s lockdowns and weaker PMI readings signaled slowing manufacturing. Supply issues remain, but attention is shifting toward weaker near-term consumption, driving bearish sentiment. | Zambia’s president said the government is very close to selecting a private investor for Mopani Copper Mines to increase output, following the state’s purchase of the mine from Glencore and ongoing need for investment. He also denied plans to return KCM to Vedanta. | Atacama Copper began a four-week trenching and channel-sampling program at its El Cofre copper-gold project in Chile. Approximately 3,000 meters of trenching will follow up geochemical and mapping targets, with initial XRF results expected in May 2022 and lab assays in June 2022. | Nornickel said Q1 nickel production rose 10% year-on-year to 51,534 tonnes after the recovery of its two mines from 2021 flooding. Copper output was stable at 91,414 tonnes. Despite Western sanctions-related logistics challenges, the company kept its 2022 nickel forecast unchanged, citing uninterrupted operations. | Resistance groups tied to Myanmar’s civil disobedience movement warn Chinese-run copper mines in Salingyi to halt operations by May 5. The timing and potential for mine disruption raise supply risk for copper, though it is unclear if the call will be implemented immediately. | Weeks of Peru-wide protests over fuel and fertilizer costs spread to mining communities. The Chinese-owned Las Bambas copper project was disrupted after community members invaded the site, prompting Las Bambas to stop producing copper. The government deployed the army, worsening uncertainty over Peru’s copper output near term.

London copper rebounded 1.4% after hitting a more-than-two-month low, driven by hopes for additional China economic stimulus and a slight retreat in the U.S. dollar. The piece also notes lingering COVID lockdown worries and broader macro uncertainty, with aluminium and nickel referenced via markets and related company updates. | Metallic Minerals reported an inaugural NI 43-101 inferred resource at its La Plata project, centered on the Allard copper-silver porphyry deposit. The estimate includes 115.7 Mt at 0.39% copper equivalent (0.35% Cu, 4.02 g/t Ag) and plans for follow-up drilling, but no production or inventory change. | Battery Mineral Resources said it intercepted 34 meters of 1.35% copper from the Cinabrio Norte target at its Punitaqui copper mine in Chile. The result is an exploration success that modestly improves expectations for future copper resources, offering limited near-term bullish sentiment for copper prices. | The article reviews copper market drivers: a still-large but narrowing refined deficit, Chinese demand slowing tied to real estate weakness, European consumption pressure from high energy costs, and uncertainty from Russia-related sanctions and financing. It also notes limited copper-aluminium substitution, leaving copper volatility high but directionally mixed. | Peru is losing over US$110mn in copper export value due to an illegal occupation affecting the Las Bambas operation. The disruption limits copper shipments from the mine, reducing near-term export flows from one of the country’s key producers and likely worsening short-term supply expectations. | Copper is reported to be rebounding as China vows additional support for its economy. The implication is improved near-term economic activity and demand expectations for industrial metals, which tends to lift sentiment and prices for copper, the benchmark industrial metal, over coming days.

Copper prices were under pressure Monday, with LME three-month copper slipping below $10,000 per tonne for the first time in a month. After weeks of stalled trade—too high for buyers and too tight for sellers to short—bears are moving back in, indicating renewed bearish momentum. | LME copper fell as concerns grew that COVID lockdowns in top consumer China will curb demand, while expectations for large U.S. rate hikes lifted the dollar to multi-year highs. The stronger dollar increased costs for non-dollar buyers. China stimulus hopes limited declines. Global risk-off also pressured industrial metals. | SMM says Yangshan copper premiums under warrants rose to $20–45/mt, with averages up $8.5/mt versus the previous day. Quotes under B/L for May also increased. The improved price ratio supported import transactions, while traders held firm due to high US-dollar pledge costs and related financing frictions. | Teck Resources reported profits jumping five-fold, attributing the increase to higher copper and coal prices. The headline implies copper prices are firming, which can support short-term sentiment and expectations for further strength in copper markets over the coming days. | Industrial metals rose as hopes for further Chinese stimulus outweighed COVID-related demand worries and fears from potential aggressive U.S. rate hikes. LME three-month copper edged up and LME aluminium gained. Nickel also rose, with investors weighing China’s monetary support amid lockdown impacts on industrial activity. | Golden Rim Resources signed an option and joint venture agreement with Teck Chile to explore the Loreto porphyry copper project in northern Chile. Teck may earn up to 75% via cash payments and US$17m exploration spending. The deal targets potential deeper copper mineralization, but is not a production change. | Chile’s SMA has launched a sanctions process against Glencore’s Lomas Bayas copper mine for failing to meet required water and flora/fauna monitoring standards, including missing observation-well records and improper aquifer measurement records. The charges could pressure operations or raise compliance costs in the near term. The mine produced 64,300t copper last year. | GNRI is exploring a sale of its Khoemacau copper mine in Botswana, which produces about 60,000–65,000 tonnes of copper annually. The reported possible deal value (~$1.5bn) is uncertain and no operational changes are announced, limiting immediate impact on near-term copper supply expectations. | Callinex began a 20,000-meter drilling program at its Pine Bay Project in Manitoba, focusing on infill and step-out drilling to define the high-grade Rainbow copper deposit ahead of a 43-101 resource estimate. A second rig will test geophysical targets along the district trend for additional mineralized zones. | GNRI is exploring options, including a possible sale, of its Khoemacau copper-silver mine in Botswana. Bloomberg reports advisers are identifying potential suitors, valuing the asset at nearly $1.5bn. While buyers may expand copper portfolios, no final decisions or operational changes are indicated. | Proactive lists investor-focused headlines including Fabled Copper reporting an airborne survey identified new anomalies at its Muskwa copper project in British Columbia, and Quebec Nickel mobilizing a second drill rig at its Ducros property after VTEM data revealed new targets. Other items are unrelated to industrial metals. | Teck reported record first-quarter profit and higher copper revenue, noting that its QB2 copper mine in Chile is over 80% complete. Management expects first copper from Line One in Q4, subject to no further COVID disruptions, and frames 2022 as transformational for copper production and cash generation. | Peruvian police evicted indigenous protesters from MMG’s Las Bambas open-pit copper mine after the camp led the company to suspend operations. MMG said production remains suspended for safety reasons and restart timing is unclear. The stoppage at a ~2% global supply mine may tighten copper supply expectations.

The EU’s proposed batteries regulation would require battery makers and importers to source bauxite (linked to aluminium) and copper responsibly, if included in the final list of regulated raw materials. A coalition argues the rules should cover bauxite, copper, and iron. Negotiations are ongoing with differing positions in EU institutions. | An EU batteries regulation proposal would require battery importers and manufacturers to ensure responsible sourcing of key raw materials used in batteries. A coalition argues the final list should include bauxite (for aluminum) and copper, alongside iron, citing human-rights and environmental risks in mining and refining. | Glencore has reduced its output guidance, according to the headline, covering copper (and also zinc and cobalt). A lower near-term production outlook for copper suggests reduced supply availability, which can tighten market balance and provide upward price pressure over the following days to weeks. | Glencore reported Q1 own-sourced copper production of 257,800 tonnes, down 14% versus Q1 2021, citing temporary geotechnical constraints at Katanga and other changes. Nickel output rose 22% to 30,700 tonnes mainly from Koniambo. The mixed supply signals imply limited net near-term price pressure. | Protesters were tear-gassed at a copper mine in Peru, signaling unrest at the operation. Such disruptions can hinder production and exports in the near term, reducing supply visibility and supporting expectations for higher copper prices over the coming days to weeks. | Osisko Metals said it has announced its maiden resource at the Gaspé copper project: an inferred resource of 456 Mt grading 0.31% copper. While not immediate production, the update can strengthen expectations for future copper supply, supporting bullish sentiment toward copper-linked markets. | Los Andes Copper Ltd. says RCF has entered into a definitive agreement to sell its 2% NSR royalty on the Vizcachitas copper project in Chile. If the sale closes, RCF will pay Los Andes a contingent US$5 million under the 2020 royalty agreement, subject to fundraising and closing conditions. | City Power said police recovered stolen copper cables from a scrapyard in Devland near Soweto and plans to intensify raids on scrapyards to curb cable theft. City Power and other utilities joined enforcement actions, citing reduced lost revenue and public inconvenience from stolen copper cables.

SMM says pandemic control and logistics problems are still disrupting copper processing enterprises, reducing return rates, pressuring sales and funds, and extending holiday downtime. Terminal demand is weakening and producers hold low raw material inventory. The outlook depends on May improvement, but traditional power grid, real estate, and appliances remain sluggish. | SMM reports cathode rod makers are still affected by pandemic-linked logistics problems, with Zhejiang producers short of raw materials and constrained production. Downstream demand is weak, new orders have declined, and firms have barely restocked ahead of Labour Day. More producers are expected to close than in previous years, extending the holiday break. | SMM survey says COVID outbreaks in some regions have left copper wire and cable producers with finished-product inventory backlogs and under financial stress, prompting lower output and longer Labour Day holidays. Demand also weakened as pandemic controls reduced end-user operating rates and slowed State Grid/China Southern Power Grid ordering. | Peru expelled protesters from the Las Bambas copper mine after a two-week production halt, deploying hundreds of police and tear gas amid clashes. The government declared a 30-day emergency, temporarily suspending free assembly. Las Bambas accounts for nearly 400,000 mt/year capacity and has suffered shipment losses from road protests. | Copper rebounded 1.4% after China’s Politburo pledged more economic support and the dollar eased, making copper cheaper for overseas buyers. However, a renewed COVID wave and tighter lockdowns are expected to hurt Chinese factory activity, with copper still headed for a sharp monthly fall. Aluminium and nickel also fell on China demand worries. | Rio Tinto will invest in copper producer Arizona Sonoran Copper through a $23.7m private placement, taking a 7.4% stake. Proceeds fund drilling and studies for the Cactus Mine Project and metallurgical testing, including Nuton leaching technologies aimed at unlocking additional copper recovery with lower waste. | Zambia’s ZCCM-IH said an adviser and restructuring for Mopani Copper Mines is imminent, with the government “very close” to finding an investor. Zambia bought Mopani from Glencore in 2021 and needs new capital for output increases, while ZCCM continues servicing loans and funding inputs in the interim. | Fastmarkets says China’s stimulus sparked a risk-on rebound in LME base metals, lifting copper and other contracts early Friday. Traders also cite ongoing supply tightness in 2022, including mining/logistics challenges and Glencore’s lower copper guidance and force majeure on African copper deliveries.

Goldplay Mining announced Phase 1 drill results at the Aparis Copper Mine in southern Portugal, including 12.2m at 1.18% Cu and 5.5m at 2.33% Cu. The company says the mineralized system appears wider than historical workings and is planning further drilling. | Goldplay drills at Aparis Mine in southern Portugal, reporting 12.2m of 1.18% copper, including 5.5m at 2.33% copper. Such positive drilling results typically strengthen expectations for potential resource quality and future copper supply, which can provide near-term support to copper prices. | London copper and aluminum dropped to three-month lows as COVID-19 restrictions in China and expectations of aggressive U.S. rate hikes raised fears of weaker global growth. The Fed meeting and potential half-point hike, along with recent weak Chinese data, intensified demand worries, pressuring LME prices. | London copper and aluminum slid to three-month lows as COVID-19 curbs in China and expectations of aggressive U.S. rate hikes raised fears of weaker global growth and metals demand. Copper fell 1.7% and aluminum 0.9% on LME, with the dollar firmer and macro data also weighing. | Talon Metals reported assay results from new drill holes at its Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt Project in Minnesota, highlighting thick, high-grade nickel and copper intervals in the CGO West area. The company plans continued drilling and feasibility work toward first production in 2026, with updates expected as new targets are drilled. | Granite Creek Copper retained SGS to update a Preliminary Economic Assessment for its Carmacks Cu-Au-Ag project in Yukon, using a 2022 resource estimate with 651 Mlb contained measured/indicated copper. The study includes potential sulphide processing benefits and revised pit modeling and economics based on specified metal prices. | Digital Journal reports Kamoa-Kakula is on track to become the world’s third-largest copper mining complex by Q4 2024, targeting about 600,000 tonnes of copper production per year. The ramp-up implies rising supply expectations, which can pressure nearby copper prices despite the forward-looking timing. | The article headline says Southern Copper’s mine is back to full output as talks with protesters are expected. This suggests an end (or easing) of production disruption, improving near-term supply visibility. The likely near-term market effect is supportive for copper prices as output constraints diminish.

Fabled Copper Corp. reported 2021 surface fieldwork at its Muskwa Copper Project’s Davis Keays Eagle vein area, including new parallel vein discoveries and rock assays up to 6.73% copper. The article is focused on exploration sampling and prospecting results, with no production or project-scale timeline changes. | Copper Fox Metals updates its 100%-owned Eaglehead polymetallic porphyry copper project in British Columbia. It describes progress toward 2022 drilling (contracts executed, NoW pending), geological modeling, and age dating supporting a copper system. The release is exploration-stage with no production or immediate supply change. | Aranjin Resources began follow-up auger geochemical drilling at the Baavhai Uul copper-nickel discovery in Mongolia under an agreement with ION Energy. Early results reported anomalous nickel (up to 2,150 ppm) and copper (up to 677 ppm), with plans to upscale auger drilling and geophysics in coming weeks. | Copper prices steadied near 4-1/2 month lows as traders weighed rising interest rate risk, China COVID outbreaks and slowing factory activity that could weaken demand. The dollar’s strength further pressures dollar-priced metals. Speculators shifted toward price-fall bets. LME aluminium and nickel also posted declines. | Aranjin Resources commenced follow-up auger drilling at the Baavhai Uul copper-nickel discovery in Mongolia under an earn-in agreement. Early results cited up to 2,150 ppm nickel and 677 ppm copper from shallow samples, with additional auger, geophysics, and other copper project work planned. | Camino Minerals announced a contract to drill 1,500 metres of core at its Los Chapitos copper project in Peru, targeting newly permitted high-grade oxidized copper outcrops at the Lourdes–Condori zones. Mobilization is planned for May 15, 2022, with results expected to follow later. | Mountain Boy reports progress on its Telegraph copper-gold project in BC’s Golden Triangle, citing renewed geological prospectivity, anomalous copper values (including a 17.95% grab sample), and planned hyperspectral, IP/MT, and soil programs to refine drill targets for the 2022 field season. No production or resource update is provided. | The piece describes the history of Britannia Mines near Vancouver, focusing on copper ore concentration, output, disasters, and later environmental remediation. While copper mining and historical production are emphasized, the article contains no present-day supply, policy, or demand event that would likely affect short-term industrial metal prices. | Bitterroot Resources provided results from a winter drilling program at its LM magmatic nickel-copper project in Michigan. The company plans further down-plunge drilling after additional land acquisition. | Bitterroot Resources updated on a completed winter drilling program at its LM nickel-copper-platinum group metals property in Michigan, reporting sulphide mineralization in 10 of 26 holes, including chalcopyrite, pyrrhotite and pentlandite. The company plans further targeting after additional land acquisition, signaling ongoing exploration progress.

TSTT says copper cable theft has risen during the pandemic, leading to significant costs and service outages. Police have brought 161 people to court. TSTT argues it is transitioning customers to wireless and fibre but still serves active copper users. It condemns scrap buyers and notes penalties under telecom law. | Report discusses Comex copper futures at $3.88/$3.50 support and $5.17 resistance, noting daily/weekly/monthly downtrends but arguing buyers may return. It cites corrective Elliott structure, oversold conditions, and a bullish breakout level above ~$5.06, with a long position and stop at $4.12. | SMM reports the SHFE/LME copper price ratio improved as China’s policy support for infrastructure and demand recovery, alongside a sharp drop in SHFE copper inventories, helped stabilize prices. Earlier, a strong US dollar and rising LME inventories pressured copper. After the USD impact eases, copper is expected to firm. | Southern Copper’s Cuajone mine has resumed full operations, improving Peru copper supply availability, while Las Bambas is still suspended, which continues to constrain supply elsewhere. The net effect is an improvement in near-term copper supply expectations, typically supportive for copper prices over the next few weeks. | Aguila Copper Corp. began an induced polarization (IP) survey at its Lida copper-silver project in Nevada, using a deep-penetrating geophysical system to guide potential drilling later in 2022. The release highlights copper-oxide/carbonate mineralization from recent sampling, but provides no immediate production or supply effects. | Chile’s copper output declined 6.36% in March, according to Cochilco. As Chile is the key source of global supply, lower monthly production can signal tighter availability and support near-term copper price expectations. Other content is largely unrelated fastmarkets/assessment process updates. | Faraday Copper Corp. closed a C$20 million upsized private placement at $0.80/share and plans to use net proceeds to advance its Copper Creek and Contact Copper projects, plus working capital. The article is about financing for exploration/development rather than any immediate production or supply change affecting copper prices. | TSTT in Trinidad and Tobago condemned “rogue scrap iron dealers” for a rise in theft and vandalism of the firm’s copper overhead line network since the start of the Covid-19 pandemic. SMM reports Yangshan copper premiums under warrants rising to $33–68/mt, with B/L premiums also increasing, reflecting higher import profits. It cites LME inventories rising but notes low Chinese social inventory supporting SHFE/LME ratios. Rising profits have led importers to keep lifting premiums for pyro- and hydro-copper cargoes. | SMM data show China’s mainstream copper inventories rose 9,400 mt to 120,000 mt after the Labour Day holiday, driven by less-than-expected downstream activity and cathode stagnation from transport problems, alongside smelters shipping directly to downstream. With improved consumption expected next week, inventory may fall, but the near-term signal is bearish. | Copper, aluminium, nickel and other commodities have fallen as China tightened Covid controls. The lockdowns reduce activity in end-use industries, cutting near-term metal demand and weighing on broader sentiment toward the metals complex. The price declines over the past couple of weeks reflect this demand shock. | Copper, aluminium, nickel and other metals have fallen over the past couple of weeks as China tightened Covid curbs. The lockdowns reduce demand from the world’s largest metal consumer and dampen sentiment toward the metal complex, creating negative price pressure across industrial commodities in the near term. | Shanghai’s Covid-19 controls affected bonded metals flows. Copper bonded stocks kept increasing in April but more slowly as transport improved and import arbitrage turned profitable. Aluminium and nickel bonded stocks declined due to closed or loss-making arbitrage windows and limited inflows/outflows, with weak trading and constrained logistics. | BHP says its failed bid for Noront Resources is “almost the tip of the iceberg” for future Canadian nickel growth, setting up teams to seek projects in Ontario’s Ring of Fire. The executive also notes focus on copper and investing in opportunities, but provides no deal or supply disruption. | The piece lists oversold stocks to buy, including Southern Copper (SCCO). It notes copper prices pulled back after a spike post–Ukraine invasion, with worries that higher rates may slow demand. It counters that EV growth could sustain copper demand, implying copper may avoid further declines.

Rystad Energy forecasts a growing global copper deficit driven by electrification demand. The article highlights Cyprium Metals’ planned restart of Australia’s Nifty mine, showing costs well below the recent copper price and potential free cash flow. It also notes analyst expectations of Chinese demand recovery later in 2022, supporting copper prices. | China’s customs data show total exports and imports rising year-on-year. Specifically, exports of unwrought aluminium and aluminium semis increased 29.2% in Jan–Apr, while copper ore and concentrates imports rose 4.5% over the same period. These trade-flow shifts can influence short-term supply expectations for aluminium and copper. | First Quantum approved a $1.25 billion expansion project in Zambia, increasing plans for copper output. The decision is likely to shift expectations toward greater copper supply, which can pressure prices in the coming days as traders start repricing the near-term supply outlook, despite construction ramp-up uncertainty. | Pacific Ridge Exploration announced it will acquire up to a 75% interest in the Chuchi porphyry copper-gold project in British Columbia. The release outlines historical drilling, copper grades, and plans for an airborne survey and summer exploration to define drill targets. It implies future supply potential, not near-term production changes. | China’s lockdowns and zero-COVID stance are keeping demand weak. Shanghai remains under restrictions and Beijing cases are rising, while Caixin Manufacturing PMI hit a two-year low. The contraction coincides with falling copper prices and an 8.8% drop in copper imports from February to March, with demand likely soft through May.

Emmerson Resources reports visual confirmation of “very high-grade” copper at the Hermitage prospect, with drill intersections including 38m of mineralisation containing native copper, malachite and chalcopyrite, and a second hole with 57m of native copper-bearing intervals. The company plans further drilling and awaits assay results. | Far East Gold started a revised scoping study for its Wonogiri copper-gold project, commissioning metallurgical test work and updating a prior study based on current commodity prices. It reports early gold and copper metallurgical results and outlines additional exploration at another Indonesian copper-gold project. | Mongolia exported 492.8 thousand tons of copper concentrate between April 29 and May 5, up 23.1% from the same period a year earlier. It also exported copper cathode since the start of the year. Faster concentrate exports can raise near-term supply expectations for copper. | SMM reports that the INE bonded copper 2207 contract trended slightly upward but finished near 63,500 yuan/mt, up only 0.05%, with short positions increasing. It notes pandemic-affected near-term demand and investor worries over growth, while the INE-SHFE price spread widened. | Mongolia exported 492.8 thousand tons of copper concentrate during April 29–May 5, up 23.1% versus the same period last year. The report also notes copper cathode exports and broader shipments of coal and iron ore, indicating greater copper feedstock availability in the near term. | Pacific Ridge Exploration agreed to buy up to a 75% stake in Canada’s Chuchi porphyry copper-gold project from AuRico Metals. Authorities report major daily losses in royalties and exports, while unrest raises fears of wider mine disruptions beyond these sites.

The article says high copper prices and rising demand are driving renewed exploration and mine expansion across Africa. It cites the DRC’s Kamoa-Kakula ramp-up to 600,000 tonnes by Q4 2024, Zambia’s Mimbula expansion and Mopani/Uganda’s Kilembe restart plans, reinforcing a bullish copper outlook. | SMM reports Shanghai bonded-zone imported copper premiums under warrants rising to $70/mt on average and bill-of-lading premiums up to $57.5/mt. The SHFE/LME copper ratio increase keeps the import profit window open versus spot and SHFE 2205, with firm offers but thin trading as buyers balk at premiums. | Marimaca Copper reported results from 10 RC infill holes at its Marimaca oxide deposit in Chile, finding broad near-surface copper oxide mineralisation with grades exceeding the resource model in several zones. Extensions outside the prior resource block and potential expansion of the high-grade MAMIX zone were highlighted. | SMM reports that SHFE metals broadly rose on May 11, with SHFE nickel up about 3%, SHFE aluminium up 2.61%, and SHFE copper up around 0.3%. It also cites disruptions affecting cobalt, nickel, lithium logistics and shows aluminium up while nickel falls. | Blackwolf Copper and Gold provides an exploration and permitting update for its Niblack (Cu-Au-Ag-Zn VMS) and Hyder-area projects (including Cantoo with Cu/Ag/Zn plus gold-silver). The company anticipates permits and plans multi-phase drilling, potentially expanding resources, which may modestly influence near-term copper expectations. No nickel or aluminium impacts are stated. | Copper prices rose about 1.4% as China’s COVID-19 infections slowed, easing immediate demand concerns, though partial “zero-COVID” restrictions still suppress activity. The piece notes April vehicle and cathode output weakness from lockdowns and logistics disruption, while LME aluminium also gained and nickel fell on the day. | ALX Resources said it completed six of eight planned holes in its Electra nickel-copper project in Ontario, intersecting sulphide mineralization and detecting nickel, copper and zinc via portable XRF. Additional lab results are pending and the update is exploratory, with no production or definitive resource revision. | Golden Arrow Resources agreed to option up to 85% of its Mogote copper-gold project in Argentina to Syndicate Minerals. Syndicate can earn 80% through $5m exploration spend and $1.9m cash over five years, with a further 5% after feasibility and JV formation. No immediate supply disruption is cited. | Vedanta agreed to suspend lawsuits connected to its copper mining operations in Zambia. Easing legal pressure can lower the probability of immediate operational disruptions or delays, improving near-term expectations for copper supply. This usually provides supportive sentiment for copper prices over the coming days to weeks. | Rio Tinto and Utah officials said the Kennecott refinery in Utah will begin producing tellurium, a critical mineral used in solar panels, from materials previously discarded as tailings. The U.S.-based plan targets about 20 tons/year and aims to reduce reliance on China, offering limited near-term impact on copper or aluminium prices.

Rio Tinto says it has started producing tellurium at its Kennecott copper refinery in Utah, with refining by 5N Plus and primary supply to First Solar. About 20 tons per year will be produced via a new circuit. The update is focused on critical minerals/semi supply rather than copper market fundamentals. | Copper traded below $9,000 as broader industrial metals weakened on global demand fears. The framing suggests investors reduced expectations for near-term consumption, driving selling pressure in copper prices over the immediate period referenced. | SMM reports INE bonded copper 2207 declining to close at 62,480 yuan/mt, down 0.97%, with rising open interest mainly from shorts. The INE–SHFE premium narrowed. | The headline reports copper dropping below $9,000 as broader metals weaken on concerns about global demand. This suggests investors are cutting near-term expectations for industrial consumption, creating immediate downward pressure on copper prices relative to other metals. | Police in Wrexham are investigating four theft incidents over 10 days involving “significant amounts” of underground copper cable, including roughly 700–800 metres per event. Authorities request information, indicating localized copper loss rather than wider market supply disruption. | Arc Minerals said Anglo American will take majority control (70%) of its Zambia copper-cobalt exploration licences, its first new investment in Zambia in 20 years. The agreement is likely to support copper market sentiment, though it is tied to exploration rather than near-term production. | Arc Minerals’ shares jumped after Anglo American agreed to take 70% control of a JV tied to Arc’s Zambia copper-cobalt licences in the North-Western province. Anglo will fund exploration over seven years and make cash payments. The move supports longer-term copper supply confidence, but not immediate output. | A U.S. appeals court upheld a decision overturning the Forest Service’s approval of a Toronto-based company’s permit for the Rosemont open-pit copper mine in southern Arizona. The court found the permit exceeded legal allowances and noted issues around waste-rock storage and mineral value. Conservationists welcomed the ruling.

Capstone Copper reported Q1 2022 results and provided consolidated guidance for 2022, forecasting 136,000–150,000 tonnes of copper output at C1 cash costs of $2.55–$2.70/lb. It also detailed ramp-up progress at the Mantos Blancos concentrator and construction of the Mantoverde sulphide project. | Capstone Copper reports Q1 2022 results with consolidated copper output of 22,500 tonnes and C1 cash costs of $2.31/lb. The company also outlines progress on ramp-ups and major construction and reiterates 2022 guidance for 136,000–150,000 tonnes of copper at C1 costs of $2.55–$2.70/lb. | The article headline says copper is “waving” near $9,000 after a fear-filled week for metals. This points to recent risk-off sentiment and weaker momentum in the copper market, which typically exerts downward pressure on prices in the immediate days following the selloff. | Community protesters occupied Peru’s Las Bambas copper mine near the open pit, forcing production to halt and stretching into a third week with no restart in sight. The mine is cited as supplying about 2% of global copper; expansion plans face further conflict, raising near-term copper supply disruption risk. | Torr Metals announces it completed the purchase of undersurface crown grants covering the Dalvenie Zone within its Latham Copper-Gold Project in northwest BC. The company touts a kilometer-scale copper-gold-silver exploration target tied to a regional copper-gold porphyry trend and plans near-term data compilation for the 2022 field program. | SMM data show copper inventories across major Chinese markets fell 2,600 mt WoW to 118,900 mt by May 13, and down versus a year ago. The decline is linked to reduced processor operating rates from pandemic/transport issues and weaker end-week restocking, while supply should rise next week. | SMM reports domestic bonded copper inventories fell 2,100 mt week-on-week to 327,000 mt as of May 13, with Shanghai down 2,800 mt to 290,000 mt while Guangdong rose slightly. SMM expects further declines due to large import profits and reduced pandemic-era port piles. | Chile’s environment minister Maisa Rojas will push tougher environmental regulation, including new climate legislation with emission caps and mandatory reporting, with added scrutiny on mining operations. As Chile is the leading copper producer, stronger enforcement and adaptation requirements could pressure mining output and increase costs, weighing on copper prices. | A federal appeals court partially blocked Rosemont Copper’s bid to develop a large open-pit copper mine near Tucson by restricting its ability to dump nearly two billion tons of waste rock on additional Forest Service land. The company can mine on land it owns, but must pursue an alternate compliant plan.

Chile’s Constitutional Convention failed to include a proposal that would replace the investor-friendly mining concession model with temporary, revocable permits. With the plan falling short of the two-thirds threshold, immediate political risk to Chile’s copper mining regime appears reduced ahead of a Sept 4 referendum. | SMM reports copper prices under pressure from a rising US dollar and tightening Fed signals. In China, weaker manufacturing/credit and pandemic disruptions hurt terminal demand (cables, construction). Supply appears tight in May but imports are beginning to flow, LME inventories rose, and inventories visible support pessimism. Prices likely trend lower. | SMM reports weekly spot copper concentrate TCs eased to around $79/mt as trading was slack and smelters drew down in-plant inventories amid resuming output at Xiangguang and Fangyuan. Las Bambas remains suspended for community disputes, reducing exports, but high port inventories cap further TC downside, keeping copper impact limited. | Arc Minerals agreed with an Anglo American subsidiary to form a copper-cobalt JV for a project in Zambia. Anglo plans to fund exploration and pay cash totaling up to $88.5 million while retaining up to 70% ownership. The deal is subject to due diligence and definitive agreements, implying limited immediate supply impact. | SMM reports China’s major-market copper inventories rose 7,900 mt WoW to 126,800 mt as arrivals increased over the weekend. Guangdong and Shanghai saw the largest jumps, attributed to higher smelter deliveries and delayed unloading due to weather/logistics. SMM expects inventories may keep rising this week with more imports and stable consumption. | OZ Minerals agreed to earn up to a 75% stake in Resolution’s Benmara copper project in Australia’s Northern Territory via staged exploration spending (initial $1m over two years, then $3m over three years for 51%). A JV would follow if spending targets are met, but no immediate production changes are cited. | Philippines authorities removed the final regulatory hurdle blocking the stalled Tampakan copper-gold project. The decision improves the likelihood the project can move forward, shifting expectations toward eventual copper supply. While production start dates are not immediate, the news is supportive for copper sentiment in the coming weeks. | Canadian North Resources reports re-sampling and re-assaying results from historic drill cores at its Ferguson Lake project, confirming high-grade copper-nickel massive sulfides and wide low-sulfide PGM mineralization. The findings support resource-estimation confidence, but do not indicate near-term supply changes, implying limited immediate impact on copper and nickel prices. | A U.S. appeals court upheld a ruling invalidating a Forest Service approval for Hudbay’s proposed Rosemont open-pit copper mine in Arizona. The decision halts the mine’s permitting pathway, raising uncertainty over future copper supply and potentially supporting nearby copper price expectations. | Nevada Copper reported progress at its Pumpkin Hollow project, including ramping underground hoisting/production, paste plant commissioning with expected throughput by end-June, and higher copper concentrate output in Q1 2022. The prefeasibility study for the permitted open pit project is advancing. Overall, near-term copper production expectations improve.

LME copper inventories climbed for over two months from a 16-year low to a seven-month high of 177,000 mt, then eased slightly to 176,550 mt by May 16. SHFE copper inventories rose 5.89% week-on-week to 55,372 mt, a two-month low, suggesting improving supply levels. | SMM reports the INE bonded copper 2207 contract rose overnight, closing at 63,280 yuan/mt (+0.80%). Copper strength was linked to a crude oil rebound and concerns about policy-driven growth risks as US Treasury yields fell. Prices are expected to trade around 63,300–63,600 yuan/ton today. | SMM reports Yangshan copper premiums at $62–84/mt, averaging $73/mt, slightly lower day-on-day, with import losses near 600 yuan/mt on the 2206 contract. A closed import window and low price ratio reduced offers and inquiries, and transactions were limited as market participants wait for logistics to resume in June. | EnGold Mines reported assays from deep drilling at its Lac La Hache property in BC, including hole AN22-05 intersecting 655m of 0.14% CuEq with chalcopyrite and bornite, plus indications of copper-bearing mineralization at depth in another hole. Results are exploration-focused with assays pending elsewhere. | Hudbay Minerals began site work at its Copper World and Rosemont copper projects in Arizona after recent legal setbacks, including surrendering a Clean Water Act permit. Opponents allege it is illegal and await further court decisions. Hudbay says it plans to extract copper, supporting potential near-term supply expectations.

Lion Copper and Gold announced final Stage 1 agreement with Rio Tinto, including $3.75m funding for permitting, studies, metallurgical testing, and a planned 6,500-ft drill program at its MacArthur and Yerington copper deposits. The company also started environmental permitting with the U.S. Bureau of Land Management. | Lion Copper and Gold announced a final Stage 1 work program agreement with Rio Tinto for its Mason Valley MacArthur and Yerington projects. Rio Tinto will fund US$3.75 million for permitting, studies, metallurgical testing, and a 6,500-ft drill program, supporting progress toward copper production. | SMM reports the INE bonded copper 2207 contract rose overnight, closing at 63,420 yuan/mt (+130). It links the rebound to improved Shanghai pandemic conditions boosting market confidence and to expectations of additional government policies to stimulate growth. Trading saw modest volume and slightly lower open interest. | Shanghai bonded zone imported copper trading was subdued as the import profit window closed. Yangshan premiums were below prior levels (under warrants $62-84/mt; under B/L $49-70/mt) and import losses were about 500 yuan/mt vs SHFE 2206. With logistics expected to recover, sellers held offers, while buyers avoided deals. | OZ Minerals signed a terms sheet to option Havilah’s Kalkaroo copper project in South Australia for A$205m over 18 months, including required drilling or a shortfall payment. The deal includes copper price-linked contingent payments and a copper exploration alliance, supporting near-term copper market sentiment. | Copper fell 1.1% as markets renewed worries about global slowdown after hawkish Fed signals and weak China data tied to Shanghai COVID lockdown impacts. The stronger dollar pressured dollar-priced metals. LME aluminum and nickel also declined, reflecting broad risk-off sentiment toward industrial metal demand. | Talon Metals announced additional infill drilling results at its Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project in Minnesota, including a record 23.44 m massive sulphide intercept of nickel and copper (assays pending). The company plans to complete infill drilling for a pre-feasibility study and references a Tesla nickel supply agreement. | Two men were arrested for allegedly stealing copper wire from Roy O. Martin Plywood Plant in Louisiana. Supervisors found trespassing at an area where copper wiring and metal items are stored; police seized about $750 worth of copper wire and equipment used to strip it. The theft investigation continues.

The article notes that copper premiums in Asia and Europe have increased, while the US market is flat amid quiet conditions. It also describes delays and corrections to Fastmarkets’ European low-carbon aluminium differential and certain aluminium billet premium assessments due to procedural errors, implying data housekeeping rather than a clear new supply-demand shock. | Chakana Copper Corp. announced a non-brokered private placement to fund exploration and development of its Soledad copper-gold-silver project in Peru, plus working capital. It also notes an inferred resource including 130 million pounds of copper. The financing is unlikely to materially affect near-term copper supply.

SMM reports that in early Shanghai trading, SHFE nickel rose 5.36%, while SHFE aluminium gained 1.69% and SHFE copper rose 0.66%. The broader move coincided with a firmer US dollar, expanding Fed balance sheet, hawkish Fed comments, and crude oil weakness tied to growth concerns. | SMM data shows copper inventories in major Chinese markets fell 6,900 mt to 119,900 mt week-on-week, with declines in Guangdong and Tianjin outweighing slight rises in Shanghai and Zhejiang. Supply was constrained by logistics/rail issues, though next week imported arrivals may increase. | SMM reports Yangshan copper premiums of $69–85/mt under warrants, averaging $77/mt, down $1 from the prior day. Import losses widened as LME copper inventories fell and carrying fees rose. The market remains sluggish, despite warrant traders’ willingness to ship; no transactions were reported. | Copper prices inched higher as China cut mortgage rates by more than expected and continued progress on easing COVID lockdowns, boosting optimism for a demand rebound. LME three-month copper rose slightly and was on track for a weekly gain, reversing earlier declines tied to fears over China’s growth and global recession.

Japan’s Electric Wire and Cable Makers’ Association data show April sales of copper wire and cables (domestic plus exports) fell 3.1% year-on-year to 52,100 mt. The drop signals softer demand for copper inputs in the short term, which can weigh on copper price expectations over the next few weeks. | Koba Resources has started an induced polarisation survey at its Blackpine cobalt-copper project to generate targets for maiden drilling. The soil and historical drilling results show cobalt-copper anomalism and high-grade intersections. The company also plans drilling at its Colson project. No production or supply impact is specified. | SMM reports China’s major market copper inventories fell 10,100 mt on the week to 109,800 mt, with declines in Shanghai and Guangdong attributed to reduced imported arrivals and stronger downstream pickup, while Guangdong fell on transport disruptions. Inventories are expected to drop slightly further as imports and domestic supply improve. | Copper prices climbed to a more than two-week high on a weaker U.S. dollar and improving China demand expectations as lockdown easing and mortgage rate cuts were seen supporting property activity. Shanghai copper also rose amid falling SHFE inventories. LME aluminium gained as well, suggesting broader industrial-metals uplift. | SMM reports Yangshan copper premiums at about $70–83/mt, averaging ~$77/mt, with slight softness versus the prior day. Import losses estimated around 550 yuan/mt over the 2206 copper contract suppressed buying interest, keeping premiums stable but with some downward potential amid backwardation. | The piece signals Chile will take measures to offset a glacier ban that would constrain operations at large copper mines. Any near-term limits on mine access, production, or permitting can raise concerns about reduced copper supply, shifting market expectations toward tighter availability and price pressure on copper.

LME copper rose to its highest level in nearly two weeks, closing up 1.17%, while SHFE also gained. Support came from a weaker US dollar, improving consumption expectations, tighter Chinese cathode spot supply, and declining LME/SHFE and regional inventories. Spot premiums are expected to stay firm. | Trinidad and Tobago’s TSTT faces rising theft and vandalism of copper cables, which the minister says intensified alongside higher international copper prices. TSTT plans to shift all remaining customers from its copper network to wireless/fibre by end-August, having moved about 100,000 so far, and begins removing disused copper lines. | Global Battery Metals was granted four new surface and mineral leases in Michigan, expanding its Sawyer Camp nickel-copper exploration area. The company plans electromagnetic surveys to guide drilling targeting mafic-ultramafic intrusions potentially hosting nickel and copper mineralization. No production or reserve update is provided. | Benjamin Hill Mining reported initial diamond-drilling results from its Sonora Copper Zone in Mexico, including multiple copper-bearing intercepts (e.g., up to 1.2% Cu over 6m and 0.55% Cu over 26.54m) confirming continuity of mineralized veins. The company expects further assays to support copper potential. | Gauteng authorities say rampant infrastructure theft is rising as demand for copper pushes prices higher. Copper is allegedly smelted, mixed with legitimate supply, and exported with manipulated customs declarations.
