The city is considering drones and micro-dot tracing to curb theft amid rolling blackouts. | Hudbay said a U.S. District Court ruled in its favor over the Copper World project in Arizona, dismissing a preliminary injunction request and clarifying that the Army Corps of Engineers has no obligation to include Copper World in Rosemont’s NEPA review. Hudbay continues early works, drilling, and expects a PEA in Q2 2022.

Gold Fields’ acquisition of units of Chakana Copper Corp suggests increased ownership and development focus on copper assets. While deal-specific production timing is not provided, the transaction can improve market expectations for copper supply prospects and support copper prices over the coming days to weeks. | A technical report on Comex copper argues for a longer-term bullish bias, citing shallow consolidation and improving chart structure. It highlights a bullish trigger above ~$5.06 and confirmation above $5.17, with expectations for a breakout toward $7.00 and potentially beyond, framing the move as a constructive setup. | Nine Mile Metals announced completion of its Phase 1 drill program at the Nine Mile Brook VMS project, reporting visual high-grade VMS mineralization in drill core with copper (chalcopyrite) plus lead/zinc. The company plans BHEM surveys and ALS-certified assays shortly; no confirmed production or supply impact is stated.

The Biden administration announced efforts aimed at preventing the Pebble copper and gold project from being built. If the mine is blocked, markets may price in tighter long-run copper supply expectations, which typically turns sentiment bearish for copper over the coming days to weeks. | SMM reports that most INE bonded copper 2207 trading trended downward on May 26, finishing at 63,380 yuan/mt. Macro concerns about inflation and interest-rate hikes hurting economic growth kept copper under pressure. The INE after-tax equivalent versus SHFE 2207 widened, with the spread expanding to about 539 yuan/mt. | Camino Minerals says core drilling has started on newly permitted Lourdes-Condori copper zones at its Los Chapitos project in Peru. The company reports near-surface high-grade copper targets, visible copper oxides in road cuts, and additional access work for other copper prospects, potentially supporting future resource delineation and discoveries. | A federal court ruled that Hudbay Minerals can continue stream filling for its Copper World copper project because it surrendered its Clean Water Act permit, removing Army Corps regulatory authority over the permit itself. Conservation groups and tribes may still sue Hudbay directly for Clean Water Act violations, but the ruling shifts enforcement burdens. | The piece reports that Globex’s NewMetal royalty property intersected high-grade copper in a “deep zone.” The information is an exploration milestone that may modestly improve perceptions of future copper resources and supply potential, offering slight near-term support to copper prices, though it is not yet production data.

Overnight, LME and SHFE copper futures rose modestly, with spot cathode premiums in China improving. The weaker US dollar (after Fed minutes) and supportive infrastructure-growth expectations supported risk assets. Falling LME/SHFE inventories and tight China supply further limited downside, keeping near-term copper bullish. | SMM says copper inventories in major Chinese markets fell 2,200 mt to 107,600 mt by May 27, with a 12,300 mt drop versus last Friday. The decline follows lower supply and higher demand, though Shanghai rose due to more imports and logistics delays. Next week’s arrivals may lift inventories slightly. | Copper rose after a three-day slump as the dollar weakened, while worries about China’s economic slowdown persisted. Nickel on the Shanghai Futures Exchange climbed up to 5% after three days of declines. Separately, a strike at Rusal’s Guinea bauxite mine raised supply disruption concerns for aluminium-linked markets. | Copper rose after a three-day slump, supported by a weaker U.S. dollar, but the article says copper was still set for weekly losses due to worries about China’s demand amid slowing growth. It also references an aluminium-related Rusal mine strike and low Chinese nickel inventories lifting nickel prices. | SMM reports Yangshan copper premiums slipping amid oversupply and import losses. Premiums are weaker under warrants and bill-of-lading ahead of June QP, with sluggish trading and minimal buyer inquiries. The SHFE/LME ratio decline and falling SHFE copper relative pricing likely weigh on near-term copper sentiment. | Copper rebounded after three sessions of losses as a weaker U.S. dollar and hopes of China easing COVID curbs boosted demand expectations. China’s copper futures also rose. The piece also cites an illegal strike at Rusal’s Guinea bauxite mine, supporting aluminium, while aluminium and nickel prices gained on the day. | Cordoba Minerals reported diamond drilling results from its 100%-owned San Matias project in Colombia, including copper-bearing intervals at the Alacran deposit and related targets. Intercepts showed grades consistent with the resource model, but no clear evidence of a deeper porphyry source was found. No immediate market supply disruption was indicated. | C3 Metals says it will commence a 5,000-metre drilling program at its Bellas Gate copper-gold porphyry project in Jamaica, starting at the Epidote Ridge target and then testing additional porphyry and epithermal prospects. The release is exploration-focused with historic copper-gold grades but no immediate supply impact. | C3 Metals says it will start a 5,000-metre drilling program at its Bellas Gate copper-gold porphyry targets in Jamaica, beginning with the Epidote Ridge area and then moving to other porphyry and epithermal targets. The release is exploration focused and does not indicate immediate changes to copper supply or demand. | A federal judge rejected a request to block Hudbay Minerals from preparing a planned new copper mine site near Tucson. Opponents sued on environmental grounds, but the court cited Hudbay’s surrender of a suspended Clean Water Act permit for the nearby Rosemont Mine as removing the legal basis. Copper supply timeline risk eased.

SMM expects copper prices to be supported short term. Weak US/Europe growth backdrop is balanced by China stabilization measures. Smelter restarts lift output slightly, while copper scrap supply remains tight despite improved selling willingness. Demand looks firmer with State Grid orders and lower wire/cable inventories, keeping spot premiums stable. | SMM says Yangshan copper premiums rose to around $68–83/mt under warrants, with spot imports in China shifting to profit-making levels. Importers raised premiums for small orders, but expected further increases are constrained by poor demand and oversupply, with limited inquiries and no bill-of-lading transactions. | SMM says copper inventories across major Chinese markets fell by 300 mt to 107,300 mt over the weekend. Guangdong dropped rapidly, while others saw smaller moves. With logistics recovery, stranded imported copper is being shipped, but near-term arrivals may not be enough, so inventories could keep declining. | Los Andes Copper reported additional copper intercepts at its Vizcachitas copper project in Chile. News of new mineralization can improve perceptions of resource potential and future output, typically providing modest near-term support to copper prices through improved supply expectations and investor sentiment. | Los Andes Copper announced additional high-grade drilling intercepts at its Vizcachitas copper-molybdenum project in Chile, including long copper-equivalent intervals. Management says results expand a Tier 1 advanced copper resource and support progress on a pre-feasibility study amid expectations of limited development projects and a looming supply deficit. | The piece argues the copper supply chain is fragmented, with concentrated mining (e.g., Peru) and concentrated processing/smelting (notably China). It cites disruptions like strikes at Las Bambas and recent import declines, and warns of a projected copper supply shortfall versus demand, supporting bullish price expectations. | A federal judge denied Native American tribes’ request to halt Hudbay Minerals’ preparations for a planned copper mine in Arizona. The ruling dismissed the lawsuit after Hudbay surrendered a previously suspended Clean Water Act permit for the Rosemont project. Hudbay’s effort pivots to the Copper World project on private land.

LME copper held near a three-week high as China demand optimism rose. Better Chinese factory data and easing COVID-19 lockdowns, including Shanghai reopening, improved near-term consumption expectations. The U.S. dollar’s limited strength provided support, while copper was steady near $9,537/tonne and other metals were mixed to slightly higher. | SMM reports the INE bonded copper 2208 contract trended higher on May 31, closing up 0.30% at 64,430 yuan/mt. It attributes the move to a continued decline in the US dollar index and expectations around the next Fed rate-hike and balance-sheet shrink timeline. | Cordoba Minerals begins feasibility-study technical work and a 40,000-metre infill drilling program at its San Matias copper-gold-silver project in Colombia. The company cites prior PFS economics using US$3.60/lb copper and notes the current spot copper price, but provides no new supply or demand changes that would move prices. Nickel/aluminium not mentioned. | Peru protests disrupted copper mining for nearly two months, cutting production and hurting revenues for Southern Copper. The article notes rising demand drivers (EVs, infrastructure) while supply remains constrained, implying potential tightening. It also highlights a concurrent increase in copper prices during the quarter. | A South African court sentenced a PRASA worker to 12 years for stealing copper cables worth R1m (about 192kg) from a railway line. Prosecutors said cable theft harms service delivery and stressed renewed enforcement, but the incident appears localized with no clear broader effect on copper supply or pricing.

Sama reports four holes at the Grata prospect in Ivory Coast returning long, thick intersections of nickel-copper sulphide mineralization, including GR-08 (297.6m) and GR-11 (212m). Grades include ~0.2–0.3% Ni and ~0.2–0.3% Cu. Assays for additional holes remain pending, with no near-term production impact stated. | Seahawk-like news highlights new drilling at the Grata prospect in Ivory Coast, with multiple holes intersecting long widths of Ni-Cu rich sulphide mineralization (notably high Cu/Ni in GR-06 zones) and additional narrow stringers. Results for further holes are pending; the company aims to fast-track resource definition. | SMM reports Yangshan copper premiums under warrants were steady at $69–83/mt, averaging $76/mt, with no transactions yet. Bill-of-lading premiums averaged $71/mt. Import losses against the SHFE 2206 contract persisted, while buying interest remained weak due to poor demand and traders’ caution over follow-up policies. | The report notes copper premium movements: premiums slid in China but rose in Italy and the Netherlands. It also contains Fastmarkets methodology/assessment publication timing and procedural-error corrections for aluminium differentials and related price assessments. Overall, the only clearly supported metal price signal is softer copper premiums in China. | Chile’s National Bureau of Statistics reported that copper output fell 9.8% YoY in April to 421,742 mt. The article notes Chile’s production has been below last year’s levels due to factors including lower ore grades, insufficient water supply, and temporary operating problems. | Faraday Copper Corp. selected consultants to update the Mineral Resource Estimate and complete a mine-to-mill assessment for its Copper Creek porphyry copper project in Arizona. The company expanded drilling, but no assay results or production decisions were provided. The work will feed a planned PEA timeline into 2023. | Chile’s environmental regulator (SMA) initiated sanction proceedings against Antofagasta Minerals’ Los Pelambres copper mine for deficiencies in tailings management and incident communication protocols. While there were no air- or water-quality standard breaches, serious sanctions could include fines up to about $9 million or even permit loss/closure.

London copper prices climbed to the highest level in over a month as China began relaxing COVID-19 lockdowns and sentiment improved ahead of possible U.S. tariff easing. The piece links the move to expectations of a faster demand recovery, alongside a broader positive tone in industrial metals. | SMM reports sluggish copper concentrate trading and stable treatment charges, but market focus is on supply risk: Peru’s Las Bambas copper mine has been suspended for over a month with renewed conflict, and authorities extended a state of emergency. Negotiations and concentrate availability keep pricing coefficients steady, with tighter supply concerns supportive for copper. | SMM reports copper prices rebounded last week as China rolled out economy-stabilizing policies, firms resumed work, and downstream wire/cable and cathode rod demand improved with grid project start-ups and restocking. Supply tightness is supported by Las Bambas suspension and delayed Daye output, despite some regional flows pressuring spots. | Rio Tinto’s subsidiary Kennecott Exploration has started farm-in drilling at Alderan Resources’ Frisco copper project in Utah, targeting porphyry and high-grade copper at the Copper Gulch prospect. Prior drilling and historical assays show substantial copper grades, strengthening near-term optimism for copper discovery and development. | MMG’s Las Bambas copper mine in Peru has been suspended for over 40 days due to indigenous community protests, with about 3,000 contractor workers laid off. Meanwhile, treatment charges for imported copper concentrate jumped 166% to $78.66/mt by June 2, reflecting tightening concentrate availability and bullish copper sentiment. | SMM said copper inventories in major Chinese markets fell 1,500 mt to 108,900 mt as of June 6, with most regions declining after the Dragon Boat Festival. Shanghai and Guangdong dropped, while Jiangsu and Tianjin rose slightly. Outlook: supply should keep rising, but weak consumption may rebuild inventories. | Western Copper and Gold announced its 2022 exploration and drilling program at the Casino copper-gold project in Yukon. The company plans to drill and scan core to evaluate a deep MT low-resistivity copper-gold anomaly and conduct geotechnical/hydrological work for project facilities. No production impacts or copper price drivers are specified. | Ivanhoe Mines says Kamoa-Kakula set a record May output of 29,800 tonnes of copper in concentrate, despite brief planned maintenance. Phase 2 ramp-up and commissioning of a filter press support higher throughput and keep 2022 guidance toward the upper end, reinforcing near-term copper supply expectations. | The news reports that Camino has completed three drillholes at its Los Chapitos copper project in Peru. Positive drilling progress can modestly lift sentiment around the project’s potential size and development outlook, which may support copper price expectations in the near term. No direct details on nickel or aluminium are provided. | Trinidad and Tobago’s Scrap Iron Dealers Association urged the Ministry of Trade to shut down the copper export industry, arguing it is the only way to stop theft of copper wires and fixtures. The group claims licensed exporters are limited and wants police to inspect export containers, potentially reducing copper export flows. | Chile’s April copper output fell 8.9% to about 420,000 tonnes versus April 2021, with declines attributed to lower refining rates and worse ore grades. Cochilco also flagged tighter concentrate supply as Chinese smelter maintenance nears end, pressuring copper concentrate TC/RC charges and refining expectations. | Infinitum Copper invites investors to visit its PDAC 2022 booth and describes its copper exploration assets, including the La Adelita copper project in Mexico and the Hot Breccia property in Arizona. The piece is promotional and does not report new discoveries, production changes, or supply-demand shocks affecting copper prices. | Scrap dealers in Trinidad and Tobago are calling for a two-to-three month suspension of copper exports until suspected theft networks are identified. The proposal includes stricter export licensing, container checks at the port, and tighter scrapyard controls on purchase of scrap, aiming to reduce the market that enables theft. | Infinity Stone Ventures begins a reconnaissance exploration program at its Zen-Whoberi copper-cobalt-(plus platinum/palladium) project in Quebec. The work aims to confirm historical results, including reported copper intercepts, before potential drill work later in 2022. The release does not suggest immediate production effects for metals markets.

SMM says China’s copper cathode output fell in May because of intensive smelter maintenance, with delayed maintenance and some raw-material tightness in the spot market. Blister copper supply remained tight, imports were disrupted, and blister TCs kept declining. June output is forecast higher but most smelters stay in maintenance. | Nevada Copper said geotechnical issues at its Pumpkin Hollow underground mine have delayed access to East South stopes, reducing expected concentrate production in Q2 2022 and pushing most stope ore production to August. It also details progress in East North development and seeks additional funding. | SMM reports INE bonded copper 2208 trending downward, closing at 64,980 yuan/mt (down 170 yuan/mt). The drop is attributed to higher US bond yields from inflation worries and a slight rebound in the US dollar index, which weighed on copper prices and encouraged shorts. | Bell Copper announced full assay results from drillhole BS-3 at its Big Sandy porphyry copper project in Arizona, including 200 meters grading 0.42% copper and metallurgical scoping work for chalcocite recovery. The company states future drilling will test supergene enrichment; no resource is yet defined. | Granite Creek Copper announced it is starting a 2022 Induced Polarization (IP) survey at its Carmacks Cu-Au-Ag project in Yukon, Canada, to improve targeting of deeper and offset mineralization. The release focuses on exploration planning and resource/PEA work; it does not indicate production or supply changes. | Goldman Sachs metals strategist Nick Snowdon warns that predictions of copper supply constraints could prove correct, potentially driving copper to $15,000/ton. The outlook rests on sharply rising demand over the next decade from electric vehicles, power and renewable projects, while supply remains insufficient to meet that growth. | Copper Fox reported interim financial results and outlined continued exploration work across its copper projects, including metallurgical drilling updates and planned 2022 field programs. Management expects further results that could support future resource estimates, but no production or supply commitments were changed, implying limited near-term impact on copper pricing.

LME copper ended lower on June 7, falling 0.47% to $9,699/mt, as a stronger US dollar reinforced concerns about China’s demand. While LME copper inventories dropped by 20,200 mt to a multiweek low, aluminium inventories fell to a 21-year low, yet aluminium futures still slipped. | KCM’s provisional liquidator said it will seek equity investors to fund Zambia’s Konkola Deep Mining Project and announced the resumption of open-pit mining. KCM also plans $17 million in annual maintenance shutdowns at the Nchanga copper smelter (311,000 mt capacity) alongside measures to expand production at other operations. | Cyprium Metals said initial reverse-circulation drilling at its Maroochydore copper-cobalt deposit in Western Australia extended near-surface mineralisation to 3,000m. Several intervals reported significant copper grades (up to 1.92% Cu) alongside cobalt, supporting prospects and potential future development, mildly bullish for copper sentiment. | A Chinese firm is demanding revisions to its contract for the Aynak Logar copper mining project near Kabul, Afghanistan. The Logar deposit is described as the world’s second-largest copper deposit. The contract dispute could delay development, weighing on expectations for future copper supply. | Chile’s central bank said copper exports in May totaled $3.76 billion, down 18.3% year on year. Despite the decline, Chile posted a $851 million trade surplus. Overall exports rose and imports increased as well, but the YoY fall in copper export receipts is a negative copper supply-demand signal. | CanAlaska, via partner Metal Energy, has started a 10,000-metre phase two drill program at the Manibridge nickel project in Manitoba, targeting nickel-copper sulphide mineralization near a past-producing mine. The release cites prior drilling success and notes a strengthening nickel market, but no assays or production changes are provided. | Bell Copper reports shareholder approval of a shareholder rights plan and a new 2022 fixed stock option plan, including granting 10 million incentive stock options. The company is focused on exploring copper projects in Arizona, but the release is mainly governance/compensation with no direct change to copper production or trade flows. | CanAlaska’s partner has started a 10,000-metre phase-two drilling program at the Manibridge high-grade nickel project in Manitoba, targeting extensions near a past-producing mine. The program aims to define nickel-copper sulphide mineralization; earlier drilling intersected nickel mineralization and copper/nickel were confirmed with assays pending. | Fourteen people were killed in an armed dispute between small-scale miners in southern Peru. Reuters notes the violence follows earlier unrest, including a fire allegedly set by wildcat copper miners at Southern Copper Corp’s Los Chancas project, raising risks of disruption to copper supply.

London copper prices eased 0.4% ahead of the ECB meeting, with analysts citing muted trading until the policy outcome. Better-than-expected Chinese economic data and falling LME inventories (down 3,875 tonnes) supported hopes for a demand recovery, but the near-term direction reported was lower. | EMX Royalty will receive about US$2.7 million in Q1 royalty distributions from Chile’s Caserones copper-molybdenum mine, based on copper output and NSR terms. The company attributes higher payments to strong copper prices and robust production throughput, reinforcing bullish copper expectations. | Luminex Resources announced expedited drilling at the El Hito copper-molybdenum porphyry target in Ecuador, citing a newly identified 320-metre zone where rock chips mostly exceed 0.4% copper. A 1,500-metre drill program is expected to start next week, while assays are pending from other holes. | Rockridge Resources says it will start a fully funded drill program at its high-grade Knife Lake copper project in Saskatchewan. While it does not change production immediately, the announcement can lift expectations for future resource definition and copper supply prospects, providing modest bullish sentiment for copper prices. | Fitch Solutions expects miners to increase investments in copper and nickel projects because demand is rising from renewable energy, batteries, and electric vehicles. While companies focus more on developing existing assets than launching new ones, the outlook supports constructive near-term sentiment for copper and nickel supply-demand fundamentals. | Battery Mineral Resources reports high-grade drill results at its Dalmacia target within the Punitaqui copper project in Chile, including multiple copper intercepts above 1.5% and up to 2.5%. The company links results to planned mine resumption in late 2022 and potential increased confidence in future copper supply from the site. | Eagle Plains and partner Rockridge plan to start a fully funded diamond drill program in mid-July at the Knife Lake copper VMS project in Saskatchewan, targeting VTEM anomalies and down-dip extensions. Prior drilling reported high-grade copper intervals, but the news is exploratory rather than production or supply-related.

Peruvian indigenous communities agreed on June 9 to lift protests against MMG’s Las Bambas copper mine after a shutdown lasting over 50 days. The communities have approved a 30-day truce and reopening negotiations, easing disruption risk. With Las Bambas among the top copper producers, copper supply expectations should improve. | SMM says Yangshan copper premiums under warrants were $45–60/mt, stabilizing after stopping their fall as domestic premiums rose and import losses narrowed. Importers slowed dumping and buyers preferred domestic copper. Traders showed weak willingness for only small orders, limiting sharp premium upside. | Congo’s state mining firm Gecamines and China Moly dispute over Tenke Fungurume (TFM) led to a temporary court-appointed administrator taking charge. China Moly denies any management change and says production continues normally. The enforced ruling and royalty/reserve concerns create copper risk, though impact seems limited short term. | Castillo Copper outlined strategic updates across its Australian and Zambian projects, emphasizing drilling campaigns and efforts to formalize off-take and processing paths for deposits including Big One and Cangai. The company is also advancing copper targets in Zambia via potential joint-venture funding and continued exploration. | Kodiak Copper Corp. provided an update on its fully funded 2022 exploration program at the 100% owned MPD copper-gold porphyry project in southern British Columbia. The company restarted drilling in March, added a second rig, completed 11 holes, and plans further drilling and 3D IP/soil geochemistry, with assays expected in late June. | Kodiak Copper Corp. provided an update on its fully funded 2022 exploration at the MPD copper-gold porphyry project in British Columbia. The company reported drilling progress at the Gate Zone, a new 3D IP survey underway, and planned assay results in late June, with further drilling across multiple targets. | MMG plans to restart copper concentrate production at Las Bambas in Peru. The restart should lift near-term concentrate availability and reduce outage risk, which can improve expectations for copper supply/demand tightness over the coming days to weeks. | China Moly denies the DRC has taken control of its Tenke Fungurume copper-cobalt mine. Congo authorities said a court-appointed administrator assumed charge amid a shareholder dispute tied to suspected reserve underreporting and royalty payments. China Moly says management and production continue normally as talks proceed.

LME aluminium slid to a six-month low as investors feared weaker Chinese demand from COVID lockdowns, higher inflation and interest-rate hikes, and a stronger U.S. dollar. Inventories are at low levels, but macro-driven demand concerns outweighed support. Copper and nickel also declined during the sell-off. | Thesis Gold reported five-hole assay results from the Bingo Zone at its Ranch Gold Project in British Columbia, including intervals with gold and copper mineralization (up to 0.45% Cu). While this indicates potential copper-bearing high-sulphidation/porphyry-related system at depth, it does not signal near-term production supply changes. | Copper Fox provided an update on its Eaglehead polymetallic porphyry copper project, including preparations for a 2022 drilling program in early July pending Notice of Work approval and permits. The work focuses on geological model verification and testing continuity of porphyry mineralization between zones, with no results or supply changes reported. | Ecuador and Chile resumed talks over the stalled Llurimagua copper-molybdenum project after Ecuador’s National Mining Company won a suspension of two arbitration processes from Codelco. The project remains in advanced exploration and faces environmental opposition, making any near-term copper supply effect uncertain within days to weeks. | Hot Chili Limited said diamond drilling at Chile’s Valentina deposit extended the high-grade copper zone by 120m, including a 17m visual estimate of 2–3% Cu from 22m and 8m at 4–5% Cu from 28m, with assay results pending. Additional holes and PFS/port workstreams are progressing. | Avino Silver & Gold Mines announced deep drill results from its ET area in Mexico, reporting significant copper-bearing intercepts alongside silver. The company says drilling confirms downdip continuity of the vein to at least 290 metres below Level 17 and suggests higher copper mineralization at depth. | Seabridge Gold says drilling will start at its 100%-owned Iskut project in British Columbia, testing gold-copper targets at Bronson Slope and Quartz Rise, supported by magnetotelluric surveys. The release describes copper-bearing mineralization in resource targets but does not indicate production or near-term supply changes. | Anteros Metals updates that Phase One VTEM airborne electromagnetic surveying is underway at its Hopedale nickel project in Labrador, aiming to detect deep conductivity anomalies tied to nickel mineralization and possibly Ni-Cu-Co sulphides. The company is awaiting results to guide future surface work and potential drilling, with no production impacts stated. | Osisko Metals announced it has filed a technical report related to its Gaspé copper project and provided an update on a previously announced private placement. Such corporate/technical milestones can influence expectations for future copper resources, though the provided content lacks details on production timelines.

LME aluminium fell to a six-month low on June 13 amid concern over possible additional China lockdowns and a sell-off linked to rising inflation and interest rates. Demand for industrial metals is expected to weaken. The piece also notes falling LME copper prices, while aluminium inventories are at a multi-year low. | Three Valley Copper’s 95.1% Chilean subsidiary Minera Tres Valles (MTV) commenced Chilean judicial restructuring to seek creditor protection. The filing is expected to trigger an immediate demobilization and a temporary halt in third-party ore processing, with curtailed operations and cash extending only through August 2022, increasing near-term copper supply risk. | Argentina will introduce a voluntary, optional tax regime for new copper investments that can replace the current 4.5% export duty with a variable rate between 0% and 8% tied to the copper price. The move aims to stimulate production and exports while improving fiscal and foreign-currency outcomes. | China’s copper concentrate imports rose to 2.189 million mt in May, up 16.2% month-on-month and 12.5% year-on-year, with January–May imports hitting a record. April fell due to the Xiangguang shutdown and weaker purchasing. Peru restart and limited port impacts helped May recover. | SMM says Fed and ECB rate-hike expectations strengthened the US dollar and weighed on base metals, limiting copper’s upside. China’s stimulus and improving cathode output, plus a large LME inventory drop, support copper prices. However, weaker demand signals (PMIs, soft orders) suggest gains may be capped into June.

Argentina has launched a flexible export tax regime for copper exports. By altering exporter incentives and potentially reducing physical shipments abroad, the policy can affect near-term global supply expectations for copper and may therefore exert downward pressure on copper prices over the coming days to weeks. | ZCCM Investments Holdings engaged Rothschild & Co to assist with a strategic review aimed at ensuring sustainability and optimal operation of Mopani Copper Mines. The review is expected to conclude in about six months (extendable). No immediate production or supply change is stated, limiting short-term copper price pressure. | Fabled Copper Corp. announced additional 2021 surface fieldwork results from its Muskwa Copper Project, detailing chip sampling along the Book 6 vein target. Samples reported multiple intervals with elevated copper, including up to 7.73% Cu in a chip sample. The release is exploratory and does not signal immediate production. | Salina police are investigating the theft of over $30,000 worth of copper wire from a grain elevator. Authorities say scrap metal thefts aren’t rising locally, noting copper theft slowed when prices fell. The article quotes local scrap prices and emphasizes the incident rather than any broader copper supply disruption. | Doré Copper Mining filed an NI 43-101 technical report for a preliminary economic assessment of its Chibougamau hub-and-spoke complex in Quebec. The PEA covers multiple copper projects feeding the Copper Rand mill, supporting plans for subsequent feasibility work and permitting. It is mainly a forward-looking copper development signal. | Doré Copper Mining Corp. filed an NI 43-101 technical report for its Chibougamau hub-and-spoke complex (Corner Bay, Devlin, Joe Mann) feeding its Copper Rand mill. The release reiterates a PEA and plans for feasibility and permits, but provides no immediate operational or supply change.

SMM said Chinese copper inventories across major markets fell to 109,900 mt on June 17, down 5,800 mt from Monday. Shanghai and Jiangsu declined sharply as copper prices dropped and downstream firms restocked with pandemic-related consumption recovery. Next week’s arrivals may rise, but further inventory falls are possible if demand improves. | Nornickel and the Murmansk Region government signed a memorandum to support local investment and “Big Copper,” a green roast-leach-electrowin copper production facility in Monchegorsk. The project includes high sulphur recovery and zero-discharge water management, plus capacity reconfiguration at Kola MMC to raise non-ferrous output. | SMM data estimates global copper mines will add up to 164,000 mt of new copper capacity in 2022, alongside further expansion lifting total new concentrate output to about 1.023 million mt. Major contributors include projects in Chile, Peru, Russia, Indonesia and Zambia, signaling rising supply expectations. | Copper prices edged up as traders priced in strike risk at Chile’s Codelco, the world’s largest copper producer, alongside slightly better China activity data. LME copper rose 0.4% on the day, while aluminium weakened on a firmer dollar and softer China prospects; nickel rose modestly.

Culpeo Minerals reports high-grade copper drilling results at its Lana Corina project in Chile, including a new breccia-hosted copper zone aligned with a major magnetic target and multiple high-priority intercepts. Drilling continues to test near-surface breccia pipes and potential to expand copper and molybdenum mineralisation. | Copper prices fell last week as tighter global liquidity, aggressive rate expectations, and a stronger US dollar pressured industrial products. Although Las Bambas resumed production and China’s smelters restarted, weak demand, low processor operating rates, higher overseas inflows, and rising domestic output suppressed spot premiums. Fundamentals remain bearish and sustainability of consumption is key. | SMM says copper inventory in major Chinese markets fell 6,300 mt over the weekend to 103,600 mt, and is far below last year. The drop is linked to lower prices last week and downstream restocking, plus smelters shipping output directly. Spot trading is cautious, and further restocking hinges on demand. | Vedanta invited expressions of interest to sell its Tuticorin Sterlite Copper smelter, closed since 2018 after a Tamil Nadu injunction. The plant produces a large share of India’s copper and sulfuric acid, and bids are sought by July 4. Any path to restart could support refined copper supply expectations. | Vedanta shares fell after the firm issued an expression of interest to sell its shut Sterlite copper plant in Tuticorin, Tamil Nadu. The move highlights reduced copper refining capacity from an idled smelter, which can worsen near-term sentiment around copper supply and inventories. | Vedanta is putting its 400,000 tpa Sterlite Copper smelter and refining complex in Tuticorin up for sale, calling for expressions of interest. The move comes while a Supreme Court appeal over reopening is pending amid local environmental opposition. Vedanta argues it affects supply, with India becoming a net importer. | China’s copper cathode imports rose in May (up MoM and YoY), with increases from Congo (DRC) and Russia supported by improved logistics and post-crisis shipments. The inflow also reflects cancelled LME Asian warehouse warrants entering China. SMM expects June imports to increase slightly as exports decline further. | China’s NBS reported copper cathode output of 912,000 mt in May (+4.7% y/y). SMM attributes slightly higher output to delayed smelter overhauls from pandemic and transportation disruptions. Despite some recovery, June remains intensive maintenance, so cathode production is expected to rise only slightly. | The article describes ongoing pollution from 100 years of copper mining in Michigan’s Keweenaw Peninsula, where storm-driven “stamp sands” are migrating into Lake Superior and smothering aquatic life. It notes elevated and sometimes toxic copper levels and a cleanup plan expected in July, potentially costing about $1 billion. | 1844 Resources says it has acquired four additional claims to complete its 299-claim land position on its Native Copper Project in Quebec, adding known copper showings and previously collected high-grade grab/trench/drilling results. It also postpones a Vortex drill program for wildlife reasons and shifts focus to the copper project. | Copper sentiment remains choppy as investors weigh the Fed’s aggressive rate-hike backdrop and Chile’s constitutional referendum. While earlier drafts avoided outright nationalization, a new constitution could tighten environmental and water-related mining obligations, raising investment uncertainty for major producers, with potential downstream pressure on global copper supply expectations. | Chile’s peso hit a record low as copper prices slid. The move suggests deteriorating near-term market expectations for copper, likely tied to weaker demand and/or improving supply outlook perceptions. The currency reaction reinforces that investors are adjusting risk and commodity pricing downward for copper in the coming days. | Chile’s peso weakened to a record low as global recession worries pushed copper to nine-month lows. The piece attributes the move to expectations of further central-bank tightening and softer industrial growth, alongside broader EM risk sentiment. It notes additional macro volatility across Latin America and markets, reinforcing copper’s near-term downside. | Vedanta said it is seeking buyers for its closed 400,000 tpa Sterlite copper smelter in Thoothukudi, Tamil Nadu, with expressions of interest due July 4. The shutdown followed deadly 2018 protests over pollution claims. Vedanta is also disputing the permanent closure at India’s Supreme Court.

Japanese firms are reportedly substituting away from copper as copper prices surge. Daikin plans to cut copper usage by about 60% in 2023, and Panasonic is considering air-conditioner models using aluminium instead of copper for core components. The shift may dampen copper demand and mildly lift aluminium demand. | Yangshan imported copper premiums under warrants rose modestly, while import losses versus the 2207 contract narrowed slightly. Buyers adopted a wait-and-see stance as profits improved, but sellers kept quotes largely steady. Cathode continues to clear from bonded warehouses, including non-standard sources, supporting stable near-term supply pricing. | Surge Copper reported a 96% increase in Measured & Indicated resources at its Ootsa Property to 439 Mt grading 0.32% CuEq (plus inferred tonnage). The estimate supports potential future development near the Huckleberry Copper Mine, but the update does not signal immediate production changes. | The Scrap Iron Dealers Association is urging the government to shut down copper exports for three months to curb theft of TSTT copper cables. The government has not yet responded. The proposal, if implemented, could reduce copper export supply and be supportive for copper prices over the near term.

SMM data shows secondary copper throughput in Linyi (East China Nonferrous Metal City) fell sharply in June 13-19, while 1# copper cathode and bare bright copper weekly averages dropped materially. The decline is linked to weaker downstream scrap demand and upstream reluctance to ship, with further falls expected June 20-27. | Afghanistan’s Taliban is pressing to restart the Mes Aynak project, run by a Chinese consortium, tied to one of the world’s largest copper deposits. Security, contract and royalty disputes have delayed development for years. Talks are “80% finished,” but the timing, permitting and environmental demands remain uncertain. | Afghanistan’s Taliban and the Chinese consortium behind the Mes Aynak project are pushing to restart mining at a major copper deposit after years of delays. Negotiations are nearing technical settlement, with demands on local processing and infrastructure. Environmental and security concerns remain, but the push for faster execution supports copper supply expectations. | Aston Bay announced a July 2022 diamond drill program at its Storm Copper Project in Nunavut, targeting high-grade copper zones (noted intersections around 2.45–3.07% Cu) and additional EM conductors. The program includes bulk sampling for direct ship product and beneficiation test work, with results to be released over time. | Three Valley Copper said its 2022 annual and special shareholders’ meeting was adjourned due to insufficient quorum, rescheduled from June 22 to June 30, 2022. The company continues soliciting votes and will proceed with proposals at the adjourned meeting. No operational or production changes were disclosed. | Codelco workers in Chile launched a major strike after the Ventanas smelter was closed for environmental reasons. Unions expected 50,000 workers (including contractors) to stop, and say protests led to detentions and halted talks. The government says mining sites are continuing, but operational discontinuity raises near-term copper supply concerns. | Residents in Peru blocked the highway used by MMG’s Las Bambas copper mine, demanding payment for road use. The incident follows another protest that previously forced the mine to shut down for over 50 days. With Las Bambas having resumed only two weeks earlier, renewed disruption raises near-term copper supply risks. | A community in Peru’s Mara district blocked a highway used by MMG’s Las Bambas copper mine, demanding compensation and citing delays in land appraisals. The disruption follows a prior protest that shut the mine for over 50 days. Reuters reports uncertainty about whether concentrate transport is affected, but supply risk remains.

Workers at Chile’s Codelco, the world’s top copper producer, began an indefinite strike after the company planned to close its Ventanas foundry in Quintero/Puchuncavi. Unions say the shutdown and protests will cost output daily, while the government disputes the extent, but production access is disrupted. | Myanmar junta forces raided villages near the Letpadaung copper mine, torching homes and displacing thousands. Local PDFs retaliated with about 100 mortar rounds aimed at a power distribution site and acid tanks at the mine project, though damage is unclear. The attacks increase near-term disruption risk to copper output. | Workers in Chile are striking at the world’s largest copper producer, raising the risk of disrupted mine output. Any stoppage can reduce near-term copper supply and increase uncertainty for the market, potentially weighing on copper prices in the coming days and weeks. | Afghanistan’s Mes Aynak, threatened by the Taliban’s push for revenue, sits atop one of the world’s largest copper deposits mined by a Chinese consortium (MCC/MJAM). Despite a $3 billion 2007 contract, the mine remains delayed by security and contract disagreements. Ongoing talks could affect copper supply plans but near-term output is unclear. | Chile’s government will reinvest 30% of Codelco profits (2021-2024) into company projects instead of turning over all profits to the state. Finance officials say it can reduce Codelco’s financing costs and improve indebtedness, supporting long-term copper production plans. Immediate production impact is not specified. | SMM reports customs data showing China exported copper wire and cable to 174 countries in May 2022. The United States was the top destination at 11,300 mt (15% share), followed by Australia at 6,900 mt (6%). Export demand appears concentrated in the US, Australia, and Southeast Asia. | SMM survey indicates scrap sellers held inventories as copper prices declined, tightening scrap availability for processors. The cathode-vs-scrap price spread narrowed and turned inverted, with cathode below scrap, which suppressed scrap consumption by processing firms. | Residents in Peru’s Apurimac region blocked a highway used by MMG’s Las Bambas copper mine, demanding compensation and citing delays in land appraisals. The blockade follows a prior 50+ day shutdown after protests. It is unclear immediately how much concentrate transport is affected, but renewed disruption raises near-term supply concerns. | China’s copper concentrate imports reportedly hit a record high in May, while Peru’s concentrate supply declined. The combination suggests tighter near-term concentrate availability and stronger feedstock demand for China’s smelters, which can lift expectations for copper supply balance and support copper prices over the coming days to weeks. | Castillo Copper granted Hyperion a 12-month option to acquire its Zambia copper-belt assets via Zed Copper, including Luanshya and Mkushi projects. Payments include an option fee and consideration in Hyperion shares tied to copper resource milestones. Hyperion would assume a 2% net smelter return royalty on concentrate. | Global markets rose as Treasury yields eased, but copper fell sharply to 16-month lows on the London Metal Exchange. Investors feared a slowdown as tightening financial conditions and soft factory activity boosted recession risks and weakened expectations for copper demand. | Global markets declined as investors feared further interest-rate hikes to fight decades-high inflation could push economies into recession. Germany’s and France’s recent Purchasing Managers’ Index data showed weaker activity, reinforcing slowdown concerns. This demand outlook weighed on risk assets and industrial commodities such as copper and likely other metals. | Gold fell and copper tumbled to its lowest level since early February 2021 as recession fears weighed on industrial metals. Copper for July delivery dropped 20 cents (5.2%) to $3.739/lb, reaching 16-month lows. The selloff reflects weakening demand expectations alongside macro pressure. | Codelco workers in Chile launched an open-ended strike after the government decided to shut the Ventanas foundry to curb pollution following sulphur dioxide poisonings. Workers blocked entrances at multiple facilities and unions said production was paralysed, though the finance minister said mining sites were not fully stopped. | Copper briefly fell below $4/lb, signaling weakening demand expectations as interest-rate hikes and recession fears curb demand. The market is also weighing whether electrification can offset a projected surplus. The piece notes Chilean labour/smelter disruptions could threaten future supply, but near-term pressure is bearish. | Australian equities are set to edge lower as commodity prices weaken. The article highlights recession worries that are deflating metals, with copper falling to a 16-month low. Oil and gold also slip, suggesting broader risk-off sentiment that typically pressures copper prices further in the near term. | Workers have ended a strike at the world’s largest copper producer in Chile. The resolution is expected to restore normal operations and reduce near-term supply disruption risk, which can ease market concerns over copper availability and typically puts downward pressure on copper prices over the following days. | U.S. stocks rose as Treasury yields fell, but copper declined to 16-month lows. The report links copper’s slump to higher interest rates and softer factory activity across major economies, with recession fears growing amid tightening policy and supply-chain disruptions from China’s curbs and the Ukraine war. | Port of Spain businesses report copper thieves hacking copper lines from rooftop air-conditioning units, with repeated theft within 24 hours and occasional full condenser theft. DOMA cites record-high international copper prices and urges protective measures. A scrap dealers leader blames authorities and calls for action on copper trade; police are investigating.

Copper prices plunged to a 16-month low as investors grew more concerned that aggressive central-bank tightening will spur a recession. LME benchmark copper fell 4.2% to $8,409/tonne, down about 14% from early-month peak, reflecting expectations of reduced industrial demand for metals. | Chile’s copper miners (about 50,000) have launched an indefinite strike at mines and refining facilities after the Boric government announced the permanent closure of the Ventanas copper refinery and planned layoffs. The work stoppage threatens near-term copper supply from Chile, a key global producer, adding upside support to prices. | SMM said copper inventories across major Chinese markets fell 1,300 mt to 102,300 mt vs Monday, and 7,600 mt vs last Friday, with the sharpest decline in Guangdong linked to higher rod-plant operating rates. However, end-June fund withdrawals are expected to bring more sellers, less buyers, and inventories to bottom out and rise next week. | Global stocks rose after Powell reiterated a fight against inflation, but risk-off factors lingered. The dollar eased and Treasury yields moved, yet copper slid to a 16-month low near $3.7/lb. The drop is attributed to recession fears, despite a strike at Chile’s Codelco, the biggest producer. | Global stocks rebounded while copper continued to slide. Reuters notes LME copper was down 0.5% to $8,367/t after earlier touching $8,122.50. The move is linked to easing inflation expectations and a growing recession outlook from aggressive central-bank rate hikes, lowering demand expectations for commodities. | Gold slipped for a second week as recession fears weighed on risk sentiment. Copper was highlighted for posting its biggest weekly percentage loss in a year, with the most-active contract down 6.8% and Thursday settling at the lowest level in about 16 months. Macro factors and the dollar drove moves. | TNR Gold highlights its 0.4% NSR royalty on McEwen Mining’s Los Azules copper project in Argentina. McEwen’s update includes multiple long copper intercepts (up to 1.38% Cu over 54m) and progress on drilling, modeling, and site access, supported by additional project financing. | World stocks were set for a weekly gain as investors hoped falling commodities, led by copper, would curb runaway inflation. The week saw steep commodity declines amid concerns the global economy is shaky and rate hikes could hurt growth, prompting traders to reduce inflation expectations and bets on hike size. | Stocks rallied as investors tempered inflation expectations, but benchmark LME copper fell further, down 0.5% on Friday and near multi-month lows. The move reflects easing recession worries from commodity price slides; however, the article still highlights continued downside pressure on copper versus recent peaks. | Copper has fallen more than 20% from its peak, entering a technical bear market, alongside intensifying recession fears. The piece attributes weakness to rapidly rising interest rates and inflation choking global growth, with analysts saying copper’s sensitivity to recession risk is rising and could weigh on demand. | Gold was slightly firmer on Friday but down for the week, with traders waiting for inflation data to gauge Fed rate-hike expectations. The key point is copper’s large weekly drop, losing 6.8%, driven by recession fears and expectations for weaker copper demand.

Codelco President Máximo Pacheco said recent weakness in copper prices may be temporary, with fundamentals and supply-demand balance remaining favorable. He noted Codelco’s stable annual production target and cost competitiveness, alongside Chile allowing the firm to retain profits to fund a large investment plan. Copper fell sharply on global growth fears. | SMM said China’s copper inventory across major markets rose 13,500 mt to 115,800 mt over the weekend—the biggest increase since March. The rise was driven by higher arrivals as the import window reopened and smelters cleared in-plant stock, while downstream demand weakened after sharp price falls. | US Copper says it aims to advance the Moonlight-Superior copper project in California toward production, citing large historical resources, existing infrastructure, and an upcoming drilling program targeting open-pit grade material. With the US a net copper importer and demand rising from clean-tech, the initiative is framed as added supply potential. | Codelco says Chile’s tightening environmental and water regulations will force strategy changes, including recirculating water, cutting emissions, shifting to renewables, and previously closing its Ventanas smelter. The drought is still affecting output, while permitting delays and potential glacier rules add uncertainty for future projects. | SMM reports Yangshan copper premiums under warrants and bill of lading rose slightly amid profitable spot imports. The Fed’s 75 bp hike and hawkish tone pressured copper prices and the SHFE/LME ratio, though China demand/import activity and low social inventory supported premiums. Bonded-zone inventories increased, with shipping and inflows rising. | SMM reports copper prices continued falling as recession risk intensified after weaker US/Europe data and Fed rate-hike concerns. LME copper dropped below technical support and overseas prices declined for 12 straight days. Fundamentals were mixed: slightly lower concentrate TCs, some restocking, but weak demand and ample spot supply pressured premiums. | A Nelson Mandela Bay security firm says theft of water meters and copper pipes has risen sharply over the past few weeks. Copper units are targeted because they can be sold for cash at scrapyards, while newer plastic meters are less affected. The firm advises residents to deter thefts. | Gold futures fell as markets digested potential G7 restrictions on new Russian gold. Copper, however, recouped part of last week’s sharp loss, with July futures settling higher after worries about an economic slowdown drove its biggest weekly percentage drop in about a year. | Battery Mineral Resources reports high-grade copper assay results from the Cinabrio Norte target at its Punitaqui copper mine in Chile, including 48 meters at 1.31% Cu. The release links the drilling to potential additional copper mineralization as feed for the concentrator and ongoing plans to restart operations in 2H 2022.

SMM reviews China’s copper rod market in H1 2022. COVID lockdowns in Shanghai and other cities sharply lowered cathode rod operating rates and hurt downstream demand. A March VAT policy and a narrowing copper-cathode/scrap spread constrained recycled supply and margins, weakening secondary rod output. Power and real-estate demand also slowed. | Xinhua reports Chilean experts say copper has fallen about 9% week-on-week to $375.6/lb, the lowest in 16 months. They link the drop to higher U.S. Fed rates boosting the dollar and recession fears that reduce global demand for copper. Brief Codelco strike news failed to support prices. | Western Copper and Gold reported positive feasibility results for its Casino copper-gold-molybdenum project, including project economics that depend on assumed base-case copper prices. The article details processing design, recovery improvements, and inflation-driven cost increases, but it does not indicate imminent production or supply disruptions that would move metal prices soon. | Fighting between Myanmar junta forces and anti-junta militias has escalated around the Chinese-owned Letpadaung copper mine near Sagaing, with nearby villages raided and residents displaced. Wanbao says production has been suspended for over 16 months, while rebels accuse the company of supporting the military. Supply disruption risk weighs on copper. | A U.S. appeals court denied Apache Stronghold’s bid to halt construction of the Oak Flat copper mine, affirming the site’s sacred status but ruling religious arguments under federal law and the First Amendment were insufficient to overturn the land swap. The ruling reduces legal risk for Resolution Copper’s project timeline.

An FNArena technical note on Comex copper futures (US$/lb) shows daily/weekly/monthly trends down and highlights support around $3.50 and $2.85 (Sep 2022) versus resistance near $4.08 and $5.17. It calls for buyers to re-enter if $3.50 holds, recommending a long only on confirmation. | Los Angeles is launching a $60,000 pilot to protect municipal street lights from copper-wire theft that has caused outages and costly repairs. City officials plan measures like camouflaging boxes, burying and reinforcing them, and welding access points. Theft leads to higher replacement demand for copper wire but volumes seem localized. | SMM says copper concentrate TC/RC for the first half of 2023 was fixed at $76/mt and 7.6 cents/lb in long-term negotiations among Jiangxi Copper, Tongling Non-ferrous, Jinchuan and Antofagasta. It compares with about $54-55/mt a year earlier and follows an earlier June 20 deal. | SMM says copper prices dropped sharply in June 20-26, with weekly average 1# cathode down about 5%. Secondary copper throughput in East China fell 12% to 19,666 mt as traders faced losses, inventory stayed high, and cathode-to-scrap spreads narrowed, reducing scrap substitution and shipments. Demand for secondary copper is expected to keep declining. | Kodiak Copper reported infill drilling results from its MPD copper-gold porphyry project, confirming copper mineralization continuity in the southern Gate Zone (e.g., weighted Cu intervals and CuEq). The company also outlines continued drilling and upcoming geophysical/soil work, but provides no near-term supply or production-impact signals. | BeMetals commenced a 2022 aircore (4,000 m) and diamond drilling (1,400 m) program at its Pangeni Copper Project in Zambia’s Copperbelt, following prior copper intercepts (e.g., 4.14 m at 0.62% Cu). The company aims to expand Q and D prospect mineralization for future drilling targets. | Copper Mountain Mining announced the date and webcast details for its Q2 2022 results conference call. The company’s main asset is a 75%-owned copper mine producing about 100 million pounds of copper equivalent annually. However, no operational or financial updates are included here. | Pacific Ridge announced mobilization of field crews and camp construction for a 6,000 m drilling program at its Kliyul copper-gold project and a partner-funded 1,500 m drill program at the RDP copper-gold project. The news is exploratory, with no immediate changes to copper supply, production, or trade. | Romios Gold reported new assay results from its Kinkaid, Nevada property, including high-grade copper values (up to 7.39% Cu) alongside gold and silver. The release describes exploratory chip and grab sampling at previously undocumented prospects and planning for mapping and potential diamond drilling. It provides no production or supply guidance. | Sanatana Resources reports the start of drilling at its Oweegee copper-gold porphyry project in British Columbia. A recent ASTER survey and new copper-bearing altered intrusion discovery inform targeting of the Molloy monzonite. Results are pending, with no production, inventory, or export changes described. | Copper prices plunged to 16-month lows, with the article citing an 11% drop over two weeks as evidence that global growth is slowing and investors are turning bearish. It includes Codelco’s view that fundamentals remain supportive, but recession fears and broader risk sentiment weigh on prices near term.

LME copper stayed near a 16-month low as investors weighed hawkish central bank signals and potential rapid rate hikes that could hurt growth and demand. LME nickel futures rose after UK sanctions involving Norilsk Nickel’s major shareholder. Aluminium premiums for July–September were agreed lower, suggesting softer near-term aluminium pricing. | Nine civilians were killed and dozens missing after junta raids on villages near Myanmar’s Letpadaung copper project in Sagaing. Sources link operations to a China-backed mine site run by Wanbao Mining. Heavy fighting and attacks—including earlier power-line destruction—have displaced thousands, raising near-term disruption risk for copper production. | SMM reports that China Smelters Purchase Team (CSPT) finalized Q3 2022 spot copper concentrate TC/RC guidance at $80/mt and ￠8.0/lb, unchanged from Q2. The SMM Imported Copper Concentrate Index averaged $78.68/mt in Q2, indicating little fresh momentum for copper prices. | Candente Copper reported that directors receiving withheld votes offered resignations under its majority voting policy. Between 96% and 98% of withheld votes were cast by Fortescue’s affiliate, Nascent Exploration. The company said the board will consult parties and decide within 90 days, with no operational or production updates disclosed.

China Molybdenum plans to invest over $1.8 billion in the Kisanfu/KFM copper-cobalt project in the DRC, including spending on engineering and construction. The mine is expected to supply about 90,000 tons of cathode copper annually after full output, starting production in the first half of next year. | SMM reviews China’s lithium-battery copper foil expansion, estimating large capacity additions in 2H2022 and a higher likelihood of oversupply as COVID hits demand. Processing fees for 6μm/8μm/4.5μm foils have fallen, and SMM forecasts 6μm and 8μm power-battery foil prices will drop below 40,000 and 30,000 yuan/mt in 2022. | SMM said copper inventories across major Chinese markets rose 10,400 mt to 126,200 mt from Monday, up 23,900 mt week-on-week. The build was driven by smelters shipping to ease mid-year pressure and continued imported inflows amid weaker restocking as downstream funds tighten. SMM expects inventories to dip slightly next week. | London copper dropped as much as 3.6% to around $7,959/ton, extending a bear market and marking the lowest level since early 2021. It fell nearly 20% in the quarter amid recession and growth fears, with similar weakness cited for aluminium and nickel. | Nevada Copper provides an operational update at its Pumpkin Hollow underground copper mine, citing restricted stope access caused by weak rock structures. Liquidity constraints and creditor/vendor non-payments have led the company to suspend most underground mining activities while seeking additional funding, increasing near-term risk of reduced copper output. | Nevada Copper provides an update on its Pumpkin Hollow underground copper mine. Restricted stope access from weak rock conditions required curtailment, and liquidity/default concerns have led to suspension of most mining activities. Further funding is uncertain, risking inability to complete ramp-up and maintain planned copper production. | Copper prices reportedly fell to a 17-month low after inflation data increased concerns about a slowing economy. The market reaction suggests traders expect weaker industrial demand ahead, putting downward pressure on copper despite no specific supply or inventory shock being cited in the prompt. | Chile’s finance minister introduced a tax reform bill that raises copper mining royalties for large producers and adds profit taxes that vary with copper prices. Given Chile’s position as the world’s top copper producer, higher government take could pressure investment and near-term supply expectations, weighing on copper sentiment.

SMM reports weaker-than-expected PMIs in Europe and the US and continued worries about Fed rate hikes, keeping recession sentiment bearish for copper. While spot supply is tight and concentrate TCs are stable, downstream order growth is limited and buyers stay cautious. The piece expects copper to struggle in the short term. | SMM says China’s copper consumption remains weak despite supportive macro policies. Traditional demand sectors (appliances, autos, infrastructure) lag, while overseas consumption shrinks. Scrap supply is tight and inventories are low, supporting spot/backwardation, but the report expects only a slight short-term rebound and overall price decline later. | CMOC says its KFM copper-cobalt mine in the DRC will start producing in 1H23, targeting average output of about 90,000 tpy copper metal (and 30,000 tpy cobalt) at full production. The company argues the additional cobalt supply helps EV demand, but near-term price effects are limited. | Copper prices are hovering near a 17-month low, and “Dr Copper” is giving a downbeat assessment of the global economy, urging that it “must do better.” Because copper demand tracks activity in appliances, power generation and cars, the gloomy growth outlook is framed as a headwind for copper. | Nova Scotia Regional Police arrested two men accused of stealing copper wire from Nova Scotia Power substations and poles in Trenton. Police found copper wire and tools in the vehicle and charged the suspects with trespassing and theft-related offenses. The article emphasizes safety hazards from live wires, not any large-scale market impact. | Copper has fallen about 26% from recent highs and is now more than 20% below them, entering bear-market territory. The decline is attributed to softer demand from renewed China lockdown measures, plus a stronger USD tied to faster US monetary tightening and recession fears. | Proactive lists several company headlines. The only directly metal-linked item is Nevada Copper agreeing non-binding terms for a loan of up to US$70M to maintain its underground mine. If financing keeps production running, it can ease short-term copper supply concerns and be mildly supportive for copper.

Lunnon Metals says drilling at its Warren nickel target in Western Australia returned stronger nickel mineralisation, including assays up to 5.28% Ni, plus smaller copper and cobalt grades. The results are expected to show Warren may host substantially more contained nickel than estimated, leading to a revised mineral estimate in coming quarters. | Trinidad and Tobago scrap dealers say copper theft from utilities and buildings has worsened, disrupting water, electricity and communications. They urge the government to stop copper exports temporarily, arguing export demand fuels theft. The piece cites vandalism incidents and calls for stronger enforcement to curb the trade. | SMM reports copper concentrate demand in Asia should stay supported in Q3 by high smelter margins and increased Chinese capacity, while clean concentrate supply remains tight. Credit Suisse’s recession scenarios point to downside for copper and aluminium: copper could drop for longer amid incoming supply, while aluminium faces negative demand pressure despite possible support from constrained supply and higher energy/input costs. The article also covers investor relations arrangements and warrant terms. | Peruvian indigenous communities protesting MMG’s Las Bambas copper mine say there has been no progress after a month of talks and the 30-day truce has ended without a clear next step. SMM says copper inventories across major Chinese markets fell by 15,300 mt from Monday to 94,500 mt, and by 26,200 mt versus last Friday. The largest drop was in Guangdong, driven by lower import inflows and shipments into warehouses, while downstream demand was steady, supporting firm spot premiums. | SMM said copper inventories in China’s bonded zones fell by 2,000 mt to 276,300 mt as of July 22, continuing a four-week decline but at a slower pace. Shanghai was flat while Guangdong declined. Poor import trading, limited warrant shipments, and steady inflow into bonded warehouses kept the drawdown modest. | Nippon Denkai will build a $150 million copper foil plant in Augusta, Georgia instead of South Carolina, targeting EV battery supply. It expects to scale output from 9,500 metric tons to 28,500 metric tons and invest up to $430 million. Officials also highlighted electricity-cost advantages and incentives. | S&P Global says energy-transition demand for copper from EVs, solar/wind and power-grid equipment will rise sharply and likely outstrip supply even with record mining and recycling. Regulatory, permitting and long mine-development timelines dampen new supply growth. The report warns net-zero goals may be threatened, implying tighter copper outlook.

Codelco, the world’s largest copper producer, will start constructing a US$1 billion desalination plant this year in northern Chile to address a decade-long drought. The project aims to cut fresh-water use by 60% via desalinated seawater and tailings reuse, supporting stable operations around Tocopilla. | CME Group announced record open interest in copper options. The update signals rising participation in copper derivatives markets, which can increase hedging and speculative positioning. However, without information on option skew, strikes, or implied price moves, it is not a clear bullish or bearish price catalyst. | Copper Mountain Mining reported Q2 2022 results, with copper output falling to 13.3 million lb due to lower ore grade, throughput limits, crusher issues, and staffing/supply chain disruptions. The company expects higher grades and recovery in H2 after Phase 4 ore release and plant optimization, while discussing higher Q2 costs. | MMG said it suspended its full-year copper output targets for Peru’s Las Bambas after protests disrupted operations and cut production by about 60% in the second quarter. The mine is currently operating at full capacity, but no new guidance was issued and tensions remain as community truce talks expire.

The piece forecasts copper near-term moves as largely driven by the US dollar and this week’s FOMC. It warns that hawkish Fed signals or weak GDP could strengthen the USD and weigh on prices. It also cites weak sentiment on China demand and a bear-flag technical setup that risks further downside. | ICSG’s monthly report shows the global refined copper market was oversupplied by 5,000 mt in May 2022, following a revised April deficit back toward oversupply. Production in May was 2.189 million mt versus 2.184 million mt consumption, with a 43,000 mt surplus over the first five months. | Copper prices rose as supply risks from major producers outweighed demand worries. MMG suspended copper production targets after a 60% output drop at Peru’s Las Bambas mine, while Vale and Antofagasta cut 2022 outlooks. A softer U.S. dollar also boosted prices. Nickel advanced alongside copper. | Marvel Discovery says it has mobilized field crews to the Duhamel Ni-Cu-Co (and Ti-V-Cr) property in Quebec to confirm historical drill holes and conduct prospecting and surveying for new nickel-copper sulphide targets. The update is exploration-focused with no indicated production or supply changes, limiting near-term price impact. | SMM data show East China copper scrap throughput fell to 84,600 mt from July 18-24, down 25.61% WoW. The week’s average prices for #1 copper cathode and bare bright copper declined. Falling copper prices and price volatility reduced cargo sales and weakened scrap demand, though SMM expects some pickup. | Freeport-McMoRan said the sharp copper price fall stems from economic uncertainty and higher rates, and could prompt adjustments to operating plans. The article compares today to 2015, noting potential production curtailments and lower LME inventories. It adds that escalating power and diesel costs have pressured aluminium output and costs.

SMM reviews China’s copper rod industry, noting oversupply, low capacity utilization, and demand weakness from wire/cable and enamelled wire amid pandemic disruptions. It discusses rising secondary (scrap-based) rod capacity, competitive pricing, and the role of lower copper prices in operating-rate rebounds. Grid and new-energy orders may support recovery later. | Fabled Copper Corp. provided additional 2021 surface sampling results from its Muskwa Project in northern British Columbia, including chip and float samples with reported copper grades up to 5.22% Cu. The update is exploratory geology-focused with no immediate implications for supply, exports, or inventories. | Peruvian mining executives say they are losing faith in President Castillo’s ability to support the sector despite his tax proposals shifting. Reuters cites social protests that caused month-long production halts at two major copper mines and delayed a pipeline of new copper projects worth $53 billion, amid high copper prices. | Peruvian mining executives say President Castillo’s moderation hasn’t stopped community protests that have triggered month-long production halts at major copper mines and delayed a large $53 billion project pipeline. While copper prices have fallen but remain high, firms warn instability and permit delays could reduce future supply and investment. | Metallic Minerals says it has started its 2022 drilling campaign at the La Plata silver-gold-copper project in Colorado, aiming to extend the Allard deposit and refine copper-silver-gold targets that could lead to an expanded 2023 resource estimate. The company also extends warrant expiry; no production changes are mentioned. | Eagle Plains announced that partner Rockridge has started a ~1,000m summer diamond drilling program at the high-grade Knife Lake copper VMS project in Saskatchewan. The program targets regional zones and infill/expansion around the deposit. Rockridge is fully funded and permitted, with drilling planned through about six to eight holes. | Aztec Minerals began a 10-hole, 3,000m core drilling program at its Cervantes gold-copper project in Sonora, Mexico to expand and test oxide cap and deeper copper-gold targets. While it highlights copper-bearing intercepts, it does not change near-term production, so price impact is likely limited. | Peruvian mining executives told Reuters they have lost faith in the government, citing social protests that caused month-long halts at the Cuajone and Las Bambas copper mines and slowed project pipelines. Several expansions are waiting approvals, keeping near-term copper supply and investment sentiment under pressure despite high prices. | Copper prices fell sharply as recession fears dominated markets despite tight inventories and studies projecting long-term demand growth. The article cites IMF downgrades and weaker trade signals, with commentary that “Dr. Copper” reflects economic slowdown expectations. Overall, near-term sentiment is bearish and selling pressure is driving losses.

Yangshan copper premiums under warrants stayed rangebound at high levels ($81-97/mt), with average prices flat. Weaker market activity led to softer domestic spot premiums and nearly 200 yuan/mt decline in spot import profits. Transactions stalled amid limited trader offers and scarce inquiries. Pyro-copper and hydro-copper premiums were steady. | Norilsk Nickel reported 1H 2022 output: nickel nearly 100 kt (+26% y/y) but down 6% q/q to 48 kt due to Harjavalta scheduled repairs, while copper rose to 204 kt (+18% y/y) and to 113 kt in Q2 (+23% q/q) due to planned furnace maintenance. Guidance from Russian feed reiterated. | DLP Resources says it has started an 1,800m drill program at its Hungry Creek copper-cobalt project, targeting the 711 copper zone after encouraging prior copper grab/float samples. The company also plans a flow-through private placement to fund drilling. The news is sentiment-positive but lacks production or supply impact. | Ascendant Resources reports drill hole ST_31 results at the Lagoa Salgada Venda Nova VMS project, including 12m at 3.3% copper and a newly outlined high-grade copper-dominated lens open at depth. The company says this may upgrade the South Zone block model, supporting feasibility and future resource updates. | The piece (via its headline) reports that Anglo American’s new boss warned about future copper supply. Such commentary can shift near-term expectations toward tighter market availability, supporting copper prices as traders anticipate a potential supply shortfall over the coming weeks.

Copper producers are pushing back against Chile’s proposed tax overhaul, with Anglo American saying it needs greater regulatory clarity before proceeding with major investments. The development may influence market expectations for future copper supply in the near term, but it does not cite immediate output impacts or clear timing. | Customs data show China exported 51,300 mt of copper plate and strip in Jan–Jun 2022 (+56% YoY), with June exports up 84% YoY. Overseas demand rebounded as lockdowns eased, while high German energy prices limited local supply. | Copper prices rose Tuesday, supported by a weaker dollar and supply risk from Peru. Three-month LME copper gained 0.8% and Shanghai September copper rose 1.3% amid ongoing demand worries. The article also reports gains in LME aluminium and ShFE nickel, suggesting broadly positive metal sentiment. | Authorities in Trinidad and Tobago report over 300 arrests for copper theft, mainly involving TSTT telecommunications cables and a copper plant. The government is considering a ban on the scrap iron industry, after PM Rowley cited severe sabotage. Dealers oppose shutdown, seeking workable solutions to curb theft. | Scrap Iron Dealers’ Association president Allan Ferguson denies allegations of buying stolen TSTT copper lines, saying his firm won a bid to dispose of aged copper cables. TSTT confirms Ferguson’s company won a contract for copper cable disposal. The dispute centers on provenance and gate passes, with no direct price signals. | Peruvian mining executives say President Castillo’s failure to restrain social protests has caused month-long copper production halts at Cuajone and Las Bambas and delayed new $53 billion projects. Even with moderated tax proposals, investor confidence is fading, risking further project and expansion delays in Peru, a leading copper producer. | Copper is set for a fourth straight monthly decline as recession fears, a weaker demand outlook in China, and an elevated U.S. dollar weigh on prices.
