The company cut full-year 2022 copper production guidance to 1.06 million tonnes from 1.11 million, keeping other guidance unchanged. | China’s bonded-zone copper inventories fell 10,200 mt week-on-week to 266,100 mt as of July 29, with declines in Shanghai and Guangdong. SMM links the drop to a better SHFE/LME ratio and high China premiums boosting customs-declaration demand, while sellers secured warrants and bills of lading, drawing inventories down. | Copper rallied to a three-week high as expectations for slower U.S. interest-rate hikes weakened the dollar and renewed supply worries tightened the market. LME three-month copper rose 1.5% to $7,875/ton, amid declining inventories and mine output cuts, including weaker Chile data and reduced Glencore guidance.

SMM reports Aug 1 copper cathode premiums in Shanghai rose to 330–410 yuan/mt but still failed to stimulate buying because premiums and SHFE futures remained high. Expected weekend imports of hydro-copper did not arrive, yet backwardation and elevated prices capped demand and pulled quotes lower. Future premiums may fall further. | Chilean authorities are investigating a large sinkhole that appeared over the weekend near land operated by Canadian Lundin Mining’s copper mine in northern Chile. While the cause is unclear, such ground damage can threaten mine stability, potentially disrupting near-term copper output and expectations. | Chile is investigating a giant sinkhole at Lundin’s underground copper mine. Such an incident raises the risk of production disruptions, potential safety shutdowns, and near-term supply concerns, which can weigh on market sentiment for copper prices over the coming days to weeks. | Koba Resources completed induced polarisation (IP) surveys over its historic Blackpine cobalt-copper project ahead of maiden drilling in September, following an earlier maiden program at the nearby Colson project. The work targets extensions of copper-cobalt mineralisation, but no production changes are reported.

Codelco’s copper output fell 9.3% year-on-year in the latest quarter, and Cochilco expects Chile’s annual output to drop further due to declining ore grades, water restrictions and labor union protests. While demand risks are noted, the supply shortfall outlook could support copper prices in the coming weeks. | Chilean authorities are investigating a 25-meter-wide sinkhole near Lundin Mining’s Alcaparrosa mine in northern Chile. Sernageomin closed areas around the site as specialists investigate, citing water at the depth. Lundin says the sinkhole stayed stable and affected no workers, but technical analysis is ongoing. | West Mining reported initial exploration results from its Blue Cove Copper Project in Newfoundland, with multiple rock samples returning grades above 1% and some above 2% and up to 3.56% Cu. The company is awaiting additional soil sampling and ongoing interpretation, with no near-term production or supply impact stated. | Mongolia reportedly increased exports of gold, copper and coal in 2022, with the article citing higher export values and volumes for hard coal over the first half-year. It also notes copper exports rose, suggesting improved supply availability that could weigh on copper prices in the short term. | SMM reports SHFE and spot copper prices rebounded in early August, citing tight inventories—domestic social inventory at a record-low 69,400 mt and SHFE inventory around 37,000 mt. Supply constraints include import delays and smelter maintenance, plus blister copper tightness, supported by a falling US dollar. | A sinkhole reportedly opened suddenly near a copper mine in Chile, with “a lot of water” flowing into the area. The incident raises the risk of operational disruption, potential damage, or short-term production losses, which can tighten copper supply expectations and support higher prices over the coming days. | Nornickel reports 1H2022 IFRS results, stating revenue was supported by higher nickel and copper prices and production volumes after earlier operational suspensions at mines and a concentrator. It also highlights logistical disruptions and inventory build-up. The company describes a nickel market in surplus overall, implying limited near-term directional price pressure. | Norilsk Nickel’s interim results note that nickel and copper prices and production volumes moved during 1H 2022, alongside logistical disruptions and macro factors. The company describes nickel price volatility, market surplus dynamics, and copper’s volatility tied to geopolitics, recession fears, and a stronger dollar. Aluminium impacts are not indicated. | Nornickel reports 1H 2022 results, noting higher nickel and copper prices and recovered volumes after prior mine/smelter disruptions, offset by logistical disruptions and inventory build. It describes nickel market surplus dynamics and copper price volatility, but provides no new operational or trade catalyst that would clearly shift near-term metal prices. | Nornickel reported 1H2022 IFRS results, noting production recovery after temporary suspensions at its Oktyabrsky and Taimyrsky mines and Norilsk concentrator, but also logistical disruptions leading to lower metal sales and higher inventories. The company discusses nickel market surplus/shortage mix and copper price volatility, reflecting near-term market conditions. | Ero Copper reported record Q2 2022 copper production of 12,734 tonnes and reaffirmed full-year output guidance while raising copper C1 cash cost guidance for Caraíba. Results were affected by issues at its domestic smelting customer, shifting more concentrate sales to international customers with longer pricing periods. | Ero Copper reported its second-quarter 2022 operating and financial results, noting record quarterly production of copper (and gold). Record output from a copper producer can shift short-term supply expectations upward, creating modest downside pressure for copper prices over coming days to weeks.

SMM says China copper cathode inventories in mainstream areas fell to 69,400 mt as of Aug 1, the lowest in six years, driven by low starting levels, reduced stock accumulation, limited net imports, reluctance to sell scrap, and smelter maintenance impacts. Rebound is expected no earlier than mid-August as arrivals increase. | SMM reports Shanghai copper cathode spot premiums sliding 160 yuan/mt day-on-day to 60-100 yuan/mt over SHFE 2208 due to continuous inflows of imported copper and traders dumping for cash after backwardation eased. With smelter overhauls finished and expectations of more imports, premiums could fall further. | Copper thieves are harvesting telecommunications cables in Mayaro, Trinidad and Tobago, repeatedly disrupting phone, internet, and point-of-sale/ATM services. TSTT attributes outages to malicious theft of fibre optic and copper cables and reports additional fibre cuts. Authorities have arrested 300 people for copper theft since January. | Fabled Copper Corp. reports progress on 2022 field exploration at its Muskwa Copper Project in northern British Columbia. Activities include sampling, geophysics, drone mapping, and a pending drill permit. It also notes a newly found copper occurrence that increased the land package by 254 hectares. | S&P Global data cited in the article says copper demand could nearly double by 2035, implying supply may not keep up with electrification-driven growth. It also covers copper exploration drilling updates and recent copper sales/production figures for several miners, reinforcing expectations of ongoing copper tightness. No nickel or aluminium catalysts are mentioned. | South Africa’s Hawks arrested two guards accused of cutting and stealing Transnet copper cables in Mooi River. Investigators recovered about 670 meters of copper cable valued around R180,000. The suspects face charges for theft and damage to infrastructure, with a court appearance scheduled soon. Quantities appear minor for copper markets. | Copper prices eased as U.S.-China tensions over Pelosi’s Taiwan visit raised fears of weaker industrial demand. Markets also discounted the prospect of more Fed rate hikes and recession risks. LME three-month copper fell 1.6%, with aluminium down 1.3% and nickel down 0.8%, reflecting broad metals pressure. | The piece says Zambia’s growth outlook is shaky as copper output declines, with copper prices plunging. The headline implies weakening supply from Zambia and immediate negative sentiment for copper, which typically translates into expectations of lower demand/supply balance and downward pressure on copper prices over the next days. | Copper prices fell for the fourth straight day after Pelosi’s Taiwan visit heightened U.S.-China tensions. While investors see geopolitical risk as less likely to drive more selling, fears of a China-led global slowdown and recession remain the bigger driver, potentially damping construction demand for copper.

Zambia’s copper output fell 7.4% year-on-year in H1 2022 to 364,089 mt, with exports also weakening. Output at major First Quantum Minerals mines dropped due to rains and COVID-19 restrictions, making the 2022 production target likely unattainable. The supply shortfall supports expectations for tighter copper availability. | McEwen Copper reported drill intercepts at its Los Azules project in Argentina, highlighting long intervals of copper mineralization, including several higher-grade sections (up to 1.38% Cu over 44.0 m). Drilling will resume in October to support an updated PEA, but there is no near-term supply impact. | CanAlaska announced winter 2022 drill assay results at its Manibridge nickel project in Manitoba, reporting high-grade nickel-sulphide mineralization in all drill holes and continuity beneath the former mine. The release also notes the mineralization contains copper and cobalt, but provides no production or inventory updates. | Atlantic Nickel reported record 1H22 performance from the Santa Rita nickel sulphide mine in Brazil, including concentrate production and cost figures, and stated it has hedged nickel and copper prices. The update is primarily company-specific without evidence of near-term supply disruption or major demand/policy changes. | Experts are investigating a large sinkhole near Lundin Mining’s Alcaparrosa copper mine in Chile’s Atacama desert. While the company reports no impact to personnel or equipment, development work in the underground mine has been temporarily suspended as a safety measure. Disruption at Chile’s major copper source is bearish for prices. | Thieves stole fibre optic cables, targeting copper despite police believing they sought copper wire. The scrap dealers association links the surge in theft to soaring copper prices and warns exports and utility operations could be disrupted. Authorities are considering shutting the scrap industry or banning copper sales amid rising losses and illegal scrap trading.

SMM says copper inventory across major Chinese markets fell 500 mt to 68,900 mt week-on-week, with Guangdong hitting an SMM record low. The decline is attributed to Guangxi smelter overhaul, temporary shutdown of Guangdong lines, and limited imported inflows. High spot premiums may reduce rod producer consumption. | SMM reports copper futures falling over multiple sessions, with SHFE copper down and LME copper weaker. Offsetting positives include rapid destocking of domestic copper inventories, limited smelter output from maintenance and scrap tightness, and rising cable demand from infrastructure spending. | Atlantic Nickel and Appian report record 1H22 performance from the Santa Rita nickel sulfide mine in Brazil, including 8,000 t of nickel contained in concentrate, low C1 cash costs of about $4.95/lb net of by-products, and debt-free positioning. The results suggest supportive near-term nickel supply outlook. | Murchison Minerals says it has commenced Phase III diamond drilling at its HPM Quebec nickel-copper-cobalt project, targeting expansion and delineation at the Barre de Fer zone and the nearby Syrah target. The company plans ~7,500 m and references assay grades, but no production or supply changes are announced. | Digicel in Trinidad and Tobago says 10,000 customers lost service due to vandals cutting lines to steal copper over five months. Digicel notes its network is fibre (no copper), so theft damages infrastructure and forces costly repairs. Thieves are also stealing batteries at cell towers and some cable-wire arrests follow. | Nevada Copper updated operations and financing for its Pumpkin Hollow underground copper mine. The company is advancing restart planning but restart activities depend on securing long-term restart funding. It is in default on some obligations and seeks interim and additional financing from creditors, including shareholder Pala. | Nevada Copper reported progress on plans to restart its Pumpkin Hollow underground copper mine, but activities remain in temporary suspension while it seeks long-term restart funding. The company is discussing a larger financing package and may rely on further interim promissory notes. Restart timing depends on securing financing.

A political leader says the government moved too late to ban copper exports after scrap dealers had requested it for months. He argues a shorter copper export ban could curb theft and reduce disruption versus a six-month scrap iron ban, which he says strains livelihoods and jobs. | China’s MIIT published H1 2022 copper industry operations. The relevant section is AIS Aeris Resources, where Bell Potter highlights stronger Tritton Copper mine production after a strategy change and raises forecasts. Improved copper output prospects can modestly boost near-term copper price sentiment. | Copper prices firmed after stronger-than-expected US jobs data, but the article says sustained gains depend on China abandoning COVID-zero. BHP’s profits and Rio Tinto’s increased copper output are described as supportive for sentiment. However, analysis of Trump’s new 50% copper tariff warns of longer-lasting pain for US manufacturers due to limited processing capacity, offsetting the bullish news. | The article argues that Donald Trump’s 50% copper tariff will create longer-lasting pain for US manufacturers because domestic processing capacity is limited. The “unintended consequences” are framed as reduced buying activity or higher costs for copper users, pressuring near-term copper demand and potentially prices. | The piece highlights concerns around Donald Trump’s proposed 50% copper tariff. It argues the US has limited processing capacity relative to copper supply, and the tariff would likely cause longer-lasting pain for manufacturers, weakening near-term demand expectations and weighing on copper sentiment. | Copper prices rose on Monday as low inventories and higher China copper imports (sparked by lower prices) outweighed recession and weaker-demand worries. Improved July export growth in China also eased concerns. Despite broader macro uncertainty, the market was broadly bid, with copper contracts up on both LME and ShFE. | The excerpt points to a “copper tariff” development in the US, noting a 50% tariff and an imbalance between copper supply and limited processing capacity. It argues the policy will create longer-lasting pain for US manufacturers, weighing on consumption and thus copper demand expectations. | SMM reports China’s copper cathode output of 840,000 mt in July, below expectations, despite abundant concentrate. Additional overseas overhauls and unstable Kansanshi output add near-term supply risk for copper. | Australian shares edged higher as copper-linked stocks rose following BHP’s multibillion-dollar bid for South Australian copper and nickel miner Oz Minerals. The move boosted sentiment toward copper and nickel supply expectations, helping lift related equities, which can transmit to near-term metal price expectations. | SMM reports copper prices rebounding on supportive fundamentals despite macro pressure from hawkish Fed expectations and weaker PMI data. It cites low inventories, rising bonded-zone and LME cash-to-three-month premiums, smelter maintenance reducing supply, and stronger cable and rod demand. Premiums are seen to ease during delivery. | Capstone Copper reported Q2 2022 production and financial results, including 45,200 tonnes of copper and C1 cash costs of $2.78/lb. Results were impacted by negative provisional pricing linked to lower realized copper prices, while management noted signs of declining diesel and sulfuric acid prices and reiterated guidance. | Capstone Copper reported Q2 2022 results, including 45,200 tonnes of copper output and higher C1 cash costs. Results were impacted by negative provisional pricing from lower realized copper prices, while management cited declining sulphuric acid and diesel costs and reiterated production guidance, with H2 copper hedges and capital/cost reductions. | Barrick Gold reported a nearly 19% rise in quarterly profit, beating expectations mainly due to higher copper output. It produced 120 million pounds of copper in Q2, up 25% from Q1, and remains on track for copper guidance. Inflation lifted copper all-in sustaining costs 15%. | SMM says copper inventories across major Chinese markets rose 3,900 mt to 72,800 mt after weekend import clearance and resumed smelter shipments. Despite lower year-on-year levels, near-term supply looks supportive ahead of SHFE 2208 delivery, while demand may weaken from high premiums and price spreads. | The story details ongoing legal and regulatory challenges to the proposed Resolution Copper mine near Superior, Arizona. Supporters say it could become North America’s largest copper mine, while opponents cite environmental and cultural impacts. Courts have recently rejected some claims, but appeals and further review continue, keeping start dates uncertain. | Barrick Gold said higher copper production in Q2 helped lift profit by about 19%. The update is mainly company-level, not a broader copper market driver. | Lundin Mining temporarily suspended underground operations at its Alcaparrosa copper mine in Chile after authorities and the company investigated a sinkhole that expanded in surface diameter. The shutdown followed safety concerns and an official order to halt. Management says the closure is unlikely to materially affect annual guidance for the broader Candelaria complex. | Barrick reported second-quarter profits rose nearly 19% as copper production increased to 120 million pounds, 25% higher than a year earlier. The company also discussed inflation-driven higher gold costs and updates on Nevada and Dominican Republic projects. The copper output growth is the key immediate link to copper prices.

Chile’s mining minister said authorities will seek harsh sanctions over a large sinkhole near a copper operation in northern Chile. The article argues global growth in new copper deposits is insufficient versus expected demand, implying a potential supply tightness narrative that can support copper prices. | Chile plans to seek harsh sanctions over a large sinkhole near a copper mine in northern Candelaria. Authorities are investigating causes and may reform inspection processes, increasing near-term copper supply uncertainty. | SMM reports China’s July copper cathode output at 840,000 mt amid smelter maintenance and technical upgrades. It also notes copper-nickel-cobalt occurrence context and anomalous nickel/cobalt, but no production impact. | Great Atlantic Resources reports new drill core results from its Mascarene Property in New Brunswick, including a 0.40m intercept grading 6.74% copper and anomalous nickel (plus cobalt) and gold/silver. The findings support copper-bearing veins, but the update is early exploration without production implications for metal prices. | Copper prices are described as moving sideways after earlier gains, with recession fears weighing on the market despite a stronger US labor report (unemployment down to 3.5%). Guixi City in Jiangxi Province was locked down from 5:30am Aug 10 with no end time announced. The near-term news supports copper demand but likely not price materially. | Go Metals began diamond drilling at its HSP nickel-copper PGE project in Quebec, testing six EM/geophysical targets after confirmed nickel-copper sulphide mineralization at surface. The initial program plans about 2,000 metres of drilling. This is exploration with no production impact reported. | Go Metals Corp. began its 2022 diamond drilling program at the HSP nickel-copper PGE project in Quebec. Surface targets were previously confirmed with nickel-copper sulphides and PGE. The company plans about 2,000 metres of drilling for initial testing, with samples sent for independent analysis. | Copper rose about 1% after U.S. inflation came in weaker than expected, easing fears of aggressive rate hikes and supporting a weaker dollar. Comex futures also climbed. The article notes aluminium fell on the same day, while nickel gained sharply, suggesting mostly supportive macro demand sentiment for copper.

Alpha Copper reports results and ongoing drill progress at its Indata copper-gold project, targeting vein gold and porphyry copper-gold mineralization with associated molybdenum. It describes chalcopyrite mineralization and encourages copper-bearing intercepts, which can affect near-term copper market sentiment. No nickel or aluminium impacts are indicated. | Reuters reports LME and COMEX speculative positioning is net bearish for copper, with funds holding large short positions and buying put options targeting lower prices. The backdrop is concerns over growth slowdown in China and aggressive interest-rate hikes that raise recession risk, threatening further declines in demand and copper prices. | Speculators are increasingly positioning for a global downturn, building bearish exposure in copper on the LME and COMEX and buying downside put options. With copper still pressured by recession risks from China growth slowdown and aggressive rate hikes, the article highlights potential for prices to fall further toward marginal production-cost levels.

Copper prices gained around 1.56% in New York as the US dollar pulled back, supporting a firmer Australian dollar. The piece also highlights higher energy prices and a weak Rhine water level affecting coal transport and stockpiling, reinforcing broader commodity strength that can lift industrial metals sentiment. | The piece attributes copper’s rebound to softer inflation (falling CPI) and visible effects of Fed rate hikes easing financial pressure. It notes copper has recovered nearly 15% and highlights domestic inventories at record lows, focusing on how tight supply and demand dynamics may sustain further gains. | Glencore and IXM reportedly stopped supplying Huludao Ruisheng after $500 million worth of copper went missing. Glencore also moved some stock from Qinhuangdao to other ports to reduce similar risks. Traders said Western exposure is limited, but the incident still threatens short-term copper supply confidence. | Thomson Geer confirmed its involvement in Havilah Resources’ proposed $205m sale of the Kalkaroo copper-gold project to OZ Minerals. OZ will evaluate for 18 months and may buy 100% subject to milestones, with potential extra payments. An alliance also targets copper exploration in the Curnamona Province. | SMM says Chinese copper inventories across major markets fell to 70,700 mt, down 2,100 mt from Monday, though still above last Friday. Shanghai dropped but Guangdong rose. Power rationing clouded east-China supply/demand, while falling SHFE premiums may boost demand elsewhere, making next week’s trend uncertain. | Codelco says it is using AI to squeeze more copper from aging mines. The move is aimed at improving extraction from existing deposits rather than starting new projects. If it translates into incremental output, markets may expect tighter supply conditions easing, which can lift near-term copper sentiment.

SMM reports copper prices rebounded after softer-than-expected US CPI cooled rate-hike expectations and weakened the dollar. Fundamentals are mixed but supportive: tight blister/scrap, July smelter maintenance leading to low cathode output, then expected August rebound. Demand from infrastructure and wire/cable orders strengthened, with some inventory trend stability. | SMM says spot copper concentrate trading improved, with the Imported Copper Concentrate Index rising to about $74.38/mt. Traded spot TCs increased to $71-72/mt and a smelter bought at nearly $74-75/mt. Rumoured Qinhuangdao resales had limited spillover, as most smelters rely on long-term orders. | Rio Tinto would have needed to raise its offer to Turquoise Hill minority shareholders to win board endorsement for a full takeover. Turquoise Hill’s chairman says talks ended, potentially blocking the plan to lift Rio’s stake to 100%. The news implies deal uncertainty rather than direct copper supply or demand changes. | Rio Tinto’s proposed takeover of a large copper mine was rebuffed, according to the article headline. The rejection likely introduces uncertainty about future ownership and development timelines for the asset, which may negatively affect near-term expectations for copper supply and thereby pressure copper prices. | SMM says copper inventory in major Chinese markets fell 400 mt to 70,300 mt from Friday, and is far lower than last year. Low imported arrivals and reduced east-China smelter output contributed to the draw, while some flow shifted to Guangdong. Resumption of smelters may later increase stockpiles. | Quebec’s environment minister set a new arsenic emission limit for the Horne copper smelter in Rouyn-Noranda at 15 nanograms per cubic metre, lower than its current 100 and above the provincial norm. The measure responds to health concerns, including higher lung cancer rates in local residents. | Quebec has allowed a copper smelter in Rouyn-Noranda to emit arsenic levels up to five times the norm. The regulatory decision may reduce compliance pressure and help sustain smelting throughput, supporting near-term copper supply expectations and potentially aiding copper prices in the coming days. | Quebec’s environment minister approved a new arsenic emissions cap for Glencore’s Horne copper smelter in Rouyn-Noranda, lowering the limit to 15 nanograms per cubic metre over five years from 100. Updated rules will also address nickel and other pollutants, with no immediate closure announced. | The piece attributes pressure on the Australian dollar to stronger USD and recession fears, citing weak U.S. data and China’s softer retail sales and industrial production. It specifically says copper prices came under pressure, weighing on risk sentiment. RBA minutes and wage expectations are also in focus.

Bravo Mining announced high-grade results from the Luanga drill hole DDH22LU047, including 11.04m at 2.04% nickel and 1.23% copper (with higher-grade inclusions). The company is starting downhole electromagnetic surveying and continuing a multi-rig Phase 1 diamond drill program, with PGM results pending. | Bravo Mining announced high-grade intercepts from diamond drilling at its Luanga project in Brazil, including 11.04m at 2.04% nickel and 1.23% copper, with additional high-grade copper/nickel sub-intervals. DHTEM surveying and further drilling are planned, with PGM results pending, supporting positive sentiment for Ni and Cu. | DLP Resources said it received a water permit for its Aurora porphyry copper-molybdenum project in Peru and has started a first-phase ~5,000m drilling program to test copper enrichment and primary copper-molybdenum mineralization. The article is exploratory and does not imply near-term production changes. | Copper prices jumped after U.S. CPI came in softer than expected, easing fears of more aggressive rate hikes. The piece cites a weaker dollar and improving risk appetite, with LME three-month copper up 1.3% and Comex futures up 1.9%, signaling bullish near-term sentiment for copper. | Sandfire Resources America reported diamond drill intercepts showing high-grade copper at its Black Butte/Lowry copper project, with several holes grading ~2.7% to 4.3% copper over meaningful intervals. The results support a planned Lowry pre-feasibility study and potential expansion, but do not indicate near-term production. | The LME suspended Russian nickel from approved UK warehouses unless exported before July 20, citing high additional costs from UK sanctions. It follows earlier April steps banning Russian copper and aluminium-related products in warehouses. LME said no Russian nickel was currently stored, though Liverpool held 198 tonnes of nickel. | The LME suspended Russian nickel from approved UK warehouses unless it is exported by July 20, citing significant additional costs risk under UK sanctions. It follows an April 1 ban on other Russian metals, including copper and primary aluminium/aluminium alloy, from LME warehouses. | Faraday Copper Corp. reported Q2 2022 results and provided an update on its Arizona Copper Creek and Nevada Contact Copper projects. The company discussed continued drilling, pending assays, metallurgical work, and upcoming PEA/MRE milestones. The release is largely exploratory and does not indicate near-term supply impacts for copper.

Aug 8–14 secondary copper supply in Linyi rose 1.97% to 12,900 mt as SHFE copper advanced and shippers sold more actively, though some holders resisted. Copper cathode and scrap spread narrowed. Terminal infrastructure demand supported rods, but copper prices were bounded and the supply increase remained limited. | OZ Minerals, after rejecting BHP’s $8.3 billion bid, urged a “mindset shift” toward greater industry cooperation in South Australian copper operations. It said synergies could emerge and that it could more than double copper production using existing growth options, while expressing confidence about funding those projects. | NorthWest Copper announced new drill assay results from the Kwanika project, including intervals of high-grade copper-equivalent mineralization and chalcocite breccia, described as very high copper by weight. The update supports exploration success and potential future resource growth, but offers no near-term production or supply impact. | SMM reports Shanghai copper cathode spot premiums rising sharply, averaging about 480 yuan/mt and reaching ~500 yuan/mt at the end of trading. Tight supply, low absolute inventory, reduced domestic smelter output from power rationing, and limited imported inflows supported active buying, pushing hydro-copper and maintaining firmer quotes despite weaker SHFE futures. | Premium Nickel reported first 2022 assay results from the Selebi Ni-Cu-Co sulphide mine in Botswana, including 25.65 metres of 0.95% Ni and 2.03% Cu with higher-grade sub-intervals. The company says BHEM suggests additional mineralization beyond legacy horizons and anticipates more assays soon. | NorthWest Copper reported additional high-grade assay results from a second round of drilling at its Kwanika project in British Columbia. The company says the new assays point to more very high-grade copper mineralization, building on earlier discoveries within a previously undrilled shallow area. | Metal Energy acquired a 70% interest in the Manibridge nickel-copper project in Manitoba and plans continued exploration, targeting milestones to complete the path to 100% ownership. The update supports expectations for future nickel and copper resource development, though it does not indicate imminent production changes.

ASX-listed Cobre Ltd announced new drilling results with visible copper, driving its share price sharply higher. The news is a bullish development for the company’s copper prospects, but it mainly affects equity sentiment rather than near-term physical copper supply or demand, so market impact on copper prices is likely limited. | SMM says copper prices may swing widely because blister copper supply is structurally tight: narrowing cathode–scrap spreads, reduced secondary scrap output, and temporary smelter disruptions in DR Congo and Zambia. Downstream demand is mixed—NEVs and renewables add support, while power grids, real estate, and home appliances remain weak. | Chongqing’s authorities expanded industrial power restrictions due to high temperatures, shutting two copper pipe producers from Aug 17–24 and potentially delaying output. Sichuan imposed a broader industrial power suspension (Aug 15–20), reducing copper cathode deliveries and lowering downstream demand in the region, tightening availability. | NorthWest Copper said drilling has started at its Lorraine project in British Columbia, following an updated Mineral Resource Estimate that uses a 0.20% copper cut-off grade. The program targets resource growth and new drilling areas (Bishop, Lower Main, Upper Main, and Tam), with about 5,000 meters planned for 2022. | Copper Fox reported updated 3D magnetic modeling and chargeability correlations at its Mineral Mountain copper-molybdenum project in Arizona. The company interprets a porphyry-like intrusive/cupola system and identifies pipe-like bodies correlated with mineralization and alteration. The release is exploratory and does not change near-term supply or trading flows. | Mistango River Resources signed an option to buy a 100% stake in the Ledden copper-gold project in Quebec and plans a phase-one drill campaign. Funding includes $1.2m for work over three years and an earn-in structure with a 2% net smelter return royalty. It mainly affects future copper expectations, not near-term supply. | Kincora Copper reported re-assay results from its Trundle Park drilling, upgrading copper grades (e.g., 2.43% Cu over 2m) and providing encouraging evidence of additional porphyry-related mineralisation. At Fairholme, it also shared anomalous gold and copper from air-core drilling. No production or supply impact is indicated.

Aldebaran reported drill results from two holes at the Altar Copper Gold project, including 1,059.5 m grading 0.40% CuEq and additional intercepts in a previously undrilled area. Management says geophysical targets correlate with mineralization, potentially expanding exploration upside. No production or reserve changes were announced. | S&P Global argues that copper scarcity could become a destabilizing threat, warning that projected annual shortfalls would strain supply chains. While the full analysis isn’t detailed in the excerpt, the core message points to tightening copper fundamentals, which typically supports bullish expectations for prices. | High temperatures led Chongqing to expand emergency power rationing (Aug 17–24), suspending some copper tube production and reducing refined copper trading and downstream purchases. Sichuan also restricted industrial power use (Aug 15–20). Net effect is lower regional demand, likely weighing on copper prices near term. | SMM reports China’s domestic copper inventories in bonded zones fell 22,600 mt to 194,000 mt as of Aug 19, with drops in Shanghai and Guangdong. Power rationing reduced domestic cathode output, boosting premiums. Meanwhile, an open import window increased purchases and accelerated movement from bonded warehouses, cutting stocks quickly. | Chilean authorities confirmed a massive sinkhole near Lundin Mining’s Alcaparrosa copper project in Chile and attributed it to mining “overexploitation.” The company’s Chilean unit is facing charges over the event, implying potential scrutiny and operational disruption risk for the mine’s copper supply. | Alpha Copper signed a non-binding LOI to acquire CAVU, creating a larger portfolio of copper exploration projects in BC and Yukon, including Star and Hopper. The deal offers a 60% premium and remains subject to due diligence and approvals. It is primarily corporate news without immediate supply impact.

SMM reported rising copper concentrate inventories at Chinese ports (up 36,000 mt WoW to 807,000 mt) with smelters having sufficient goods. Treatment charges were lower than the prior week and some delayed cargos are arriving soon, supporting ample supply. While power rationing had limited impact, overall conditions are bearish for copper concentrate tightness. | SMM says copper prices may stay high as power rationing extends smelter outages, with possible 30,000–40,000 mt output reductions in August. Port arrivals remain limited, while Chinese inventories fall and premiums stay elevated (500–600 yuan/mt), limiting any near-term sharp decline. | SMM says copper inventories across major Chinese markets fell 2,800 mt over the weekend to 58,500 mt, far below last year. Power rationing in east China reduced smelter output and copper arrivals to Shanghai, despite slightly higher imports. Further power rationing may limit consumption and keep supplies tight. | Xanadu Mines received Australian FIRB approval for Zijin to acquire shares and fund phases of its Kharmagtai copper-gold project in Mongolia. The approval supports ongoing investment, including prefeasibility and exploration funding, with expected average output around 37,000 tonnes of copper per year, subject to further approvals. | Reuters reports copper fell 0.4% as traders focused on weakening demand prospects. China’s manufacturing slowdown from COVID lockdowns and macro troubles in the US and Europe reduced expectations for power and construction demand. Fed rate hikes boosted the dollar and hurt sentiment, although falling LME stocks offered some support. | Power Metal Resources’ shares rose after drilling at its Molopo Farms Complex Project in Botswana intersected nickel-sulphide mineralisation, including 0.443% Ni over 7m and higher-grade intervals. Management said the magnetic conductor edge is mineralised and more drills are planned, boosting near-term nickel supply expectations. | Twin Metals says it will sue the Biden administration to reclaim federal mineral leases for a proposed underground copper-nickel mine near Ely, after the administration cancelled its leases earlier this year and restarted withdrawals of more than 200,000 acres from mineral development. | Twin Metals says it will sue the Biden administration to reclaim federal mineral leases for a proposed underground copper-nickel mine near Ely, Minnesota. The administration previously canceled the leases and restarted withdrawal of 200,000+ acres from exploration and development, prompting the dispute. The outcome timing is uncertain. | Antofagasta’s Twin Metals subsidiary sued the Biden administration to overturn the January cancellation of mineral leases for its proposed underground copper and nickel mine in northern Minnesota. Twin Metals argues the decision was illegal and should allow a regulatory review to demonstrate the project meets state and federal standards. | Antofagasta’s Twin Metals sued the Biden administration to reverse the cancellation of Minnesota mineral leases tied to its proposed underground copper and nickel mine. The company argues the January action was illegal and it should be allowed a proper regulatory review. Near-term production changes are uncertain, limiting immediate price impact. | Antofagasta’s Twin Metals unit sued the Biden administration to overturn the January cancellation of mineral leases for its proposed underground copper and nickel mine in northern Minnesota. The firm argues the decision was illegal and seeks to re-enter regulatory review to demonstrate compliance with state and federal standards. | Antofagasta’s Twin Metals unit sued the Biden administration to overturn the cancellation of mineral leases for its proposed underground copper and nickel mine in northern Minnesota. The company argues the January decision was illegal and it should be allowed to demonstrate compliance in the regulatory review process. | Antofagasta’s Twin Metals sued the Biden administration to reverse Minnesota mineral-lease cancellations for its proposed underground copper and nickel mine. The company argues the January decision was illegal and should allow it to demonstrate compliance through regulatory reviews. The move could ease supply-risk expectations for both metals. | Antofagasta’s Twin Metals sued the Biden administration to overturn the January cancellation of mineral leases for its proposed copper and nickel mine in northern Minnesota. Twin Metals argues the cancellation was illegal and that it should be allowed to demonstrate compliance with state and federal standards during regulatory review. The move supports supply expectations. | Antofagasta’s Twin Metals subsidiary sued the Biden administration to reverse the cancellation of mineral leases for its proposed underground copper and nickel mine in northern Minnesota. The company says the January decision was illegal and should be revisited through the regulatory process, potentially improving near-term supply expectations. | Antofagasta’s Twin Metals subsidiary sued the Biden administration to reverse cancellation of mineral leases for its proposed underground copper and nickel mine in northern Minnesota. Twin Metals argues the January decision was illegal and seeks a regulatory review where it can demonstrate the project meets state and federal standards. | Antofagasta’s Twin Metals sued the Biden administration to reverse the cancellation of mineral leases for its proposed underground copper and nickel mine in northern Minnesota. Twin Metals argues the January decision was illegal and it should be able to demonstrate compliance with state and federal standards in the regulatory review process. | Antofagasta’s Twin Metals sued the Biden administration to overturn the January cancellation of mineral leases for its proposed underground copper and nickel mine in northern Minnesota. Twin Metals argues the decision was illegal and that it should be allowed to demonstrate in regulatory review that the project meets state and federal standards. | City Power praised police for arresting two suspects found with stolen copper cables worth R1.2m near Alexandra in Johannesburg. Officers intercepted a truck on its way to a recycler and recovered copper cables and bars. City Power said it has seen multiple theft incidents over the preceding nights. | Antofagasta’s Twin Metals sued the Biden administration to overturn cancellation of mineral leases for its proposed underground copper and nickel mine in northern Minnesota. The company argues the January cancellation was illegal and it should be allowed to prove the project can meet state and federal standards in ongoing review. | Antofagasta’s Twin Metals subsidiary sued the Biden administration to reverse the January cancellation of mineral leases for its proposed underground copper and nickel mine in northern Minnesota. Twin Metals argues the decision was illegal and that it should be allowed to demonstrate compliance during the regulatory review process. | Antofagasta’s Twin Metals sued the Biden administration to challenge the cancellation of Minnesota mineral leases for its proposed underground copper and nickel mine. Twin Metals said the January decision was illegal and should allow its project to be assessed under regulatory standards, potentially improving near-term supply expectations.

Culpeo Minerals reported expanded high-priority drill targets after mapping confirmed large-scale copper oxide and breccia mineralisation at the Lana Corina project in Chile. Recent drilling returned multiple substantial copper and molybdenum intercepts, and the mineralised footprint increased to 500m by 400m. The update is exploration-focused. | China’s copper scrap imports rose 4.02% year-on-year to 155,168.64 mt in July, though they fell 6% versus the previous month. The US remained the biggest supplier at 26,559.12 mt, down 15.21% month-on-month but up 16.55% year-on-year. | SMM reports East China copper scrap throughput rose sharply week-on-week as cargo holders sold some high-priced inventories, while downstream secondary rod firms urgently restocked. Copper cathode prices stayed high, spreads to scrap widened, imports fell, inventories were extremely low, and power rationing restricted output—supporting bullish near-term copper sentiment. | Aston Bay announced that drilling at its Storm Copper Project in Nunavut intersected over 68m of stratiform copper sulfide mineralization linked to a large EM conductor plate, with six other similar deeper anomalies still untested. The discovery suggests a potentially major sedimentary copper system; assays are expected next month.

S&P Global/IHS Markit research warns a copper supply gap will open by 2025, with demand surging from electrification and recycling/substitution not closing the shortfall. It argues higher prices are the likely market response to persistent deficits, potentially constraining the net-zero energy transition. Aluminium substitution is limited to about 1% of demand. | SMM reports Shanghai spot copper premiums for cathode versus the SHFE 2209 contract fell to around 520 yuan/mt, down from the prior day, as imported copper inflows rose. Improved east/central China smelter output after power rationing also pressured premiums, though declines were moderated by still-high levels in Guangdong. | Fluor announced first copper output from Anglo American’s Quellaveco mining project in Peru. The start of production suggests incremental copper supply is beginning to reach the market, which can weigh on near-term copper price expectations, though full volume ramp-up may limit the magnitude. | QC Copper and Gold reported new drill assay results from the Opemiska property in Quebec, with multiple holes intersecting wide in-pit mineralization zones including grades up to about 1.49% copper. The program aims to upgrade inferred to indicated copper resources via strip-ratio reduction, with no immediate production impact.

Rio Tinto raised its offer to buy the remaining 49% stake in Turquoise Hill Resources, which controls the Oyu Tolgoi project in Mongolia. Rio already operates the mine and holds 51%. The move aims to secure full value and control over a major copper asset, potentially supporting copper supply expectations. | South32 posted record profit, saying higher realised prices—especially aluminium and metallurgical coal—boosted earnings. The company also highlighted moves to low-carbon metals, adding copper exposure via Sierra Gorda and expanding aluminium capacity through Mozal and a restarted Brazil smelter. No direct near-term price shock is announced. | Rio Tinto increased its offer to $3.1 billion to buy out minority shareholders of copper developer Turquoise Hill Resources, after Turquoise Hill rejected an earlier $2.7 billion bid and halted talks. Rio is seeking full control, raising its stake from 50.79% to 100%, in a bid-driven corporate process. | Clean Air Metals reported 2022 drill assay results from the Thunder Bay North Current deposit, including copper grades up to ~0.98% and nickel up to ~0.56%, and it is advancing a prefeasibility study with metallurgical testing and resource updates. The news is exploration-focused with no immediate production impact, limiting near-term price effects. | Faraday Copper Corp. announces that its shares began trading on the OTCQX in the U.S. under symbol CPPKF, improving visibility and liquidity for U.S. investors. The company remains listed on the Canadian Securities Exchange and continues developing its U.S.-based copper projects. | Aston Bay announced further near-surface copper mineralization from three diamond drill holes at the Storm Copper Project in Nunavut, expanding the high-grade 2750N zone and leaving mineralization open along strike and at depth. Assays are pending, and no immediate supply or production changes were stated. | Aranjin Resources announced follow-up auger drilling at its Baavhai Uul copper project in Mongolia, reporting extensive geochemical anomalies at the “Victory” copper-nickel discovery, including up to 1,252 ppm copper and 494 ppm nickel. The anomaly remains open and further drilling is planned to outline extensions. | Aranjin Resources announced results from initial follow-up auger geochemical drilling at its Baavhai Uul Copper Project in Mongolia, highlighting a large copper-nickel discovery (“Victory”) under shallow alluvial cover. Reported results include up to 1,252 ppm copper and 494 ppm nickel, with the anomaly remaining open for further drilling. | American West Metals reported further visual results from diamond drilling at its Storm Copper Project on Nunavut’s Somerset Island. Holes ST22-07 and ST22-08 intersected thick intervals of massive chalcocite-dominant copper sulphides, and ST22-09 tested an EM anomaly. The copper mineralization remains open along strike and at depth. | Reuters (Aug 25) cites ANZ analysts saying China’s energy shortage could accelerate grid and renewable projects, lifting demand for copper used in solar/wind and power cabling. It also expects aluminium benefits from ultra-high-voltage line and electrical cable needs. The report forecasts deficits that could pressure prices higher. | ANZ says China’s energy shortage and power constraints may accelerate grid and renewable projects, increasing demand for copper used in solar, wind and cabling. The report also expects higher aluminium demand from ultra-high-voltage transmission lines and electrical cables, widening deficits and supporting higher prices.

Nevada Copper said it has agreed non-binding terms for up to US$93 million of liquidity to restart and ramp up its Pumpkin Hollow underground copper mine. The plan includes de-bottlenecking and mill restart timing, targeting higher hoisting rates in 2023, improving expected copper output despite current production pause. | Nevada Copper announced non-binding terms for up to $93 million in liquidity to restart and ramp up its Pumpkin Hollow underground copper mine. The funding package supports de-bottlenecking and mill restart planning, with working capital deferrals until copper deliveries resume. Overall, it reduces restart risk and supports copper supply expectations. | Nevada Copper agreed non-binding terms for up to US$93 million to fund and de-risk the restart and ramp-up of its Pumpkin Hollow underground copper mine. The plan includes de-bottlenecking, capital works, and a scheduled mill restart, targeting higher underground production rates from 2023 onward. | Nevada Copper announced non-binding financing of up to $93m to restart and ramp up its Pumpkin Hollow underground copper mine in Nevada. The liquidity package and creditor deferrals are intended to de-bottleneck operations and enable mill restart, with copper production targeted to rise through 2023 as deliveries resume. | SMM reports that as summer heat cools, copper smelters in eastern and central China are resuming output after earlier power-rationing cuts. This is complemented by higher customs clearance and increased imported copper inflows, easing spot-market worries about tightening supply. | Triple Flag agreed to provide up to US$30 million in financing to support Nevada Copper’s restart plan for the Pumpkin Hollow underground copper mine. The liquidity is intended to fund and ramp up production, improving short-term supply expectations, though closing depends on definitive documentation and regulatory approvals. | Peru’s Ministry of Economy and Finance predicted copper prices could fall to $3.40/lb in 2023 from $3.90/lb this year. Peru is a major copper producer and mining taxes depend heavily on copper output. Quellaveco’s planned ramp is cited as an offset, but the outlook remains pessimistic. | SMM said China’s domestic bonded copper inventories fell 18,900 mt to 175,100 mt as of Aug 26, with nine straight weeks of declines. The drop is linked to shipments from warehouses as the import window opened, limited Guangdong port arrivals, tighter social inventories, and higher spot premiums drawing more metal into bonded zones. | SMM says copper inventories across major Chinese markets reached 68,300 mt as of Aug 26, up 9,800 mt from Monday. While Guangdong fell, Shanghai rose sharply due to greater imported arrivals. Total inventories remain far below last year, but SMM expects weekly inventories to keep rising as power rationing eases and demand may weaken. | China imported 96,000 mt of copper anode in July, up MoM, but spot blister copper transactions remain scarce and logistics from Africa (including Zambia) is still unsmooth. African supply is tight due to maintenance and incidents (LCS, CCS boiler leakage). Lower scrap availability also limits blister output as smelter demand rises. | London copper rose to a two-week high as China demand optimism grew after new stimulus measures and infrastructure plans. Prices also found support from tight supply and very low inventories. US and Germany data improved broader demand sentiment. The LME three-month contract and SHFE copper futures ended higher. | EMX Royalty announced an agreement for its Mesa Well porphyry copper project in Arizona, where Intrepid will fund earn-in payments and exploration. EMX will retain a 2% NSR and receive advance and milestone royalty payments. The update supports future copper optionality but offers no near-term market supply shock. | Nevada Copper plans to restart and ramp up its Pumpkin Hollow underground copper mine in Nevada, citing de-bottlenecking and completion of critical work. The company also agreed non-binding terms for up to US$93 million in financing to support the restart, affecting expectations around future copper supply rather than immediate output.

The study develops Cu2O-containing marine antifouling coatings that use amphiphilic diblock copolymers to control and prolong copper-ion release in seawater. It reports strong antifouling performance in marine field tests and comparable results to commercial self-polishing coatings. Overall, it suggests niche copper use but no immediate market impact. | Granite Creek Copper announced a non-brokered private placement to raise up to $640,000 for exploration and development. Funds target its Carmacks copper-gold-silver project and a newly acquired Star copper-nickel-PGE project in Canada. The company positions these projects for future copper output, but it doesn’t indicate immediate production changes.

SMM reports East China copper scrap throughput rose 29% WoW to about 22,000 mt as copper prices stabilized above 63,000 yuan/mt. The cathode vs scrap spread widened, improving scrap economics and raising secondary rod operating rates to 43.51%. Demand may cool slightly, but improving logistics should support supply. | Sandfire Resources plans to invest $71.9m to expand the Motheo copper mine in Botswana, following completion of a feasibility study for the A4 deposit and processing plant upgrades. Copper output is expected to grow toward a 5.2 Mtpa hub. Botswana can take up to a 15% interest. | Sandfire says it has completed a definitive feasibility study to expand its Motheo copper mine in Botswana from 3.2 mtpa to 5.2 mtpa, including development of the A4 deposit and a larger processing plant. Construction is scheduled to start March quarter FY23 with higher throughput from FY24, subject to approvals. | Kentucky Transportation Cabinet officials say copper thieves have damaged thousands of Louisville highway light poles, stealing roughly 66,000 feet of copper wire (about $45,000) across Interstates 64, 65 and 264. Crews and police are responding to deter theft, but the local volume is unlikely to move copper prices meaningfully.

El Mercurio reported that Codelco’s output outlook will likely deteriorate further next year because of project delays and lower ore grades at Chuquicamata. Codelco cut its 2022 production estimate to around 1.5 million mt from 1.61 million mt, citing difficulties implementing investment projects. | Chile’s National Statistics Institute reported that copper production fell 8.6% year-on-year in July to 430,028 metric tons. As Chile is the world’s largest copper producer, reduced output can signal tighter copper supply expectations, typically exerting downward pressure on nearby copper price sentiment. | Copper is reportedly extending its worst losing streak since 2008, with prices pressured by renewed growth fears. The news framing points to deteriorating near-term demand expectations for industrial metals, supporting the view that copper faces continued downside pressure in the immediate trading window. | Rio Tinto won approval for its $3.3 billion takeover of Turquoise Hill Resources, clearing the way to partner with Mongolia’s government on developing the Oyu Tolgoi copper mine. The deal supports ambitions for the mine to become one of the world’s top copper producers by 2030, boosting sentiment toward copper. | Sandfire plans to spend an additional $71.9 million to expand its under-construction Motheo copper mine in Botswana, adding a new processing plant and open pit mine. Output is expected to ramp up after environmental and mining approvals, raising long-run copper supply expectations in the Kalahari Copper Belt. | Aston Bay announced initial assay results from drilling at its Storm Copper Project in Nunavut, highlighting 41 metres at 4.18% copper and narrower intervals exceeding 20% Cu, with mineralization remaining open for expansion. Further assays and planned metallurgical work are expected over coming days and weeks. | First Tellurium Corp. says work is underway to investigate potential copper porphyry targets at its Deer Horn property in west-central British Columbia, following earlier copper showings and a prior PEA suggesting a hidden porphyry system. The company aims to define drill targets for future exploration. | MacDonald Mines reported Phase 1 drill results at its SPJ property, including a 0.41% CuEq (with copper values and associated gold/silver) over 90.44m at the Alwyn trend and cobalt anomalies. While it highlights potential for broader polymetallic systems including nickel, no nickel/aluminium results are given.

SMM reports copper wire and cable producers’ operating rate is expected to rise in September as power rationing eases and government investment in power supply and non-fossil energy (especially PV) increases. Pandemic-related disruption and weak real estate/state grid demand limit the upside, but copper-linked production should improve. | SMM says Chinese copper inventories across major markets totaled 75,000 mt, up 1,700 mt from Monday, with increases in most regions except Shanghai. Shanghai fell due to fewer imported arrivals, while Guangdong rose on higher imported and domestic inflows. Consumption is expected to improve next week as prices decline, but near-term inventories are supportive. | Copper prices fell as hawkish ECB and Fed messaging supported higher interest rates, pushing up bond yields and the US dollar and pressuring USD-priced commodities. Fundamentals also weakened: power-restricted smelters resumed production and cathode supply rose, while downstream demand/order flow stayed sluggish despite softer supply conditions. | Los Andes Copper announces closing an additional US$5 million convertible debenture investment by Queen’s Road Capital. Proceeds will fund expansion drilling at the Vizcachitas copper project in Chile and related studies. Management says drilling will commence soon, supporting progress toward potential future copper production. | Copper prices touched a one-month low earlier amid China’s extended COVID restrictions in Shenzhen and a lockdown in Chengdu, raising recession and demand concerns; potential smelter shutdowns could reduce supply. Risk sentiment improved after U.S. jobs data eased Fed hike fears. Aluminium support came from Aldel mothballing capacity due to energy costs.

Udokan Copper plans to begin copper product exports in 2023, mainly to China. The company says volumes are small and Far Eastern port throughput should be sufficient, citing Ministry of Transport projects to expand port capacity. The note also highlights increased 2022 transport loads from Russia’s shift to Asian exports. | SMM reports that Goldman Sachs expects copper prices to rise from about $7,200/mt in the second half of 2022 to $8,000/mt in the first half of 2023.
