The company says it will begin development actions, valuing the reserves at more than $50 billion.

Culpeo Minerals reports maiden drilling at its Lana Corina project in Chile returned shallow, high-grade copper intercepts (up to 4.01% Cu over 2m) plus molybdenum. Management says the grades support an open-pittable resource potential and follow a recent large-scale copper discovery, with multiple drill targets designated. | Peru’s July copper output fell 6.6% year over year to 195,234 tonnes, after Antamina and Southern Copper underperformed. With Peru supplying about 60% of its shipment exports through mining, renewed protests and potential supply risks following the Las Bambas truce raise bearish near-term expectations for copper. | SMM reports spot copper cathode premiums rising to 380-440 yuan/mt over SHFE 2209, up 10 yuan/mt, as SHFE 2209/2210 spreads narrowed. Failed customs clearance for imported copper and low transaction sources supported higher premiums, though some traders were cautious due to still-expected imports and SHFE 2209 delivery. | Copper futures rose on a weaker dollar and expectations that China will accelerate infrastructure spending. Reuters cites the shift as the main driver for improved metals demand. LME cash/three-month and Shanghai copper gained, alongside price increases in aluminium and nickel, reflecting broad bullish sentiment across industrial metals. | Proactive lists several company headlines, including Chalice’s work on a polymetallic nickel-copper deposit extension, Poseidon’s partnership while refocusing on restarting its Black Swan nickel project, and Caspin’s “significant” nickel-copper-PGE drilling results. These updates are sentiment-supportive for nickel and co-product copper near term. | Mundoro Capital and JOGMEC announced the start of an exploration program on the newly approved JOGMEC-Mundoro copper project in western Bulgaria, following government approval and an earn-in/option structure. The release focuses on geology and historical production but does not signal any near-term production or supply impact. | Australia’s Foreign Investment Review Board has approved Metals Acquisition Corp’s acquisition of the CSA copper mine. The decision clears a key regulatory hurdle for the ownership transfer of a copper-producing asset, which can reduce disruption risk and provide mild near-term support for copper supply expectations. | McEwen Copper said it entered a binding term sheet with Rio Tinto’s KEX for an option to earn a 60% interest in the Elder Creek porphyry copper project in Nevada by spending $18 million over up to seven years. Afterward, a 60:40 joint venture would form. No near-term production timeline was given. | Electric Royalties closed its acquisition of a 0.5% gross revenue royalty on the Zonia copper oxide project in Arizona, with options to acquire additional royalty interests. The transaction involves cash and shares and is tied to future prefeasibility work. It is a project-funding event, not an immediate supply shift. | World Copper announced the closing of a royalty sale for its Zonia copper oxide project, receiving cash and equity and allowing Electric Royalties options to increase the royalty and acquire similar rights on Zonia Norte. The release reiterates that project economics are sensitive to copper prices and plans a PEA update. | Mundoro Capital and JOGMEC announced an exploration program for the JOGMEC-Mundoro copper project in western Bulgaria, following government approval and an earn-in agreement. The project targets sediment-hosted stratiform copper(-silver) mineralization with historical production and many copper-lead-zinc occurrences, but no near-term production changes are stated. | BHP and Riverside extend their exploration funding agreement for a fourth year, directing about US$1.6M toward five copper properties in Sonora, Mexico. The program emphasizes derisking and preparation for future drilling and drill decisions, with additional potential budgets in later operational phases if projects advance. | The piece says shares of Taseko Mines, a copper miner, were weak because copper prices retreated from prior highs. It also notes commodities spiked earlier on supply concerns tied to Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, implying that easing concerns and/or supply expectations contributed to softer copper.

China’s August unwrought copper and copper product imports rose year-on-year as July-August price declines prompted producer buying and arbitrage between Shanghai and London increased inflows. Electricity rationing during a heatwave tightened domestic supply and lowered SFE stocks, supporting copper availability, though overall demand remained soft amid property weakness. | China Customs data show copper ore and concentrate imports of 2.27 million mt in August and 16.64 million mt in Jan-Aug, up 9% year-on-year. This suggests higher feedstock supply for Chinese processing, which can support near-term copper market sentiment. Rare earth flows are also reported but are not directly tracked here. | Faraday Copper announced assay results from five drill holes at its Copper Creek Arizona project, reporting multiple copper intercepts including 1.05% Cu over 165.8 m and 2.61% Cu over 34.0 m. The company plans to incorporate results into an updated resource estimate and PEA, aiming to advance project development. | Infinitum Copper reports completion of an MT geophysics interpretation at its La Adelita project in Mexico, identifying untested conductive anomalies aligned with copper-gold-silver skarn mineralization at Cerro Grande and Las Trancas. Diamond drilling results and assays are pending, and Phase 2 drilling is planned once rain improves conditions. | Camino Minerals reported final results from its 2022 drilling at the Los Chapitos copper project in Peru, including multiple high-grade copper oxide intercepts such as 55.5m at 0.93% Cu from surface and 7.5m at 2.58% Cu. The company plans solubility testing and more drilling for potential heap-leach development. | The report (via its headline) states that Panama’s copper exports rose as Cobre Panamá increased production. Higher output and export availability can add near-term supply to the market, which generally puts downward pressure on copper prices over the coming days to weeks. | Flying Nickel reported initial drill results from its Minago nickel sulphide project, with assays averaging 0.41% nickel over 113.55 meters and trace copper. Management says results support down-dip nickel mineralization expansion, with continuous Pt/Pd intercepts. The update is exploration-focused with no production changes. | The piece argues that copper is central to decarbonization and highlights projected shortfalls: demand is expected to nearly double by 2035 and rise further, while primary copper production growth is much smaller. It suggests higher prices may suppress demand but still implies tightening fundamentals. | Vale forecast global nickel demand to rise 44% by 2030 on strong EV-battery growth, alongside an increase in Vale’s own medium-term production. It also expects copper demand to grow about 20% by 2030 and projects a copper structural deficit due to insufficient supply, supporting bullish sentiment for both metals. | News describes widespread force majeure and shutdowns across metals smelting and fabrication, citing sky-high energy prices and weak demand. Examples include an aluminum smelter closure in Montenegro and primary aluminum cutbacks in Slovenia, plus copper mine/smelt-related disruptions (e.g., Cuajone suspension, Kennecott force majeure), implying near-term supply tightness. | The article says several copper and aluminium production sites worldwide have halted or declared force majeure, citing high electricity costs, weak demand, and disruptions such as protests, earthquakes, or strikes. Examples include an aluminium smelter shutdown and copper cathode/ mine curtailments, implying reduced near-term supply. | Multiple metals smelting and production operations are shutting down worldwide under force majeure and citing very high electricity prices and weak demand. Examples include an aluminium smelter closure in Montenegro, copper mine/smelter disruptions in Peru and Chile, and an electrochemical strike affecting a nickel refinery in Europe.

Workers at BHP’s Escondida copper mine in Chile voted to strike over safety concerns. The action includes partial stoppages Sept. 12 and 14, then an indefinite stoppage until BHP reaches a deal. Escondida says it complied with obligations and temporarily closed affected areas, calling the strike unlawful. | Workers at BHP’s Escondida copper mine in northern Chile voted nearly unanimously to strike over safety concerns. The plan includes partial stoppages on Sept. 12-14 and an indefinite stoppage until a deal is reached. The company says it complied with obligations and temporarily closed affected areas, calling the strike unlawful. | China’s August imports of unwrought copper rose 8.7% month-on-month and 28.2% year-on-year, while Shanghai Futures Exchange stocks fell to the lowest since January before rising only modestly by early September. The higher imports likely reflect arbitrage and only mild demand recovery, but falling inventories can still underpin copper. | The piece reports that Panama’s copper exports were lifted by increased output from Cobre Panamá. Higher production and export volumes can raise near-term supply availability, which typically weighs on copper prices over the short horizon as market expectations shift toward greater availability. | Aston Bay announced drilling results from its Storm Copper Project on Somerset Island, including 48m at 2.92% copper and other high-grade intervals. The company expects further assays and metallurgical tests for potential direct-shipping product development. It advances the 2750N Zone toward resource estimation. | Talon Metals announced assay results from 13–14 new drill holes at its Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project in Minnesota, including a 29.39-meter interval grading 1.71% nickel and 2.21% nickel equivalent. The company says drilling will continue to expand resources and supports ongoing feasibility planning for 2026 production. | York Harbour Metals reports preliminary assays from Phase 3 drilling at its Newfoundland York Harbour Copper-Zinc Project, including a drill interval with 2.86% copper over 14.77 metres. Management says the discovery expands the D Zone and could extend along strike and downdip. Results for additional core are pending. | Soaring European power and gas prices have driven widespread aluminum smelter curtailments, including cuts at Neuss (Germany) and Dunkerque (France). Despite the supply reductions, aluminum prices are still falling to a 17-month low, with recession fears and rising Chinese output capping upside. Eurometaux warns the crisis also affects copper and nickel. | NiCAN Ltd. announced initial drill results from its Wine project in Manitoba shortly after listing on the TSX Venture Exchange. Hole 22-05 intersected multiple mineralized zones, including 27.3 metres grading about 2% nickel and 1.81% copper, supporting exploration optimism and mildly improving sentiment for both metals. | Comex Copper for September delivery settled 2.85% higher at $3.5360 per pound, gaining 9.80 cents. The contract posted its largest one-day dollar and percentage gain since late July, rising on three of the past four sessions and reaching the highest settlement since Aug. 30, 2022. | Vale is reportedly looking to ramp up output of copper and nickel in Brazil. If investors expect higher near-term supply, it typically reduces price support for both metals. However, the provided excerpt lacks specifics on timing, capex, and expected volumes, keeping the price impact magnitude uncertain. | Osisko Gold Royalties said it acquired a 1.0% NSR royalty on the Marimaca copper project in Antofagasta, Chile for US$15.5 million. The release describes the project’s oxide heap-leach profile, resources, and plans for a resource update and definitive feasibility study, without indicating imminent production changes.

Forecasts for a copper “super cycle” are temporarily on hold as global demand weakens amid slowing economies. The copper spot price has fallen sharply since March, with China data, recession fears, and higher rates weighing on demand. Analysts cut forecasts, implying further near-term downside. | Hot Chili Limited announced strong copper-silver drill intercepts at its San Antonio resource with grades above current model, supporting an upgrade from Inferred to Indicated and enabling inclusion in open-pit reserve estimates. It also highlights promising Valentina results and planned follow-up drilling, boosting copper project outlook. | Hot Chili reported strong copper-silver drill intersections at Chile’s Costa Fuego, particularly upgrading the San Antonio deposit from Inferred toward Indicated status. Higher-grade results support adding ore to open-pit reserve estimates for the Pre-feasibility Study and planning further drilling at the adjacent Valentina prospect. | SMM reports spot copper concentrate trading improved, with imported copper concentrate TCs rising above $80/mt and Qinhuangdao port re-trading showing $84–85/mt levels. Inventories at the port fell week-on-week, while higher costs and potential stoppages/strikes at major mines raise concentrate supply risk. | Mitsubishi Materials will buy out remaining partners in the Onahama copper smelter in Fukushima, taking full ownership from March 31. The company says the change will improve decision-making to help the smelter handle rising electricity and fuel costs, and it plans recycled-material pre-processing. | Talon Metals and Rio Tinto’s Tamarack nickel project in central Minnesota continues to expand as explorers find thicker, higher-grade nickel-copper mineralization outside the defined resource zone. The updated results improve perceived future supply prospects for nickel and, secondarily, copper, potentially supporting prices modestly. | The headline says Rio’s bid to take over a giant copper mine faces opposition from a top investor. Such disputes over control of a major copper asset can increase uncertainty around production and supply timing, potentially weighing on short-term copper market expectations over coming days to weeks. | Comex Copper front-month for September delivery ended the week 4.66% higher at $3.5810, up 15.95 cents per pound. The contract gained for multiple sessions, reaching the highest settlement since Aug. 29, and is up modestly month-to-date despite remaining far below recent highs.

Camino Minerals said it received the DIA environmental permit for its Maria Cecilia copper-gold porphyry project in Peru, authorizing up to 23,000 metres of drilling. The company plans drilling on 20 platforms after additional start-of-operations permitting, aiming to test higher copper grades and extend mineralization. | C3 Metals reported partial assay results from its Bellas Gate Project in Jamaica, including 309 metres grading 0.44% copper (from 15 metres downhole) and 0.33 g/t gold. Two additional holes were completed at Epidote Ridge with assays pending, supporting continued drilling on copper-gold targets. | Mitsubishi Materials will buy the remaining stakes in its Onahama Smelting and Refining copper JV to gain full ownership. The plan targets higher operating resilience amid rising electricity and fuel costs, including new pre-processing and e-scrap capacity. The smelter is a key eastern Japan copper supply source. | Tartisan Nickel released a preliminary economic assessment for its Kenbridge underground nickel sulphide project in Ontario, citing low pre-production capital, expandable resources and potential early production in about 3.5 years. The PEA includes ore grades of ~0.97% nickel and ~0.52% copper, but no immediate supply impact. | Comex copper for September delivery settled 1.24% higher at $3.6255 per pound, gaining 4.45 cents. The contract rose for three straight sessions, up 18.75 cents (5.45%) over that span and five of the past six days, with the highest settlement since Aug. 29, 2022.

SMM data shows copper inventories in China’s domestic bonded zones fell 25,000 mt from Sep 2 to 134,700 mt as of Sep 9, the lowest level since tracking began. Shanghai and Guangdong bonded inventories dropped further, with ongoing inflows to the domestic market and expectations of continued declines this week. | SMM data shows copper inventories across major Chinese markets rose 19,100 mt to 89,800 mt, up versus last week’s level and higher across most regions during the Mid-Autumn holiday. SMM expects continued domestic supply-driven builds this week, while downstream restocking remains weak near SHFE 2209 delivery. | Lara Exploration reported results from seven follow-up drill holes at the Cupuzeiro target within its Planalto Copper Project in Brazil, including a long sulphide-copper intersection of 380.79 metres at 0.575% copper equivalent and higher-grade zones. The company also drilled around the Homestead target, finding some copper potential. | Infinitum Copper reported initial diamond drill results from the La Adelita project, including 20.60 m grading 1.91% copper (plus Au and Ag) in skarn mineralization at the Cerro Grande zone. Geophysics suggest mineralization may strengthen at depth, supporting continued exploration and potential future supply improvements. | The piece cites BloombergNEF projections of rising long-term copper demand and notes copper-focused corporate updates: Torq’s expanded drilling and funding for Chile projects and the EPA draft UIC permit for the Florence Copper Project. It also references company results affected by lower copper prices, outages, and higher costs. | Go Metals says initial drilling at its 100% owned HSP nickel-copper-PGE project in Quebec visually confirmed nickel-copper sulphides across five zones, including several wide intercepts. Assay results are pending, and the company plans more drilling as samples are sent for lab analysis. | Go Metals reported that drilling at its 100% owned HSP nickel-copper project in Quebec visually identified nickel and copper sulphides across five zones. The company sent initial samples for lab assays and noted wide mineralized intervals. The update is exploration-focused with no confirmed assay or production impact. | Three miners died in a mudslide at Sierra Metals’ Yauricocha copper-zinc mine in Peru, prompting a temporary suspension until conditions are safe. The company produces significant copper from Yauricocha. Given Peru’s large role in copper supply, the outage may pressure near-term copper prices downward. | Premium Nickel Resources Ltd. released additional assay results from its 100% owned Selebi mine in Botswana, reporting 6.40 metres of 0.52% nickel and 2.63% copper. Improved assay information can strengthen market expectations for future nickel output, with a smaller positive signal for copper.

Prosecutors allege a man broke into shuttered coal-fired power plants in Pennsylvania and Maryland to steal copper wire for resale at scrap yards, causing substantial local damage. Copper theft increases scrap availability and regional supply into recyclers, but reported volumes appear too limited for broader copper price moves within weeks. | SMM reports copper cathode rod producers’ operating rates rose slightly MoM but fell YoY to 74.44% in August. Pandemic in Jiangxi, power rationing in parts of East China, and high south China cathode premiums reduced or suspended production. September operating rates are expected to recover as disruptions fade. | SMM says copper foil producers’ average operating rate rose to 86.19% in August, helped by restocking when copper prices retreated and improved lithium-battery and some electronics demand. However, September operating rates face downward pressure as COVID outbreaks delay capacity commissioning and weaken electronics consumer sentiment. | Highland Copper provided an update on its Copperwood and White Pine projects in Michigan, including an expected feasibility study update, permitting progress, and potential production of copper concentrates and possibly cathodes via on-site leaching. The company cites strong EV/renewables-driven copper demand and higher expected input costs. | Fabled Copper Corp. reports completion of its 2022 exploration program on the Muskwa Copper Project in northern British Columbia. The company completed extensive field days, sampling, mapping, geophysics, and permitting progress toward future drilling and potential 43-101 work. No production or near-term supply changes are announced. | Highland Copper updates on its Copperwood and White Pine projects in Michigan, including plans to issue an updated feasibility study, ongoing permitting (incl. federal pumping-station approval), and potential process/cost improvements. The company cites strong copper demand for electrification but no immediate production or supply shock is announced. | Proactive’s article is a roundup of investor news including copper and nickel exploration updates. It notes Majuba Hill Copper’s Nevada drilling completion and Fabled Copper’s Muskwa exploration results, while Copper Fox delays drilling. It also cites Nickel North Exploration expanding mineralization and other battery-metal project work, implying mixed signals.

Magmatic Resources says it has achieved a very large copper drill hit, described as half a kilometre long and still being extended, in an “elephant country” setting. The announcement relates directly to potential copper resources, which can slightly lift sentiment, though it does not confirm near-term output. | France’s regulator Arcep has cleared Orange to begin decommissioning copper telephone lines in selected communes from end-2024, with full country disconnection by 2030. Orange cites €500m annual savings and says copper won’t be cut without fibre or other alternatives, though critics warn rural fibre delays. | Technavio’s report forecasts growth for the copper alloy wire market (about USD 18.6B incremental growth) driven by electrical/electronics demand and APAC’s large market share. It describes longer-term market expansion but provides no concrete near-term supply disruptions, inventory changes, tariffs, or contract price impacts for copper, nickel, or aluminium. | The piece examines China’s copper spot tightness, explaining what is tightening the market and the likely duration. It also assesses whether China’s copper consumption will improve and what factors drive demand. The discussion centers on copper market balance, implying near-term price support from supply tightness. | Fastmarkets’ pre-conference report highlights tight refined copper fundamentals in 2022: low on-warrant stocks on COMEX and SHFE, recovering but still constrained LME availability, and persistently elevated physical premiums in Europe and China. It links supply frictions and limited warrant deliverability, including some Russian cathode restrictions, to supportive near-term copper pricing. | Rio Tinto’s increased control of the Oyu Tolgoi copper-gold mine faces opposition from minority shareholders, led by Pentwater, after additional stake purchases. The project is expected to reach large-scale copper output, but investor disputes raise near-term uncertainty as experts still forecast a copper supply shortfall by 2025. | Aluminium prices rose as Europe’s power crisis forced about half of EU aluminium capacity offline and analysts warned shortages could worsen in winter. Copper fell amid recession demand fears and a sharp jump in LME on-warrant stocks; nickel dropped even more with broader risk-off sentiment and tighter economic prospects. | Comex copper for September delivery settled 0.62% lower at $3.5235 per pound, dropping 2.20 cents. It marked three consecutive down sessions, with the biggest three-day decline since Sept. 2, 2022. The settlement was also the lowest since Sept. 7, 2022.

Report expects copper to remain high due to tight supply: LME inventories continue to fall (~100,000 mt), cash-to-3-month backwardation hits multi-month highs, and SHFE warrant stocks are very low. It flags possible Escondida strike risk and easing power rationing, while warning hawkish Fed/firm USD adds volatility and medium-term pressure. | Shanghai aluminium rose up to 2% on fears of wider and longer China production cuts as power restrictions in Yunnan could expand and remain in place amid low rainfall and hydropower supply. Gains were capped by record August output and global macro worries, which also weighed on copper and nickel. | SMM data show copper inventories in China’s domestic bonded zones fell by 19,300 mt from Sept 9 to 115,400 mt as of Sept 16, the lowest in SMM records and the twelfth straight weekly drop. Shanghai declined sharply on sustained warrant outflows, later slowed by a weaker SHFE/LME ratio; further narrowing is possible next week. | Infinity Stone Ventures announced new assay results from its Zen-Whoberi Project, identifying a Centauri Zone where one grab sample returned 15.5% copper, alongside anomalous silver, gold, nickel and cobalt. Additional samples show lower copper grades. The update is exploration-focused, with no production or inventory changes stated. | The article headline says Agnico Eagle and Teck Resources are partnering on a Mexico copper-zinc mine. The development supports copper supply expectations, but without details on start dates, output changes, or disruptions, it is unlikely to create immediate pricing pressure over the next days to weeks. | Copper is set for a weekly decline as a weaker yuan and stronger dollar make dollar-priced metals costlier for Chinese buyers. Investors also fear slowing growth amid a deepening China property slump and broader recession risks. Aluminium on the LME is also down, consistent with risk-off macro pressure. | Teck agreed to sell half its San Nicolás copper project in Mexico to Agnico Eagle, with Agnico contributing $580 million to fund development. The mine is expected to start production in 2026, and copper is projected to be a smaller part of Agnico’s revenue. Overall, near-term copper effects seem limited.

Far East Gold started its first drilling at the Anak Perak copper-gold prospect within Indonesia’s Woyla project, after securing a borrow-use licence for advanced exploration. The program will test the vein system and support a future maiden resource estimate, but no assay or production changes are reported. | SMM reports active spot copper concentrate trading and higher Imported Copper Concentrate Index levels. Clean concentrates from the Americas flowed into China, and some copper concentrates were rerouted to China after a South Korea smelter accident, lifting TCs. Mine safety issues in South America and Peru add supply risk, while port inventories fell. | Copper prices fell as markets turned more pessimistic about global growth, raising concerns of weaker metals demand; the weak U.S. dollar limited declines. Fitch Solutions cut its copper forecast. Aluminium also weakened on lower Chinese import volumes tied to strong domestic production, while nickel moved higher amid mixed signals. | Callinex reported intersections of 33.67 meters grading 4.29% copper at its Rainbow Deposit in Manitoba’s Flin Flon Mining District, plus other high-grade intervals. The update is supportive of potential future copper supply, but is unlikely to materially move copper prices in the near term on its own. | Agnico Eagle agreed to buy a 50% stake in Teck’s Minas de San Nicolas, forming a JV to develop the San Nicolás copper-zinc project in Zacatecas, Mexico. Investment terms include $580m subscription and further shared costs, but the project remains undeveloped and scheduled to close in H1 2023. | Cordoba Minerals reported additional infill drill results at its 100%-owned San Matias/Alacran copper-gold-silver project in Colombia, including 30.48m of 1.87% copper and other high-grade intervals. Results confirm higher-grade domains and CBM vein continuity, supporting bullish sentiment toward copper prospects. | Battery Mineral Resources says Chile’s environmental authority has approved the environmental impact declaration for mining at its Punitaqui copper complex. The company cites positive drill results, a resource update, strong metallurgical recoveries, and expects copper concentrate production to start in Q1, supporting incremental near-term supply sentiment. | Doré Copper’s PEA outlines restarting the Chibougamau copper camp in Quebec, centered on the Corner Bay deposit feeding the Copper Rand mill. The plan targets about 492 million lb of copper over 10.5 years and includes feasibility, mill refurbishment, and ongoing infill drilling. This supports bullish sentiment for copper. | Callinex Mines shares jumped more than 15% after drilling results from its Pine Bay project in Manitoba, including work at the Rainbow copper deposit. The update was strong enough to lift the company’s outlook, which can improve market expectations for future copper supply and sentiment in the near term.

Belmont Resources began its Phase II 2022 exploration program on the Come By Chance copper-gold porphyry target in southern British Columbia. Ground truthing and geophysical interpretation aim to refine drill targets ahead of a planned drill program before year-end, supported by prior propylitic alteration and pyrite anomalies. | Tertiary Minerals announced an agreement with FQM covering exploration interests at Zambia’s Mukai and Mushima North licences. The Mukai area is adjacent to FQM’s Sentinel copper mine and includes an enterprise nickel mine nearby. The parties will collaborate via a technical committee and exchange historical exploration data. | Golden Dawn Minerals announced it has started drilling at its Phoenix Copper-Gold mine in southeastern British Columbia, targeting VTEM conductivity and magnetic anomalies linked to historic skarn copper-gold mineralization. The news is an exploration update with no production or supply impact, so any copper price effect is likely minimal. | Europe is expected to become the main driver of copper’s outlook as surging gas and power prices (stemming from Russia-linked supply cuts and sanctions) may force higher power consumption cuts. Citi and Macquarie forecast a seasonal surplus and regional demand decline, implying bearish pressure on LME copper toward the winter period. | Rio Tinto’s CEO said the near-term copper outlook is “challenged,” indicating a more difficult near-term balance for copper than previously expected. Such guidance typically weighs on market sentiment and can reduce short-term copper price expectations as traders adjust to potentially weaker conditions. | Rio Tinto’s CEO reportedly said its offer for a major copper mine is final, signaling closure on a potential takeover or deal renegotiation. The news may influence near-term expectations for copper supply continuity, but the excerpt provides no operational or timing specifics to gauge a strong price reaction.

FLSmidth will supply a 5km-plus overland conveyor for a major copper-gold mine in Southeast Asia, building on earlier 2022 orders for gearless SAG and ball mills and thickener technology. The project is valued at about DKK 330 million and is designed to cut energy use and OPEX, supporting mine expansion. | First Tellurium plans a mid-October drill campaign at its Deer Horn property in British Columbia to investigate the Pond copper porphyry target, following a prior copper porphyry investigation and awaiting new assays. The update is exploration-focused without production or supply changes, making copper price impact likely limited near term. | Athens-Clarke police charged a suspect in thefts of copper wiring from an AT&T facility in Athens, recovering nearly 2,000 pounds of wire and citing multiple losses over August and September totaling about $80,000. No forced entry was reported, and cameras later helped identify the suspect. | The article says copper has remained resilient but highly volatile, trading near resistance after breaching short-term ranges. It attributes swings to smelter shutdowns (notably China), weak European demand from energy strain, rising LME warehouse stocks, a strong U.S. dollar, and core inflation/Fed-hike fears weighing on prices.

Aguila Copper Corp granted additional BLM lode claims at its Lida copper-silver project in Nevada to cover an IP chargeability anomaly, extending the secured area to 58 claims over three high-chargeability zones. The company plans drilling in Q4 2022 after obtaining permits and drilling contracts, based on high-grade copper in surface samples. | Power Nickel reported its NI 43-101 technical report and mineral resource estimate for the Nisk nickel project and has started a second round of drilling (~5,500 m) to infill gaps and expand high-grade mineralization. The update is intended to grow resource confidence, with only limited copper linkage via NiEq. | TRU Precious Metals announced assay results from a new discovery at its Golden Rose project in Newfoundland, including multiple copper-bearing zones. Highlighted intervals include high-grade copper with associated gold and silver, plus indications the system remains open for expansion. The release is exploration-focused and early-stage. | Copper Mountain Mining announced it received an award for mine reclamation and fish habitat offset work at its Wolfe Creek realignment project near its Copper Mountain Mine in British Columbia. The release describes reclamation activities and mine production details, but provides no direct near-term supply, cost, or policy impact for copper markets. | Power Nickel announced a NI 43-101 compliant mineral resource estimate for its Nisk nickel-copper project and has begun a second drilling round (~5,500 m) to infill and expand higher-grade zones. The disclosure may affect market expectations for future nickel/copper supply but not near-term production. | CanXGold Mining Corp. says diamond drilling has started at its Phoenix property in southeastern British Columbia, targeting a conductive anomaly near the historic Phoenix copper-gold mine. The company frames the work as exploring for potential new mineralization below 160 meters, but no confirmed copper output change is indicated. | Tribes are seeking additional information on how water is being used at an Arizona copper mine. The request signals potential scrutiny around water rights and environmental/operational constraints, which could lead to disruptions or limits on production. Any operational uncertainty can tighten near-term copper supply expectations. | CanXGold Mining Corp says it is targeting British Columbia’s next major copper deposit and that “Hole #1” is underway, approaching a long anomaly identified from a 2022 airborne survey. The update is discovery-focused, but drilling progress may lift near-term expectations for future copper supply prospects. | The article headline suggests aluminium supply problems are intensifying, implying tighter near-term availability that can push aluminium prices higher. It also references China ordering output related to copper, pointing to potential demand support for copper. However, the excerpt contains no further specifics on volumes or timing.

OZ Minerals has given the go-ahead and started work on a $1.7 billion copper-nickel mine in remote Western Australia. The company says EV and battery makers are interested in stakes, and the decision may also affect takeover dynamics involving BHP. The project points to future supply, likely modestly pressuring copper and nickel sentiment. | OZ Minerals has reportedly given approval for a A$1.7 billion copper project. The decision suggests higher prospective copper supply in coming years, which can improve market sentiment for copper. The effect on spot prices is likely limited and gradual because new production is not immediate. | Oz Minerals approved its $1.7b West Musgrave project in Western Australia, a copper-and-nickel development expected to start production in late 2025. Management projects nickel entering sustained undersupply, with higher early production volumes. BHP may revisit its Oz takeover bid, reinforcing bullish sentiment for nickel and copper markets. | The article is largely nickel-focused, highlighting BHP’s WA nickel shutdown and additional Australian nickel job losses attributed to cheaper Indonesian nickel, implying weaker near-term nickel demand/supply balance. It also mentions Russia-related factors that “rocket” BHP’s copper outlook, suggesting some copper support. | OZ Minerals’ board has approved the $1.13bn West Musgrave copper-nickel project in Western Australia, backed by a A$1.2bn syndicated term loan. The project will include Nebo/Babel nickel-copper and Succoth copper deposits, targeting first concentrate in H2 2025 and long-life production of copper and nickel. | Si6 Metals says soil sampling at its Maibele East, Mashambe and Gobajango prospects in Botswana found elevated copper and nickel and suggests possible nickel-sulphide deposits under ultramafic geology. The company plans more mapping and geophysics but provides no new resource or production, keeping any near-term price effect limited.

London copper slid to a two-month low, falling about 1.1% to $7,352/t, as the U.S. dollar hit a two-decade peak and the Fed outlook turned more hawkish. Weak growth expectations pressured industrial metal demand. LME aluminium also hit an 18-month low, while ShFE nickel dropped 3.1%. | SMM says copper faced major macro pressure after a Fed rate hike with more hawkish dot plots, strengthening the US dollar and pulling LME prices down. It notes inventories fell but expected tight spot supply weakened. With RMB depreciation, copper is seen with some downside risk and fluctuating trading. | Anglo American said it will start commercial copper shipments from its Quellaveco project in Peru after successful testing and final regulatory clearance. The company revised 2022 Quellaveco guidance downward but expects strong output in 2023-2024 and tightened Chile production due to water restrictions and ore changes. | Anglo American says it will begin copper shipments from the Quellaveco project. If shipments ramp in the near term, the added physical supply could increase availability in the market and exert downward pressure on copper prices over the coming days to several weeks. | Eagle Metals says copper-based purchases dominate its volume and that copper mills remain on allocation, with limited contract flexibility. It also warns European alloy mills could shut due to high energy prices, disrupting US supply of materials like phosphor bronze. Meanwhile, EV and electrification demand is outpacing supply.

Nimy Resources says early diamond drilling at its Mons project in WA intersected substantial nickel sulphide mineralisation at the Godley prospect, with about 368m and 427m averaging ~0.14% nickel. It also reported nickel, copper and cobalt mineralisation in ultramafic rocks. Additional drilling and assays are pending. | Austral Resources announced diamond-drill assay results from its Lady Colleen copper project, including multiple high-grade copper intersections (up to 5m @ 5.74% Cu) and continued evidence of a high-grade core open along strike and down dip. The board approved a scoping study to assess economic potential and update the market. | Comex Copper futures for September delivery settled down 1.72% to $3.3135 per pound, losing 5.80 cents. The contract has fallen for six consecutive sessions, down 6.95% over that span, with the lowest settlement value since July 21, 2022. | Nevada Copper reported progress on Phase 1 restart activities at its Pumpkin Hollow underground copper mine, including advancing a key dike crossing and preparing additional underground development and capital projects. It also updated a planned ~US$93 million liquidity package to fund restart and ramp-up, expected to reduce restart bottleneck risk. | Nevada Copper outlined progress toward restarting its Pumpkin Hollow underground copper mine, including Phase 1 development activity and a proposed US$93 million restart financing package. Funding is intended to support restart and ramp-up, with non-binding terms targeted for closing in early October 2022, reducing restart execution risk. | Nevada Copper outlines progress toward restarting the Pumpkin Hollow mine, describing Phase 1 development work (dike crossings, capital projects like dewatering and fans) and a planned funding package to enable subsequent underground development and mill startup. The news is primarily supportive for near-term copper supply expectations. | SMM says aluminium scrap throughput in Linyi (Sep 19–25) rose 20% WoW as retailers shipped in panic to mitigate falling prices. A00 aluminium stayed volatile and declined WoW; downstream scrap users bought less. Inventory fell, but oversupply of scrap is expected to persist this week, weighing on aluminium prices. Copper scrap throughput also increased. | China’s CSPT set a Q4 2022 copper concentrate TC/RC floor at $93/tonne and 9.3 cents/lb, above Q3 2022 and Q4 2021 levels. The guide affects global treatment charges, reflecting expectations of steadier concentrate demand despite rising overseas supply and ongoing mining/logistics uncertainty. | SMM reports copper cathode spot premiums at 520-580 yuan/mt over SHFE 2210, stable on the day. Premiums held despite a widening front-/next-month spread, as the market is concerned about tight spot supply. A decline in copper prices spurred downstream restocking and supported sellers’ prices amid low inventories. | SMM reports East China copper scrap throughput rose to 29,400 mt WoW as pre-National Day stocking increased trading, while SHFE and physical prices fluctuated narrowly. The cathode-vs-scrap spread narrowed but still supported scrap consumption. Near-term, falling copper prices and weaker restocking could thin deals; throughput may drop to ~20,000 mt in Linyi. | Rio Tinto said it will start underground mining at its Kennecott copper operations. The move can shift near-term expectations toward higher copper supply, depending on ramp-up pace. While the immediate output increase may be gradual, the headline is directionally bearish for copper prices. | Rio Tinto said it approved a $55 million investment to start underground mining and expand output at its Kennecott copper operations in Utah. The project targets around 30kt of additional mined copper, with first ore expected in early 2023 and processing via existing facilities, supporting supply expectations. | China’s renewable and grid buildout is expected to raise copper demand, led by EVs and renewable installations, while construction demand declines. Analysts also point to power shortages potentially accelerating grid expansion, and reference a potential supply squeeze. The net effect is supportive for copper prices near term. | World Copper reports additional drilling at its Escalones project in Chile, confirming that oxide copper mineralization at the Mancha Amarilla target extends at least 150 metres south and reaches depths to 280 metres. The company plans further drilling at East Skarn and skarn targets, aiming to grow resources and improve grades. | Copper prices rose Tuesday as a softer U.S. dollar made the dollar-priced metal cheaper, but growth and demand concerns from aggressive global rate hikes limited upside. LME copper added 1.2%. The piece also notes small declines in LME aluminium and ShFE nickel amid generally mixed moves across metals inventories. | Element 29 reported an initial inferred mineral resource for its Elida porphyry copper-molybdenum deposit in Peru, including 321.7 million tonnes grading 0.32% copper. The update informs future drilling and potential project economics but does not indicate near-term production changes, limiting immediate price impact mainly to copper. | Element 29 announced an initial NI 43-101 Inferred Mineral Resource at its Elida porphyry copper-molybdenum deposit in Peru: 321.7 million tonnes at 0.32% copper. The update provides project scale and molybdenum/silver grades, but remains inferred with no production timeline, limiting near-term market impact. | Glencore increased its stake in Argentina’s Mara copper project. The move is generally supportive for copper development expectations, but the article provides no near-term operational details (e.g., output start dates or capacity changes). As a result, any effect on copper prices is likely modest over the coming days. | Rio Tinto earmarks US$55 million to begin underground development and expand output at its Kennecott copper operations in Utah. The project targets higher production at the world’s largest open-pit copper mine, potentially improving short-term copper supply expectations and providing price support.

Kennecott will move part of its Bingham operation underground and invest $55 million in development, targeting over 30,000 tons of high-quality copper over five years. The project also pilots electric mining vehicles and emphasizes air-quality benefits, though critics warn about potential water impacts. | Analysts cite macro slowdown and power/energy issues to explain large aluminium declines and potential transitory spikes from Russian delivery and potential smelter curbs. Citi notes a recent nickel rally but expects Europe-led demand weakness to dominate. Copper is seen flat but forecast down on recession risk in Europe. | The piece reviews broker outlooks: it attributes aluminium’s sharp decline to recession and energy-cost-driven production cuts, while noting potential short-lived spikes. For nickel it remains bearish despite a September rally, citing demand slowdown. Copper is expected to stay weak under Europe recession fears, with no bullish near-term catalyst. | JP Morgan and others cut iron ore forecasts, while Longview and Citi discuss aluminium, nickel and copper. Aluminium is pressured by recession and power-cost-driven smelter cuts, with potential Russian delivery and Yunnan hydropower outages. Nickel is bearish despite a Sept rally, while copper remains range-bound with recession-driven downside risks. | Rio Tinto’s Kennecott mine in Utah will shift additional mining underground, investing $55 million to develop the lower commercial skarns. Management expects over 30,000 tons of high-quality copper over five years and is piloting electric mining equipment to improve efficiency and emissions, signaling continued copper output. | Rio Tinto’s Kennecott mine in Utah plans to resume underground mining at the “Lower Commercial Skarn” deposit after more than 100 years underground operations ceased. Crews are expected to produce ore by early next year, processing it in existing facilities, alongside testing electric mining equipment and completing feasibility work in early 2023. | MAX Resource Corp. reports strong geophysical correlations and channel sampling at its URU-C and URU-CE copper-silver project in Colombia, with drilling expected to start by Oct. 11. It describes drill targeting, expanded TMI/IP surveys, and copper-rich intervals, which can support bullish copper sentiment near term. | Lotte Chemical plans to buy a 53.3% stake in Iljin Materials for KRW2.7tn, taking control of a leading copper foil producer for EV batteries. Iljin supplies firms including BYD and LG Energy Solution, and plans a European plant in Spain, supporting near-term copper demand expectations. | Copper Mountain Mining announced a 57% increase in proven and probable copper mineral reserves and an updated life-of-mine plan for its Copper Mountain Mine. The revision supports a 65ktpd mill expansion and longer mine life, extending production over decades, with first 20-year average output of ~114 million pounds of copper equivalent annually. | Copper Mountain Mining reported a 57% increase in Proven and Probable copper mineral reserves and updated a revised life-of-mine plan for its 75% owned Copper Mountain Mine. The plan includes a 65 ktpd mill expansion and higher long-term output, starting in 2028, but does not signal near-term supply changes. | Rio Tinto said it will resume underground mining at the Kennecott Copper Mines in Utah, after more than 100 years at the site. The company approved $55 million for development capital to add additional volumes of high-quality copper and expand capabilities for larger underground operations. | Copper Mountain Mining updated mineral resources and reserves for its 75%-owned copper mine in southern British Columbia and released a revised life-of-mine plan that supports expansion. The positive project update may improve near-term market expectations for future copper supply, offering a modest bullish bias for copper prices.

Hindustan Copper Limited plans to expand the Malanjkhand Copper Project, increasing copper ore capacity from 2 MTPA to 5 MTPA by developing an underground mine and targeting much higher long-run production. It also mentions resuming operations at Ghatshila. Changes are largely multi-year, so near-term copper price impact is limited. | Infinitum Copper reported diamond-drill results from its La Adelita project in Mexico, including 32.25m grading 1.01% Cu and 19.30m grading 0.54% Cu, expressed as copper equivalent. The company frames the findings as expanding the skarn mineralized system, supporting future drilling plans. | Aluminum surged 8.5% on the LME after Bloomberg reported the exchange may discuss blocking new Russian metal deliveries to its warehouses. SMM surveyed 55 copper processing enterprises on National Day holiday production plans. Many segments (enamelled wire, copper billet, parts of tube) face sluggish demand and longer holidays, with cautious raw-material restocking. Wire/cable orders are steadier, but overall stocking falls short, implying near-term pressure on copper. | SMM says copper inventories across major Chinese markets fell to 66,200 mt on Sep 30, down 10,100 mt from Monday, mainly due to downstream pre-holiday restocking. Customs clearance of imported copper was limited. Inventories are expected to rise sharply after National Day as deliveries approach. | Vale said Brazilian nickel and copper assets will be reorganised into two entities. Copper assets will move to Salobo Metais S.A, while nickel assets will be placed into a new Brazil company. Vale clarified there is no decision on new Base Metals transactions, and assets remain fully consolidated and owned by Vale. | Vale said Brazilian authorities approved a reorganisation of its base metals operations: copper assets will be transferred to Salobo Metais S.A, while nickel assets will go to a new company Vale will establish. Both sets remain consolidated and wholly owned by Vale, suggesting no immediate production shift. | The piece says China’s spot copper premium should stay elevated as demand improves. A higher and persistent premium typically reflects stronger near-term buying interest and relatively tighter spot supply against benchmark pricing, which tends to support upward pressure on copper prices over the coming days to weeks. | China’s spot copper premium in the main consumer market is expected to stay elevated as government stimulus supports demand and utilization improves across copper wire-rod and tube sectors. Tight supply, record-low visible inventories, and power-curb disruptions further support the premium, keeping import arbitrage active despite volatility from a stronger dollar. | Emerita Resources reported additional assay results from delineation drilling at the La Romanera deposit in Spain. Drill holes LR019 and LR023 intersect two lenses of massive sulphides, including a 14.0 m interval grading 4.3% copper. The company is expanding drilling activity and expects a future NI 43-101 resource estimate. | Rusal denied plans to offload aluminium into LME warehouses amid LME consideration of a consultation over whether Russian aluminium, nickel and copper should remain tradable and storable. The uncertainty sparked a buying frenzy, with benchmark aluminium jumping 8.5% to $2,305/t, as traders feared delivery constraints.

CanXGold reports completion of the first drill hole at its Phoenix property in British Columbia, targeting a large conductivity anomaly that the company frames as potential copper-related mineralization. The hole reached 337 meters and intersected altered rocks and pyrite, with a second anomaly to be drilled next. No production or resource estimates are provided. | Japan’s Nittetsu plans to raise second-half (Oct–Mar) copper output by 1.4% versus the prior year. The company outlines copper production targets for H2 FY22/23 and compares them with H1 FY22/23 and H2 FY21/22 actuals, signaling slightly higher supply expectations.

London copper rose 0.8% and aluminium gained 1.1% as a softer U.S. dollar followed weak U.S. manufacturing data and lower Treasury yields. Trading stayed thin due to holidays in China and reduced Asian participation, with analysts expecting low demand and sideways price action. | Palladium One reports preliminary assay results from its Tyko Project West Pickle discovery in Canada, including intersections with 4.8% Ni and 3.7% Cu over 1.8m (and other high-grade Ni-Cu intervals). The company highlights exploration success and expects further EM results, supporting bullish sentiment for nickel and copper. | Taseko provided an update that the U.S. EPA has concluded the public comment period for a draft Underground Injection Control permit for its Florence Copper project. The company says comments were overwhelmingly supportive and expects issues to be addressed before issuance of the final permit, reducing permitting uncertainty for future copper supply. | The post references a Bloomberg item claiming a massive copper mine in Arizona has been blocked. A blockade implies halted or slowed mining output, tightening near-term copper supply expectations. With no corroborating data provided here, the likely immediate market reaction is bearish for copper.

Falcon Gold announced it has acquired a strategic nickel and copper claim group contiguous to Go Metal’s HSP property. While the move could broaden future nickel and copper supply prospects for the issuer, the article does not indicate any immediate production start or inventory impact, limiting short-term price effects. | Falcon Gold announced the acquisition of a strategic nickel and copper claim group contiguous to Go Metals’ HSP property. Such acquisitions can strengthen expectations for future nickel and copper supply, providing a modest sentiment boost. However, without production timelines, immediate effects on prices are likely limited. | Ivanhoe Mines says its Kamoa-Kakula complex in the DRC produced a record 97,820 tonnes of copper in concentrate in Q3 2022, with concentrator plants regularly exceeding design throughput. The de-bottlenecking and Phase 3 expansion are progressing, supporting higher future copper output guidance. | ALX Resources provided an exploration update from its Flying Vee nickel project in Saskatchewan, reporting EM-confirmed conductive trends and newly located gossan and boulder zones with sulphide mineralization, including up to ~2% chalcopyrite locally. It also outlines further planned work, but no production or supply changes. | The piece argues decarbonization will drive strong refined copper demand growth while inventories remain in deficit, implying higher copper prices. It also covers Gama Explorations expanding copper- and nickel-related projects through acquisitions and financing, but the clearest near-term market signal is bullish copper fundamentals. | Granite Creek Copper amended and closed a first tranche of a private placement, issuing common-share units and flow-through shares to fund exploration. Proceeds will support the Carmacks copper-gold-silver project and the newly acquired Star copper-nickel-PGE project, plus working capital, subject to regulatory approvals. | Nickel is poised for another rebound as the LME considers options to manage Russian metal trading, including possible bans on new Russian nickel deliveries. With Russia supplying about 20% of global nickel, a change could mirror March-style Western supply fears, while aluminium could be affected too via similar LME action.

Copper and zinc reportedly gained after the LME banned new metals from a Russian giant. The move implies tighter availability and improved supply sentiment for metals exposed to Russian origin flows, which can lift near-term price expectations—particularly for copper as flagged in the headline. | The article headline says copper gained after the LME banned new metal from a major Russian producer. Such an exchange-linked restriction can reduce available supply and alter near-term market expectations, helping lift copper pricing. Zinc is mentioned too, but it is not tracked here. | Copper Mountain Mining agreed to sell its Eva Copper Project and exploration tenements in Queensland to Harmony for up to US$230 million. Some contingent payments depend on incremental revenue above a US$3.80/lb copper price and on copper resources discovered, making the transaction copper-price linked but mainly corporate in nature. | South African courts sentenced offenders for stealing and damaging Transnet infrastructure, including copper cables and related equipment. Incidents included a thief caught with copper cables worth about R50,000 and another case involving alleged damage at a substation estimated at R300,000. These are localized cases with limited market-wide copper implications.

Nevada Copper updates on advancing a restart financing package for its Pumpkin Hollow underground copper mine, including increased liquidity via Pala’s $25m backstop and progress on key restart milestones. Delays hinge on a potential change-of-control proposal, but funding and execution progress reduce near-term supply interruption risk for copper. | Copper prices fell on Friday, extending losses, as a firmer U.S. dollar and hawkish Fed commentary ahead of jobs data increased recession and demand-damping fears. Reuters links the move to tighter monetary expectations. Aluminium was up modestly, indicating less consistent pressure across industrial metals. | Harmony Gold agreed to buy Copper Mountain’s Eva copper project in Queensland for $230m, including a large copper resource estimate and an open-pit operation targeting over 100 million pounds of copper annually. The deal is pending approvals and due to close in Q1, extending Harmony’s copper exposure. | European participants are debating and implementing premiums for “green” low-carbon copper. Boliden-style products and Montanwerke-Brixlegg’s planned €295/tonne low-carbon premium are cited, alongside LME moves to delist non-compliant brands. Yet traders warn sustainability definitions and customer price acceptance remain uncertain, tempering immediate price pressure. | Harmony Gold will acquire Copper Mountain Mining’s Eva copper project in northwest Queensland for up to $230m, including a $170m upfront payment and up to $60m contingent. The deal expands Harmony’s copper exposure and reserves, potentially supporting copper market sentiment despite no immediate production changes.

After the National Day holiday, the spread between front-month and next-month SHFE copper contracts widened, making downstream buyers cautious and reluctant to purchase. As a result, copper cathode premiums in North China fell sharply to average about +90 yuan/mt, with trade around 62,500 yuan/mt, and quotations were lowered or discounted. Premiums are expected to drop further before delivery. | Copper prices rose, supported by renewed concerns over copper availability in LME warehouses (higher cancelled warrants) and a wider cash premium. However, Chinese demand worries, expectations of further U.S. rate hikes, a stronger dollar, and rising inventories weighed on sentiment. Aluminium and nickel fell on the day amid the same macro backdrop. | Comex copper front-month for October delivery settled 1.20% higher at $3.4535 per pound, gaining 4.10 cents. The move snapped a two-session losing streak and marked a strong one-day dollar/percentage gain. The contract is up eight of the past 10 sessions and slightly positive month-to-date.

Transnet declared force majeure after South African port workers went on strike over a wage dispute. The disruption is expected to reduce copper anode shipments to China and tighten copper anode supply locally. That should raise pressure on domestic copper anode RCs, though SMM expects the strike may be short-lived. | LME copper slipped 0.2% and aluminium fell 0.4% as a stronger U.S. dollar, supported by robust labour data and sticky inflation expectations, increased costs for non-dollar buyers. Nickel rose on China’s SHFE, showing mixed momentum across venues rather than a uniform market move. | Lotte Chemical will buy a 53.3% stake in Iljin Materials for 2.7 trillion won, seeking to expand into battery components. Iljin makes copper foil used as an anode current collector, with planned capacity growth and long-term supply contracts, supporting expectations for higher near-term copper demand. | MAX Resource announced the start of an inaugural drill program at its URU-C and URU-CE copper-silver targets within the Cesar project in northeastern Colombia, including reported visible chalcocite and channel assay highlights. The news signals exploration progress but does not change near-term copper supply or inventories. | Flying Nickel Mining reported assay results from three Minago nickel sulphide drill holes, including 69.6 meters grading 1.10% nickel with small copper content. The company plans further assays and metallurgical testing as part of a feasibility study. As an exploration update, it is unlikely to materially change near-term nickel or copper supply expectations. | Base metals dropped as recession fears and rising COVID-19 cases in China raised concerns about weaker demand. A stronger dollar also pressured USD-priced commodities. LME aluminium fell, while copper initially slipped but later edged up; Comex copper rose. Nickel declined, reflecting the overall risk-off sentiment.

Comex copper futures for October delivery settled 0.71% higher at $3.4780, gaining 2.45 cents per pound. The contract was up for two consecutive sessions, with gains totaling 6.55 cents or 1.92% over two sessions, reflecting modest bullish momentum in copper prices. | C3 Metals reports expanding the Arthurs Seat epithermal gold-copper vein swarm in Jamaica by 1.5 km east, with rock chips assaying up to 11.1% Cu alongside high gold and silver. A drill permit application is progressing and drilling is planned after permits, but this remains early-stage exploration. | Murchison Minerals reports additional drill intersections at its Barre de Fer project, expanding near-surface nickel-copper(-cobalt) sulphide mineralization. Highlighted pXRF results include metres of high-grade NiEq/CuEq and deeper/greater modelled zones. Exploration progress can modestly support expectations for future nickel and copper supply. | Sources say Russian copper is accumulating in LME warehouses, suggesting inventory builds. Additional near-term stock availability can reduce tightness in the physical market, typically pressuring copper prices downward over the following days as traders adjust expectations for prompt availability and deliverable supply. | Solis Minerals reports granted tenements for its Cinto copper project and highlights ongoing remote-sensing and field work at Ilo Este and Ilo Norte in Peru’s copper belt near the Toquepala mine. The company plans mapping, sampling, and drill permitting, with no immediate production changes. | Russian-origin copper has accumulated in LME warehouses in Germany, the Netherlands and Taiwan since mid-September, with stocks rising sharply. Some consumers reportedly reject Russian copper, turning LME storage into a last-resort outlet. LME also discussed possible bans on Russian aluminium, nickel and copper.

SMM reports that copper scrap traders have become more willing to sell their inventory as copper prices have risen and they still maintain a bullish outlook. Downstream scrap users entered and bought selectively, favoring sellers with lower prices, indicating active but price-sensitive demand. | Yangshan copper premiums rose to new highs (warrants $130–145/mt; bills of lading $117–140/mt) as bonded-zone inventory stayed low and warrant transactions were absent. Overseas shipments fell and South African port strikes hurt logistics, reinforcing a supply deficit. Traders expect premiums to keep rising short term. | Copper Road Resources announced results from a 3,000-metre diamond drilling campaign at its Tribag Mine Zone, reporting near-surface copper mineralization continuity and grades in multiple breccia zones. The company plans further exploration and validation work. The update is exploration-focused and unlikely to alter near-term copper supply materially. | Vale Canada officially opened the first phase of its CDN$945 million Copper Cliff Complex South Mine project in Sudbury. The expansion is expected to nearly double ore production and add about 13,000 tonnes/year of copper and 10,000 tonnes/year of contained nickel, supporting supply and regional investment sentiment. | Vale announced the official opening of its $945M Copper Cliff Complex South Mine Project. The development signals increased copper supply capacity from a major producer. While this may take time to fully impact shipments, it can still weigh on near-term copper price expectations by improving supply outlook. | Vale Canada opened the first phase of its $945M Copper Cliff Complex South Project at Sudbury, adding tunnels and ventilation for expanded underground handling. The phase is expected to increase contained production by about 13,000 tonnes/year of copper and 10,000 tonnes/year of nickel, supporting supply expectations.

SMM said Chinese copper inventories across major markets rose to 94,200 mt, up 11,500 mt from Oct 9, though south China declined slightly. Downstream firms took a wait-and-see stance as SHFE spreads widened post-holiday, pressuring consumption. Inventories may fall slightly after 2210 delivery, but imports should keep supply supported. | SMM reported copper inventories in China’s domestic bonded zones fell 13,600 mt from Oct 9 to 38,100 mt by Oct 14. The Shanghai and Guangdong bonded zones declined by 11,600 mt and 2,000 mt. Warehouses resumed normal operations and import profits rose, boosting customs declarations and reducing stocks. | SMM survey shows copper cathode rod enterprises’ operating rates averaged 73.84% in September, below expectations, citing high spot copper premiums, weak demand from enamelled wire plants, and maintenance during Mid-Autumn and National Day holidays. After the holidays, a wide SHFE price gap made firms cautious on orders. | Chilean authorities will add specialized police, air support, and better communications to protect trains carrying copper cargoes after a wave of violent heists. FCAB previously suspended copper shipments, and security is being restored to resume flows amid concerns about further disruptions and theft quantities. | Chile is boosting police security for copper freight trains after repeated armed heists linked to organized crime. FCAB suspended copper shipments earlier this week, prompting mines to consider alternative routes. Authorities say specialized personnel, air support and better communications will resume protected transport, but theft frequency and violence remain high. | Vale Canada began the first phase of its C$945m Copper Cliff Complex South Mine Project in Sudbury, adding about 13,000 tonnes of contained copper and 10,000 tonnes of contained nickel per year for Vale’s Copper Cliff operations. The phased launch supports future supply, but immediate market price effects likely limited. | Vale Canada has inaugurated phase I of its $945m Copper Cliff Complex South Mine Project in Sudbury, expanding underground ore and waste handling and ventilation. The project is expected to nearly double Copper Cliff output, adding about 13,000 tonnes of copper and 10,000 tonnes of contained nickel per annum, supporting supply sentiment. | European copper buyers face record term premiums for 2023 as they self-sanction against Russian metal following UK actions affecting UMMC-linked brands. Aurubis, Codelco and Montanwerke Brixlegg quote much higher premiums amid low visible stocks and potential benchmark concerns. The piece warns Russian copper could redirect to China, affecting regional pricing. | Vale opened the first phase of a $945 million expansion at its Copper Cliff Complex South Mine in Sudbury, Ont. The initial phase is expected to nearly double some output at the project. As new copper capacity ramps, the news is modestly supportive for copper market expectations. | Vale has officially opened the initial phase of its $945m Copper Cliff Mine South expansion, involving re-opening a dormant mine and rebuilding key shafts and infrastructure. Vale forecasts nearly double output at Copper Cliff, adding about 10,000 tonnes of nickel and 13,000 tonnes of copper annually, supporting low-carbon mineral supply.

A weekly broker-rating roundup highlights Citi’s view of short-term headwinds for base-metals demand from sluggish China, higher US interest rates and a stronger USD. Citi trims near-term price forecasts for aluminium, copper and nickel (while being comparatively more bearish on copper). Some company ratings are upgraded on valuation, but metal-price expectations lean weaker. | Copper prices were subdued as demand uncertainty from China’s continued zero-COVID curbs and global recession fears offset worries over tight supply. LME and Shanghai copper prices gave back gains. Aluminium and nickel also declined as risk sentiment weakened, despite lower Chile output and tight inventories. | Copper rose on tightening global supplies: LME/COMEX/ShFE stocks fell and Chilean production dropped sharply. China demand improved at the margin via premiums, but gains were capped by ongoing zero-COVID uncertainty and global recession fears. Aluminium and nickel fell in the same session, showing mixed risk appetite. | SMM says spot copper concentrate trading stayed sluggish, but the weekly Imported Copper Concentrate Index rose to $86.38/mt. With spot premiums also seen improving, SMM expects SHFE 2211 copper to rise this week. | Nelson Mandela Bay’s electricity department is replacing copper cables with cheaper aluminium to deter cable theft and vandalism.
