The city reports ongoing streetlight repairs and arrests of cable thieves, and says aluminium cables are installed higher to deter removal. This may modestly shift demand from copper to aluminium locally. | SMM data shows copper inventories across major Chinese markets rose by 16,100 mt to 110,300 mt, with increases in all regions. The build is attributed to smelters shipping cathode into warehouses amid a wider SHFE front/back spread and recent import arrivals, while downstream demand is slowed by COVID conditions. | Copper Fox reports completion of 4,688 meters of metallurgical drilling at its Schaft Creek copper-gold-molybdenum-silver project, aimed at improving geometallurgical data for planned metallurgical testwork. Analytical results are pending. The update is operational but lacks production or market-impacting findings in the near term. | SMM reports Linyi East China scrap market throughput surged in Oct 10-16, especially aluminium scrap, reaching 19,200 mt (+83.6% WoW). This coincided with rising A00 aluminium prices and improved downstream recycler buying after the holiday. Aluminium supply fell on trader risk concerns, while inventories declined; prices were expected stable this week. | Copper prices were subdued as demand uncertainty outweighed supply woes. LME copper gave up gains and hovered around $7,535/t while inventories fell and Chile production dropped sharply. China’s reiterated zero-COVID stance and recession fears limited upside, with aluminium and nickel also slipping. | Element 29 Resources reported its first resource estimate for the Elida copper project in Peru, with inferred tonnes grading 0.32% copper and small molybdenum credits, and plans further drilling and metallurgical studies. It also plans to update resources at Flor de Cobre. Overall, it’s copper-positive for sentiment, not a direct near-term supply driver. | The piece says copper is range-bound after a rebound, with macro pressure from Fed rate hikes weighing on demand. It also cites bullish offsets from strong Chinese refined import growth and supply disruptions in Peru and Chile, plus LME restrictions on new Russian UMMC deliveries potentially tightening supplies. | Comex copper futures for October delivery settled 0.14% higher at $3.4555 per pound, gaining 0.50 cent. The piece summarizes recent trading—up four of six sessions, near-term month-to-date up slightly, and year-to-date still down. No fundamental catalyst is discussed.

Faraday Copper announced additional drill results from its Copper Creek, Arizona project, including 36.0 metres at 3.02% copper and longer intervals averaging 0.87% copper. The company says mineralization remains open and could expand the resource, feeding into an updated MRE/PEA. | SMM links US CPI and a stronger dollar with softer global growth expectations, while noting LME copper inventories rose to about 140,000 mt and cash-to-3M premiums narrowed before later warrant-related tightening. It expects abundant concentrate supply and steady smelter output, with demand improving for State Grid but not lasting, leaving copper weak-balance and prone to short squeezes. | Excelsior Mining says well-stimulation modeling for its Gunnison Copper Project in Arizona shows pre-existing fractures could be inflated by significant volumes, improving connectivity and copper production. The company expects EPA approval for field trials in Q1 2023, but notes results may differ from modeling. | Barrick Gold’s CEO visits Baloch communities regarding the Reko Diq copper-gold mine in Pakistan’s Balochistan. While the company discusses local socio-economic work, the article reiterates separatist opposition and past attacks on regional infrastructure and CPEC-linked projects, implying ongoing operational and development risks for copper. | Deep-South Resources provides an update on exploration programs for three large copper exploration licences in Zambia’s Copper Belt. It describes environmental brief submissions awaiting approval from ZEMA and planned geochemical soil sampling to identify drilling targets. No resources are estimated and no immediate production impact is indicated.

American West Metals says new metallurgical tests at its West Desert Utah project show 78% copper recovery from oxide material using traditional sulphuric acid heap leaching, with low acid consumption. The results support improved recoverability and potential low-cost extraction, strengthening near-term sentiment for copper supply prospects. | Meridian Mining UK S reports new drill assays from the Cabaçal gold-copper VMS deposit, including multiple intervals with high-grade copper (e.g., up to 6.2% Cu over 0.7m and 1.9% Cu over 4.8m). The results expand copper-rich trends ahead of a planned 2023 resource update. | BHP and Antofagasta kept annual guidance after strong quarterly copper output, including higher volumes from expanded production and improved mine conditions. The piece notes global economic uncertainty has weighed on copper prices, but the maintained outlook and supply growth support expectations for near-term copper availability. | Copper prices fell further as soaring inflation, recession worries and expectations of more rate hikes pressured global demand sentiment, while a stronger dollar weighed on metal prices. LME aluminium also slipped amid additional inflows into approved warehouses and rising inventories. Nickel rose slightly, but the overall tone remains bearish. | New Mexico’s Supreme Court denied a petition to further appeal a decision upholding the groundwater discharge permit for New Mexico Copper Corporation’s Copper Flat Mine (DP-1842). With appeals exhausted, the permit remains in effect, lowering regulatory uncertainty for the project and modestly supporting near-term copper supply expectations. | Talon Metals announced a near-doubling of indicated resources at its Tamarack Nickel Project, including a 570% rise in high-grade massive sulphide contained nickel. The update strengthens expectations for future nickel concentrate supply potential from the Tamarack nickel-copper deposit, with limited secondary implications for copper.

WBMS reported a 657,000 mt global copper supply deficit in January-August 2022, following a 283,000 mt deficit in 2021. LME stocks rose vs. end-2021, but mine and cathode output increased less than demand (up 4.3% YoY). August balances also show demand above production. | Minto Metals reported copper intercepts of 2.20% over 20 metres and 3.52% over 7.26 metres from the Minto North and Cu Keel South discovery areas. The update is copper-focused, indicating promising resource potential, but it is unlikely to materially shift near-term copper prices by itself. | Freeport-McMoRan reported a sharp profit drop as recession fears and COVID-19 lockdowns in China pressured copper prices. Reuters notes LME copper has fallen about 30% from March highs and is trading in a $7,200–$8,000 range since late August. Copper production rose year-on-year, but realized prices fell. | The piece centers on a copper market that a miner describes as “strikingly tight,” suggesting supportive near-term supply conditions. The title also notes a concurrent price drop, implying either other offsetting factors or that the tightness signal may not be translating into higher prices immediately. | Strike Copper Corp. announced an exploration agreement with the Lac La Croix First Nation for its Sungold property near Thunder Bay in Ontario. The deal includes consultation, cultural/heritage protections, and compensatory benefits. The news is supportive of continued copper exploration but lacks details on near-term production or supply impacts.

SMM reports copper is pressured by heightened recession fears after the Fed’s hawkish signals and weakening global PMIs. Offsetting this, supply growth is capped by lower South American output and tight scrap/power rationing, while global inventories are at 20-year lows with high China premiums. | Shares of NFC jumped after it signed a USD2.3 billion contract to build a copper processing plant in Kazakhstan for Kaz Minerals’ Baimskaya project. The plant is planned to start construction in December 2023 and open years later, implying limited immediate impact on short-term copper prices. | Aurubis began a pilot at its Hamburg copper wire rod production site using blue, low-carbon ammonia shipped from the UAE by ADNOC. The company says ammonia can cut natural-gas use for production by up to 20% and supports its decarbonization goals. The project is presented as practically feasible to build a UAE–Germany ammonia value chain. | Aurubis, Europe’s largest copper smelter, is starting tests to use ammonia for low‑carbon copper rod production. A first shipment of blue ammonia arrived at its Hamburg plant. The report indicates an emissions-reduction pilot rather than a near-term change in copper output or supply, limiting near-term price effects.

The piece argues copper is “disconnecting” from net-zero expectations: copper prices have fallen ~30% since March despite lower refined-stock coverage. It cites recession risk, China’s weakening copper-intensive property demand, Europe’s energy/war effects, and a stronger US dollar from higher US rates as reasons for price weakness. | SMM reports slack spot copper concentrate trading as smelters hold sufficient inventories and the market enters a negotiation period for 2023 long-term TCs. Reported TC ranges and weekly concentrate port inventories are cited alongside quarterly copper output updates, but no clear immediate directional price shock is indicated. | First Quantum Minerals says it will apply for new exploration licenses in Zambia late 2022 or early 2023, citing improved business conditions under President Hakainde Hichilema after years of stagnant copper output. The planned exploration targets copper and nickel, aimed at easing global shortages, though timing limits near-term price effects. | China’s September unmanufactured and semi-finished copper imports rose 25.6% year-on-year to about 510,000 tonnes and increased from August. Reuters attributes the pickup to infrastructure stimulus plans and seasonal demand, alongside supply tightness from lower output and importer liquidity issues, supporting copper prices. | China’s September unwrought and copper-product imports rose 25.6% year-on-year as local governments and power utilities ramped infrastructure spending to counter economic slowdown. Lower prices and favorable import parity encouraged buying, while tighter domestic supply from power curbs and liquidity issues at Maike Group reinforced support for copper demand. | SMM reports that Daye Nonferrous (Yangxin Hongsheng Copper) began operations on a 400,000 mt high-purity copper cathode clean production project on Oct 23, 2022. The $8.7 billion investment uses a double-flash smelting process and is running smoothly, adding copper cathode supply capacity in China. | An opinion piece argues there is a disconnect between net-zero copper expectations and current market pricing. Copper has fallen ~30% since March even as refined inventories drop to very low days of consumption. It attributes weakness to recession fears, China’s property slump, Europe’s energy shock, war and a stronger USD. | China’s September unwrought and copper product imports rose 25.6% year-on-year to 509,954.1 tonnes, up from August. The report links stronger demand hopes to planned infrastructure spending and stimulus, plus supply tightness from summer power curbs and importer liquidity issues, supporting a bullish outlook for copper prices. | BNN Bloomberg reports that copper buyers are pushing for longer-term supply agreements as Codelco points to ongoing supply concerns. The emphasis on securing future material points to tighter near-term availability expectations, which typically supports copper pricing in the coming days to weeks. | Los Andes Copper says it is working to publish a pre-feasibility study for the Vizcachitas porphyry copper-molybdenum project by year-end while preparing to restart drilling. The planned advancement suggests continued development momentum for future copper supply, which can provide modest bullish sentiment.

The report says cash-for-copper trading has “grinded to a halt” around Shanghai’s free-trade zone warehouses that historically anchor global physical copper pricing. It notes traders track flows in and out of the bonded copper stockpile, implying liquidity/physical exchange conditions are deteriorating, but no specific price impact mechanism is stated. | Comex copper for October delivery settled 1.23% lower at $3.4795 per pound, losing 4.35 cents. The move snapped a two-session winning streak. The contract remains down from recent highs and slightly up month-to-date, signaling near-term bearish price pressure for copper. | Copper fell on Tuesday on the LME after the Chinese yuan weakened further against the dollar. A weaker yuan makes dollar-priced commodities costlier for China, the largest copper consumer. Three-month LME copper dropped about 0.5% to around $7,516/t as the market adjusted to FX-driven demand concerns. | Falcon Gold expands its HSP South project by staking additional claims, increasing its land position by 70% in Havre St. Pierre’s anorthosite complex. The area is described as newly recognized nickel source and contiguous to Go Metals’ recent nickel-and-copper sulphide discovery, with airborne surveys planned. | Falcon Gold reported a 70% increase in its land position at the HSP South property in Quebec, adjacent to Go Metals’ drilling and discovery of wide intervals of nickel and copper sulphides. Falcon plans airborne EM surveys and expanded staking across prospective contact, potentially boosting near-term sentiment for nickel and copper. | Cordoba Minerals reported shallow high-grade copper (0.69–0.74% Cu over 152–175 m) from infill drilling at its San Matias Alacran deposit in Colombia, with copper-gold-silver intercepts to be included in an updated mineral resource estimate. The update is sentiment-supportive but not near-term production-changing. | Elemental Altus completed the sale of its Morocco-focused copper subsidiary Aterian Resources to Eastinco, generating a 2.5% NSR royalty over 15 copper-focused projects. The company also received Eastinco shares and warrants, retaining 25% equity exposure. The release is mainly a royalty/corporate transaction with indirect copper impact. | Nickel North Exploration reports 2022 sampling and backpack drilling confirming high-grade, up to 400m-long Ni-Cu sulfide mineralization at the Hawk Ridge Project’s Gamma Zone in Northern Quebec. Reported intercepts and outcrop/backpack results include strong nickel and copper grades, supporting further 2023 drilling plans.

Cyprium Metals’ reverse-circulation drilling at the Nanadie Well project in Western Australia intersected broad anomalous copper-nickel-PGE zones, including multiple strong copper intervals and sections with meaningful nickel. The company plans follow-up work to test an expanded 1.6 km corridor, improving sentiment for future supply. | SMM says East China copper scrap throughput fell 6.7% in Oct 17–23, with pandemic-driven lower secondary rod operating rates and weaker shipments. Cathode and scrap prices also declined slightly WoW. SMM forecasts a steeper drop in Oct 24–30 due to a Linyi lockdown, while scrap remains tight and imports limited. | SMM reports Shanghai #1 copper spot premiums fell nearly 50% to 425 yuan/mt in two days. The decline is linked to eased warehouse congestion, weaker downstream demand after rising copper prices, wider cathode-scrap spreads shifting purchases to secondary material, more imported copper entering, and warrant drawdowns after SHFE 2210 delivery. | T2 Metals received a permit from the Bureau of Land Management to construct five drill sites and begin drilling at its Lida copper-silver project in Nevada. The company describes high-grade copper oxide/carbonate mineralization and strong IP anomalies, aiming to advance toward potential economic skarn-style mineralization. | Clean Air Metals reported infill drill results at its Thunder Bay North PGE-Cu-Ni project, including copper (0.61%) and nickel (0.43%) in a 10m interval, and said it is advancing prefeasibility engineering and metallurgical testing. The update is exploration-focused with no immediate production or supply disruption signals. | Clean Air Metals reported 2022 drill results from its Thunder Bay North Current deposit, including 10.0m grading 0.61% copper and 0.43% nickel, and said it is advancing a prefeasibility study with metallurgical optimization and resource validation. The update may support longer-term supply expectations, but no immediate production impact is cited. | The article headline says copper miner Freeport is in talks to buy an Arizona smelter. If the deal progresses, it could expand or secure copper refining capacity, improving expectations for treatment and concentrate processing and near-term copper supply availability, generally supportive for copper prices.

Osisko Metals reported additional infill drill results at its Gaspé copper project, including 102.0 m of 0.57% copper and associated silver grades. Updated drilling information can support the resource outlook and future development expectations, which may modestly underpin copper sentiment, though no immediate production or supply disruption is indicated. | Freeport-McMoRan says it is in early talks with Indonesia to extend Grasberg mine operating rights beyond 2041. The Grasberg district is a major copper source, and extending permits would lower long-run supply disruption risk. Freeport is also building a smelter and expanding the mine in Indonesia. | Midland and Soquem announced new results from their recent copper-gold discovery in Nunavik. Updated exploration outcomes can strengthen confidence in potential copper resources, which may translate into near-term support for copper prices if investors perceive improved supply prospects. Gold relevance is not assessed here. | A sinkhole at Lundin’s Chilean mine is expected to disrupt operations and reduce copper output. The outage could tighten near-term supply expectations for copper, potentially weighing on prices over the coming days as markets adjust to the production loss and related logistics or repair timing risks.

The headline indicates that financing for restarting Nevada Copper could be finalized by Oct. 28. A restart typically increases expected copper supply from the mine, which can reduce tightness and pressure copper prices in the near term, depending on the timing and likelihood of actual restart. | A sinkhole at Lundin’s Chilean mine is expected to disrupt operations and reduce copper output. The immediate effect is lower near-term supply from the site, which can tighten market availability in coming days and weeks and generally exerts downward pressure on copper prices. | Glencore said its own-sourced copper production for the first nine months fell 14% to 770,500 tonnes versus the prior year. The decline was attributed to land access, geotechnical and processing constraints at Katanga, basis changes after a sale, and lower ore mined at Collahuasi, reducing copper units. | SMM data show China’s copper inventories in domestic bonded zones fell 4,800 mt from Oct 21 to 29,700 mt as of Oct 28, with Shanghai and Guangdong declines. Limited arrivals of imported copper and importers shifting stock from bonded warehouses to the domestic market amid low social inventories support a tighter supply outlook. | The headline says Europe’s top copper producer Aurubis was hit by a cyberattack. Such an event can disrupt smelting/refining operations and affect near-term copper supply availability, often pushing expectations toward tighter inventories and supporting copper prices in the first days after the disruption. | Nevada Copper closed a restart financing package totaling up to US$123m to fund and ramp up its Pumpkin Hollow underground copper mine and processing operations underway. Financing includes equity, royalty/stream adjustments, KfW facility extension, and working-capital support, improving near-term expectations for copper production and supply. | Teck Resources increased the construction cost estimate for its Quebrada Blanca phase 2 (QB2) copper project in Chile again, adding at least US$500 million. The project’s revised economics raise the prospect of delays or slower progress, which can tighten near-term copper supply expectations and support prices. | Nevada Copper says it has closed a restart financing package providing up to about US$123 million to support the restart and ramp-up of its Pumpkin Hollow underground copper mine. This improves short-term prospects for copper production after financing was secured, which can be modestly bullish for copper prices.

The piece reports that copper prices are extending losses, attributing the decline to China’s factory slump and gains in the U.S. dollar. Softer Chinese industrial activity can reduce near-term demand expectations, while a firmer dollar often pressures commodities by making them costlier for non-dollar buyers. | Capstone Copper reported Q3 2022 copper output of 45.7 kt and highlighted cost inflation pressures, including high sulphuric acid and diesel, alongside declining realized copper prices. The company noted additional 2023 copper hedges and progress on the Mantoverde project, while provisional pricing losses reduced earnings. | Kuusakoski is investing in a new Finnish copper centre to recycle copper- and aluminium-containing cables, radiators and transformers. The plant will crush and separate material and produce cleaner copper and aluminium fractions, claiming ~40 tons of copper per week capacity. Completion is targeted for 2023, enhancing recycled supply locally. | Freeport-McMoRan and analysts warn a significant copper shortage could emerge as energy transition-driven demand rises faster than limited mine supply development. The company says current prices are insufficient to fund new projects, with few major new supplies beyond delayed COVID-era starts. Wood Mackenzie projects a large future deficit without heavy investment. | Capstone Copper reported Q3 2022 results, including quarterly copper production of 45.7k tonnes and C1 cash costs of $2.76/lb. The quarter saw a lower realized copper price and provisional pricing losses alongside inflationary cost pressures, with input costs like sulphuric acid trending lower. Additional copper hedges were added for 2023. | Comex copper for November delivery ended the month at $3.4135, down 0.83% (2.85 cents/lb). The contract is down for seven consecutive months, with weakness in recent sessions, and the lowest settlement value since Oct. 19, 2022, reflecting bearish near-term price pressure in copper. | Nevada Copper has closed a new financing package that brings its Pumpkin Hollow underground copper mine in Nevada closer to restart and ramp-up. The update relates to funding for the two fully permitted components, an underground mine and processing facility, improving near-term production outlook for copper.

SMM cites China customs data showing September imports of unwrought copper and copper semis rose 25.6% YoY and 2.4% MoM, linked to traditional peak-season demand and attractive import margins. Copper concentrate imports rose from Peru on Las Bambas recovery, while Chile volumes fell due to mine grade declines, water issues, and accidents. | SMM reports China imported 84,800 mt of copper anode in September, down 16.67% MoM, citing reduced shipments from Zambia, Congo (DRC), and South Africa. Maintenance at Zambia/DRC smelters, a CCS production accident, and a Durban Port strike disrupted African anode and blister copper exports, though operations are recovering. | China’s copper scrap imports rose in September to 167,000 mt (+7.99% MoM) and 1.36 million mt over Jan–Sep (+9.97% YoY), helped by higher LME copper prices boosting overseas shipments. Despite the higher inflows, domestic supply tightness persisted due to COVID disruptions, so SMM expects imports to stay near current levels. | BHP’s CEO warns of a timing mismatch where demand for decarbonization metals rises before supply can scale up, implying an eventual copper shortage. Trafigura notes copper stockpiles cover only about 4.9 days of consumption and could fall further. Multiple research reports project large mine-supply gaps, supporting a copper price rebound view. | Faraday Copper Corp. started a planned 10,000-metre Phase II diamond drill program at its Copper Creek copper project in Arizona. The work aims to expand the open-pit resource, delineate underground zones, support metallurgical testwork, and add monitoring infrastructure and new targets. No production changes are announced. | EU sectors call for cautious implementation of the CBAM/ETS as part of “Fit for 55”. CBAM would charge importers for emissions certificates from 2026, initially for aluminium and other high-carbon sectors, while companies warn soaring carbon costs and energy prices have already driven unprecedented aluminium capacity idling. | Ero Copper reported Q3 2022 results, including 11,189 tonnes of copper production and ongoing cost pressure, noting a $10.3m impact from final settlements on provisionally priced copper concentrate sales and higher operating costs. The firm also disclosed progress and financing guidance for growth projects and ongoing nickel exploration.

SMM reports Yangshan copper premiums quoted at $135–150/mt under warrants, largely flat day-on-day, with bill-of-lading premiums also steady to slightly higher. The import market is slack, but tight supply of available goods keeps sellers aiming for higher prices, sustaining physical copper support. LME cash to 3M at $65/mt; import losses ~300 yuan/mt. | Fabled Copper Corp. reported 2022 surface sampling results from its Muskwa Copper Project in northern British Columbia, including multiple rock samples above 0.5% copper and peak assays up to 16.05% copper with gold and silver. The company plans follow-up releases, but no production or supply change is announced. | Copper prices edged lower as bleak near-term manufacturing and metals demand outweighed hopes that China may ease COVID-19 controls next year. Global risk sentiment weakened ahead of a large U.S. rate hike, likely stifling activity. Tight supply and low inventories were cited as support, leaving overall bias mixed. | Base metals prices rose on Tuesday after widespread rumors that China might change/ease its zero-Covid policy. Three-month futures gained for copper (+2.7%), aluminium (+0.9%), and nickel (+8.2%). Analysts said reopening hopes lifted China risk assets and triggered short covering; nickel additionally benefited from strong EV sales. | BHP has signed a deal with Neoen for a 70-megawatt “renewable energy baseload” contract to supply round-the-clock electricity to its Olympic Dam copper mine from mid-2025. The wind-plus-battery power will cover about half of the mine’s expected power needs, improving energy supply reliability. | Arizona Sonoran Copper says its Parks-Salyer porphyry copper deposit infill drilling returned a 217.7-metre intercept grading 1.01% copper, from multiple additional holes. The updated drilling results bolster confidence in the project’s copper resource potential, which can support sentiment toward copper markets.

Thunder Gold announced an amended option agreement for its 95% interest in the Okohongo copper-silver property in Namibia. The deal includes exploration plans, royalties, and milestone payments tied to defining a large copper resource. It does not announce production changes, exports, or policy that would materially affect near-term copper prices. | Copper prices fell as markets digested a hawkish Fed stance signaling prolonged policy tightening, weakening risk sentiment and near-term demand expectations. China’s elevated COVID cases added pressure on industrial and construction activity. The report also notes lower tin and lead, with aluminium dipping and nickel edging up. | Aston Bay reported final 2022 assay results confirming near-surface high-grade copper at the Storm Project in Nunavut, including multiple strong intercepts (e.g., 19m @ 2.08% Cu). Management says the mineralized envelope may expand and further drilling and beneficiation work are planned for future resource definition and potential direct shipping. | Nevada Copper reported progress toward restarting its Pumpkin Hollow underground mine, including completion of restart financing, development of higher-grade stopes, and capital project work such as ore bin, dewatering, vent shaft and fans. It also secured a long-lead item (jaw crusher) and expects contractor award soon, supporting future copper output prospects. | Chile’s major copper producers reported weaker output in September. Codelco production fell 7.92% y/y to 123,200 tonnes, and Collahuasi declined 3.5% to 44,500 tonnes. Escondida rose 3.51% to 85,500 tonnes, but overall the headline points to reduced supply growth. | Investor insight says copper demand is rising due to global electrification and expects markets to stay in deficit. It describes Los Andes Copper’s Vizcachitas project in Chile, highlighting funding and strong project economics, which may support bullish sentiment for copper, though no immediate production change is indicated.

Copper rallied about 2% as hopes grow that China will ease COVID curbs, boosting demand sentiment. Investors also reacted to supply jitters: Peru’s Las Bambas mine began reducing operations amid blockades, while LME and SHFE copper inventories fell, underscoring tight market conditions. | SMM said Chinese domestic bonded copper inventories rose 1,200 mt from Oct 28 to Nov 4, reaching 30,900 mt. Shanghai bonded stocks increased while Guangdong declined. The build is attributed to port arrivals and arbitrage as BC copper prices exceeded domestic prices, prompting inflows into INE warehouses. | Copper rose sharply, posting its biggest gain since 2016, driven by optimism around China’s reopening and a slump in the US dollar. The combination of improved growth-demand sentiment for industrial metals and weaker currency support tends to boost buying and short-term price momentum for copper. | Gold futures rose after the October jobs report weakened the U.S. dollar. Copper futures surged 7.6% to the highest since late August, with the week up 7.5%. Analysts linked the rally to speculation that China could ease COVID restrictions, improving demand expectations for industrial metals.

Cannindah Resources announced assay results from drilling at the Mt Cannindah copper-gold-silver project, with hole 14 intersecting thick massive copper (182m) and multiple high-grade zones. The company says mineralization extends laterally and supports further drilling/exploration in coming months. | SMM reports copper gains after the US Fed signaled slower December hikes and strong ADP data boosted risk sentiment. Copper fundamentals also improved near term: LME and SHFE warrants fell, visible stocks stayed low, and a widening spread/short squeeze was seen supporting SHFE prices ahead of delivery. | Copper’s price “wobbled” after China dismissed hopes of easing COVID-19 restrictions. While a weaker US dollar briefly lifted metal prices on Monday, the overall move retraced Friday’s sharp rally driven by bets on stronger Chinese demand. Copper, tied to global activity, ended the session lower.

SolGold obtained $50 million in royalty financing via Osisko Gold Royalties to fund its Cascabel copper-gold project in Ecuador. The deal swaps funding for a 0.6% net smelter return royalty. With BHP and Newcrest as major shareholders, the update supports progress toward future copper supply. | SMM reports Yangshan copper spot premiums under warrants were flat, with LME cash to 3M around $10.25/mt and the import window open. Although the SHFE/LME ratio improved and LME backwardation narrowed, high domestic discounts suppressed buying, leaving few quotes and extremely slack transactions. | Ivanhoe Electric reported promising drilling results and an East Ridge oxide copper discovery at its Santa Cruz Copper Project in Arizona. Infill hole SCC-084 and step-out/parcel drilling returned high-grade copper intercepts and suggest the mineralization is open for expansion. Results will feed an updated mineral resource estimate. | Newcrest approved an A$214 million (US$140 million) expansion of its Telfer gold-copper operations, including open pits and underground mines. The investment supports increased future copper production from the West Dome. Markets may price in higher supply expectations, weighing on copper sentiment in the near term. | Five men allegedly hijacked a truck carrying copper cables worth about R2m and attempted to offload them using a hired forklift before police raided the warehouse. The driver was reportedly shot and killed. The court case was postponed, but there’s no indication of wider supply or pricing effects. | BASF and Moleaer announced a partnership combining BASF’s LixTRA leaching reagent with Moleaer nanobubble technology to improve copper recovery from hard-to-leach chalcopyrite ores. The article cites rising copper demand and potential deficits, but it provides no immediate supply, mine output, or inventory changes. | BASF and Moleaer announced a partnership to improve copper recovery from hard-to-leach primary sulfide ores using BASF’s LixTRA leaching aid and Moleaer’s nanobubble technology. The testing claims higher extraction rates for chalcopyrite heap leaching, potentially improving supply efficiency, but near-term output impact is unspecified. | Los Angeles Councilman John Lee highlighted a more-than-sixfold increase in theft and damage to street lighting systems, including copper wire theft that leaves neighborhoods without light for weeks or months. He requested enforcement and prosecution updates to strengthen the network. The higher theft-driven replacement demand is mildly supportive for copper. | Magna Mining says its first resource estimate at the Denison Sudbury Basin project shows “significant potential” and plans additional drilling. The project targets nickel and copper, with the company exploring a restart of a past-producing underground mine. The news is more sentiment-driven than supply-increasing immediately.

The piece reports Goldman Sachs lowered copper price assumptions, expecting a 170,000-tonne refined surplus in 2023 driven by weak Chinese property and European industrial demand. Demand growth forecasts were reduced, while the firm notes visible stocks are at 18-year lows. Net effect is bearish copper sentiment near term. | Copper prices fell after China’s weaker economic indicators and a fresh rise in COVID-19 cases increased concerns about industrial demand. Despite some support from tight physical supplies, three-month LME copper slipped around 0.5% and SHFE copper diverged. Market focus now shifts to US inflation and politics. | China’s leading copper producers urged the government to expand ore prospecting and open new mines at home and abroad amid concerns over global supply disruptions. It cites reduced operations at Peru’s Las Bambas due to blockades and a longer Chuquicamata smelter maintenance in Chile, raising short-term supply tightness expectations. | Ascendant Resources said it found high-grade copper intercepts and extended a footwall lens to a 550-metre strike length at the Venda Nova South deposit, using a 200-metre step-out drill hole at Lagoa Salgada. The results are supportive for copper project prospects and potential supply expectations. | Romios Gold reports a strong IP/MT anomaly at its Trek South project and newly discovered copper-tungsten skarn, with copper assays up to 0.98% Cu and tungsten assays from 0.04%-0.68% WO3. The company highlights drill-ready targets near inferred plutons, which could be mildly supportive for copper sentiment. | Morocco-based Aterian seeks a JV partner to develop its Agdz copper project and reports high-grade copper and silver discoveries at another Moroccan asset, Azrar. It has acquired a portfolio of Moroccan copper-silver and base metal projects and discusses funding via Rwanda trading, boosting near-term sentiment for future copper supply development.

The piece argues copper could breakout as the U.S. dollar cools after Fed hikes and as rumors of China exiting zero-COVID by March 2023 boost sentiment on demand. It notes officials quickly reaffirmed support for restrictions, but suggests reopening could still lift copper prices. | First Quantum restarted fixed-loop electromagnetic surveys over the Money nickel-copper-PGE prospect in Western Australia and plans additional drilling targets. Prior 12 holes reported nickel and copper grades, and management highlights similarities to China’s Jinchuan deposit. Results are expected next year. | First Quantum restarts FLEM surveys at Dreadnought Resources’ Money nickel-copper-PGE prospect in Western Australia under a 70% earn-in. The survey aims to identify shallow massive sulphides after prior drilling returned meaningful nickel and copper grades. Additional drill targets and results are expected later next year. | Copper prices are reported lower as concerns about weakening global industrial demand weigh on the market. The article also argues prices may have fallen too far because supply tightness could persist for years, but the near-term narrative emphasizes demand risk driving the current decline. | SMM says Yangshan copper premiums under warrants fell to $135–147/mt, down about $5/mt from the prior day. Bill-of-lading premiums also eased. Import trading was sluggish with no early deals, as high import losses deterred buyers, while sellers lowered offers, pressuring copper buying interest. | Zambia’s mines cadastre office reopened on October 19 after being disrupted by corruption concerns that tainted some prior license grants. The office will accept new mining license applications from November 21, which Anglo American partner Arc Minerals and others view as positive for Zambia’s copper prospects. | Copper Fox provided an update on its 100% owned Van Dyke in-situ copper recovery project in Arizona, detailing community engagement and planned access upgrades for well re-entry. It outlines downhole video surveys, hydrogeologic monitoring/testing, and refurbishment of monitoring equipment. The release references the prior PEA but no near-term production changes. | Faraday Copper Corp. reported Q3 2022 financial results and outlined ongoing work on its Arizona Copper Creek copper project, including a Phase II drill program, updated mineral resource estimate activity, and geological/metallurgical studies. The company also discussed future milestones for updated MRE and PEA, without any production changes. | Metallic Minerals announced it acquired additional patented claims (Morning Star property) contiguous to its La Plata Cu-Ag-Au project in Colorado. The company highlights potential porphyry copper mineralization and intends to incorporate the claims into 2023 exploration planning. The update is primarily exploration-focused, with limited near-term impact on copper markets. | Copper futures are down about 17% year-to-date amid worries over global demand. However, the piece argues prices may have fallen too far because visible copper inventories at global exchanges continue to decline in a long downtrend, suggesting supply remains tight for years.

Adventus Mining and Salazar Resources say Ecuador’s government investment institution approved a foreign direct investment agreement for the El Domo copper-gold project. The deal supports development of a new copper asset in Ecuador, potentially improving long-run supply expectations, though near-term effects on current copper supply appear limited. | LME nickel surged 12% (and briefly ~15% to $30,960/t) on low volumes, narrowly avoiding a 15% daily limit that would trigger a disruption event. Copper rose slightly; aluminium dipped modestly amid stable Russian metal acceptability. | Reports of a possible U.S. ban or sanctions on Russian aluminum imports are cited as a bullish catalyst. The article says aluminum prices spiked after the White House consideration became public and could extend gains if restrictions are enacted. It also links Russian supply share to nickel and copper risk. | Comex Copper futures for November delivery settled 2.43% lower at $3.8550 per pound, falling 9.60 cents. The drop ended a four-session winning streak and marked the largest one-day percentage decline since late October. The estimate totals 321.7 million tonnes grading 0.32% copper (plus Mo and silver). As an Inferred, non-reserve resource, near-term copper price effects appear limited.

The report expects copper to rise in November, citing macro support from a softer USD after the Fed and mixed US data, and tight physical markets. LME inventories are at very low levels with high cancelled warrants, while SHFE stocks are also lean, increasing short-squeeze risk despite some demand softness. | China’s copper ore and concentrate imports rose to 2.27 million mt in September 2022, a new monthly high, up 7.7% YoY and slightly MoM. Total Jan–Sep imports increased 8.8% YoY, with notable changes by supplier (Peru up, Chile down). | The news reports that Russia’s largest copper mine in Siberia is expected to start operations. The release discusses prior copper sulphide/chalcopyrite intersections and planned follow-up holes, but no immediate production or supply change. | BeMetals reports completion of an aircore program and the start of core drilling targeting six copper prospects at its Pangeni project on Zambia’s Copperbelt. It cites encouraging prior copper intersections and discusses geological targets to be tested, with results expected in early 2023. | Adventus Mining and Salazar agreed with Ecuador’s government on incentives for the El Domo copper-gold project, including reduced income tax, exemptions on capital outflow taxes and import duties for equipment, and tax/legal stability until 2033. A gravity survey is also underway. No new production or quantified reserve changes are reported.

Copper prices fell as rising COVID-19 cases in China heightened demand uncertainty, with Beijing and Guangzhou reporting more infections. Geopolitical concerns tied to an explosion in Poland also pressured risk sentiment. The article cites declines in LME three-month and SHFE December copper contracts during the session. | Deep-South Resources said Namibia’s High Court postponed its verdict on whether to review a ministerial decision not to renew the company’s exploration and prospecting licence, pushing a decision to February 23, 2023. The delay extends through court closure in December–January, affecting the company’s copper project timeline. | BHP disputed union accusations for a strike at its Escondida copper mine in Chile scheduled for Nov. 21 and 23. The company called the strike unjustifiable, but the planned stoppage raises short-term copper supply disruption risk from a major producer, likely pressuring copper prices lower over coming days.

A safety stand down has curtailed operations at the Highland Valley Copper mine in British Columbia, reportedly linked to an incident involving a Bucyrus digging machine. The United Steelworkers union says the shutdown is related to safety concerns, while Teck says it is working with employees for a safe resumption of operations. | Cyprium Metals reported anomalous copper and nickel mineralisation from reverse-circulation drilling at the Cue project in Western Australia, including long intercepts of copper and a nickel interval. The firm is also working to restart its Nifty copper mine, but no near-term production changes are announced. | Cyprium Metals reported anomalous mineralisation from RC drilling at its Cue project, including significant copper and nickel intercepts, plus some gold and zinc. It also reiterated work toward financing and restarting its Nifty copper project, which has existing infrastructure and planned H1 FY2024 copper plate production. | Antilles Gold confirmed high-grade copper porphyry mineralisation at its El Pilar oxide-sulphide project in Cuba, reporting a 134m intercept with strong copper grades and open depth, plus preliminary gold results. Assays are preliminary pending replication at a certified lab, with further drilling planned; no production impact yet. | MAX Resource Corp. provided an update on initial drilling at its CESAR copper-silver project in NE Colombia. Eight holes totaling 1,421m were completed, with visible chalcocite and malachite in core and assays pending for early December. Drilling is moving to the URU-CE zone into mid-December. | Codelco began trials of a fully electric 14t loading shovel at its El Teniente copper mine in Chile. The trial is expected to cut diesel use, emissions, noise, and maintenance costs, with hopes for greater battery-based autonomy. Codelco plans testing for one year while pursuing broader decarbonization goals. | Palladium One reports grab samples from its Canalask nickel-copper-PGE project in Yukon with assays up to 2.1% Ni and 6.17% Cu, alongside a drone magnetometer survey refining an ultramafic dyke target. Permitting is underway for systematic drilling of historic EM targets. | Adventus Mining said it has been awarded a mining contract for its El Domo copper-gold project and committed US$5 million in equity investment. The contract award can be read as incremental progress toward copper production, which may provide mild near-term support for copper price sentiment. | Vale and GM signed a term sheet for long-term supply of battery-grade nickel sulfate from Vale’s proposed Bécancour, Quebec plant, equivalent to 25,000 tonnes per year of contained nickel for GM’s Ultium nickel-based cathodes. Deliveries are targeted to start in 2H 2026, supporting nickel demand outlook. | Salazar Resources said negotiations concluded for a 48-month mining contract for the El Domo–Curipamba open-pit copper-gold project in Ecuador, including pre-strip mining and construction of tailings, waste rock, and mine infrastructure. The milestone is expected to move the project toward production, modestly supporting copper supply expectations. | BHP is reported to have raised its bid for OZ Minerals to $6.4 billion. Because OZ Minerals is a copper miner, the deal could affect expectations for future copper supply and production continuity. Still, the article lacks specifics on output timing or disruption, so near-term price impact is likely limited. | Proponents and opponents are still fighting over a proposed Arizona copper mine. The continued campaign against the project could delay permitting or development, reducing the near-term outlook for incremental copper supply. That supply risk can support copper prices over the coming days to weeks.

Comex Copper (front month) settled 2.60% lower at $3.6895 per pound, losing 9.85 cents. The contract is down for four consecutive sessions, totaling a 6.62% decline over that span, and reached the lowest settlement since Nov. 7, signaling bearish momentum for copper. | A federal appeals court set aside prior rulings and will rehear the legal challenge to the Resolution Copper project on land considered sacred by Apache Stronghold. Opponents want delays to prevent destroying Oak Flat, while supporters say the mine could meet a large share of domestic copper demand. | SMM says Chinese major-market copper inventories rose to 128,000 mt as of Nov 18, up 10,900 mt from Monday, largely driven by SHFE 2211 copper delivery. Imported arrivals were limited and domestic smelter shipments were low, despite brief spot deficits. SMM expects inventories to fall next week and consumption to improve. | Arizona’s Court of Appeals overturned a permit issuance for the proposed Resolution Copper underground mine and ordered the state to restart the Clean Water Act permitting process. The mine is targeted for massive copper output. The decision raises near-term regulatory risk of delay, potentially supporting copper market sentiment. | SMM survey data show China’s domestic bonded copper inventories fell 3,700 mt from Nov 11 to 24,600 mt as of Nov 18, including declines in Shanghai and Guangdong. The drop is linked to copper entering the domestic market after contract delivery, amid signs of rising cleared imported arrivals at ports. | Arizona’s Court of Appeals ruled the state environmental agency illegally issued a Clean Water Act permit for the proposed Resolution Copper Mine, ordering ADEQ to restart the process. The San Carlos Apache Tribe opposes the mine, and further appeals are planned, adding uncertainty and potential delay to copper supply plans. | Lundin Mining plans to fill a large sinkhole near its Alcaparrosa underground copper mine in Chile. The firm is expelling leaked water and has reduced inflow rates sharply, investing $10m so far. It also plans to pump 1.3 million cubic metres of water to local industrial users, aiming to seal the affected area. | Arizona’s Court of Appeals ruled the state environmental agency illegally issued a Clean Water Act permit for the proposed Resolution Copper mine and ordered the permit process restarted. The San Carlos Apache Tribe argues the project would destroy Oak Flat, while the company reviews the decision. The mine is projected to produce large volumes of copper.

Citi downgraded Sandfire, saying demand for copper is unlikely to appear before the first half of 2023, despite hopes that China’s reopening could support sentiment. Even with copper spot up 7% and Sandfire shares up 24% since mid-September, Citi is “very bearish” for the near term. | The article reports that Dilawar will work with MCC to launch development activities on a copper project. The announcement is directly related to copper investment and future supply prospects. While it may be mildly supportive for copper prices via sentiment, it is unlikely to change near-term fundamentals quickly. | The news roundup reports progress on several mining companies, including Poseidon Nickel’s positive bankable feasibility study for the Black Swan nickel project and Blackstone Minerals’ drilling confirming continuity and high grades of nickel/PGE at Ta Khoa. It also notes Dundas Minerals exploring nickel and copper, supporting modest near-term sentiment. | A PRNewswire piece summarizes a copper pipe and tube market report, citing steady demand drivers such as HVAC/refrigeration growth, construction activity, and automotive production. It forecasts moderate long-run growth to 2027 but provides no specific near-term supply or policy event that would likely impact copper prices immediately. | Wallbridge Mining has sold all its Quebec and Ontario nickel assets, rights, and obligations to Archer Exploration, including a 100% stake in the Grasset nickel sulphide project. Wallbridge will receive Archer shares and retain a 2% net smelter royalty, with the rationale to refocus on gold. | Granite Creek Copper announced IP survey results at its Carmacks copper-gold-silver project in Yukon, identifying new high-priority targets (e.g., Sourtoe Zone, 147 Deep, Gap and 58 targets) for follow-up exploration. It also closed a private placement to fund Canadian exploration expenses. No near-term production changes were disclosed. | The piece argues that rising USD and easing China zero-COVID expectations are lifting copper, while aluminium gains are supported by hopes for China reopening and LME decisions on Russian metal sanctions. Nickel is described as in a short-term bull market with breakout moves amplified by LME liquidity after a short squeeze, though Indonesia supply risks loom.

Universal Copper reports completion of 3D geological modeling at its Poplar copper project, identifying higher-grade copper-gold-molybdenum-silver targets and citing recent drill intercepts. The work is intended to guide exploration drilling in 2023, but the release does not imply near-term changes in copper supply or operations. | Amarc Resources said it signed an earn-in agreement with Boliden to advance the Duke porphyry Cu-Au district in British Columbia. Boliden can earn up to 70% by funding staged exploration and development (including $5m committed in 2022/early 2023). Drilling plans are being finalized to expand copper targets. | Doré Copper Mining reports remaining assay results from its 2022 infill drilling at the Corner Bay property in Québec, highlighting high-grade copper intercepts and continuity of mineralization. The drilling supports an upgrade for a feasibility study and expects feasibility completion in 2024, with further infill planned in 2023.

Victims of the 2018 Sterlite Copper-related firing case are now supporting reopening of the Thoothukudi copper smelting plant, arguing inquiry findings show no plant role in the incident and that environmental clearances were granted. Advocates cite large livelihood losses from closure and urge immediate government action, implying higher copper supply expectations. | Peru’s copper mining has faced disruption from protests and road blockades, raising concerns over potential higher taxes or restrictive mining legislation under President Castillo. However, sources argue the government remains pro-mining with stable contract/legal systems. Recent signs of production recovery and a higher copper concentrates TC index support improving short-term copper sentiment. | Codelco has told major clients it may slash copper deliveries to China by 50% and could lose up to 200,000 tonnes of output next year due to production challenges at the Chuquicamata smelter. Clients say cathode deliveries under evergreen contracts may be jeopardized, tightening market supply. | Chile’s state copper producer Codelco is reported to cut sales to China next year, signaling lower planned copper deliveries to a key market. The change can prompt traders to revise near-term expectations for copper supply and tighten sentiment, likely exerting downward pressure on copper prices. | Comex copper for November delivery settled slightly higher at $3.6175, up 0.08% (0.30 cent/lb) and extending gains to two sessions. The piece mainly reports price performance and broader percentage changes versus recent highs/lows, without detailing new fundamentals or supply-demand catalysts. | Camino Minerals says it has confirmed continuity of mineralization at a new copper discovery in Peru, reporting high solubility results over copper oxide intercepts in the Lourdes zone at its Los Chapitos property. The announcement highlights ongoing exploration success, driving a stock jump and positive copper sentiment.

Pakistan’s Supreme Court was told that the Reko Diq site contains no strategic minerals beyond copper and gold, based on $240 million studies. The hearing covers a reconstituted mining agreement after Barrick Gold’s earlier dispute and suspension ended. Legal clarity may support redevelopment of a major copper project. | Boway promoted new copper alloy strip products for connectors at Electronica 2022 in Germany, targeting uses in consumer electronics and electric vehicles. The release highlights performance properties of several Cu-based alloys and expansion of production capacity in Germany, but provides no data on volumes or market tightness. | Anglo American and Aurubis signed an MoU to develop a copper product offering aligned with the Copper Mark chain-of-custody standard. The effort focuses on technology-driven traceability covering mining, processing, transportation, and market delivery. The article does not cite any near-term changes to supply, demand, or pricing. | Anglo American and Aurubis signed a deal to enhance green copper traceability using Copper Mark chain-of-custody standards and technology to track the full production cycle. The firms aim to improve Scope 3 emissions transparency and support low-carbon supply-chain demand, but the news does not indicate immediate physical supply or pricing changes.

Freeport has agreed six-year-high copper charges for 2023 with Chinese smelters, settling refining/treatment terms at elevated levels. The report points to firmer smelter-related costs or availability in China. While this can influence copper pricing terms, it may be more backward-looking than a fresh near-term supply shock. | Los Cerros acquired nickel-copper projects in Papua New Guinea from Footprint Resources and plans diamond drilling and geophysical work. Historical results include a trench assay with 2.6% copper and float nickel sulphide up to 45.8%. The move is exploratory and meant to attract JV partners, not to signal near-term production changes. | Los Cerros acquired five nickel-copper projects in Papua New Guinea via Footprint Resources and plans a 3,000m drill program at Kusi and geophysical work at Veri Veri, targeting high-grade nickel sulphide and historical copper-bearing assays. The news boosts exploration activity but offers no immediate supply impact for benchmark copper or nickel prices. | SMM said copper inventory across major Chinese markets fell to 113,400 mt, down 8,300 mt from Monday and 14,600 mt from last Friday. Most regions declined except Guangdong. East China buying increased as prices fell; high Guangdong premiums and transfers supported the flow. Supply rises near month-end may be offset by weaker consumption. | Transnet Freight Rail and Sasolburg police have arrested six suspects for stealing copper cables worth about R750,000 each, with multiple arrests reported over two weeks. Total recovered cable value is estimated at R3.4 million, and authorities seized vehicles and equipment used in the thefts. | Technology Minerals reports grab-sample assay results from its Aramo project in Asturias, Spain, including up to 28% copper, 1.88% cobalt, and 1.68% nickel. The company completed a 3D laser survey for underground mapping. The news is exploration-positive, potentially improving short-term sentiment for copper and nickel. | SMM says copper inventories in China’s domestic bonded zones increased by 2,000 mt to 26,600 mt by Nov 25. Shanghai bonded inventories rose to 23,000 mt while Guangdong eased to 3,600 mt. Customs clearance slowed due to an unfavorable SHFE/LME price ratio, prompting traders to move cargo into bonded warehouses. | Asiamet Resources’ CEO discusses ongoing work on an updated feasibility study for the BKM copper project. The update could affect market expectations about the project’s potential viability and timing of future copper supply. However, it does not indicate immediate production changes, so near-term price impact is likely modest. | Asiamet Resources’ CEO discussed recent work on an updated feasibility study for the company’s BKM copper project. The news is directly related to a copper development pathway, but feasibility-study updates generally do not change short-term supply or inventories quickly, so near-term copper price effects are likely limited. | The government ends a six-month scrap-iron trade ban two months early, except dealers cannot handle copper for now. Copper remains the industry’s key traded metal. | Comex copper for November delivery settled at $3.6265, down 0.15% on the week, and down for two consecutive weeks. Despite the weekly decline, prices rose for three straight sessions in the current timeframe, with the latest move up versus earlier declines.

Power Nickel reports encouraging drill assay results from the Nisk project, including high-grade nickel-copper-cobalt sulfide and PGE-bearing mineralization. Intercepts extend mineralization further at depth and eastward, and drilling will be expanded. As exploration progresses, this can modestly support nickel and copper sentiment. | SMM says Chinese copper inventories across major markets rose by 600 mt over the weekend to 114,000 mt, driven mainly by higher stocks in Guangdong. While some regions fell, low import clearance and COVID-19-related efficiency issues weigh on shipments. SMM expects inventory to edge up slightly amid weaker end-month demand. | Copper Mountain Mining said it received bondholder approval to sell its Eva Copper Project and Australian exploration tenements to Harmony, with closing expected by Q1 2023. Proceeds fund a bond amendment and a bond buyback offer. The release reiterates Copper Mountain Mine copper-equivalent production outlook but no immediate market shock. | Global Energy Metals reported encouraging assay results from initial field sampling at its Nevada Lovelock project, including copper up to 26.3% and nickel up to 0.309%, plus cobalt. The update is exploration-focused with no production or confirmed reserves, so any short-term price effect is likely limited and sentiment-driven. | Global Energy Metals released sampling results from its Nevada Lovelock project, reporting assays up to 26.3% copper and 0.309% nickel, alongside cobalt. The update is based on limited surface rock sampling and recommends further mapping and potential drilling. It signals exploration potential but does not affect near-term supply. | Copper futures fell 1.3% to near a three-week low, with traders citing renewed COVID-19-driven civil unrest in China and concerns about further demand destruction and weaker commodity imports. The piece notes supplies tightening concerns are being ignored, keeping the immediate tone bearish for copper. | Unionized workers at BHP’s Escondida copper mine in northern Chile accepted the latest offer, preventing a planned strike on Nov. 28 and 30. With no labor disruption, the near-term risk of reduced copper output declines, supporting expectations for steadier supply in the coming weeks. | Metal Energy expands drilling at the Manibridge nickel sulphide mine in Manitoba, reporting prior high-grade nickel intercepts (assays pending) and planning further 10,000-metre drilling. The nickel belt is described as underexplored, supporting positive sentiment for nickel supply prospects, with minor stated copper co-product potential. | MMG’s Peru mine has resumed copper production, which improves short-term expectations for available supply. Still, transportation remains halted, likely delaying shipments to market and limiting the immediate effect on inventories. The net implication is moderately supportive for copper prices within the next days to weeks.

CCTV shows a substation explosion in Melbourne during an attempted copper theft. Authorities are investigating. | Palladium One reported high-grade intercepts at its Tyko Project’s West Pickle discovery, including 10.4% nickel and 3.4% copper over 2.3 meters, and resumed drilling with a second rig. The news supports exploration success for nickel and copper, but offers no immediate production change. | Ivanhoe Electric reported its first drill hole at the Typhoon “Far Southwest” anomaly intersecting chalcopyrite/pyrite copper mineralization at ~1,059 meters depth, plus additional oxide copper at East Ridge. Infill drilling continues to refine resources for the Santa Cruz copper project, with a PEA planned for 2023. | Usha Resources filed and mailed an information circular for a proposed spin-out of its Nicobat nickel-copper project into Formation Metals. The release describes recent drilling confirming high-grade nickel-copper shoots and outlines transaction conditions. While Ni and Cu are mentioned via grades, it is primarily corporate restructuring, not a market-moving supply or demand change. | Murchison Minerals announced drillhole BDF22-002 results from its Barre de Fer zone, reporting 121.2 m at 1.36% NiEq (including higher-grade intervals) and associated copper-equivalent grades in nickel sulphide mineralization. The update supports exploration potential but does not affect near-term production or inventories. | C3 Metals received drill permits and plans an initial 5,000m program at its Arthurs Seat copper-gold-silver project in Jamaica. The update supports potential future resource growth but has no immediate production impact. | BASF partnered with Moleaer to apply nanobubble technology plus BASF’s LixTRA reagent to enhance copper leaching and recovery, especially from chalcopyrite sulfide ores. The firms cite rising copper demand tied to electrification and potential future supply gaps, implying incremental supply support and bullish sentiment for copper. | Copper prices rose 1.1% on Tuesday as investors bet China’s COVID protests could force faster easing of curbs, boosting growth and metals demand expectations. Easier property developer financing also supported the metals-intensive sector. The yuan’s strength made dollar metals cheaper for Chinese buyers, lifting LME copper. Aluminium and nickel rose too. | Palladium One reported drill results featuring grades of 10.4% nickel and 3.4% copper over 2.3 metres. Such assay announcements can lift market expectations for the potential resource base and future supply of nickel and copper, providing modest near-term support to prices. | Palladium One reports drill results including 10.4% nickel and 3.4% copper over 2.3 meters. Such high-grade findings can support market expectations for future nickel and copper output, potentially adding modest upside pressure to prices in the near term.

Comex copper for December delivery settled 0.51% higher at $3.6300 per pound, up 1.85 cents. By limiting scrap exports and illicit supply, the measures could tighten nearby copper availability and support prices over coming days. | The piece highlights that copper is heading for its best month since April 2021, driven by optimism around China. That framing suggests improving near-term expectations for industrial demand, which tends to support copper prices over the coming days to weeks. | South Africa announced a six-month ban on exporting copper and copper-alloy waste and scrap, with a permit system for semi-finished copper exports. Tighter controls begin immediately upon gazetting, likely affecting near-term copper export flows. | CanAlaska reported assay results from the first 14 holes of its Manibridge Phase 2 drill program confirming nickel-copper-cobalt mineralization. Intersections include ~0.8% Ni over 20m and ~0.6% Ni over ~20–25m near the historic Manibridge Nickel Mine. Further drilling is planned and two assays are pending. | Alianza and partner Allied Copper reported initial drilling at the Klondike property in Colorado, with Hole KDB22-05 intersecting 4.26% copper over 1.06 metres within broader anomalous intervals. The article highlights prospects on the West Graben Fault and notes prior and surface copper assays, but does not address production or supply changes. | Gold and copper were steady as markets awaited Fed Chair Powell’s speech and key US payroll data. The article says copper is set for its best monthly gain in 2022, helped by expectations that rate hikes may slow and by speculation about potential scaling back of China’s COVID restrictions. | NBN Co says copper-based broadband faces rising operational and maintenance costs it can’t avoid, citing higher fault volumes and truck rolls versus fibre. It warns copper degradation will worsen costs and seeks favourable regulatory conditions to fund more upgrades. Estimated copper-to-fibre upgrade costs are $2,000–$3,000 per premises.

South Africa’s trade minister announced a six-month ban on exporting copper and copper alloys to curb widespread theft of copper cables and vandalism of infrastructure. The editorial says costs are far beyond the metal’s value and cites major losses for utilities and transport. Temporary restrictions hit legitimate traders and alter export flows. | South Africa imposed a six-month ban on exporting copper and copper-alloy scrap (and most ferrous scrap) to curb rampant metal theft tied to public infrastructure. The restriction, part of a broader three-phase intervention, is likely to reduce global copper scrap flows and support copper price expectations over coming days. | Artisanal copper mining in Peru’s Andes has surged after high copper prices, using permits that overlap with formal concessions held by Southern Copper’s Los Chancas and MMG’s Las Bambas. The overlap fuels permit disputes and permit cancellations, raising risks of delays and reduced certainty for large-scale copper project development. | BMW Group, via BMW i Ventures, invests in US startup Jetti Resources developing catalytic leaching to extract copper from previously unused sulfide ores and mine waste. Historical sampling at the Puerta Verde 2 mine reported up to 3.8% copper and 774.9 g/t silver, with 3D IP outlining a target. No production or quantifiable near-term supply impact is stated.

BMW i Ventures invested in Jetti, a low-carbon copper processing technology provider, to help secure critical raw materials amid rising EV demand. The article cites higher copper intensity for electric vehicles and forecasts potential copper shortfalls. Jetti uses leaching instead of smelting, requiring less power and boosting extraction from low-grade sources. | Comex copper front-month futures settled 2.27% higher at $3.8140 per pound, gaining 8.45 cents. Prices were higher for three straight sessions and six of the past seven, reaching the highest settlement since Nov. 15, 2022, signaling bullish near-term momentum in copper markets. | SMM reports China’s major-market copper inventories at 112,700 mt on Dec 2, down 1,300 mt from Monday. Arrivals were limited and December purchases increased, but smelters shipped more while Guangdong demand was weak. Supplies look higher next week with import arrivals rising, while inventories should fall slightly amid some demand recovery. | The EPA proposed restrictions that would block or limit plans for the Pebble mine in Alaska’s Bristol Bay region, citing unacceptable adverse effects on salmon habitat. The copper project faces further regulatory hurdles before a final determination, potentially tightening expected copper supply prospects. | BMW took a stake in U.S. copper miner Jetti Resources to secure raw material supply for electric vehicles. Jetti’s low-power leaching technology targets copper from low-grade resources, potentially expanding supply while cutting emissions. With EV demand rising, the investment and reported production gains are supportive for copper sentiment. | Rio Tinto, Freeport-McMoRan and others are deploying new (including bacteria-based) leaching methods to extract copper from waste rock stored at mine sites. The firms say the approach could boost copper recovery and add production within a few years while avoiding lengthy permitting, supporting near-term supply expectations. | The article expects weaker near-term copper prices, citing a widening refined copper market deficit and mixed production trends (Chile down, DRC up). It highlights rising China consumption uncertainty amid renewed COVID restrictions and protests, noting the copper index fell and March Comex copper dropped to about $7,915/ton.

Cobre Limited reported high-grade assay results from drill hole NCP20A at its Comet Target in Botswana’s Kalahari Copper Belt. Results include a 30m copper-silver intersection averaging 1.25% Cu and standout intervals up to 10.9% Cu, reinforcing the potential for an economic deposit and more drilling. | New World Resources reported “exceptional” deep drilling results at its Antler copper project in Arizona, including an 8.1% copper interval over 10.7 metres and ongoing mineralisation open at depth. The company says results could raise overall resource grade and indicated tonnes as scoping and pre-feasibility studies continue. | Copper rose to a three-week high as more Chinese cities eased COVID curbs, lifting expectations for fuller economic reopening. The US and EU are weighing new climate-based tariffs on Chinese steel and aluminum to curb carbon emissions, amid concerns about Chinese overcapacity and pressure on domestic industries. While still early-stage and not formally proposed, tariff prospects could tighten aluminium import supply expectations. Copper effects appear indirect through wider market sentiment. | Teck Resources said Highland Valley Copper near Logan Lake temporarily closed the Valley Pit due to geotechnical instability. The pit will reopen after remediation and safety confirmation. Teck reports no worker-safety or environmental risk and says operations continue with stockpiled ore, but the disruption can pressure near-term copper supply expectations. | Copper prices gained as China began easing COVID-19 curbs, boosting hopes for demand recovery in the top consumer. LME three-month copper rose 0.5% and Shanghai January copper edged slightly lower. Gains were limited by signs of softer imported copper demand and slowing inventory withdrawals, while the dollar stayed steady. | SMM reports sluggish Yangshan copper trading as demand remains weak.
