LME contango widened and quotes were lowered; hydro- and pyro-copper were traded at reduced levels under warrants. | Pacton Gold filed an NI 43-101 technical report for the Adeline copper project in Labrador, Canada, tied to its proposed acquisition and pending financing and regulatory acceptance. The news is copper-specific, but the transaction is exploration-stage and approval-dependent, so immediate influence on copper prices is likely small. | LME aluminium fell 0.9% and copper slipped 0.2% as markets weighed uncertainty over China’s easing of zero-COVID restrictions. Despite earlier optimism for stronger demand, investors took a wait-and-see stance amid a firmer U.S. dollar after strong U.S. services data, pressuring dollar-priced commodities. | The piece’s headline says Glencore believes this time is different regarding an upcoming copper shortage. If the market interprets this as stronger-than-expected near-term supply tightness, it would likely raise expectations for copper inventories and pricing, creating upward pressure over the next days to weeks. | Candente Copper Corp. announces a reconstituted board following Fortescue-related majority voting outcomes. The company states it will advance its Cañariaco project, including planned high-priority drilling in 2023. No production, permitting, funding, or supply/transport changes are announced that would affect near-term copper prices. | Chile’s Marimaca Copper reports additional positive higher-grade results from northern infill drilling at its MOD. Better-than-expected grades can improve the perceived resource outlook and future production economics, typically supporting copper market sentiment. The impact on near-term spot prices is likely modest due to longer development timelines. | Comex copper for December delivery settled 0.58% higher at $3.8115 per pound, gaining 2.20 cents. The contract rose on eight of the past 10 sessions. The piece frames current performance versus recent 52-week high/low and year-to-date movement, signaling modest positive price momentum.

Goldman Sachs forecasts copper prices will reach record levels as a looming supply shortage meets growing demand, leaving the physical market in deficit in 2023. Glencore’s CEO also expects a “huge deficit,” reinforcing a bullish near-term supply-tightness narrative for copper. | China’s unwrought and copper-product imports rose 5.8% in November, supported by expectations of stronger demand next year as Beijing backs the property sector and eases COVID restrictions. Customs data show more inflows and bonded stocks. The article links improved sentiment with higher LME and Shanghai copper prices in November. | Fabled Copper Corp. announced 2022 surface fieldwork results from its Muskwa Copper Project, including assays from Target 11 and surrounding areas. Several samples show copper mineralization (some >0.5% Cu). The company plans additional releases, but the update is exploratory with no production impact. | Ring of Fire Metals (Wyloo Metals’ renamed unit) signed an MoU with Webequie First Nation to collaborate on exploration and negotiations for the proposed Eagle’s Nest mine in Ontario’s Ring of Fire. The deposit is described as high-grade nickel-copper, but the agreement signals partnership framework rather than near-term output. | Glencore says a huge copper deficit is coming, arguing current prices do not reflect the tightness. It highlights lagging mine development, elevated country/operational risks, and reduced copper investment (capex) expectations, while demand is supported by grid expansion and energy-transition targets. | The headline reports that Vale plans to split off its metals group to better capitalize on battery demand. Because the move is framed around battery-linked metals, including nickel and copper, it may boost sentiment for near-term supply and demand expectations, supporting prices even if immediate physical effects are unclear.

Comex copper front-month futures for December delivery settled 1.15% higher at $3.8555, gaining 4.40 cents per pound. It was the largest one-day dollar and percentage gain since Dec. 1, and copper has risen for two consecutive sessions. The contract is up over two sessions and has hit multi-week highs. | Vale plans to split its base metals operations into a separate entity to ensure nickel and copper assets receive dedicated funding. Management expects accelerating battery/EV demand and a premium for battery-grade nickel, projecting higher production. The restructuring aims to attract capital and optimize capacity, supporting bullish sentiment for both metals. | Vale said it will ring-fence its nickel and copper operations into a new Vale Base Metals unit, with Canadian, Indonesian and Brazilian assets, and plans to name a strategic partner in the first half of next year. The move aims to attract capital for base-metal investments as EV battery demand rises. | Vale plans to split off its base metals operations into a separate entity, a move aimed at ensuring nickel and copper assets receive sustained capital. Executives cite rising battery and EV demand, highlight North American production, and set ambitious nickel and copper output targets requiring major investment. | Vale said it will ring-fence and split out its copper and nickel assets into Vale Base Metals, including Canadian and Brazilian mines, and later select a strategic partner. The move accompanies plans for major base-metal investment as EV battery demand rises, supporting improved short-term sentiment for copper and nickel supply outlooks. | The U.S. is proposing carbon-emissions-based tariffs on imported steel and aluminum, creating a “club” of lower-emission producers with lower tariff rates and charging higher tariffs for higher-emission exporters. The framework is being discussed with the EU and targets “dirty” metal, potentially altering aluminium supply and pricing in the region. | Faraday Copper updated the Preliminary Economic Assessment and Mineral Resource Estimate for its Copper Creek copper project in Arizona. The company incorporated new drill results, historical core assays, and geotechnical/metallurgical test work, with the updated study planned for Q2 2023. The news is a technical milestone, not an imminent supply change. | Amarc and Boliden have started core drilling at Amarc’s 100% owned Duke porphyry copper-gold district in central British Columbia. The program is part of an earn-in agreement where Boliden can build up to 70% by funding exploration and development. Results are expected through 2023, with drilling continuing into early 2023. | Norway says a WTO panel ruled in its favour against U.S. import duties imposed in 2018 on steel and aluminium. The Norwegian foreign ministry stated the U.S. aluminium tariff breached WTO rules. No immediate U.S. response was provided, and rulings in similar cases are expected later. | Comex copper for December delivery settled up 2.50 cents per pound, or 0.65%, at $3.8805. Prices rose for three straight sessions, reaching the highest settlement value since Nov. 11. The move comes alongside a month-to-date increase of 4.05% and remains well below prior highs.

London and Shanghai copper prices rose to multi-month highs as China eased COVID-19 curbs and property support improved demand expectations. Goldman lifted its copper price forecast and cited tightening visible stocks. Non-ferrous prices broadly moved higher, with aluminium and nickel also up, supported by a weaker dollar. | SMM says China’s domestic bonded copper inventories rose 4,200 mt from Dec 2 to 32,600 mt by Dec 9. Shanghai bonded inventories increased to 29,200 mt, while Guangdong was unchanged. The build followed exports from smelters and bill-of-lading arrivals, with sluggish port inquiries tied to low SHFE/LME ratio and softer Yangshan premiums. | Cordoba Minerals and JCHX will jointly develop Cordoba’s Alacran copper-gold-silver project in Colombia, with JCHX acquiring a 50% stake for $100m. Payments are tied to closing, feasibility study/EIA submission, and EIA approval. The deal could affect copper expectations but appears too slow for near-term price pressure. | Azimut and KGHM International sign a JV for Quebec’s Kukamas copper-gold property. KGHM can earn an initial 50% stake by spending $5m over four years and may invest at least $4.2m more to add 20% to fund a PEA. No production timing or near-term supply impact is stated. | Vale plans to separate its base metals operations from its iron ore business, covering nickel assets in Canada and copper/nickel production elsewhere. Management says the move will attract capital and better position Vale to meet expected EV and battery demand, with nickel and copper receiving more focused investment. | Vale plans to split its base metals business from its iron ore unit in mid-2023, isolating nickel assets including Sudbury and Voisey’s Bay. Executives cite surging battery-driven demand for nickel and copper, alongside major planned capex to expand output, supporting a bullish near-term sentiment for both metals. | US copper scrap discounts for No.1 and No.2 scrap were unchanged in recent assessments, with traders citing quiet physical demand and stable spreads into year-end. Fastmarkets’ Comex March copper settled higher, supporting a floor for brass-related prices. Overall, copper signals are mixed-to-stable and unlikely to drive a major move.

The report says Pakistan’s Reko Diq copper project in Chagai is expected to resume and complete feasibility by next May, targeting about 150,000 tonnes of copper per year with potential expansion. It cites $1.5bn initial investment, major stakeholder backing, and security/infrastructure needs, supporting copper project sentiment. | Copper prices fell from near six-month highs as the U.S. dollar firmed on stronger-than-expected U.S. producer inflation, raising concerns about higher-for-longer Fed rates. Markets also turned cautious ahead of central bank meetings. LME aluminium and SHFE nickel also dropped alongside copper. | SMM says Chinese copper inventories across major markets totaled 107,900 mt, up 3,100 mt over the weekend. Shanghai rose on higher domestic and imported arrivals; Guangdong fell due to smelter maintenance but should improve next week. Domestic shipments are expected to grow ahead of SHFE 2212, keeping supply higher and inventories likely to rise slightly. | Beroe reports copper demand is expected to grow about 3–5% CAGR into 2024, supported by electronics, transportation, and construction, plus supply-chain localization efforts in the US and China and continued innovation in copper products. The market is described as stable/possibly favorable despite earlier recession concerns. | Kazakhstan’s President Tokayev ordered the government to draft legislation requiring large producers to supply more aluminum and copper raw materials to the domestic market. He cited shortages for processors and noted 87% of aluminum and 98.8% of copper are exported abroad. More domestic supply could pressure prices. | Kazakhstan’s President Tokayev instructed the government to draft legislation requiring large producers to supply more raw materials to the domestic market. He cited that 98.8% of copper and 87% of aluminium are exported abroad due to lack of available domestic inputs, prompting concern from processing enterprises. | Teck’s new CEO is looking to generate and redeploy cash from coal to fund copper expansion. The announcement is relevant to copper supply expectations through changes in investment priorities, but it is unlikely to materially affect near-term (days to weeks) physical supply or inventories. | Copper fell on the LME and Comex as concerns grew that China’s economy will weaken due to spreading COVID cases and ongoing factory curbs. Uncertainty over whether the Fed and other central banks will slow rate hikes added pressure. The same macro concerns weighed on aluminium and nickel. | Unrest in Peru has threatened planned strikes and disruptions at copper mines operated by Glencore and China-controlled MMG. After President Dina Boluarte promised elections two years early in 2024, it was unclear whether protesters would back down. Easing unrest would support short-term copper supply expectations.

London copper edged up 0.2% to $8,393/tonne, supported by China property shares and data on weekly new home sales. However, surging COVID-19 infections raise concerns about manufacturing disruption. SHFE January copper fell. Peru protests near Las Bambas add supply uncertainty as markets watch central banks. | SMM says copper prices should maintain upward momentum as dovish Fed expectations weaken the dollar and China loosens COVID and supports real estate. Fundamentals remain supportive: copper concentrate TCs/RCs rose, domestic and LME inventories fell to tight levels with higher cancelled warrants. Off-season demand risks limit upside but do not negate bullish tone. | Capella Minerals provided an exploration update for its Løkken copper-cobalt project in Norway, highlighting ground surveys, soil geochemistry, and a drill-ready Åmot target with permits issued. Work focuses on advancing drill targeting around the former Løkken mine; no production or supply changes are reported. | ERG and Gécamines are restarting the Boss Mining operation in the DRC, producing copper cathodes and cobalt hydroxide. The phased restart adds copper supply (around 1,800 tonnes/month) as demand—especially in related end-use sectors—is described as weak, reinforcing near-term price pressure. | Angkor Resources announced assay results from core samples at its Andong Bor copper/gold license in western Cambodia. Two 2016 drill intervals reportedly grade 0.53% copper equivalent over 108 metres and 0.51% over 46 metres, indicating a robust porphyry copper system and potential for larger scale follow-up exploration.

Lion Copper and Gold reported positive assay results from trenching at its Mason Pass copper oxide prospect in Nevada, including multiple intervals averaging up to 0.28% TCu and bedrock zones with copper oxide veins. The company says mineralization remains open and will consider further exploration to expand targets at depth and laterally. | Copper edged higher on the LME after the U.S. dollar weakened following softer-than-expected U.S. inflation. China’s reopening raises short-term COVID infection risks that could hurt industrial recovery, with a falling spot premium suggesting weaker demand. Still, the article reports copper up versus prior levels. | Metso Outotec said it received orders worth about EUR 18 million for four copper anode casting shop projects from major copper producers in the USA, China and Africa. The equipment supports the casting of copper anodes used in smelting, and is booked in Q4 orders received. | Orion Minerals secured an $87m funding package (including a precious-metals stream) to advance development of its Prieska copper-zinc mine in South Africa. Funds will support feasibility work and commissioning of dewatering and water-treatment systems, subject to approvals and additional financing conditions. | A report says a Peruvian copper mine is running out of storage space, creating a risk to continued operations. If stockpiles cannot be handled and production is curtailed, near-term copper supply expectations may weaken, likely weighing on copper prices over the coming days. | KoBold Metals will invest $150 million to develop the Mingomba copper project in Zambia, a deposit described as high-grade and among top-tier potential mines. The deal with EMR Capital and ZCCM-IH supports Zambia’s push to raise copper output, which can bolster copper sentiment near term. | Pan Global reported assay results from 14 drill holes at its Escacena Project in Spain, expanding La Romana near-surface copper mineralization and identifying a new copper horizon (e.g., 3.9m at 1.04% Cu). The update is exploration-focused, with no stated near-term production or supply impact. | Comex copper for December delivery settled 0.92% higher at $3.8750, up 3.55 cents/lb. Prices rose for two consecutive sessions and gained 2.01% over the past two sessions, the largest two-day increase since Dec. 2, 2022, while year-to-date remains down.

Copper Mountain Mining said Australia’s FIRB approved the sale of its Eva Copper Project and exploration tenements to Harmony, completing the final condition for the transaction. The company expects closing imminently after receiving cash. The news is copper-asset related but does not detail immediate production changes. | KoBold Metals plans a $150m investment in Zambia’s Mingomba copper-cobalt project, including a $115m payment tied to stakes and $35m for exploration. It aims to develop the high-grade deposit using AI-enabled drilling analysis. Zambia targets much higher copper output by 2032, but near-term production impact is unclear. | Panama rejected First Quantum Minerals’ last-minute proposal to amend its US$375 million/year royalty agreement for the Cobre Panama copper mine. With no deal in place, the mine’s continued operation is now uncertain, raising near-term supply disruption risk and likely weighing on copper prices. | The note says copper has already fallen ~20% from its 2022 high, but demand from the energy transition will eventually lift prices again. It cites higher copper intensity in EVs and wind, and warns supply growth is constrained by long mine timelines and permits, implying future tightening. | Copper Mountain Mining says it has closed the sale of its Eva Copper Project and Australian exploration tenements for up to US$230 million, including contingent payments tied to copper prices (e.g., 10% of incremental revenue above US$3.80/lb). Proceeds fund bond buybacks and working capital, with no immediate production-supply change described. | Panama’s government missed a midnight deadline to agree with Minera Panamá on the contract terms for operating the Cobre Panamá copper mine, after the company rejected the initial proposal and later submitted changes to economic terms such as royalties. The government will brief the public on negotiations and efforts to sign a deal.

Panama is reported to have shut down First Quantum’s copper mine after talks failed. The move signals an abrupt supply disruption for mined copper, which can quickly change near-term market tightness expectations and push copper prices lower in the following days. | Panama ordered First Quantum Minerals to stop work at its large Donoso copper mine after the deadline for a new concession contract expired. Only maintenance will continue while the contract payment and terms are renegotiated. The mine supplies most of Panama’s exports and producing 300,000 tons of concentrate annually. | Panama ordered First Quantum Minerals to pause operations at its Cobre Panamá copper mine after missing a deadline to sign a $375 million/year payment agreement. Talks over tax/royalty terms continued, but legal protections for termination and stability were not agreed. The mine could produce ~340,000–350,000 tonnes of copper in 2022. | China Nonferrous Metal Group and Jiangxi Copper agreed benchmark refining charges (RCs) for imported blister copper for 2023 at $125/mt CIF, down from $155/mt in 2022. The sharp decrease signals lower refining economics and may weigh on expectations for copper-linked pricing in the near term. | Eldorado Gold approved a full restart of construction at the Skouries gold-copper project in Greece after permit delays, contingent on the first drawdown of an €680m project finance facility expected in Q1 next year. The feasibility study targets 67 million pounds of copper annually, supporting supply expectations. | The piece focuses on First Quantum maintaining hope for a late-stage copper-related deal in Panama. While details are limited in the excerpt, the implication is improved project or supply certainty. That kind of development risk reduction typically supports near-term copper sentiment and may pressure prices upward over days. | Norilsk Nickel’s board unanimously approved its 2023 budget, with CAPEX projected to rise 10% to about $4.7bn. The investment programme supports mining, infrastructure maintenance in the Norilsk Industrial District, equipment replacement and capital repairs, alongside initiatives to address operational challenges from partner refusals. | Panama’s government ordered First Quantum Minerals to halt operations at the Cobre Panama copper mine after contract negotiations failed. The temporary shutdown represents an immediate reduction in copper supply from a major producer, likely weighing on near-term market sentiment and expectations for copper prices. | Eldorado Gold said its board approved an investment decision to fully restart construction activities at the Skouries gold-copper project in northern Greece. The company’s proposed mine has reserves of 3.7 million oz., and the restart implies progress toward bringing copper supply online. | Harmony Gold completed the purchase of Copper Mountain’s Eva copper project in north-west Queensland for up to $230m. The deal adds 1.72 billion pounds of copper reserves and expands Harmony’s copper exposure. Harmony will review feasibility over up to 12 months before deciding execution and financing. | A Canadian company says Panama is responsible for the closure of a copper mine. The report implies a near-term reduction in copper production and potential supply tightening, which would likely drive expectations of higher copper prices in the short run, barring details on size or duration. | Rocky Mountain Power reported vandalism at three substations in Sandy, Utah, including missing copper ground wire and stolen copper wire from two sites. Crews rerouted power after quick repairs, briefly cutting electricity to about 3,500 homes. Officials may add security measures after multiple similar incidents nationwide.

SMM said copper inventories across major Chinese markets fell 12,400 mt to 81,900 mt over the weekend, a new year-on-year low. The drop was driven by fewer imported arrivals and downstream restocking after prices fell, while Guangdong rose slightly on weaker consumption. Inventories are expected to fall further if arrivals don’t increase. | SMM says spot treatment charges for imported copper concentrates are under pressure as tight spot supply persists for three straight weeks. Peru road blockages near Cusco threaten Las Bambas warehousing limits and raise odds of output cuts. Port inventories slipped and parties miss 2023 TC benchmarks, weighing on copper sentiment. | Copper explorer Cobre returned from equity capital markets fundraising, securing $5 million in fresh funds, including investment from Sprott Asset Management and its largest shareholders. The news is tied to copper exploration funding, but no immediate operational changes or supply disruptions are mentioned. | The piece argues copper is poised to rise as softer U.S. CPI data eases Fed tightening expectations and the U.S. dollar weakens. It also highlights Peru’s protests disrupting mine logistics and shipments from major copper operations, tightening near-term supply and supporting bullish price momentum.

Nickel Creek Platinum says nickel prices should rise amid a perceived nickel supply shortage for EV growth. It highlights the Nickel Shäw Project in Yukon, recent drilling intersecting near-surface massive/semi-massive nickel-copper sulphides, and completed studies toward saleable nickel/copper concentrate. It anticipates a prefeasibility study in 2023. | Panama has ordered First Quantum to halt operations at a copper mine. A sudden suspension of mining output can quickly reduce expected near-term copper supply, tightening availability in the days ahead. Markets typically react to such disruptions through higher prompt copper pricing expectations. | SMM reports three straight weeks of declines in the Imported Copper Concentrate Index to $87.67/mt, citing tight spot supply. Production and transport risks at Peru’s Las Bambas (road blocks; near-full inventory) are worsening availability, while Cobre Panama faces operational uncertainty after failed talks with the Panamanian government. | SMM reports Guangdong 1# bare bright copper at 60,500–60,700 yuan/mt with unchanged quotes, while the cathode-to-scrap spread widened to 1,462 yuan/mt. Secondary copper rod prices in Jiangxi were mostly 65,400–65,600 yuan/mt, up 100 yuan/mt, but trading stayed muted. | Emerita Resources reported additional 2022 delineation drilling assays at the La Romanera deposit in Spain, including multiple copper-bearing massive sulphide and stockwork intercepts (notably ~0.3% Cu to ~1.0% Cu over varying widths). The company plans an NI 43-101 resource estimate around Q1/Q2 2023. | 1844 Resources announced completion of its 2022 reconnaissance program at the Native Copper project in Quebec, including prospecting, trenching, sampling, and road rehabilitation. The compilation and sampling highlight copper mineralization in multiple sectors with assay values up to ~10% Cu, but no near-term production impacts are stated. | Aurubis, a major copper smelter, reported higher FY operating earnings and said it will invest about 530 million euros. Its CEO attributed gains to strong copper demand from renewables and electric vehicles, offsetting weaker construction demand. The company did trim its 2022/23 earnings forecast corridor.

Taseko shares jumped after it announced a partnership with Mitsui to develop the Florence copper project in Arizona. Mitsui committed an initial US$50 million investment to support the project’s development. The deal is a constructive supply-development signal for copper markets and can lift near-term sentiment. | Xanadu Mines executed further investment agreements with Zijin for its Mongolian projects, including funding earmarked for the Kharmagtai pre-feasibility study and potential construction. Kharmagtai has an estimated resource and expected copper output of about 37,000 tonnes per year, which may support bullish sentiment for copper. | Chakana Copper Corp. modified payment terms for its Aija Option Agreement and is advancing environmental permitting to drill south targets at its Soledad project in Peru. The project’s inferred resources include copper alongside gold and silver. The update is primarily corporate/permitting and unlikely to impact copper supply or demand immediately. | Copper thieves cut and stole about three metres of copper piping at Chris Hani Baragwanath Hospital, compromising sterilisation steam and cooling systems in two operating theatres. The incident follows Monday’s theft of 10 metres of copper cable affecting ICU oxygen supply. Authorities say the hospital is targeted; alternative arrangements were made. | ICSG reported the world refined copper market had a 46,000-tonne surplus in October, reversing the prior month’s 85,000-tonne deficit. October output was 2.2 million tonnes versus 2.16 million tonnes consumption. For the first 10 months, the market remained in deficit. | Foran Mining said it will use a US$150 million loan from Sprott Resource Lending, alongside about US$200 million proposed by Ontario, to help fund construction of its McIlvenna Bay copper project in Saskatchewan. The update supports future supply but does not indicate immediate output changes.

More than 6,000 residents fled after troops entered villages near China’s Wanbao-run Letpadaung copper mine in Myanmar. Mine operations were cut by over 80% after workers walked off post-coup, and locals report renewed attempts to restart. The heightened security risk and unrest threaten copper output continuity. | China imported 358,300 mt of copper cathode in Nov 2022, a monthly high, as the import window stayed open and import profits exceeded 1,500 yuan/mt. Tight supply and low inventories kept spot premiums elevated despite expected Dec imports near 320,000 mt. | Xanadu Mines signed a non-binding gas offtake MoU with Jade Gas Holdings to supply cleaner, low-cost natural gas to power its Kharmagtai copper-gold project in Mongolia. The near-field gas supply option supports progress toward binding commercial terms and potential construction decisions, improving outlook for copper project development. | SMM says Yangshan copper premiums for LME-warrant and bills of lading cargoes were $30-50/mt and $25-45/mt, respectively, with little change. Import losses versus SHFE were about 700 yuan/mt as the SHFE/LME ratio stayed steady, causing sluggish demand, subdued trading, and delayed spot settlements. | SMM reports Shanghai spot copper premiums over SHFE 2301 rose to about 570 yuan/mt, supported by low copper inventory and cargo holders insisting on high quotes. SHFE 2301 was volatile but ended higher, while small imported arrivals and suppressed downstream demand failed to ease supply tightness. Spot prices likely stay firm next week. | Black & Veatch will design and deliver a seawater desalination plant beside PT Freeport Indonesia’s Manyar copper smelter in East Java. The plant will supply desalinated water to support processing of concentrates from Grasberg and improve operational efficiency and reliability for the planned 1.7 MTPA smelter. | Premium Nickel Resources announced additional assay results from its Selebi Ni-Cu-Co sulphide mine in Botswana, including multiple drillhole intervals with reported nickel and copper grades and NiEq calculations based on LME prices. The update is exploratory/resource-expansion oriented and does not change near-term production or inventories, limiting short-term price impact.

Premium Nickel Resources reports drill results at its Selebi nickel-copper-cobalt project in Botswana support the idea that past-producing deposits connect at depth. The company is working to reopen the Selebi mine, which could improve perceived future nickel supply and modestly affect copper expectations due to co-product potential. | Reuters reports copper trading in a narrow range as recession fears and uncertain demand from China pressure prices, despite support from Chinese liquidity measures and easing COVID restrictions. China’s infection spike disrupts logistics and lowers operating rates. Strong U.S. data lifts the dollar. Aluminium and nickel also slip modestly. | SMM says copper inventories in China’s domestic bonded zones were flat from Dec 16 to Dec 23, including Shanghai and Guangdong. Slower customs clearance in Shanghai from rising COVID-19 cases supported unchanged stock levels. With only three LME copper trading days and cautious downstream buying, bonded-zone inventories may rise, but near-term net impact looks limited. | SMM reports that Shanghai copper cathode spot premiums have surged about 400 yuan/mt versus SHFE 2301, staying firm despite a small dip. COVID-19 outbreaks disrupt processing and downstream buying, while a closed import window, declining social inventories, and hindered north-to-Shanghai transfers tighten spot supply, lifting premiums. | Class 1 Nickel reported progress on its Somanike drilling program in Quebec, with two active drills and assays pending. Drilling intersected sulphide mineralisation in 18 holes and identified geophysical anomalies for follow-up near the historic Marbridge nickel mine. Positive results may support near-term nickel and copper sentiment. | Copper prices edged higher as low inventories and hopes for economic recovery next year offset weaker near-term demand in China from a surge in COVID-19 cases. LME copper rose 0.5% on the day. ShFE copper stocks fell, limiting downside if demand improves. Nickel rose; aluminium fell. | Comex copper ended the week up 1.10% to $3.8030 per pound, with the front-month contract also rising sharply on the day. The piece is primarily market data recap (daily/weekly gains and declines vs prior highs and lows) and does not mention new fundamental drivers. | Titan Minerals’ maiden drilling reportedly delivered shallow copper-gold results at its project. Such exploration success can raise market optimism about future copper resources and development timelines, offering mild near-term support to copper sentiment, though it does not confirm production or immediate supply.

London and Shanghai copper prices rose, with LME three-month copper hitting a two-week high, as China signaled it will end inbound quarantine from Jan. 8 and resume overseas visas. Optimism over demand and falling SHFE copper inventories supported prices, though analysts warned near-term demand weakness from cases and holidays. | Yangshan copper premiums under warrants stayed around $30-50/mt and were flat, while bill-of-lading premiums were $25-40/mt. Import losses rose to about 720 yuan/mt amid a weaker SHFE/LME ratio. Domestic spot premiums fell further (avg -135 yuan/mt), leaving buyers offering very low prices and trading near-stalled. | Copper prices rose to a two-week high as China began easing zero-COVID rules, boosting market mood and prompting buying. Reuters notes that recession risks and expectations of weaker demand limited upside, while supply concerns offered some support. The focus is primarily on near-term copper demand expectations.

China’s copper concentrate imports rose sharply in Nov 2022, but December saw frequent supply-side and port disruptions. A fire at Chile’s Ventanas port, Peru unrest impacting Matarani port and roads, Las Bambas warehousing constraints, and a US truck strike likely hinder Americas shipments, weighing near-term supply and supporting copper prices. | CSPT set spot copper concentrate treatment charges for Q1 2023 at $93/mt ($0.093/lb), according to SMM. The TC level is unchanged from Q4 2022. Since treatment charges impact smelter economics but this update is flat, any near-term copper price effect is likely limited. | The piece says copper could fall nearly 20% over the next 12 months. It attributes the expected decline to demand cooling amid a likely economic slowdown, while supplies are described as staying stable. The message points to weaker copper demand and downside price pressure. | Copper prices fell as markets focused on poor near-term demand prospects. Surging COVID infections in top consumer China and recession fears raised expectations of slower industrial activity. Traders looked to upcoming Chinese manufacturing PMIs for clues. LME copper ended down 0.2% and technical levels were highlighted. | Altiplano Metals reported November 2022 operating results from its Farellon copper-gold mine in Chile. Copper grade improved to 2.10% from 1.87% in October, with higher production, processing, and copper sales of 131,153 pounds. Revenue rose to about US$308,000 after costs, alongside stockpiling and ongoing ramp-up. | SMM reports that China’s 2023 import and export tariff settings for copper-related products will remain unchanged from 2022, effective Jan 1. The update indicates no new tariff shock for cross-border copper product flows, likely stabilizing expectations rather than adding bullish or bearish near-term price pressure. | An SMM survey says copper cathode-rod operating rates improved in H2 2022 due to restocking when copper prices fell and tight copper scrap supply that lifted scrap prices above cathode. Still, September-October season weakness, high premiums, COVID disruptions, and price spread swings hurt consumption and orders, weakening Q4 output. | Copper Mountain Mining said its Copper Mountain Mine and corporate IT systems were hit by ransomware on Dec. 27. Operations were isolated, manual processes used, and the mill preventatively shut down to assess control-system impact. The company expects temporary disruption and additional safeguards, with no safety or environmental incidents reported. | Nevada Copper said a second US$20 million tranche of restart financing was released from escrow, enabling progress on ramp-up at its Pumpkin Hollow underground copper mine. The company also outlined continued capital and development work. The funding and restart momentum may modestly improve near-term expectations for copper supply. | Osisko Bermuda agreed to a revised deal involving a silver stream and a backstop copper stream to help fund Metals Acquisition Corp’s $1.1B purchase of the CSA copper-silver mine in New South Wales. The financing structure and copper-focused stream support near-term copper supply expectations and sentiment.

SMM says copper inventory across major Chinese markets rose to 97,900 mt, up 18,200 mt from Monday, the highest in three weeks. The build reflects suppressed downstream buying from higher prices and COVID-19-driven consumption disruptions. SMM expects limited imported arrivals and only a gradual further inventory increase next week. | The Northern Miner reports that surging demand for battery metals is offsetting higher construction costs for PolyMet Mining’s copper-nickel NorthMet project in northeastern Minnesota. Updated estimates suggest the first phase of the open-pit operation remains economically supported despite cost inflation, which can improve near-term sentiment for both copper and nickel. | Estimates for PolyMet Mining’s NorthMet open-pit copper-nickel project in Minnesota suggest surging battery-metal demand is offsetting higher development costs. The first phase of the operation near Lake ... indicates investors see demand support for copper and nickel even as project expenses rise. | Mercuria reports acquiring an additional 63,411,540 common shares of Nevada Copper through the second tranche of a restart financing subscription, totaling up to US$20 million. The tranche closed on Dec. 29, 2022 at a 15% discounted subscription price. The filing is investment-related and does not cite copper supply or demand changes.

SMM reports the Imported Copper Concentrate Index fell WoW and cites multiple Chilean and Panamanian disruptions: Ventanas port closure after a fire, force majeure at Los Bronces, a community blockade at Los Pelambres, and suspension of Cobre Panama operations. Port inventory declined and SMM expects copper concentrate TCs to trend lower, likely weighing on copper pricing. | The piece says Freeport’s CEO is bullish because copper prices are rising, attributing demand growth to Biden’s climate bill and EV expansion. It argues electric vehicles need two to four times more copper than combustion cars, implying stronger copper demand, while also highlighting environmental damage from mining. | Iran plans to invest $15bn to expand copper production capacity from 280,000t to over 1 Mt annually and to lift exports from about $1.7bn to more than $10bn. The programme is underway (starting this summer) with projects gradually coming on stream. | Chile’s economy is weakening, with industrial production and consumption contracting for multiple months. The piece also highlights a lower copper price outlook: an average near US$4/lb with a forecast around US$3.55/lb by end-2023, down from higher 2022 levels, implying bearish near-term copper sentiment. | FLSmidth said it will supply a very high-capacity in-pit crushing and conveying gyratory crusher system to a South American copper miner. No direct near-term supply or trade shock is cited. | PT Freeport Indonesia hired Black & Veatch to design and deliver a seawater desalination plant for its Manyar Smelter in East Java. Manyar is a $3 billion, world-scale single-line copper processing facility. | Panama and First Quantum dispute taxation and contract terms for Cobre Panama, the country’s only major operating copper mine. Talks failed, leading Panama to halt operations and First Quantum to begin arbitration. The disruption risk, plus potential investment slowdown and higher costs, points to immediate bearish pressure on copper sentiment. | A review of ore deposits at Parys Mountain, near Amlwch on Anglesey, suggests potential extraction of about 75,000 tonnes of copper over the next 12 years. Copper fell to two-month lows as traders prioritized demand fears from slowing global growth and rising COVID cases in China, outweighing dollar weakness. Aluminium also dropped on sliding energy prices that raise smelting economics. Nickel plunged alongside the broader base-metals sell-off. | Reuters’ analysis highlights hardened “battle lines” between Panama and First Quantum regarding a key copper mine. The dispute raises uncertainty over mine operations and potential production delays or disruption, which can increase near-term supply risk. Such conditions often weigh on copper price expectations over days to weeks.

Magna Mining reported additional drill results from the Crean Hill 101 Footwall Zone, including 31.1m at 4.0% Ni and also notable associated Cu grades. The company says results show continuity of high-grade massive sulphide mineralization and expects further assays. This is exploration-focused, not production-related. | Lion Copper and Gold said Rio Tinto’s Nuton venture agreed Stage 2 funding of $5m and advanced $2.5m for Stage 3 toward development and exploration of its Yerington Nevada copper projects. The release highlights copper mineralization results and Nuton leach technologies aimed at improving recoveries and low-carbon copper production. | Panama ministers were reportedly summoned to clarify doubts about the status of a flagship copper mine. While the full details are behind a paywall, such inquiries typically relate to operational or regulatory uncertainty that can quickly change market expectations for copper supply and near-term price sentiment. | Lion Copper and Gold announced agreement with Rio Tinto’s Nuton venture for Stage 2 funding of $5m and an advance on Stage 3 funding of $2.5m for the Mason Valley project in Nevada. The release highlights ongoing permitting, studies, and a 17,000-ft drilling program targeting copper prospects and prior mineralization.

SMM surveys Chinese copper industry-chain firms as CNY approaches amid a renewed COVID outbreak. Many copper rod, pipe, sheet/strip, and wire/cable makers plan earlier closures due to weaker orders and lower operating rates. Resumption dates are mostly unchanged for some large firms, but overall demand appears soft pre-holiday. | Copper prices rose as expectations grow that China will support its ailing property sector, improving demand outlook and helping inventory restocking. LME copper was up 1.2%. Nickel fell sharply to a one-month low on supply expectations after Tsingshan’s new electronickel project started production. Aluminium also edged higher. | Copper prices rose on hopes China will add property-sector support measures, improving the near-term demand outlook. LME three-month copper and SHFE February copper both increased, while Chile production fell. In contrast, Shanghai nickel fell on supply expectations from Tsingshan’s new project starting production. Aluminium also edged up. | SMM says copper inventories in China’s domestic bonded zones increased by 2,800 mt from Dec 30, 2022, led by a rise in Shanghai (+3,000 mt) while Guangdong fell slightly. With sluggish imported trading ahead of Chinese New Year, SMM expects bonded inventories to grow further, weighing on sentiment. | Copper Mountain Mining reports that after a Dec. 27 ransomware attack, its Copper Mountain Mine resumed crusher and mill operations by early January and is stabilizing at full production. The company says it kept shipping copper concentrate on schedule during downtime from inventory to Vancouver, limiting immediate copper supply disruption. | Taseko reported 2022 Gibraltar copper sales of 101 million pounds and production of 97 million pounds, but noted December output was hit by an unplanned mill downtime triggered by a sitewide power outage and extreme cold. Throughput fell to ~63,000 tons/day in December versus ~88,000 in October-November. | Copper rose to a three-week high on a weaker U.S. dollar and optimism that China will boost property demand. LME three-month copper climbed about 1.2%. The piece also references Chile output declines supporting copper, while nickel weakness reflects Tsingshan project-related supply expectations and aluminium advanced. | Copper Mountain Mining said it reopened its southern British Columbia copper mine after a Dec. 27 ransomware attack. The company restarted its primary crusher and reported deliveries were unaffected. The restart should ease near-term supply disruption concerns, providing a modest supportive tone for copper markets. | AL-Monitor reports that Saudi Arabia intends to increase copper mining output to satisfy rising global demand. The announcement is expected to influence near-term market expectations for copper supply and balances, potentially supporting trading sentiment, depending on how quickly production changes versus global demand growth. | Fluor won an EPCM contract for Eldorado Gold’s Hellas Gold Skouries project in Greece. The mine is expected to produce about 67 million pounds of copper annually, with construction starting in 2023 and first production targeted for 2H 2025. The contract may modestly support copper supply expectations.

SMM says its imported copper concentrate index fell $1.44/mt WoW as Chile’s Ventanas port shut after a fire disrupted concentrate shipments and suppressed spot TC quotes for Feb–Mar cargoes. It also cites weaker Chile output, royalty bill review, mine disruptions, and rising Chinese port concentrate inventories. | SMM says Chinese copper inventories totaled 122,500 mt as of Jan 9, up 5,000 mt from Jan 6 and sharply year-on-year. COVID-19 cases disrupted production and pre-holiday shutdowns increased stockpiling. Smelters sped up shipments to dump in-plant inventories, while processing demand stayed cautious, implying inventories may keep rising. | Northwest Copper says it has drilled 41.20 metres of 3.20% copper equivalent in the 421 zone at its Stardust project. The reported results can strengthen expectations for potential future copper supply, providing a mild supportive cue for copper prices in the near term as investors react to exploration progress. | First Quantum says it wants to appeal a Panama court order that would halt its copper mine. The move could delay or overturn the shutdown, improving expectations for near-term copper supply and tightening the immediate balance. | Transparency Market Research projects continued growth in the global copper market, driven by demand from electronics, telecommunications, construction, automotive/EVs, and medical equipment in emerging economies. Korea’s FTC approved Lotte Chemical’s acquisition of a copper foil producer (Iljin Materials) via a 53.5% stake deal. Copper foil is used as the anode current collector in EV lithium-ion batteries. The regulator said competition should not be significantly harmed, and Lotte plans funding for the purchase. | Faraday Copper provides an exploration update from its Copper Creek project in Arizona. Phase II drilling has intersected massive copper sulphide (about 15m from 234m) and multiple generative targets show surface copper anomalies; assay results are pending. The company plans further reconnaissance and resource-delineation drilling, without any production changes. | Metal Energy reported assay results from five Phase Two drill holes at its Manibridge project in Manitoba, intersecting shallow, stacked zones of high-grade nickel sulphide mineralization. Results include thick intervals and continuity along strike and dip, supporting potential resource expansion. Copper-cobalt is mentioned, but aluminium is not. | Metal Energy reports five drill holes intersecting shallow, high-grade nickel sulphide mineralization at its Manibridge project, including thick stacked zones and composite results up to 65% host rock mineralized. The announcement strengthens near-term market expectations for nickel supply potential and carries a modest positive link to copper via Ni eq reporting. | Copper prices rose to a 6-1/2-month high on expectations that China’s reopening and stimulus-backed lending will boost metals demand. LME futures extended gains despite a holiday-related demand slowdown. Aluminium also climbed and nickel edged up, reflecting broad base-metal optimism tied to China’s improving demand outlook. | Sama Resources reported assay results from four additional drill holes at the Grata nickel-copper prospect in Côte d’Ivoire, with thick intervals grading around 0.30–0.33% nickel and 0.34–0.40% copper in parts of the intersections. Exploration is ongoing and no production or supply disruption is indicated. | Sama Resources announced assay results from four additional drill holes at the Grata nickel-copper prospect in Côte d’Ivoire, reporting substantial nickel and copper mineralised intervals (with palladium also mentioned). The update is exploration-focused with no production or market supply changes, making any short-term impact on nickel and copper prices likely minimal. | First Quantum Minerals said it will appeal a Panama government order to halt its large copper mine while negotiations continue on a new contract with the state. It links Chile’s regulatory and ESG hurdles, declining ore grades, and slower new supply to potential shortages, arguing copper prices may rise with the shortfall. | Copper extended gains to a 6-1/2 month peak as investors priced in a quicker China reopening and further stimulus, supported by unexpectedly higher bank lending. LME three-month copper rose 0.8% to $8,926/t. Aluminium also climbed, while nickel edged up on the same demand optimism. | First Quantum said it is close to agreeing with Panama on a new contract to keep the Cobre Panama copper mine operating after the government halted discussions and ordered care and maintenance. The company is seeking resolution on tax, surface rights, and expropriation protections, aiming to avoid production stoppage.

Ma’aden and Ivanhoe Electric plan a 50/50 joint venture to explore for copper (along with gold and silver) in Saudi Arabia. While this is a supply-side development, the article provides no information on immediate mine output. Near-term copper price impact is expected to be minimal. | The article highlights copper reaching above $9,000, attributing the rally to improving demand outlook as China reopens. By strengthening expectations for industrial consumption in the near term, the news supports higher copper prices and market sentiment toward physical and forward demand. | Luminex reports that Anglo American has started a maiden five-hole, 3,200-metre drill program at the Pegasus project’s Medusa copper-porphyry target. The company cites geochemical and geophysical signs of copper mineralization and notes further drilling is contingent on initial results. No production or resource updates are provided. | Golden Arrow commenced an IP-resistivity survey and plans its first diamond drilling program in Q1 at the San Pietro Cu-Au-Co project in Chile. It is exploration activity with no immediate production impact. | Government data suggest India, historically a copper exporter, is set to remain a net importer of copper for the fifth straight year in 2022-23. The shift aligns with the May 2018 closure of Sterlite Copper’s 400,000 tpa smelter in Tamil Nadu, reducing domestic supply and lifting import needs. | India is likely to remain a net importer of copper for the fifth year in 2022-23 as domestic refined production stayed lower after Sterlite Copper’s Tuticorin smelter shutdown in 2018. Rising consumption from power, electronics, infrastructure and EVs sustains deficits, requiring continued imports and supporting copper tightness. | Copper on the LME surged past $9,000/t and rose for a fifth straight session, up as much as 1.8% on Wednesday. The rally is linked to a weaker US dollar and improving expectations that China’s demand will rebound after the COVID wave. Interest-rate-hike hopes also support copper prices. | Copper prices topped $9,000 per tonne on the LME, up 1.1% and in a fifth straight daily gain, as traders expect China’s border reopening to revive demand. Analysts also note the weaker U.S. dollar could support dollar-priced metals, though some caution copper may not rebound as strongly as other commodities. | Armed robbers in Chile stole 13 shipping containers of copper from a port near San Antonio, taken from Codelco shipments destined for China. Estimated value is about $4.4 million. The heist adds pressure on transport and port security, following prior thefts from northern copper cathode trains.

Comex copper for January delivery settled 2.15% higher at $4.1545 per pound, gaining 8.75 cents. The market extended a five-session rally, reaching its highest settlement since June 15, 2022. It is also up month-to-date and year-to-date, underscoring near-term bullish momentum. | Power Nickel reports assay results extending the PN-22-009 nickel-copper-Co-PGE mineralized interval to 40.3m, with high-grade Ni-Cu values and plans to extend drilling further in Q1 2023. The update supports continuity of the Nisk resource, but provides no near-term production change. | Palladium One reported additional drill intercepts at its Tyko Project in Ontario, including high-grade massive sulphide assays with up to about 10.3% nickel and 2.9% copper over 1.8 meters. The company frames results as reinforcing West Pickle’s potential and plans further exploration to connect mineral zones in 2023. | Palladium One reported additional high-grade drill intercepts at its Tyko nickel-copper project, including zones with up to ~10.3% nickel and ~2.9% copper over drill intervals. The release focuses on exploration success and connecting mineralized zones, without any near-term production or supply-change information. | Glencore said vandals attacked its copper mine in Peru. The incident is expected to disrupt operations and raise short-term supply uncertainty, which typically weighs on copper pricing as markets adjust for potential output losses and timing delays. | Comex copper front-month settled 0.76% higher at $4.1860, gaining 3.15 cents per pound. The move extends a six-session rally, with the settlement at the highest since June 13, 2022. The piece frames copper as trending upward month-to-date and year-to-date.

China’s December copper imports fell 12.7% y/y to 514,049 tonnes as COVID infections and shrinking factory activity cooled demand. Copper prices in London were higher than Shanghai, also weighing imports. | SMM reports treatment charges for spot and long-term copper concentrates for early 2023, with TCs roughly flat versus Q4 2022 but below benchmarks due to fewer traders quoting, delayed settlements, and spot supply disturbances. Disruptions include Chile’s Ventanas fire, Peru port blockages, US truck strike, and mine capacity constraints. | China’s faster-than-expected reopening is described as a “game-changer” for commodity markets, driving bullish repositioning and lifting raw-material prices. Softer US inflation reduces pressure for aggressive Fed tightening, weakening the dollar and supporting expectations for global growth, which underpins rallies including copper and likely industrial metals demand. | SMM says copper inventory across major Chinese markets rose 13,000 mt on the week to 135,500 mt, extending a three-week rise since late December. Drivers include weak downstream buying (CNY holiday) and active eastward smelter shipments. Next week’s imported inflows are low, but overall inventory is expected to grow further. | SMM says China’s domestic bonded copper inventories rose 13,300 mt from Jan 6 to 70,800 mt by Jan 13, with larger gains in Shanghai. The SHFE/LME ratio fall pushed spot imports above 1,400 yuan/mt over the front-month contract, suppressing customs declarations and limiting warrant shipments, while smelters shifted goods into bonded warehouses for delivery after February. | Taseko secured an underwritten US$25 million financing commitment to fund SX/EW plant costs for its Florence Copper project. Drawdowns are tied to receiving the final EPA Underground Injection Control permit. The funding improves balance-sheet flexibility ahead of 2023 construction, which can modestly support expectations for future copper supply. | Taseko said Florence Copper construction is complete and commissioning of its SX/EW plant is progressing, with wellfield acidification and recovered solution grades meeting levels required for operations. It also reported Gibraltar’s 2025 copper production of 98 million pounds and stronger Q4 output amid prior downtime, with cathode production continuing. | Taseko secured US$25 million in additional financing to fund the solvent extraction and electrowinning (SX/EW) plant for its Florence Copper processing facility in Arizona. The raise supports construction for commercial copper production, potentially improving short-term expectations for future copper supply.

SMM data show lithium-ion battery copper foil output fell slightly in December and is expected to drop further in January as new-energy-vehicle subsidy effects fade and order indices weaken. Some producers switch to electronic-circuit foil during certification delays, reducing lithium-foil demand. Maintenance around CNY also cuts output. | SMM reports spot copper concentrate trading was sluggish, with weekly imported concentrate TCs edging lower to $87-89/mt and clean concentrate TCs offered at $81-85/mt. Supply-side disturbances include Ventanas port fire impacts and Las Bambas shipping delays, while concentrate inventories rose but near-term supply improvement remains limited. | Copper prices slipped from a seven-month high as subdued physical demand and concerns over global economic conditions weighed on the market. China’s weaker factory activity and falling copper premiums signaled weak spot demand, prompting a short-term correction. Peru supply disruption and low inventory levels offered limited support. | Wolverhampton councillors approved felling a tree used by thieves to scale a wall and steal copper from Mueller Europe Ltd. The company, which makes copper tubing, reports five copper theft break-ins in two weeks, attributing thefts to the high copper price. Replacement trees will be planted. | Copper prices are rebounding after breaking prior highs, with the article citing bullish signals from China’s easing zero-COVID disruption and expectations of a rapid economic rebound. Support also comes from a weaker U.S. dollar as inflation cools. However, construction/property weakness and rural COVID risk could add volatility and pullbacks. | The piece reports that China’s reopening is boosting the copper outlook. With China representing a large share of global copper demand, expectations for higher industrial activity and demand growth can provide upward support to copper prices over the near term. | Sources expect Europe’s copper market to face weak demand risk from a likely recession, but supply is tight: Russian copper imports are declining, LME warehouse stocks are at low levels, and off-warrant inventories have fallen. Record-high annual contract premiums and rising spot premiums suggest bullish near-term pressure on copper in Europe. | Rio Tinto indicated it may resume growth in export volumes after a softer year, citing high stockpiles at its Western Australia network and a major mine ramping. It also said it plans higher volumes across its alumina, aluminium and copper businesses, adding supply outlook pressure on prices. | Rio Tinto signaled it may restart growth in export volumes, citing high stockpiles at its Western Australian network and ramping a new mine. It also suggested higher 2023 volumes for its alumina/aluminium and copper businesses, implying increased near-term supply that could weigh on copper and aluminium prices.

QC Copper and Gold reports the resumption of drilling at its Opemiska copper project, targeting a final 10,000 meters to support an updated mineral resource estimate expected around June 2023. Management says incorporation of new drill data should increase contained metal content and improve mine economics, boosting copper development sentiment. | NorthWest Copper reported additional 2022 Kwanika drill results, including hole K-22-255 intersecting 399.8m at 1.01% CuEq and multiple higher-grade copper-gold zones. The company notes the mineralized system may continue and that results were not in the PEA, with remaining assays pending. | QC Copper resumes drilling on the Opemiska Property, completing a planned 10,000 metres to update its Mineral Resource Estimate targeted for June 2023. The updated geologic model is expected to increase contained resources and improve mine economics, supporting potential development of the copper project in Quebec. | BHP selected seven companies for its BHP Xplor accelerator to fast-track early-stage exploration of copper and nickel. The firm plans further regional surveys to define drilling targets and potential partnerships. | Trafigura plans to withdraw large amounts of copper from LME-registered warehouses, likely keeping the metal in Europe. LME registered stocks are low and cancelled warrants have surged, raising supply concerns. The cash discount to the three-month contract has narrowed, alongside a sharp LME copper price rally. | Copper prices surged above $9,000/t as China abandoned zero-Covid and markets anticipated a demand rebound after Lunar New Year. The piece cites supportive China property/economic stimulus, low LME/SHFE/COMEX inventories, and a weaker US dollar easing monetary-tightening concerns, offering bullish near-term upside despite COVID-case risks.

LME copper rebounded to a 7-month high while SHFE lagged. The company said the FY22 decline matched expectations and forecast 2023 copper output of 670,000–710,000 tons. | Fabled Copper Corp. reported 2022 surface sampling results from the Muskwa Project’s Ringarooma copper occurrence in northern British Columbia. The update is exploration-focused with no production or supply commitments. | African Energy Metals will acquire GoviEx’s Malian subsidiary holding the Falea uranium project, with disclosed copper resources (including indicated and inferred copper grades) and additional exploration upside. Copper prices have rebounded sharply early in the year, regaining much of the prior-year losses. The article attributes the strength to prospects for higher demand from China as Beijing eases Covid-19 restrictions, suggesting improved consumption that supports copper prices in the coming weeks. | PRNewswire/MarketsandMarkets projects rapid growth for the copper wire and cable market through 2032, citing electrification, grid modernization, renewable integration, EV charging, and underground power projects. While the piece is a long-run market forecast, it signals improving copper demand expectations that could support copper prices over coming weeks. | The piece discusses Zambia’s goal to triple copper output to nearly 3m tonnes per year by 2031, attributing the outlook to improved investment conditions under President Hakainde Hichilema and supportive policy direction. Copperbelt Energy Corporation says opportunities are perking up, but the timeline is largely long term. | Talon Metals announced new drilling results at its Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project, identifying a “Raptor Zone” with high-grade nickel-copper massive sulphide intersections in multiple holes outside the current resource area. The company frames this as evidence of district-scale potential, which could affect near-term nickel sentiment and, secondarily, copper. | Amarc Resources said core drilling has resumed at its Duke porphyry Cu-Au district in central British Columbia after a holiday break, with one rig now drilling and a second joining soon. The 2023 program tests the Duke deposit and nearby IP anomaly under a Boliden earn-in funding plan. | Granite Creek Copper reported positive Preliminary Economic Assessment results for its Carmacks copper-gold-silver project in Yukon, including NPV/IRR sensitivity to copper prices and planned open-pit processing. The news highlights project viability and expansion potential but provides no near-term production or supply impact for the copper market. | Copper has rebounded sharply at the start of the year, recouping most losses from 2022. Analysts link the move to expectations of higher Chinese demand as Covid-19 restrictions end and the economy recovers, alongside a shift toward macro tailwinds from a less hawkish Fed. | The article compiles interviews covering multiple companies. The copper-relevant item is Fabled Copper’s report of 2022 surface work results at its Muskwa copper project in British Columbia. The other announcements concern equity listings, zinc-air funding, and AI or power-plant plans, with no direct impact on copper prices. | The piece highlights that unrest in Peru may threaten to disrupt copper output, potentially affecting close to 2% of global supply. Talon Metals says it has identified a new nickel and copper mineralization zone about 3.2 km from its Tamarack project in central Minnesota. The discovery is expected to boost sentiment around potential future output. Near-term effects are likely limited pending further drilling, assays, and resource definition. | Talon Metals says it has discovered a new system of nickel and copper mineralization about 3.2 km from its Tamarack project in central Minnesota. No outages occurred, suggesting mainly localized disruption rather than broader market supply changes. | Copper prices edged lower as China’s Lunar New Year reopened trade demand expectations but near-term consumption signals stayed weak. This has lifted Guangdong and Shanghai local premiums to multi-year highs, potentially pressuring users’ margins while expectations for recovering Chinese demand support global copper. | Reuters reports China’s copper buyers face higher costs as Maike’s financial woes cause import supply disruptions and domestic smelters undergo maintenance. Copper premiums over Shanghai futures have surged to multi-year highs, suggesting tighter refined supply. Analysts also flag uncertainty around import volumes despite improved demand expectations. | Reuters reports China’s copper buyers are facing sharply higher costs as Maike Group’s financial problems disrupt refined copper imports and domestic smelter maintenance reduces output. This has pushed China’s domestic copper premiums over Shanghai futures to multi-year highs, potentially straining demand from construction and renewables. | The article headline reports that China’s copper costs are being driven higher by problems at Maike and associated output glitches. This suggests reduced supply or production disruptions in the short term, which typically tightens availability and increases cost pressures for copper. | Protests reportedly sparked a fire at Glencore’s Antapaccay copper mine in Peru, seen in the workers’ housing area, with video footage showing flammable material near the site. The incident raises the risk of short-term operational disruption, supporting expectations for tighter copper supply and potentially higher prices in the coming days. | Protesters allegedly set flammable material near fences at Glencore’s Antapaccay copper mine in Peru, causing a fire to spread through nearby worker housing. The incident follows prior disruptions when the mine was operating at restricted capacity due to anti-government protests. | Glencore’s Antapaccay copper mine in Peru faced another disruption after a fire spread through nearby worker housing, believed to have been started by protesters. Such interruptions heighten near-term copper supply risk. | Reuters reports protesters attacked Glencore’s Antapaccay copper mine in Peru, with a fire breaking out near worker housing and possibly started by demonstrators. The mine had been operating at restricted capacity earlier in the week due to unrest, indicating continued supply disruption risk for near-term copper output.

London copper rose 0.58% as hopes grew for China’s post-COVID reopening demand recovery and the dollar weakened. Trading stayed subdued due to Lunar New Year closure. The piece also flags potential copper supply risk from social unrest in Peru. LME aluminium fell 0.56% at the same time. | Metal Energy announced assay results from three drill holes at its Manibridge nickel project in Manitoba, reporting broad high-grade nickel intersections including 85.3m of 0.67% NiEq and additional intervals with similar NiEq grades. Glencore’s Antapaccay copper mine in Peru resumed normal operations after an 11-day shutdown caused by local protests. The mine was closed Jan. 20 and restarted Jan. 31 with “increased safety” once protests abated, indicating improved near-term copper supply conditions. | Chile’s central bank data show copper export revenue in January 2023 fell to $2.98 billion, down 21.6% year-on-year. Given Chile’s position as the world’s largest copper producer, the decline signals weaker realized export performance, which may weigh on near-term copper price sentiment. | Titan Minerals said grades at its Copper Ridge project compare favourably, implying improved ore quality. While such results can strengthen expectations for future copper production, the news is more likely to affect the company/project narrative than to materially move physical copper balances immediately. | Copper rose on Wednesday as a weaker U.S. dollar followed perceived dovish comments from Fed Chair Powell and as supply disruptions supported prices. LME three-month copper gained 0.7% and Shanghai March rose 0.7%. Chile export declines and the resumption of Peru’s Antapaccay operations were cited. | Spot copper concentrate TCs have been falling as disruptions to concentrate supply and seaborne logistics intensify. Peru protests, Asian weather, African power rationing, and Chile/Peru port constraints delayed shipments and reduced output, including mine suspensions. Sellers lowered TC offers, reflecting tighter concentrate availability. | Eurasian Resources Group plans to invest $1.8 billion to double its African copper and cobalt output, including Congo projects needing more reliable electricity. The company is considering small hydro and solar with battery storage due to erratic grid supply, while earlier coal power plans may face transmission constraints. | Camino Minerals reported identifying a new satellite drilling target, the Diana Zone, at its Los Chapitos copper project in Peru. Surface channel sampling returned 1.05% copper over 56m and management expects an EIA permit expansion in Q2 2023 to allow broader drilling. It is exploration-stage information with limited immediate market impact. | Oil and copper prices have stayed weak even after China’s reopening from COVID restrictions. Economists cited slowing growth expectations and tighter financial liquidity from rising interest rates, which can widen bid-offer spreads and reduce market depth. Copper’s continued decline since mid-January suggests bearish near-term sentiment for demand and pricing. | Glencore reopened its Antapaccay copper mine in Peru after an 11-day closure caused by violent local attacks and security concerns. Resuming output lowers immediate disruption risk to copper supply, which can support copper prices in the near term as markets adjust supply expectations. | Aguia Resources reported a “sizeable” measured and indicated copper resource for its Brazil metallic green copper project: 22.6Mt grading 0.43% Cu (plus an inferred 3Mt at similar grade). The update supports progressing to pre-feasibility and an environmental impact assessment, alongside a Brazilian green-processing grant.

SMM said #1 copper cathode spot prices increased on Feb. 9 versus the prior day across key Chinese regions, including Guangdong, Shandong, East China and North China. Discounts narrowed and premiums widened, while the average quote rose by 15 yuan/mt, pointing to firmer near-term copper demand or supply conditions. | Copper concentrate TCs continue falling, with SMM’s imported copper concentrate index at $80.73/mt. Peru protests have disrupted mining and transportation in south Peru, cutting access to Antapaccay output and worsening shipment performance. Glencore’s Antapaccay restart hasn’t offset unrest-driven supply risks, keeping copper sentiment bearish.

Bloomberg reports GM is in advanced negotiations with Vale for a stake in Vale’s newly established base metals division, which supplies nickel and copper for EV batteries. With automakers scaling EV production, Vale expects sizable growth in nickel and copper demand, and GM’s bids could tighten expectations despite no deal being finalized. | Copper eased on China factory-gate price weakness and weaker U.S. employment data that raised demand and recession worries, while Las Bambas in Peru maintained reduced output after road blockades. Nickel surged after Trafigura disclosed a large nickel-fraud-related loss and rising expectations around low-stock liquidity. Aluminium also edged lower. | Copper fell as China factory-gate prices and weaker U.S. unemployment claims revived demand and recession worries. Nickel jumped after Trafigura disclosed a large charge tied to alleged systematic fraud involving nickel cargoes. Aluminium also eased on the day, indicating a mixed but mainly risk-driven tape across industrial metals. | Copper is set for a third straight weekly loss as weak Chinese demand and soft Chinese inventory/factory data weigh on prices. The dollar firmed after strong U.S. employment data and investors wait for U.S. inflation signals. LME copper fell, and LME aluminium also declined on the broader negative sentiment. | Lawmakers and copper industry groups are lobbying Interior/USGS to add copper to the formal US critical-minerals list. Copper is currently excluded despite rising supply-risk and rising import dependence. A designation would enhance domestic-sourcing benefits under IRA EV tax credits and potentially increase demand expectations for copper. | Comex copper for February delivery settled 0.87% lower at $4.0275/lb, falling for three consecutive weeks and down 10 of the past 14 sessions. LME copper cash was also lower (-0.27%). The day’s move was particularly weak, with a steep one-day percentage decline. | Authorities will allow a partial reopening of the scrap-iron industry, but the prohibition on exporting copper will continue for at least another year. Only limited by-product copper manufacturers are exempt. A transitional period before full licensing applies restricts new scrap-dealer participation, keeping export supply constraints in place.

Copper futures edged lower as the US Dollar Index rebounded ahead of upcoming US inflation data, raising investor caution. LME and SHFE copper finished down. Domestic copper inventories fell modestly, mainly on stronger Guangdong consumption, but east China stocks still rose. With demand still weak, prices may drift lower. | SMM data show China’s mainstream copper stocks rose 3,400 mt to 316,300 mt over the weekend. The increase is tempered by limited imported arrivals and smelters slowing shipments to Shanghai amid spot-premium levels. Consumption in Shanghai is improving, while downstream replenishment may increase if prices stop falling. | London copper edged up 0.2% but stayed near a one-week low as traders were cautious ahead of U.S. CPI. The dollar’s rise weighed, but China bank-loan data supported demand hopes. LME aluminium also rose 0.4%, while SHFE nickel fell 3.4% amid weaker sentiment. | SMM reports the Imported Copper Concentrate Index falling to $79.38/mt, with further TCs reduced amid tepid spot activity. Improving logistics at American mines is offset by South America shipment issues affecting contract deliveries. Deals show lower clean ore TCs (e.g., $75/mt and $65/mt), tempering buyer inquiries and sentiment for copper. | After the Chinese Lantern Festival, copper scrap sales picked up as sellers moved aggressively amid high copper prices and inventories built before the CNY holiday due to weak consumption. Scrap supply is described as adequate, with still-large volumes available at Ningbo port, despite prior loss-making conditions late December to January. | Nevada Copper said its Pumpkin Hollow underground mine restart is progressing, with completed dike crossings, definition drilling/assays of initial EN Zone stopes, and a construction contract for key mill/ore-handling and dewatering systems. The company targets a mill restart in Q3 2023 and ramp-up toward nameplate capacity, supporting near-term copper supply expectations. | Trilogy Metals will publish updated feasibility study results for its Arctic copper-zinc-lead-silver-gold project in Alaska, and hold an investor call/webinar. The project is copper-dominant, which can affect market expectations about future copper supply, but no immediate production change is indicated.

Comex copper for February delivery settled up 1.48% at $4.0870 per pound, gaining 5.95 cents. The contract was up on two of the past three sessions and remains above recent lows, though still below its 52-week high and off its 2023 settlement peak. | LME and SHFE copper rose overnight as the U.S. dollar index fell, with traders focused on upcoming U.S. CPI and interest-rate expectations. Chinese tracked copper stocks increased, but inventory growth slowed due to reduced smelter shipments to warehouses and scarce imported arrivals, while downstream demand remained tepid. | Copper edged higher as the U.S. dollar weakened ahead of U.S. inflation data. LME three-month copper rose 0.2% and Shanghai March copper gained 0.7%. Gains were also supported by Freeport-McMoRan cutting its copper sales forecast after rains and landslides shut the Grasberg mine until end-month. | ASX-listed Xanadu Mines advances its Kharmagtai copper-gold deposit in Mongolia near Rio Tinto’s Oyu Tolgoi, citing a large resource, PEA economics and new exploration and funding steps with Zijin. Approval-driven funding supports longer-term supply expectations, but first copper concentrate is targeted for 2027, limiting near-term price impact. | Meridian Mining UK reported that georeferenced historical BP archives uncovered the strongest copper stream anomaly at its Cabaçal Alvorada prospect (peak 164 ppm Cu) and plans additional mapping/geochemical/ground geophysics followed by diamond drilling. The company also restarted near-mine programs and is progressing a PEA for the project. | Ivanhoe Electric announced a major resource upgrade at its Santa Cruz copper project in Arizona, increasing indicated tonnage and contained copper, adding resources at East Ridge and Texaco, and reporting high potential recovery from metallurgical leach-float tests. The update is intended to underpin an initial economic assessment later in Q3 2023. | Copper prices have stalled since early February as China’s demand recovery disappoints. CME data show copper futures near recent lows. The bearish tone is reinforced by rising SHFE inventories, a stronger dollar, weaker manufacturing, and disruptions including strikes and temporary mine shutdowns in Peru alongside Chile’s output decline. | New Energy Metals announced TSX Venture Exchange approval for its Troitsa Copper property option, outlining cash and share payments to potentially acquire a 100% interest. It also provided an update on a Roslyn lithium option agreement, but the only direct tracked-metal item is the copper property approval, with no near-term production impact described.
